## Cycle 560 — **kids** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 2 render approaches, shipped 1

- **[/dream/964-kids-coral-garden](/dream/964-kids-coral-garden)** — Coral Garden: **tilt the tablet and a glowing bioluminescent reef GROWS ITSELF across the screen — and the coral *sings* the slow, warm melody its growing tip traces. A calm, never-silent, never-wrong bedtime toy.** `demoable` · Cycle 560 (kids 4+ / **device-tilt (primary)** + tap-to-seed / **pure Canvas2D bioluminescent glow**). **The lab's FIRST differential-growth piece** — a genuinely never-used (grep-0×) named generative-art technique, deliberately chosen *after a grep killed the obvious pick:* a squishy soft-body "jelly" toy would have been the lab's **6th** kids blob/jelly/Verlet piece (286/451/460/603/755) — the exact "too similar in design and theme" trap the JURY 2026-06-25 named. Instead the coral is a real **differential growth** simulation: each strand is a polyline whose nodes (1) attract toward connected neighbors, (2) **repel all nearby nodes via an O(n) uniform-grid spatial hash** (so the line refuses to self-intersect and wrinkles into organic brain-coral folds), (3) smooth curvature, (4) **insert new nodes as edges lengthen** (so the line *must* fold into the space — the algorithm's signature), and (5) occasionally **branch** a growing tip; ~2200-node cap → slows to a shimmer. A fresh garden looks like a reef after ~30–60s and is denser/different after 3 min (**genuine long-form state + memory** — answers the menu's thin "long-form generative" lane), with a ~12-min "goodnight" fade. **Music without a harmony engine:** a calm ~380–520ms clock maps the most-active growing TIP's vertical position to a warm **C-major pentatonic** note (top = higher) on a soft sine/triangle bell, over an always-on low-C+G drone, with brighter octave-up bells on branch events — **the coral literally composes the slow melody it traces** (pitch/melodic-CONTOUR IS the idea — answers JURY #2 — but melodic, NOT the over-used vertical voice-leading the kids lane hardened into: 941 voice-leading, 950 role-harmony, 957 cadence). **Calm + bedtime** directly answers the jury's "kids builds are too bright/active, calm is under-served." **Input = device-tilt PRIMARY** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission()` inside the Start tap → a gravity/light vector the coral tips lean toward within ~0.5s; **tilt is 1×/10 in the kids audit — deliberately dodges the touch-drag monoculture, 5 of the last ~7 kids builds**); tap-to-seed is a secondary garnish; desktop pointer-drag + idle auto-demo so a hands-free 06:30 glance sees coral blooming + hears the melody within ~1s. **Output = pure Canvas2D** luminous glow (layered `shadowBlur` bloom/mid/core + radial-gradient seed/tip nodes + trailing-alpha persistence over an indigo→violet→teal underwater gradient) — the **bulletproof everywhere-render with zero GPU-context risk on a phone**, and the **fresher/scarcer surface** per the latest jury (three.js BANNED 7×; WebGPU + raw-WebGL2 both recently hit the audit cap; Canvas2D collapsed to 3/15 → fresh-again). **Kids-safe audio:** `masterGain (≤0.26) → lowpass ~6.5kHz → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`, no wrong notes, loudness normalized 1/√voices (a busy garden never gets louder), no harsh/high transients. **Ambition floor 4/5** (#1 never-used technique [**differential growth**; grep-0× in INDEX/READMEs] · #2 ≥3 subsystems [differential-growth solver w/ spatial-hash repulsion + tilt/gravity input + melody/branch scheduler + Canvas2D glow render = 4] · #3 named refs [**Anders Hoff / *Inconvergent* "Differential Line"** · **Entagma** differential-growth tutorials · **arXiv:2504.18040 "Cabbage" (Apr 2025)**] · #5 in-README dated **RESEARCH §560** — 25 straight cycles 535–560). #4 NOT claimed (single-cycle ship). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×), WebGPU-OUTPUT (audit cap), raw-WebGL2-OUTPUT (audit cap), touch-drag-pitch INPUT (kids monoculture 5×), voice-leading/harmony-engine TECH (kids 941/950/957 + adult 7-of-10), soft-body/blob/Verlet THEME (5 prior kids jelly pieces)] · picked=[device-tilt INPUT (1×/10, non-touch, embodied), **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (fresh-again, bulletproof, non-three.js/WebGPU/WebGL2), **differential-growth TECH** (0×; melodic-contour music, NOT a harmony engine, NOT soft-body), calm bioluminescent-underwater bedtime VIBE (off the bright-playful kids cluster + off cosmic-nebula adult cluster)]` — clears every ban. **No API route → no `guard`** (pure client; zero AI/network/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (Web Audio + Canvas2D). **Compile + ESLint + full-project type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` → **`✓ Compiled successfully in 2.4min`** → Linting/type-check PASS; grep-confirmed zero warnings/errors attributable to 964/coral — all lint warnings are pre-existing `src/lib/**` + other prototypes); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT device/ear-verified** (no tilt sensor or audio in container; the differential-growth solver + contour→pentatonic mapping are reasoned-correct + tsc/eslint-clean, but the glow feel, growth pacing, and tilt response are unconfirmed by hand/eye/ear — the always-on drone + auto-demo guarantee a sounding, blooming glance with zero interaction). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 2-render fire (Canvas2D vs WebGL2 of the same engine); banked **`963-kids-coral-bloom` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (kids)** — the IDENTICAL differential-growth coral on **raw WebGL2 additive-glow quads** (the richer-bloom twin; resurrect on a desktop/Tauri context where the GPU lands, or fold its glow into 964). Returned complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean; `cp`'d to scratchpad then `rm -rf`'d before the winner-only build (NOT committed; IDEAS §560). Design notes: `src/app/dream/964-kids-coral-garden/README.md`

## Earlier (Cycle 558 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 "real melody/harmony IS the toy" explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/957-kids-come-home](/dream/957-kids-come-home)** — Come Home: **drag a glowing firefly up a hill and feel it want to fall *home* — near the top it trembles at the leading tone, pulled toward the tonic, and when you let go it swoops down and lands on a warm chord. A real V→I cadence a 4-year-old plays with one finger.** `demoable` · Cycle 558 (kids 4+ / **touch-drag (release = resolve)** / **raw WebGL2 → Canvas2D fallback**). **The first KIDS piece in the lab where real tonal tension-and-resolution IS the toy — the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-25 #2** ("make music from PITCH again; pitch as expressive material, not the thing you apologize for omitting") on the kids axis, where *every* prior kids build held pitch deliberately dumb ("no wrong notes" + drone) — the exact crystallized formula the same jury flagged as a pruning candidate (935-kids-shake-critters). Here the music is the **felt pull of harmony**: the hill is a C-major diatonic ladder (do–re–mi–fa–sol–la–**ti**); dragging the firefly up raises the pitch and the *tension* (hotter color, faster shimmer, a detune wobble), near the top the **leading tone (ti)** visibly trembles wanting home, and releasing swoops it down to the **tonic** with a warm I-chord "ahh" bloom — a genuine **V→I leading-tone cadence**. Under the melody an **auto-harmony crossfades I↔V** by tension² so the child hears the cadence, not a lone note. **Grounded in 1-day-old research:** François Pachet's **arXiv:2606.24911** ("Attractive and Repulsive Pattern Control in Sequence Generation," Jun 2026) frames a target pattern as an *attractor* the sequence is drawn toward — the **tonic is the attractor**, the firefly is *pulled home*; the literal today's-research→today's-build chain. **Hands-off auto-demo within ~0.9s** — a ghost firefly rises to the leading tone, trembles, releases, resolves home on a ~7.5s loop, so an untouched 06:30 glance is alive and sounding the cadence; real touch overrides, 4s idle resumes. **Renderer = raw WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00: dusk gradient that warms with tension, dark rolling hill, home glow, additive firefly bloom + trail, GPU tremble) → **Canvas2D fallback** painting the identical scene (`text-rose-300` "(showing the simple view)" notice) → context-loss handling + full teardown; **NOT three.js** (jury-banned 7×), **NOT WebGPU** (at the diversity edge ~4×/10 across 954/950/947/942 + unvalidated headless — the robust WebGL2/Canvas2D cascade is the bulletproof kids surface for the unattended glance). **Kids-safe audio** (`gain 0.24 → lowpass 7000Hz → DynamicsCompressor(-10/20:1) → destination`): always-on soft pad, triangle+sine-octave melody voice with a tension detune-wobble LFO, `bloomHome()` warm C-E-G resolution; pitch quantized to 7 rungs so **no wrong note is possible**; AudioContext created/resumed in the Start gesture (iOS); ≥64px Start, ≥44px controls; dark-bg typography rules. **Single drag gesture, no permission flow, no mic, no granular, no network** — the most robust straight-to-prod path of the three. **Ambition floor 3/5** (#1 never-used technique [**functional tonal tension → V→I cadence as the interaction itself**; grep-0× in INDEX/READMEs — the lab has scales, chords, JI, voice-leading, but never *the felt pull of resolution* as the toy, and never in the kids lane] · #3 named refs [**Pachet, arXiv:2606.24911, Jun 2026** · "Sad syntax? tonal closure in children," arXiv:2008.01810 · **Bernstein** on the appoggiatura] · #5 in-README dated **RESEARCH §558** — 23 straight cycles 535–558). #2/#4 NOT claimed (single audio source+canvas = 2 subsystems; single-cycle ship). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×), WebGPU-OUTPUT (audit ~4×/10), voice-leading/chord-progression-engine (kids recipe 941/945/950)] · picked=[touch-drag-release INPUT (non-mic/camera/tilt), **raw-WebGL2→Canvas2D OUTPUT** (fresh, non-WebGPU/three.js, bulletproof), **functional-tension/V→I-cadence TECH** (real harmonic *pull* IS the idea — the opposite of pitch-held-dumb; NOT granular, NOT a voice-leading lookup), warm-dusk tender VIBE (off both the cosmic-nebula adult cluster and the bright-playful kids cluster)]` — clears every ban and is the directest jury-#2 hit. **No API route → no `guard`** (pure client; zero AI/network/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (Web Audio + WebGL2/Canvas2D). **Compile + ESLint + full-project type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached **`Collecting page data`** = compile/lint/type all PASS; zero warnings/errors attributable to 957 — every lint warning is pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; `ulimit -n` 4096, cgroup-locked; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/ear-verified** (no WebGL device or audio in container; the cadence mapping + I↔V crossfade are reasoned-correct, static-only). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire, all three making real melody/harmony the toy via a *different* renderer × input × technique; banked **`959-kids-chord-kites` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (kids)** — tilt to fly two kites, the *interval* between them is the harmony (organum), **raw WebGPU compute** particle wind-field — and **`958-kids-stepping-stones`** — place lily-pad stones at heights, a frog hops your melody's *contour* (step vs leap), **Canvas2D**. Both returned complete + self-reported clean; `cp`'d to scratchpad then `rm -rf`'d before the winner-only build (NOT committed; IDEAS §558). ## Earlier (Cycle 557 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 harmony explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/954-sync-bloom](/dream/954-sync-bloom)** — Sync Bloom: **a field of 1024 coupled oscillators on the GPU — scribble coupling into them and watch sub-populations spontaneously phase-LOCK into glowing colour-bands, each locked cluster snapping to a just-intonation pitch, so the chord you hear is literally the field reaching consensus.** `demoable` · Cycle 557 (adult / **pointer-brush + autonomous K** / **raw-WebGPU `@compute` → WebGL2/CPU fallback**). **The lab's first Kuramoto coupled-oscillator synchronization piece, and the single object that answers BOTH dominant jury provocations at once** — JURY 2026-06-25 **#1** (WebGPU compute is *still* the scarce 1×/15 surface — push GPU onto **raw WGSL `@compute`**, NOT three.js) **and #2** (kill the "pitch held deliberately dumb" boilerplate; make real **harmony** the idea). **The concept is the surprise:** instead of looking a chord up from a grammar, you *grow* it — harmony as **emergent synchronization consensus**. The mechanism is real physics: N=1024 oscillators step `dθ_i/dt = ω_i + K_i·r·sin(ψ−θ_i)` via the mean-field order parameter `r·e^{iψ}=(1/N)Σe^{iθ_j}` (O(N), not O(N²)); when coupling K beats the natural-frequency spread, sub-populations **phase-lock** — which is *physically what consonance is* (simple ratios phase-lock; dissonance beats). **On the GPU:** a WGSL `@compute` **reduce pass** (tree-reduction → order parameter) + **advance pass** (each phase steps toward the mean field) + instanced glowing render (hue=phase, bloom=sync); async `mapAsync` reads phases back, a histogram detects locked **clusters**, each cluster's effective frequency snaps to the nearest **just-intonation partial** (1, 9/8, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 15/8, 2) of a drifting root → the set of locked clusters = the current chord, voiced by warm **additive + FM** tones over a drone (NOT granular, NOT samples). **Interaction:** pointer brush drips coupling K into a soft region (scribble energy in → that patch crystallizes into sync and its pitch locks); a global-K slider sweeps scattered/beating ↔ locked/resolved; **autonomous K-breathing** on a slow sine so an idle glance self-organizes within ~1s. **Ambition floor 4/5** (#1 never-used technique [**Kuramoto coupled-oscillator synchronization → emergent JI harmony**; grep-0× in INDEX/READMEs] · #2 ≥3 subsystems [WGSL `@compute` solver + GPU phase-readback/cluster-detector + JI-snap harmony synth + instanced render = 4] · #3 named refs [**Y. Kuramoto 1975** · **Strogatz, *Sync* 2003** · **KoPE, arXiv 2604.07904, 2026** — the days-old DL revival of Kuramoto sync that prompted reading synchronization as a generative primitive] · #5 in-README dated **RESEARCH §557** — 22 straight cycles 535–557). #4 NOT claimed (single-cycle ship). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular-texture synth (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×)] · picked=[pointer-couple INPUT (non-mic), **raw-WebGPU `@compute` OUTPUT** (the scarce 1× surface — jury #1; WebGL2/CPU fallback, NOT three.js), **Kuramoto-sync→emergent-JI-harmony TECH** (real harmony IS the idea per jury #2 — NOT granular, NOT a drone, NOT a voice-leading lookup), luminous self-organizing sync-field VIBE (off the over-rep cosmic-nebula adult cluster)]` — clears every ban and is the *only* recent build on raw-WebGPU-compute. **No API route → no `guard`** (pure client; zero AI/network/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (Web Audio + WebGPU/WebGL2; the repo's existing `webgpu.d.ts`/`@webgpu/types` resolve the WGSL types). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached **`Collecting page data`** = compile/lint/type all PASS; zero warnings/errors attributable to 954 — all lint warnings are pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/ear-verified** (no WebGPU device or audio in container; the GPU readback timing + cluster→JI mapping are reasoned-correct, static-only). Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire, all three answering jury #2 via a *different mechanism × surface*; banked **`955-cadence-river` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (adult)** — an autonomous long-form **functional-harmony chorale** (T→PD→D grammar + SATB voice-leading + circle-of-fifths modulation, raw-WebGL2 voice-ribbons) — and **`956-counterpoint-weave`** — draw a cantus firmus, a **species-counterpoint** engine (Fux) weaves 1–3 voices on Canvas2D. Both returned complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean; `cp`'d to scratchpad then `rm -rf`'d before the winner-only build (NOT committed; IDEAS §557). Design notes: `src/app/dream/954-sync-bloom/README.md`

## Earlier (Cycle 556 — **kids** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 2 render approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/953-kids-iron-garden](/dream/953-kids-iron-garden)** — Iron Garden: **drag glowing magnet-flowers around a dark field and watch thousands of iron filings snap into the invisible lines of force between them — then slide two flowers together and their notes bloom into a chord the instant their fields connect** 🧲🌸✨. `demoable` · Cycle 556 (kids 4+ / **multi-touch drag** / **Canvas2D particle field**). **Invisible-physics-made-visible applied to MAGNETISM, not the over-done cymatics.** This cycle's research dive found the 2026 cymatics-toy resurgence (CymaVis, Cymatica, Chladni Screen — all 2026), and the lab already did cymatics twice (`19-cymatics`, `165-cymatics`); so I applied the *same* "make the invisible field visible + audible" idea to a physics the lab has **never** touched — Faraday's magnetic **lines of force**. **The field is real:** each flower is a 2D dipole (a + pole and a − pole offset along its axis); ~4,200 iron filings each frame sample the superposed field `B(P)=Σ q·(P−pole)/|P−pole|³` (`field.ts`), orient as short streaks **along** the local field direction, slide along it to pile into the visible lines of force, and take their nearest flower's hue — drawn as additive (`globalCompositeOperation:"lighter"`) hue-bucketed strokes for glowing chains. **Harmony is emergent + physical, not a voice-leading engine:** 6 flowers on a pentatonic scale (C3 E3 G3 A3 C4 E4 — **no wrong notes**); when two flowers' fields bridge (proximity²), a glowing connecting ribbon draws and their two notes ring together as a soft chord (gain ∝ proximity²); grabbing one alone hums its single note scaled to motion. **Kid-safe audio chain:** two detuned triangle oscillators per voice → lowpass → convolver plate-reverb bloom + `DynamicsCompressor` limiter → master; a very quiet in-key pad so a cold load isn't silent. **Self-demo:** untouched ~4s, the flowers drift along smooth incommensurate-sine noise paths, attracting/separating on their own (a "tap to begin" gate satisfies autoplay; `text-rose-300` notice if Canvas2D/Web-Audio is missing). **Distinct from `192-kids-magnet-notes`** (point-attraction between orbs) — this renders the FIELD ITSELF, thousands of filings tracing the lines of force. **Ambition floor 4/5** (#1 never-used technique [real magnetic-dipole field + iron-filing alignment as the instrument; grep-0× — 192 is point-attraction, the existing cymatics are *acoustic* Chladni] · #2 ≥3 subsystems [multi-touch magnet drag + dipole-superposition field solver + filing alignment/advection sim + pentatonic synth + field-bridge harmony detector + Canvas2D render = 5] · #3 named refs [**Michael Faraday's "lines of force"**; the classic iron-filings demonstration] · #5 in-README dated **RESEARCH §556** — 21 straight cycles 535–556). #4 NOT claimed (single-cycle ship; GPU-dense sibling banked). **Renderer choice = the jury read:** the jury flagged a **12-of-15 GPU monoculture**; my audit found WebGPU over the cap (950/947/942/941) and Canvas2D fresh (only 952 in the last-10), so a robust **Canvas2D** kids piece (runs on any iPad, no float-render-target dependency, no per-frame React churn) is the deliberate counter-swing. `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb" stance (JURY ~10×), WebGPU-OUTPUT (audit ≥4×/10), voice-leading/chord-progression-engine (kids recipe 941/945/950)] · picked=[multi-touch-drag INPUT (non-mic/camera/tilt), Canvas2D OUTPUT (fresh, non-WebGPU/three.js), magnetic-dipole-field + iron-filing-alignment TECH with field-bridge EMERGENT harmony (non-voice-leading, non-granular), science-wonder/tactile VIBE (off the harmony-garden recipe)]` — clears every ban. **No API route → no `guard`** (pure client; zero AI/network/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (Web Audio + Canvas2D only). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 43s`** → lint/type PASS, grep-confirmed zero "Failed to compile"/"Type error"; one type error caught + fixed in the one allowed attempt — TS resets `canvas` null-narrowing inside nested closures, aliased to a declared-non-null binding); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT ear/touch-verified** (no audio device or multi-touch in container). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 2-approach fire; banked **`954-kids-iron-garden-gl` ⭐** (same concept, **raw-WebGL2** GPU-advected ~80k filings — denser/silkier; IDEAS §556). Design notes: `src/app/dream/953-kids-iron-garden/README.md`

## Earlier (Cycle 554 — **kids** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 role/render approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/950-kids-sky-conductor](/dream/950-kids-sky-conductor)** — Sky Conductor: **two 4-year-olds, two iPads, one tiny orchestra — one child CONDUCTS (sweeps the device to set the tempo and which warm chord the sky is on) and the other PLAYS notes that automatically fit the conductor's current chord, so they make real chords-and-melody together and nobody can play a wrong note** 🎼🌈🤝. `demoable` · Cycle 554 (kids 4+ / **device-tilt conductor + tap player + serverless WebRTC multi-user** / **raw WebGPU → raw-WebGL2 fallback**). **The deepening of the lab's WebRTC category-opener** (`918-kids-starlight-friend`, where two kids fling *identical* twinkles with no roles/structure) into **"a conducted two-player ensemble with roles"** — the *literal* phrasing of **JURY 2026-06-25 #4** ("develop the category-openers, don't open a fifth … the WebRTC room → a conducted two-player ensemble with roles"). Also the directest kids answer to **#1** (push GPU onto **raw WebGPU**, the scarcest 1×/10 surface — NOT three.js) and **#2** (make music from real **pitch/harmony** again — here a warm **I–IV–V–vi** progression with the melody voiced into the live chord, the idea carried by the *role split* not a voice-leading engine, so it dodges the hardening 550/551/942 voice-leading template). **INPUT** device-tilt/sweep (conductor) + tap (player) + **serverless WebRTC** co-play (`harmony.ts` `voiceTap()` snaps every player tap to a chord tone of the conductor's current chord → no wrong note); on-screen **drag fallback** when tilt is unavailable; **NOT mic** (jury-banned 4×). **OUTPUT** primary **raw WebGPU/WGSL** fragment sky (chord-colored gradient, tempo-pulsed shimmer, note-blooms) → hand-written **raw-WebGL2** fallback painting the identical `SkyState` → `text-rose-300` notice if neither; **NOT three.js**. **Networking** reimplements 918's serverless handshake (host `createOffer()` → ICE → **gzip SDP via `CompressionStream` → base64url `#join=` link** → guest answer code → host paste; data channel `ordered:false/maxRetransmits:0`; tiny `{role:'conductor',chord,bpm}` / `{role:'player',note}` events; both peers synth+render locally) — **no signaling server, no TURN, zero backend, no api route / no `guard`**. **Ghost-friend** auto-plays whichever role the local child isn't, within ~1s, driving a full chord-progression-with-melody demo on a cold untouched device, and bows out the instant a real data channel opens (networking purely additive) — guarantees a sounding, color-changing, two-player-looking glance with zero interaction. **Kids-safe chain** `gain(≤0.24) → lowpass 6.5kHz → DynamicsCompressor(-10/20:1) → destination`; always-on warm pad chord-bed that glides on chord changes; bell/triangle melody (≥20ms attacks); ≥64px tap targets; no reading; full teardown (rAF + oscillators + `ctx.close()` + deviceorientation/resize listeners + RTCPeerConnection/channel + ghost timer + GPU/GL resources). **Zero new npm deps** (WebGPU/WebGL2 + Web Audio + built-in WebRTC/CompressionStream + the already-present `@webgpu/types`). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [WebRTC role-networking + shared clock/progression engine + dual-role pad+melody synth + WebGPU/WebGL2 render = 4] · #3 named refs [**The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers**; **Orff Schulwerk** children's-ensemble pedagogy; the **Frontiers Psychology 2026** group-music-game study where the instrumental-*ensemble* phase itself drove cooperation] · #4 multi-cycle [this IS cycle-2 of 918's stated commitment — cycle-1 shared sky, cycle-2 roles/structure, cycle-3 3+ players] · #5 in-README dated RESEARCH §554 — **19 cycles running** 535–554). #1 NOT claimed (WebRTC + WebGPU both have lab priors; the fresh axis is *role-complementarity as the deepening*, not a lab-first technique). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×)] · picked=[device-tilt + WebRTC-multiuser INPUT (non-mic; deepens the 1× WebRTC opener per jury #4), raw-WebGPU→WebGL2 OUTPUT (the scarce 1× GPU surface — jury #1; NOT three.js), role-split chord-context-+-voiced-melody harmony TECH (real I–IV–V–vi, pitch IS the idea, NOT granular, NOT a voice-leading engine), warm social kids-ensemble VIBE]` — clears every ban. **Mode = DEEP** (1 concept × 3 role/render approaches — alternating after two WIDE nights 552/553, and jury #4 explicitly says "go DEEP on one" category-opener). **Love-aligned** — extends the loved loop/layering + kids-character cluster (`172-loop-station`❤️, `111-kids-shape-loop`❤️, `234-kids-hand-creature`❤️, `317-kids-color-bells`❤️): "you and a friend make a song together" is the social form of that layered-build joy. **Siblings banked (IDEAS §554):** **`948-kids-duet-grove` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (kids)** — groove-keeper + melody-maker on ONE shared 8-step clock, **raw WebGL2** (the most viscerally legible "we made a song" + the bulletproof no-WebGPU twin; banked only because WebGL2 sits at the 4×/10 audit ban-edge where the winner lands the scarce WebGPU) + **`949-kids-echo-birds`** — call-and-response turn-taking bird-conversation, **Canvas2D** (the freshest renderer + most dialogic, but doesn't advance the GPU ask + harmonically thinnest). Both returned complete + self-reported clean; `cp`'d to scratchpad then `rm -rf`'d before the winner-only build (NOT committed). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 54s`** → Linting/type-check PASS; grep-confirmed **zero** "950"/"sky-conductor"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS" — all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; `ulimit -n` 4096, cgroup-locked; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/device/ear-verified** (no WebGPU/audio/motion-sensor/second-peer in container — unverified: whether the WGSL compiles + the WebGL2 fallback matches on real hardware [robust cascade + ghost cover every path], whether two iPads connect over the serverless gzip-SDP link [great same-LAN/hotspot = the two-iPads-side-by-side case; symmetric-NAT pairs may not, but that path is purely additive], and whether a 4yo reads "tilt = conduct the sky / tap = sing into it"; the always-on pad + ghost-friend auto-co-play guarantee a sounding, harmonising, two-player glance regardless). Design notes: `src/app/dream/950-kids-sky-conductor/README.md`

## Cycle 553 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1

- **[/dream/947-overtone-loom](/dream/947-overtone-loom)** — Overtone Loom: **play a chord and SEE why it sounds sweet or sour.** Each note is a stack of harmonic partials; a **raw WebGPU compute shader** paints the live **sensory-dissonance field** (Plomp–Levelt roughness) between every pair of sounding partials along a log-frequency axis — octaves & fifths **lock dark and smooth**, tritones & minor-2nds **churn bright** — and a **timbre knob** moves which intervals read as consonant, making tangible the 2026 finding that *consonance is a property of the spectrum, not abstract ratios* 🟦🎹📈. `demoable` · Cycle 553 (adult / **note-keyboard + MIDI** / **raw WebGPU compute**). **The lab's first dissonance-curve-as-instrument** and the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-25 #1** (push GPU onto **raw WebGPU compute** — the scarcest 1×/10 surface, NOT three.js) **and #2** (make music from real PITCH/harmony again — here the *psychoacoustic substrate of consonance itself* IS the idea, a fresh facet distinct from the 550/551 voice-leading cluster). **note-keyboard + Web-MIDI INPUT** (~1.75-octave on-screen chromatic keyboard, pointer/touch; optional `navigator.requestMIDIAccess` — **NOT mic**, jury-banned 4×). **raw-WebGPU/WGSL compute OUTPUT** — one compute invocation per frequency bin (512 bins dispatched) sums roughness against every sounding partial, rendered as a clinical cyan/white Ikeda interference field; degrades **WebGPU → raw-WebGL2 fragment (same math) → Canvas2D CPU** with a visible amber notice. **Additive synthesis** (banks of Web Audio oscillators per voice, 1/n harmonic rolloff — **NOT granular**, jury-banned 4×) + the canonical **Sethares/Plomp–Levelt** model (`dissonance.ts`: `pairRoughness`, `totalDissonance`, `fieldAt` — the same math mirrored in WGSL + GLSL). A scalar **total-dissonance meter** rises/falls as you stack notes; the timbre/partial-count sliders prove the §553 paper's point by shifting the consonant intervals. Master chain `gain(≤0.22) → lowpass 7kHz → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; adding notes busies the field, never spikes loudness. **~1.2s-idle auto-demo** cycles unison → octave → fifth → triad → tritone → minor-2nd → cluster so a hands-off glance *hears & sees* the consonance/dissonance contrast within ~1s; full teardown (oscillators + `ctx.close()` + rAF + GPU buffers + MIDI/resize listeners). **No API route / no `guard`** (all client-side, no network). **Zero new npm deps.** README cites **RESEARCH §553 (2026-06-25)** — "Elementary spectrum for the dissonance curve" (*Journal of Mathematics and Music* 2026, DOI 10.1080/17459737.2026.2628778), **Helmholtz, *On the Sensations of Tone* (1863)**, **Plomp & Levelt (1965)**, **Sethares, *Tuning, Timbre, Spectrum, Scale* (1998)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **18 cycles running** (535–553). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#1 never-used technique [dissonance-curve / sensory-roughness field as a playable instrument — grep-0× in the lab] · #3 named refs [Helmholtz · Plomp–Levelt · Sethares · the 2026 paper] · #5 dated RESEARCH §553). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×), cosmic-ambient adult vibe (5×)] · picked=[note-keyboard/MIDI INPUT (off banned mic), raw-WebGPU-compute OUTPUT (the scarcest 1× GPU surface — jury #1; degrades WebGL2→Canvas2D), additive + Sethares-dissonance TECH (consonance IS the idea, non-granular), clinical Ikeda spectral-lab VIBE (off the cosmic-nebula cluster)]` — clears every ban. **Mode = WIDE** (3 orthogonal explorers; the 2026-06-25 jury is dominantly a spread/ban signal and its named DEEP targets were just-done [depth, by 551] or rotation-mismatched [AI-chain voice-ban, WebRTC is kids] — so three fresh-tag bets beat one more harmony concept). **Love-aligned** — sits exactly at the intersection of Karel's loved **spectral cluster** (`223-fourier-paint`❤️, `243-spectral-cloud`❤️, `267-spectral-drift`❤️, `321-spectral-flight`❤️) and **GPU compute** (`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️). **Siblings banked (IDEAS §553):** **`948-cantus-engine` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (adult)** — a long-form (5+ min) self-developing **counterpoint** engine (seed a 4-note motif → Fux/Bach inversion·retrograde·augmentation·stretto·modulation over a ~330s escalating arc; raw-WebGL2 scrolling score); the lab's long-form-stateful gap, lost only because WebGL2 is less scarce than WebGPU compute + the counterpoint lane was just mined (550/551/552). **`949-maqam-road`** — a microtonal Arabic **maqam** instrument (4 maqamat by exact cents, true quarter-tone portamento + ornaments, tonic+fifth drone; Canvas2D "road"); the non-Western-pitch surprise. Both returned complete + self-reported clean; `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-553/` then `rm -rf`'d before the winner-only build (NOT committed). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 110s`** → Linting/type-check PASS; one fix attempt landed = corrected a Web-MIDI ambient-type collision [`MIDIAccess` vs DOM `MidiAccess`] by adopting the DOM WebMIDI types like `902-harmonic-mirror`; 947 carries one **non-fatal** `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` warning [ref-in-cleanup — same class as the pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings; warnings don't fail `next build`]; all other warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/ear-verified** (no WebGPU/audio in container — unverified: whether the WGSL compute shader compiles + the roughness field reads as intended on real hardware [robust WebGL2 + Canvas2D + auto-demo fallbacks cover every failure], and whether the additive timbre + dissonance contrast are audible on a real speaker; the auto-demo guarantees a sounding, evolving glance with zero interaction).

## Earlier (Cycle 552 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/946-kids-bird-round](/dream/946-kids-bird-round)** — Bird Round: a 4-year-old **drags a glowing bird up & down a tree to sing a little tune** (height = pitch, snapped to a friendly pentatonic so it's always musical); let go and the bird **remembers the melody and loops it**, then tap *add-a-bird* and a second bird sings the **same tune offset in time** — a real **canon / round** ("Row, Row, Row Your Boat") that blooms into layered counterpoint as the flock grows 🐦🎶🌅. `demoable` · Cycle 552 (kids / **touch-drag** / **raw WebGL2**). **The lab's first KIDS canon/counterpoint piece** (302-mirror-canon was adult-only) and the directest kids answer to **JURY 2026-06-25 #2** (*make music from real PITCH/melody again* — melody IS the playable idea here, breaking the banned "pitch held deliberately dumb" stance). **touch-DRAG INPUT** — vertical finger position over time = a melody; live chirp under the finger (throttled), released drag → `makeMelodyFromDrag` quantizes the samples to 8 steps over ~4 beats + snaps each to a C-pentatonic scale → stored as the shared melody. **raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT** (`gl.ts`, hand-written GLSL — **NOT three.js**, jury #1) — a soft dawn-gold treetop with additive glowing birds + light-trails that pulse when they sing; **Canvas2D fallback** renders the same scene if WebGL2 is unavailable. **Shared-clock CANON scheduler** (`audio.ts/makeScheduler`) — ONE look-ahead clock anchored to a fixed start; each bird is a voice with a constant time offset (1 beat per added voice), all voices read the same melody so adding a bird = a time-shifted copy = a genuine round; loop wraparound handled, `pulses[]` exported so the renderer bobs/glows the exact bird that just sang. **Bright chirp synth** (triangle+sine with a quick upward pitch-glide attack, light 6.5 Hz vibrato, short bell/pluck env — **NOT granular**, jury ban) + a quiet tonic+fifth **pad** bed. Master chain `gain(≤0.26) → lowpass 7kHz → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; **more birds must NOT mean louder** — `applyFlockGain = MASTER_CEIL/√voices`. **Kids-safe & hands-off:** gesture-gated AudioContext, ≥64–96px emoji controls (▶️ start / 🐦➕ add-bird / 🔄 reset), no reading required, instant chirp/loop response; **~2s-idle auto-demo** sings a default tune and adds a 2nd bird so a hands-off glance hears a little round + sees two birds glowing within ~1–2s; full teardown (scheduler interval + pad + rAF + `ctx.close()`). **No API route / no `guard`** (no network, no AI gen — all client-side). **Zero new npm deps.** README cites the **round/canon** structure (Reich phasing as adult cousin), the **melodic-contour developmental finding**, and **Toshio Iwai's Electroplankton**. **Clears ambition floor 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [drag-to-melody capture/quantizer + shared-clock canon scheduler + chirp synth + WebGL2 renderer = 4] · #3 named refs [round/canon · melodic-contour research · Electroplankton]). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×)] · picked=[touch-drag INPUT (touch scarce 1×), raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT (→Canvas2D fallback; off banned three.js), melody-capture + CANON/counterpoint TECH (melody IS the idea; non-granular chirp synth), warm dawn-treetop kids VIBE]` — clears every ban. **Mode = WIDE** (3 orthogonal explorers; deliberate alternation after two DEEP cycles 550/551 + a direct attack on the jury's "house style" critique). **Love-aligned** — extends the loved loop/layering lineage (`172-loop-station`❤️, `111-kids-shape-loop`❤️, `160-kids-paint-loop`❤️). **Siblings banked (IDEAS §552):** **944-kids-melody-coaster** (draw a hilly track, a cart rolls + sings the contour as a diatonic melody; WebGL2) + **945-kids-harmony-garden** (tap to plant note-flowers; consonant pairs grow "friend-vines" + the chord nearest-tone voice-leads; Canvas2D — the directest harmony/voice-leading answer, deferred only because it overlaps 941's harmony territory). Both returned complete + self-reported eslint-0; `rm -rf`'d before the winner-only build (NOT committed). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 2.0min`** → Linting/type-check PASS; grep-confirmed zero "946"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS"; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/ear-verified** (no WebGL2/audio in container — unverified: whether the canon offsets read as a genuinely harmonious *round* on a real speaker [always-on pad + auto-demo guarantee a sounding, layered glance], and whether drag-to-pitch feels intuitive on a real iPad; robust Canvas2D + pointer fallbacks cover desktop review).

## Earlier (Cycle 551 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 2 render approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/942-depth-harmonic-room](/dream/942-depth-harmonic-room)** — Depth Harmonic Room: stand in front of the webcam and your **distance + lateral position in the room** place you inside a neo-Riemannian **Tonnetz** of harmony — walking through space glides you through smooth, **voice-led chord changes** (P / L / R), rendered as a candle-warm WebGPU lattice that blooms as you lean in 🎹🕯️🟠. `demoable` · Cycle 551 (adult / **webcam depth** / **raw WebGPU**). **THE DEEPENING OF 927-depth-room — and the directest possible multi-provocation answer to JURY 2026-06-25.** It hits **#4** (develop a 0× category-opener — depth-camera — *don't open a fifth*: this turns 927's "distance → texture" into a multi-zone spatial *harmonic instrument you walk through*), **#2** (make music from real PITCH / voice-leading again — *harmony IS the idea here*, the literal opposite of 927's "pitch held deliberately dumb"), and **#1** (push GPU work onto **raw WebGPU**, the scarcest 1×/10 surface — NOT three.js). **webcam-DEPTH INPUT** — **Depth Anything V2 (small)** runs in-browser on WebGPU via **Transformers.js** (CDN at runtime, no npm dep), a 256×192 frame → per-pixel depth (larger = nearer) → 16×12 grid → **nearEnergy** (proximity → brightness + chord openness), **centroidX** (lateral → Tonnetz column), **depthBand** (lean → row), **motion** (→ shimmer/attack). Depth is the *deepened* category-opener (1× in last-10, sanctioned by jury #4). **Raw WebGPU/WGSL OUTPUT** — a full-screen fragment shader paints a warm volumetric room (amber/rose/deep-teal) with a triangular Tonnetz of glowing pitch-nodes; the **sounding triad-triangle glows brightest and blooms** with proximity, a transient fires on each transform, a focal glow marks where you stand. Degrades **WebGPU → raw WebGL2 (`webgl-fallback.ts`, same lattice) → DOM chord-view** — a 3-tier render cascade. **Neo-Riemannian voice-leading ENGINE** (`harmony.ts`) — every cell is a major/minor triad; adjacent cells differ by a single parsimonious **P** (parallel, third moves ½), **L** (Leittonwechsel, root down ½) or **R** (relative, fifth up a tone), each holding **exactly 2 of 3 common tones** (builder unit-verified across all 24 triads — involutions + 2-common-tone retention + a real R/L progression C→Am→F→Dm→B♭→Gm→E♭). centroidX walks the R/L third-chain (related keys, one step at a time); lean in/out applies **P** (major-bright ↔ minor-dark). **SATB nearest-tone voicing** (`voiceTriad`) — bass=root low; each upper voice takes the octave of a chord tone nearest where it already sits (all 6 assignments tried, min total semitone motion — Tymoczko's minimal voice-leading), so common tones literally don't move and the one changed tone slides shortest; warm pad voices (detuned sine+triangle → soft lowpass + slow vibrato) over a root **drone bed**, each re-voice **glides (portamento ~120–300ms)** — smooth, never clicky. Master chain `gain(≤0.28) → lowpass 7kHz → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; additive/subtractive synthesis (**NOT granular** — jury-banned). **Degrades fully:** no camera / model fails → **synthetic Lissajous presence-blob** auto-walks the Tonnetz within ~1s of Start (a hands-off glance *hears evolving harmony + sees the lattice glow*); laptop with no camera → **pointer** steers the lattice; on-device only (camera never recorded/uploaded); full teardown (rAF + listeners + webcam tracks + oscillators + `audioCtx.close()` + GPU destroy). **No API route / no `guard`** (no network, no AI image/voice gen — all client-side). **Zero new npm deps** (Web Audio + WebGPU + the runtime CDN Transformers.js import). README cites **RESEARCH §551 (2026-06-25)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **17 cycles running** (535–551). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [webcam-depth ML pipeline + neo-Riemannian Tonnetz/voice-leading engine + SATB synthesis + WebGPU/WebGL2 lattice render = 4] · #3 named refs [**Cohn, *Audacious Euphony* 2012**; **Tymoczko, *A Geometry of Music* 2011**; **Aldwell & Schachter, *Harmony and Voice Leading***; **Depth Anything V2 / Transformers.js**] · #5 dated RESEARCH §551). #1 NOT claimed (depth-camera has a lab prior — 927; the Tonnetz appears as a §533 seed `896-tonnetz-loom`; the lab-fresh axis is *depth-position → Tonnetz navigation on WebGPU*, not a brand-new technique). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7×), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×), raw-WebGL2-as-primary (audit 4×/10 edge), cosmic-ambient adult vibe (5×)] · picked=[webcam-depth INPUT (deepening the 1× category-opener per jury #4), raw-WebGPU OUTPUT (scarcest GPU surface 1×/10 — jury #1; degrades to WebGL2/DOM), neo-Riemannian voice-leading harmony TECH (harmony IS the idea — direct anti-"pitch-dumb" pivot, NOT granular), warm-inhabited-room VIBE (candle amber/rose — off the cosmic-nebula cluster)]` — clears every ban. **Mode = DEEP** (one massively-bigger concept × 2 render approaches; jury #4 explicitly said "go DEEP on one" category-opener, not WIDE). **Sibling banked (IDEAS §551):** **`943-depth-tonnetz-room` ⭐** — the *same* depth→Tonnetz concept on **raw WebGL2** (warm depth-room + glowing constellation; same P/L/R engine + SATB voicing); the bulletproof no-WebGPU twin — resurrect if 942's WebGPU path disappoints on real hardware, or when WebGL2 is cold again (its builder honestly flagged a rectangular-grid limitation: a 2D grid can't make *both* axes globally parsimonious, so it keeps the lateral axis perfectly P/L/R and lets proximity use P/Slide). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 42s`** → Linting/type-check PASS; grep-confirmed zero "942"/"depth-harmonic"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS"; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/depth/ear-verified** (no WebGPU / camera / audio in container — unverified: whether Depth Anything V2 actually loads + runs on a real machine's WebGPU [robust synthetic + pointer + WebGL2/DOM fallbacks cover every failure], whether the warm pads + portamento read as genuinely *voice-led* on a real speaker, and whether body-distance maps intuitively to "lean in = brighter / step sideways = new chord"; the always-on drone + synthetic auto-walk guarantee a sounding, harmonising glance with zero interaction).

## ⭐ Earlier (Cycle 550 — **kids** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 2 render approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/941-kids-choir-bloom](/dream/941-kids-choir-bloom)** — Choir Bloom: a 4-year-old conducts a choir by dragging singing blob-creatures up and down to PITCHES, and a voice-leading brain turns their little melody into real four-part harmony — glowing GPU metaballs that bloom brighter as the voices come into a chord 🎶🫧🌈. `demoable` · Cycle 550 (kids 4+ / **touch pitch-drag** / **WebGPU metaballs**). **For**: kids (4+) / a warm, cozy, sing-together playspace / iPad-or-desktop. **THE LAB'S FIRST CONDUCT-A-CHOIR-BY-PITCH PIECE — and the deliberate breaking of the "pitch held dumb" template the jury just banned.** This is a piece where **melody and harmony ARE the idea** (the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-25 provocation #2** — "make music from real pitch / voice-leading / a harmonic arc again; it must BE the idea, not the thing you apologise for omitting"). **touch pitch-drag INPUT** — drag the rose **soprano** blob up/down to set its pitch, snapped to a C-major diatonic scale (always in tune, but the child genuinely *chooses each note and hears a melody*); the blob is the ≥64px drag handle; inner voices are independently grabbable (gentle chord-tone re-snap on release). Touch is **0× in the last-10 audit** — the freshest input (off the jury-banned mic, off the recent tilt/camera/shake). **WebGPU/WGSL metaball OUTPUT** — `navigator.gpu` fragment pipeline evaluates a four-source metaball iso-field → warm additive glow (soprano=rose, alto=amber, tenor=emerald, bass=violet) that **blooms brighter as the voices become consonant and close** (a `consonance()` measure drives it); WebGPU is **the scarcest GPU surface at 1×/10** — the directest answer to **jury provocation #1** ("ban three.js, push GPU work onto raw WebGPU compute"). **Voice-leading harmony ENGINE** (`voices.ts`) — the soprano's scale degree implies a diatonic triad (1→I, 2→ii, 3→iii, 4→IV, 5→V, 6→vi) and the bass/tenor/alto each glide (`setTargetAtTime` ~90–140ms) to the **nearest chord tone within their range** (Aldwell & Schachter's smooth voice-leading), bass nudged toward the root, inner voices kept off unisons — so **drawing a melody with the lead blob makes real chord changes emerge underneath, a harmonic arc you can hear**. **Formant source-filter "ahh" singing SYNTH** (`audio.ts`, NOT samples / NOT granular / NOT AI) — each voice is two slightly detuned band-limited sawtooth glottal sources with ~5Hz vibrato → a parallel bank of 3–4 bandpass formant filters (warm /a/ ≈ F1 700 / F2 1100 / F3 2600 Hz), brighter highs / darker bass per register, so the blobs read as a *singing* choir, not a synth. **Kids-safe chain:** sum → `masterGain(0.26)` → lowpass 6.5kHz → `DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)` → destination; a harder/faster drag NEVER gets louder or harsher — only pitch changes; always-on (never silent). **Degrades fully:** no WebGPU → hand-written **raw WebGL2** fragment metaball render (`webgl-fallback.ts`, same field/bloom math, NOT three.js) → if neither GPU path, a plain-DOM radial-gradient glow + `text-rose-300` notice keeps audio alive; ≥72px **Start** gesture-gates the AudioContext + GPU init (iOS); ~2s-idle **auto-demo** drifts the soprano through a calm diatonic tune so a hands-free glance both *sees the metaballs bloom* and *hears the choir + harmony* within ~1s; full teardown (rAF + audio nodes + `device.destroy()` / `WEBGL_lose_context` + `audioCtx.close()`). **No API route, no `guard` needed** (NO mic, NO network, NO AI model — fully offline + private, exactly right for a kids app); **zero new npm deps** (Web Audio + WebGPU/WebGL2). README cites **RESEARCH §550 (2026-06-25)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **16 cycles running** (535–550). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [touch pitch-drag conductor + diatonic voice-leading SATB engine + 4-voice formant source-filter synthesis + WebGPU metaball render w/ WebGL2 fallback = 4] · #3 named refs [**Blob Opera / David Li, Google Arts & Culture 2020**; **Cantor / Chorus Digitalis** formant gesture-singing, IRCAM/LIMSI; **Aldwell & Schachter, *Harmony and Voice Leading***] · #5 dated RESEARCH §550). #1 NOT claimed (formant source-filter synthesis has lab priors — 287-mirror-choir, 413-kids-mouth-mirror, ~61 README hits; the fresh axis is the *conduct-by-pitch Blob-Opera interaction + a real voice-leading engine as the playable idea*, not a lab-first technique). `diversity: banned=[three.js (JURY 7× + audit), mic-as-primary (JURY 4×), granular-texture synthesis (JURY 4×), "pitch-held-dumb/drone+texture" stance (JURY ~10×), raw-WebGL2-as-primary (audit 4×/10 edge)] · picked=[touch-pitch-drag INPUT (0×/10, fresh), WebGPU-metaball OUTPUT (scarcest GPU surface 1×/10 — jury provocation #1), formant + voice-leading harmony TECH (pitch/harmony IS the idea — the direct anti-"pitch-dumb" pivot, NOT granular), warm playful kids-choir VIBE (off the recent cosmic-ambient cluster)]` — clears every ban. **Mode = DEEP** (one massively-bigger concept × 2 render approaches; deliberate alternation after 549 WIDE; the jury's dominant signal is a focused thematic pivot — harmony-is-the-idea — not breadth). **Sibling banked (IDEAS §550):** **`940-kids-blob-choir` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST** (the same conduct-a-choir concept on **Canvas2D** — soft glowing character-face blobs; Canvas2D is *fresh again* in the new jury (collapsed to under-rep) and is the bulletproof no-GPU render path; de-selected only because WebGPU is the scarcer surface the jury wants). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 109s`** → Linting/type-check PASS → reached "Collecting page data"; grep-confirmed zero "941"/"choir-bloom"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS"; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT GPU/ear-verified** (no WebGPU / audio in container — unverified: whether the WebGPU metaball path initializes on a real iPad [robust WebGL2 → DOM fallbacks cover it], whether the formant voices read convincingly as "singing ahh" on a real speaker [vowel formant Q/gains may want ear-tuning], and whether a 4-year-old reads "drag the rose blob up = higher" unaided; the always-on voices + ~2s auto-demo tune + the gentle voice-leading guarantee a sounding, blooming, harmonising glance with zero interaction).

## (Cycle 549 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/939-aurora-harp](/dream/939-aurora-harp)** — Aurora Harp: hear space weather *right now* — the live solar wind streaming past Earth raises a shimmering aurora curtain and plays the magnetosphere's resonances; calm wind = slow ULF drones, a gust or southward Bz = flares, beating and crimson sky 🌌🌠🟢🟣. `demoable` · Cycle 549 (adult / **live external data (NOAA SWPC)** / **raw WebGL2**). **For**: adult / nocturnal-cosmic-contemplative / desktop-or-iPad. **THE LAB'S FIRST LIVE-PLANETARY-INSTRUMENT.** **live-external-data INPUT** — two NOAA **SWPC** real-time solar-wind products fetched browser-side and polled ~60s: `plasma-5-minute.json` (density, speed, temp) and `mag-5-minute.json` (Bx/By/**Bz_gsm**/Bt) from ACE/DSCOVR at the Sun–Earth **L1** point (0× in the last-10 audit — the freshest possible input). **raw-WebGL2 fragment OUTPUT** (GPU, NOT the banned three.js/Canvas2D/SVG): a full-screen **aurora curtain** — fbm-folded vertical light sheets, green→violet→crimson by activity, star field over a dark arctic horizon, folding faster with wind speed, reaching higher and shifting crimson-violet on southward Bz. **HARP-style space-weather sonification TECH** — after NASA **HARP (Heliophysics Audified: Resonances in Plasmas)**: a sustained drone bed on inharmonic resonance **bands**, each a **detuned oscillator pair** producing real *beating* whose rate tracks Bt/turbulence + southward Bz; density gates how many partials sing; gust swells retrigger faster as wind speed rises; a southward-Bz crossing (≈ −7 nT) fires a flare — sub-rumble + ripple of beating tones synced to an aurora flash. **Pitch held deliberately non-melodic** (drone + resonance bands, NO chord, NO scale — the *Sun* is the composer; music from data/resonance, the directest JURY 2026-06-24 #3 answer) and it deliberately **drops the consonant-mode safety crutch the jury BANNED** for external-data sonification (it audifies the actual resonances/beating, never an in-tune-by-construction chord). **Master compressor/limiter** for safety; always-on drone (never silent). **Degrades fully:** Start seeds an immediate **synthetic solar wind** so sound + a moving sky appear within ~0.6s before the live feed returns; any fetch failure (offline / CORS / feed gap) → the synthetic generator (with periodic southward-Bz "substorm" gusts) takes over behind a calm **amber notice**; no WebGL2 → audio-only + amber notice. A live readout (speed km/s, density p/cm³, Bz nT, Bt nT) + a "what am I hearing" panel explain the mapping; pointer to look around. **No API route, no `guard` needed** (all client-side fetch, zero AI/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (raw WebGL2 + Web Audio). README cites **RESEARCH §549 (2026-06-25)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **15 cycles running** (535–549). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [live SWPC fetch+parse + HARP sonification engine + raw-WebGL2 aurora shader + Bz-event/smoothing state = 4] · #3 named refs [NASA HARP; NOAA SWPC ACE/DSCOVR; OVATION] · #5 dated RESEARCH §549). #1 NOT claimed (external-data sonification has a lab prior — 836/877/898 — but those are the jury-banned consonant-crutch template; the fresh axis is *HARP-style live-resonance audification that drops the consonant guarantee*). `diversity: banned=[three.js (4×/10), Canvas2D, SVG, touch (JURY), interval/JI-harmony (JURY), tilt (2× consecutive + rejected-933 would 3-peat the concept)] · picked=[live-external-data INPUT (0×), raw-WebGL2 aurora OUTPUT, HARP space-weather-resonance sonification TECH (data/resonance not pitch), nocturnal-aurora/cosmic VIBE]` — clears every ban. **Mode = WIDE** (3 orthogonal explorers, after 3 consecutive DEEP cycles + a ban-heavy jury verdict). **Siblings banked (IDEAS §549):** **`938-morphogenesis` ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST** (Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on **WebGPU compute** — the scarcest GPU surface — → granular texture) and **`937-cathedral-phase`** (Euclidean + Steve Reich phasing → raymarched SDF cathedral, the cleanest rhythm-not-pitch build). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 54s`** → Linting/type-check PASS, grep-confirmed zero "939"/"aurora-harp"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS"; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT data/GPU/ear-verified** (no live network / GPU / audio confirmation in container — unverified: whether services.swpc.noaa.gov CORS resolves from the deploy edge [fully covered by the synthetic-wind fallback + amber notice], the beating-rate→turbulence + Bz-flare mappings on a real speaker, whether real-day quiet wind reads as too calm [synthetic generator injects gust energy for the demo]; the always-on drone + synthetic fallback + immediate aurora guarantee a sounding/showing glance with zero network).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/939-aurora-harp/README.md`
- **[/dream/935-kids-shake-critters](/dream/935-kids-shake-critters)** — Shake the Critters!: a 4-year-old shakes the iPad like a rattle and a little band of glowing jelly-creatures comes alive — the rhythm you shake is caught into a loop the critters keep grooving 🫨🟡🔵🟣🟢. `demoable` · Cycle 548 (kids 4+ / **devicemotion-shake** / **three.js GPU**). **For**: kids (4+) / a bright, playful, laugh-first playspace / iPad-or-desktop. **THE LAB'S FIRST SHAKE-DRIVEN STOCHASTIC-SHAKER INSTRUMENT** — the rattle is **PhISEM (Physically-Informed Stochastic Event Modeling, Perry Cook 1997)**: a shaker is modeled as N virtual beans in a gourd, a per-frame *collision probability rises with shake energy*, and each collision is a short white-noise grain through a resonant bandpass at the shell frequency (~1.7–6 kHz) with a few-ms exponential decay; summing many grains = the characteristic rattle. **Shake energy → grain RATE + resonance BRIGHTNESS, NEVER loudness** (harder shake = more+brighter grains, kids-safe). **devicemotion-SHAKE INPUT** (`acceleration` magnitude + jerk; iOS `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` gated in the Start tap — the one fresh 4yo-native channel left: camera was AUDIT-banned 4×/10, touch JURY-banned, mic 3× / tilt-orientation 2× recent; shake is distinct from the tilt-orientation 931/932 just used). **three.js GPU OUTPUT** (the *scarcer* GPU surface after 931+932 both shipped raw-WebGL2; NOT the banned Canvas2D/SVG): five blobby icosphere **critters** with a custom vertex-noise-displacement shader squash/stretch/jiggle on shake energy, collisions puff colored sparkle particles, a rim-glow/tonemap gives a bloom feel — bold primary critter colors on a warm bright daylight sky (the jury's "active joyful middle", not calm bedtime glow). **PhISEM-shaker + shake-rhythm-loop TECH** — three voices (soft maraca / woody cabasa / bright shaker) + a low stomp on hard onsets sound like a small band over an always-on warm drone; **pitch held deliberately dumb** (single drone, NO chimes, NO melody, NO notes to miss — music from **timbre + rhythm**, the directest kids answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #3, and the deliberate inversion of `866-rainstick-sky`'s calm pentatonic). **The groove loop:** a rolling ~3.2s buffer samples the shake-energy envelope; pause → the window freezes into a **loop** the critters keep re-triggering, so a warm groove accretes (up to 3 stacked, oldest drops; 🧹 clears). **Kids-safe chain:** master-gain ceiling → lowpass ~6 kHz → soft compressor/limiter; always-on drone (never silent); full teardown (rAF + listeners + three.js dispose + audio nodes). **Degrades fully:** no sensor/desktop/permission-denied → pointer/touch **drag** = shake (plays fully by mouse-swipe) + 3s idle auto-demo gently auto-shakes so a hands-free glance sees jiggle+sparkle and hears the rattle within ~0.6s; no WebGL → friendly amber notice, audio keeps playing. **No API route, no `guard` needed** (fully client-side, zero AI/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (three@0.182 already present + Web Audio). README cites **RESEARCH §548 (2026-06-25)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **14 cycles running** (535–548). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [devicemotion-shake + three.js GPU critter render + PhISEM stochastic-shaker synth + shake-rhythm groove-loop engine = 4] · #3 named ref [PhISEM / Perry Cook 1997] · #5 dated RESEARCH §548). #1 NOT claimed (PhISM cited once before in `429-kids-texture-paint-tap`; the fresh axis is shake-driven shaker *instrument* + groove-loop). `diversity: banned=[camera (audit 4×/10), touch (JURY), Canvas2D/SVG (JURY), interval/JI-harmony (JURY), tilt-orientation (2× recent), mic (3× recent), raw-WebGL2 (931+932 consecutive)] · picked=[devicemotion-SHAKE INPUT, three.js GPU OUTPUT (scarcer surface), PhISEM-stochastic-shaker + shake-rhythm-loop TECH (texture+rhythm not pitch), bright-playful daylight-critter-band VIBE]` — clears every ban. **Mode = DEEP** (kids input palette too narrow for WIDE's distinct-input-per-brief rule → 1 concept × 2 GPU approaches). **Sibling banked (IDEAS §548):** **`936-kids-rattle-bloom`** (the same shake→PhISEM→groove concept as a raw-WebGL2 **metaball** fragment shader — gooey candy-bright blobs that merge with energy; de-selected because abstract blobs read less "band of creatures" than characters, and raw-WebGL2 is hot after 931/932). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: exit 0, zero "Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS", zero 935-folder refs in errors, reached "Collecting page data"; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT device/ear/GPU-verified** (no shake sensor / GPU / audio in container — unverified: iOS DeviceMotion permission flow + acceleration→energy feel on a real iPad, PhISEM grain balance on a real speaker, whether the groove-loop reads as "the critters kept my rhythm"; the always-on drone + pointer-drag + 3s idle auto-demo guarantee a sounding/jiggling glance with zero hardware).

## Cycle 547 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 2 technical approaches, shipped 1

- **[/dream/932-tilt-orrery](/dream/932-tilt-orrery)** — Tilt Orrery: tilt your device and a little cosmos tips like a bowl; every body that swings through perihelion rings, and the music is the emergent polyrhythm of orbits 🪐🔔. `demoable` · Cycle 547 (adult / **device-tilt** / **raw WebGPU WGSL compute**). **For**: adult / cosmic-contemplative / iPad-or-desktop. **The whole N-body sim AND the perihelion detection run on the GPU** — an inline **WGSL compute shader** integrates 3000 bodies in double-buffered (ping-pong A/B) storage buffers (semi-implicit Euler, central inverse-square gravity + a tilt acceleration "tipped bowl"); perihelion is flagged on the GPU as a local max of 1/r and only a tiny per-frame flag buffer is read back **non-blocking** via `mapAsync` (last frame's flags, ≤24/readback, 90ms per-body gate) to fire Web Audio bells. **device-tilt INPUT** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`; iOS `requestPermission()` gated in the Start tap; tilt 1×/10 — fresh, off the over-used touch/mic). **raw-WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (the scarcest GPU surface — 0× in last-10, the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #1 "swing to the scarce GPU surface"; NOT three.js, NOT Canvas2D/SVG). **gravitational perihelion-polyrhythm TECH** — bell timbre from radius+speed (close+fast = bright bell, far+slow = soft thud), **stereo pan from `cos(angle)`** so you hear the cosmos rotate, tilt-steepness → pulse density; **pitch held deliberately dumb** (fixed pentatonic over a sub-bass drone, NO harmony engine — music from rhythm/space, JURY #3). **The soul / research enrichment:** seven amber bodies are seeded in the **TRAPPIST-1 mean-motion resonance chain** (adjacent period ratios ≈ 8:5,5:3,3:2,3:2,4:3,3:2) on locked analytic orbits, so a steady hypnotic pulse **self-organizes inside** the chaotic violet field — Kepler's 1619 *Harmonices Mundi* "harmony of the spheres" made literal, after NASA / SYSTEM Sounds' 2017 TRAPPIST-1 sonification. **Render:** instanced additive glowing quads + a translucent indigo trail veil. **Audio chain:** master gain 0.26 → `DynamicsCompressorNode` limiter → destination (dense bursts never clip). **Degrades fully:** `navigator.gpu` missing or any WebGPU init throw → a 360-body **CPU N-body sim** runs identical physics + perihelion detection and renders additive glow on a 2D `<canvas>`, still sounding (calm amber notice, never a crash); no motion sensor → auto-drift Lissajous + pointer-drag + tilt x/y sliders (sounding/moving glance within ~0.6s, zero hardware). **No API route, no `guard` needed** (fully client-side, zero AI/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (raw WebGPU + Web Audio). README cites **RESEARCH §547 (2026-06-25)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **13 cycles running** (535–547). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [tilt sensor + WGSL GPU N-body solver + GPU-perihelion-flag `mapAsync` readback + granular bell synth + WebGPU render = 5] · #3 named refs [Kepler *Harmonices Mundi* 1619; TRAPPIST-1 / NASA SYSTEM Sounds 2017; Gillon et al. *Nature* 2017] · #5 dated RESEARCH §547). #1 NOT claimed (raw WebGPU/WGSL compute has a lab prior — loved `130-tsl-particle-compute`; the fresh axis is *tilt-driven N-body gravitational-rhythm + resonance-chain-in-chaos*). `diversity: banned=[Canvas2D, SVG, touch-as-primary, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine (JURY 2026-06-24); + three.js as the more-represented GPU output in last-10] · picked=[device-tilt INPUT (fresh, 1×), raw-WebGPU-WGSL-compute OUTPUT (scarcest GPU surface, 0×), gravitational-perihelion-polyrhythm + TRAPPIST-resonance-in-chaos TECH (rhythm/space, not pitch), cosmic/orrery VIBE]` — clears every ban. **Mode = DEEP** (the ⭐ standing adult resurrect-first concept `928-tilt-orrery` × 2 approaches). **Sibling banked (IDEAS §547):** **`933-tilt-orrery`** ⭐ **RESURRECT-FIRST** (the same concept as a lush three.js `WebGLRenderer` galaxy + a dynamic-`import()` `WebGPURenderer`+TSL compute enhancement — the proven render path + a reusable build-safe way to use `three/webgpu` here). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 54s`** → Linting/type-check PASS, grep-confirmed zero "932"/"tilt-orrery"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"/"error TS";
