## Cycle 546 — **kids** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 2 technical approaches, shipped 1

- **[/dream/931-kids-tide-pool](/dream/931-kids-tide-pool)** — Moonlit Tide Pool: tilt the iPad at bedtime and a glowing sea of light flows downhill, pools in the low corner, and every pool rings a soft bell 🌊🔔. `demoable` · Cycle 546 (kids 4+ / **device-tilt** / **raw WebGL2 all-GPU shallow-water**). **For**: kids (4+) / a calm bedtime playspace / iPad. **THE LAB'S FIRST ALL-GPU SHALLOW-WATER SIM** — the *entire* physics state (water height + four per-cell pipe fluxes) lives in ping-pong **RGBA32F float textures** (256×256) and is advanced every frame by two fragment-shader passes, so a 4-year-old's tilt makes the light-water *genuinely* run downhill and **pool** (real conserved flux, not a faked damped membrane). **device-tilt INPUT** (`DeviceOrientationEvent` beta/gamma → gravity uniform; iOS `requestPermission()` gated inside the Start tap; tilt 0–1× in last-10 kids — the freshest input, off the banned touch). **virtual-pipes shallow-water TECH** (Mei/Decaudin/Hu 2007): flux-update pass (neighbour height-diff + tilt-gravity, damped, outflow-scaled so depth never goes negative, closed no-flux walls) → height-update pass (net inflow − outflow + slow central trickle so it breathes). **Pooling rings the bells** — a handful of fixed "lily" probes read back cheaply (1×1 `gl.readPixels` from the sim FBO, throttled ~30ms) and on a depth rising-edge (0.22s cooldown) ring one warm pentatonic note {C4 D4 E4 G4 A4 C5 D5 E5} — **NO harmony/interval engine, pitch held deliberately dumb; the music lives in the RHYTHM of how the child tilts** (gentle rock = sparse drops, fast sway = a flurry). **raw-WebGL2 GPU OUTPUT** (the scarce surface JURY #1 wants — NOT three.js, NOT the banned Canvas2D/SVG): a shade pass renders the height field as a moonlit sea (deep = cool indigo, pooled = warm gold, additive glow + height-normal specular shimmer), lily nodes brighten the instant they ring so the rhythm reads sound-off. **The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #1** (swing all-in to GPU — the *whole sim* is GPU) **and #3** (music from rhythm/space, NOT pitch theory). **Kids-safe envelope:** `masterGain(0.24) → lowpass 6kHz → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`, a hard tilt makes MORE soft bells never louder; always-on C2+G2 drone (never silent); bells ≥40ms attack, gentle decay. iOS gesture-gated AudioContext; full teardown (rAF + listeners + all GL textures/FBOs/programs/buffers/VAOs + `WEBGL_lose_context` + `audioCtx.close()`). **Degrades fully:** no sensor/desktop/permission-denied → pointer-drag tips the sea + idle auto-demo rocks the tide so a hands-free glance sees flow+pool and hears bells within ~0.6s; no WebGL2/float-texture → `text-rose-300` notice, drone keeps playing. **No API route, no `guard` needed** (fully client-side, zero AI/cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (raw WebGL2). README cites **RESEARCH §546 (2026-06-25)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **12 cycles running** (535–546). **Clears ambition floor 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [tilt sensor + GPU virtual-pipes solver + bell scheduler + WebGL2 render = 4] · #3 named ref [virtual-pipes / Mei, Decaudin & Hu 2007; lisyarus/webgpu-shallow-water 2025] · #5 dated RESEARCH §546). `diversity: banned=[touch-as-primary, Canvas2D, SVG, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine (JURY 2026-06-24); + three.js as the more-represented GPU output in last-10 kids] · picked=[device-tilt INPUT (freshest, 0–1×), raw-WebGL2 all-GPU-solve OUTPUT (scarcer than three.js), virtual-pipes flow→pooling-RHYTHM TECH (not pitch theory), serene moonlit tide-pool VIBE]`. **Mode = DEEP** (the ⭐ standing kids resurrect-first concept × 2 approaches; AGENT.md: develop/resurrect the bench). **Sibling banked (IDEAS §546):** **`930-kids-tilt-tide`** ⭐ **RESURRECT-FIRST** (the same concept solved on CPU [56×56] + rendered with three.js — the bulletproof no-float-texture render path). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 112s`** → Linting/type-check PASS, zero warnings/refs in the 931 folder; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT device/ear/GPU-verified** (no tilt sensor / GPU / audio in container — unverified: the iOS DeviceOrientation permission flow, the RGBA32F float-texture path + `gl.readPixels` readback timing on a real iPad, whether the pooling tilt-feel reads as "I poured the light," the audio balance on a real speaker; the always-on drone + pointer-drag + idle auto-demo guarantee a sounding/flowing/pooling glance with zero hardware).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/931-kids-tide-pool/README.md`

## (Cycle 541 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 second-model approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/915-resonant-cinema](/dream/915-resonant-cinema)** — Resonant Cinema: your playing dreams a short FILM of itself — your music distilled to a still, bloomed into a living video, then kept moving by your own live sound 🎬✨. `demoable` · Cycle 541 (adult / **mic + synth bed** / **three.js + AI-image + AI-video**). **For**: anyone with a voice/instrument / immersive listening / a stage projection · **THE LAB'S FIRST TRUE MULTI-MODEL AI PIPELINE CHAIN** (JURY 2026-06-24 #4, 0× after being named every jury; Karel's #1 directive "AI image generation INSIDE audio-visual experiments"). **mic/synth INPUT** (a generative ambient pad bed over ONE fixed A2 drone — pitch deliberately dumb; optional live mic swaps the analysis source, never routed to destination [anti-feedback]; AnalyserNode FFT-2048 → energy/centroid/flux/hue every frame). **2-model FAL chain TECH**: audio mood → poetic prompt → **`fal-ai/flux/schnell`** (still seed, 16:9, 4 steps) → **`fal-ai/ltx-video/image-to-video`** (blooms the still into a 5s clip). **three.js + AI-VIDEO OUTPUT** (0× output surface): the returned clip is a `THREE.VideoTexture` on a fullscreen quad, and the **live FFT runs a fragment-shader pass over it every frame** — ripple/displacement∝energy, bloom/exposure∝centroid (brightness), chromatic-aberration/RGB-split∝flux. **The AI video is the SEED; the live audio is the ongoing ANIMATOR.** **What if you didn't prompt an AI to make a music video — what if you PLAYED, and the AI only dreamed the canvas your sound then keeps moving?** **Cinematic / Anadol latent-cinema VIBE.** **Cost-safe for public prod**: the paid chain (~$0.003 still + $0.02 video) fires **only on an explicit "Dream the film" click** (disabled in-flight, **zero timers**, never on load/idle); page-load / idle / "dreaming…" / error / no-FAL_KEY all run a **synthesized GPU nebula** (domain-warped FBM cloud, audio-driven) so a hands-off glance is **always a living, sounding scene**. `api/route.ts` is **guarded** (`guard(req)` first, FAL_KEY→501, try/catch→500). **The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #4** (AI-pipeline-chain, 0×) **and #1** ("swing back to GPU, full stop") **and #3** (music from NOT pitch theory — one drone, the idea lives in image/chain). **Named ref:** **Refik Anadol's latent cinema (*Machine Hallucinations*)** — the AI-dreamed image as living projection. README cites **RESEARCH §541 (2026-06-24)** — in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **7 cycles running** (535–541). **Clears 3/5 ambition floor** (#1 **never-used technique** — first true multi-model AI chain / first AI-video output [441 was a single audio→image model; this adds the video model in series] · #2 ≥4 subsystems [synth+mic audio engine + feature/prompt extractor + 2-model FAL chain + three.js GPU compositor + synthesized fallback] · #3 named ref · #5 dated RESEARCH — honestly 4-of-5). `diversity: banned=[Canvas2D, SVG, touch-as-primary, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine, external-data→structure-w/-consonant-crutch (all JURY 2026-06-24)] · picked=[mic/synth (non-touch), three.js + AI-image + AI-VIDEO output (all GPU/0×), audio→image→video 2-model chain (pitch held to ONE drone), cinematic/Anadol]`. **Mode = DEEP** (1 massively-bigger concept, 3 second-model approaches; AGENT.md maps "jury calls for massively bigger" → DEEP). **2 sibling chains banked (IDEAS §541):** **`916-latent-loom`** ⭐ (flux → **flux-redux** img2img re-dream loop, GPU crossfade morph, cost-bounded ≤6 steps; **resurrect-first** on a desktop morph cycle) + **`917-the-telling`** (**any-llm** writes a prose-poem of the music → flux paints it, kinetic typography, **text-not-voice**). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 36.3s`**, lint+type phase ran with **zero warnings/refs in the 915 folder**; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio/WebGL/FAL in container — the LTX clip quality, the flux↔mood prompt fit, VideoTexture CORS from the fal CDN, and per-frame shader cost on a phone are unverified; the always-alive nebula + explicit-click gating guarantee a sounding/moving glance and zero idle cost regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/915-resonant-cinema/README.md`

## (Cycle 540 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/911-kids-shadow-dance](/dream/911-kids-shadow-dance)** — shadow dance: a 4-year-old DANCES and their whole body becomes a cloud of light + rhythm 🕺✨. `demoable` · Cycle 540 (kids 4+ / **camera whole-body pose** / **three.js GPU particles**). **For**: a 4-year-old on an iPad/phone / immediate cause-and-effect / no-reading / the *moving body*, not notes · **camera POSE INPUT** (front camera → **MediaPipe Pose** 33 whole-body landmarks via runtime CDN import, `webpackIgnore`, never bundled; video analysis-only, never recorded/uploaded; **zero pitch detection** — per frame we compute the three children's-sonification *movement qualities*: **energy** = mean landmark speed, **impulsivity** = high-passed jerk, **fluidity** = inverse speed-jitter; non-touch, full-body — fresh for kids vs 862 *hands* / 869 adult; no-camera → synthetic ghost-dancer auto-demo) · **three.js `THREE.Points` GPU OUTPUT** (~5,000 additive-blended particles, custom `ShaderMaterial`, indigo→violet→warm-gold; energy→density/brightness, impulsivity→outward burst shockwave, fluidity→swirl-vs-scatter; the JURY-scarce GPU surface, **no Canvas2D/SVG**) · **movement energy/fluidity/impulsivity → granular-percussion TECHNIQUE** · **luminous shadow-dance VIBE**. **What if a kid makes music by DANCING — their whole body's movement (not fingers, not voice, not any pitch) scatters a glowing cloud of light and grants rhythm + texture?** Energy → warmth/brightness of an always-on granular texture bed; a sudden move (impulsivity) fires a soft *capped* percussive bloom + a particle burst — **the kid's sharp moves ARE the beat**; smooth motion (fluidity) brightens a shimmer pad and streams the particles. **Pitch is held deliberately dumb** — one warm always-on drone (C2+G2+C3) + a few fixed safe accent tones; no scale, no chord, no melody the kid "plays." Kids-safe master chain `masterGain(0.26) → lowpass(≤6.5 kHz) → Compressor(−10, 20:1)`, attacks ≥10ms, full teardown (rAF, mic tracks, `pose.close()`, three.js dispose + `forceContextLoss`, `audioCtx.close()`). **The clean answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #3** ("make music from rhythm, timbre, texture or space — NOT pitch theory") **and #1** ("swing back to GPU"): the lab's kids canon is almost all pitch toys; this makes music from the *quality of a child's free movement*. **Named refs:** **Myron Krueger *Videoplace*** (full-body interactive, 1970s) · the children's-movement-sonification model (energy/fluidity/impulsivity granular mapping, **Frontiers in Neuroscience, PMC5104747**) · **"The Moving Mandala"** (2025 — rhythmic music + embodied movement → child synchrony). README cites **RESEARCH §540 (2026-06-24)** — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **6 cycles running** (535–540). **Clears 3/5 ambition floor** (#2 ≥4 subsystems [pose tracking + energy/fluidity/impulsivity extractor + granular/percussion synth w/ dumb-drone + three.js GPU field + ghost auto-demo] · #3 named refs · #5 dated RESEARCH; #1 honestly NOT claimed — pose/granular/particle all have lab priors). `diversity: banned=[touch-as-primary, Canvas2D, SVG, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine, external-data→structure-w/-consonant-crutch (last-10-kids + JURY 2026-06-24)] · picked=[camera whole-body pose (non-touch), three.js GPU particles (scarce), motion→granular-percussion (pitch held to ONE drone), luminous shadow-dance]`. **Mode = WIDE** (3 orthogonal explorers on one "body-not-pitch" theme; deliberate alternation after 539 DEEP). **2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §540):** **`913-kids-spin-galaxy`** ⭐ (device **gyroscope** spin · hand-written **WebGPU `@compute`** 40k-particle galaxy + WebGL2 fallback · ring-crossing percussion — the purest JURY-#1 GPU answer; **resurrect-first** on a desktop/Tauri window) + **`912-kids-wind-garden`** (mic-**breath/blow** via spectral flatness · **three.js** grass field · wind force + dandelion-seed chimes — calm dawn-meadow). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 56s`**, lint phase ran with **zero warnings in the 911 folder**; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear/hand-verified** (no camera/audio/WebGL in container — whether MediaPipe Pose tracks a small child across lighting/angle, the motion-scaling constants, and whether the bloom reads as "I am the light" are unverified; the always-on drone + ~1s ghost-dancer auto-demo guarantee a sounding/blooming glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/911-kids-shadow-dance/README.md`

## (Cycle 539 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 GPU-render approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/909-resonant-field-volume](/dream/909-resonant-field-volume)** — Resonant Field: sing, and your voice blooms as a volume of light + grains placed in space by its **timbre, not its pitch** ☁️🔊. `demoable` · Cycle 539 (adult / **mic** / **three.js raymarched volume**). **For**: anyone with a voice or instrument / immersive listening / headphones · **mic INPUT** (non-touch — sing/hum/speak/play; **zero pitch detection** — a pitch-free extractor reads only RMS, spectral centroid [brightness], flatness [noisy↔tonal], flux [change], and an 8-band silhouette; mic→Analyser + a rolling ~3.5s capture ring, **never routed to destination** [anti-feedback]; no-mic → synthetic auto-demo) · **three.js fullscreen raymarched volumetric OUTPUT** (a fragment-shader nebula — 24-step domain-warped FBM whose density/turbulence/hue/altitude ARE the timbre, with a `uAge` **memory** accumulator that warms while you sound and slowly cools in silence so it evolves like a long-form cloud, not a VU meter; runs on **any WebGL** → actually GPU-renders on a phone; Canvas2D glow fallback on no-WebGL) · **timbre-features → HRTF spatial-granular re-synth TECHNIQUE** · **immersive / Anadol-volumetric VIBE**. **What if you composed by TIMBRE × SPACE instead of pitch — your own captured sound scattered back as a 3D cloud of grains, each placed around your head by how bright / noisy / loud it is, over a single dumb drone so harmony is held deliberately flat?** Each Hann-windowed grain reads from the rolling capture buffer and is panned through its **own HRTF `PannerNode`** at a 3D position dictated by timbre (brightness→elevation/forward, flatness→azimuth spread, RMS→grain density); grains keep the source's own pitch — we never repitch by any musical rule. A fixed 55 Hz + 82.5 Hz drone pins harmony flat so the piece **lives entirely in texture and placement**. Master chain `gain(≤0.28) → lowpass(7 kHz) → compressor → destination`; idle nebula breathes from load; ~1s auto-demo (synthetic evolving-noise + formant sweep through the identical pipeline) blooms + sounds on mic-deny or 2.5s silence; full teardown (rAF, oscillators, mic tracks, three.js dispose + `forceContextLoss`). **The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #3** ("make music from rhythm, **timbre, texture or space** — NOT pitch theory") **and #1** ("swing back to GPU, full stop") in one piece. **The inversion of a brand-new instrument:** Delta Sound Labs **XStream** (NAMM, 25 Jan 2026) is a *spatial live granular* synth where the **producer hand-dials** grain placement; this makes placement **automatic & timbre-driven** — played by sounding, not by tweaking. **Named refs:** Delta Sound Labs XStream (2026) · **Curtis Roads *Microsound*** (2001) · **Trevor Wishart *Audible Design*** (timbral morphology) · Refik Anadol (volumetric light). README cites **RESEARCH §539 (2026-06-24)** — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **5 cycles running** (535/536/537/538/539). **Clears 3/5 ambition floor** (#2 ≥4 subsystems [pitch-free timbre extractor + HRTF spatial-granular engine + dumb drone + raymarched GPU volume w/ memory + auto-demo] · #3 named refs · #5 dated RESEARCH; #1 honestly NOT claimed — granular/spatial/WebGPU-compute all have lab priors). `diversity: banned=[Canvas2D, SVG, touch-as-primary, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine, external-data→structure-w/-consonant-crutch (all JURY 2026-06-24)] · picked=[mic (non-touch), three.js raymarched-volume (GPU, scarce), timbre→spatial-granular (pitch held to ONE drone), immersive/Anadol]`. **Mode = DEEP** (1 concept, 3 GPU-render approaches; breaks a 3-WIDE adult streak). **2 sibling approaches banked (IDEAS §539):** **`908-resonant-field-compute`** ⭐ (the frontier **WebGPU WGSL compute** version — 120k curl-noise particles; **resurrect-first** on a desktop/Tauri window once WebGPU phone support clears + ScriptProcessor→AudioWorklet) + **`910-resonant-field-flow`** (**three.js GPGPU** ping-pong float-RT flow field — ~147k points "river of light"). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 52s`**, lint phase ran [only runs post-compile] with **zero warnings in the 909 folder**; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio/WebGL in container — whether the HRTF grain cloud reads as "my own sound around me," whether the raymarched volume reads as "my timbre's shape," and per-grain PannerNode CPU cost on a low-end phone are unverified; the breathing idle nebula + ~1s auto-demo guarantee a sounding/moving glance).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/909-resonant-field-volume/README.md`

## (Cycle 538 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/907-kids-stomp-garden](/dream/907-kids-stomp-garden)** — stomp garden: clap a beat, grow a garden, it loops back 🌱👏🌸. `demoable` · Cycle 538 (kids 4+ / **mic-rhythm** / **three.js**). **For**: a 4-year-old on an iPad / immediate cause-and-effect / no-reading / the *beat*, not notes · **mic-ONSET INPUT** (claps / stomps / table-taps / "buh-tss" — a spectral-flux onset detector with adaptive threshold + 120ms refractory; **pitch is NEVER estimated**, the centroid is read only to pick a thump-vs-shaker *timbre*; mic used live, never recorded/stored; no-mic → tap-the-garden fallback + auto-demo groove) · **three.js (WebGL GPU) OUTPUT** (a procedural glowing garden — elastic-ease plants spring up per beat, always-on breathing, loop-pulse sway/glow, warm fog & lighting; deliberately the JURY-scarce GPU surface, **no Canvas2D/SVG**) · **onset-detect + quantize-and-loop-back + procedural-growth TECHNIQUE** · **bright/warm/garden VIBE**. **What if a 4-year-old could CLAP, STOMP, or beat-box and their RHYTHM (no note, no tune) grew a glowing garden that then loops their beat back so they can dance along and lock into it?** Each detected beat grows a plant + plays a soft capped percussion hit (<50ms); after ~3 beats a rolling-window looper infers tempo (median IOI), lightly quantizes, and **replays the rhythm continuously** while the garden pulses in time — handing the child the steady external pulse the developmental literature says scaffolds the still-forming beat-synchronization skill. Steady clap → orderly rows; wild rhythm → a swaying jungle; the child keeps layering beats. Always alive (ambient pad + heartbeat + breathing from load; auto-demo groove with no mic). Kids-safe master chain `busIn → Gain(0.26) → lowpass(6 kHz) → Compressor(−12, 12:1) → destination`, 6-voice cap (oldest-steal) so even a loud clap can't blast, full teardown. **The clean answer to JURY 2026-06-24** ("make music from rhythm, timbre, texture or space — NOT pitch theory"): the lab's kids canon is almost all pitch toys; this is its first true **rhythm-loop** piece. **Named refs:** the TR-808 / live loop-station "record your rhythm, it loops back" model · spectral-flux onset detection (Bello et al., IEEE TASLP 2005). README cites **RESEARCH §538 (2026-06-24)** — the developmental beat-synchronization anchor (*"Development of full-body rhythmic synchronization in middle childhood,"* Nature Sci Rep) — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) **4 cycles running** (535/536/537/538). **Clears 3/5 ambition floor** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [onset detect + tempo-infer/loop + procedural garden + percussion synth] · #3 named refs · #5 dated RESEARCH). `diversity: banned=[Canvas2D, SVG, touch-as-primary, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine, external-data→structure-w/-consonant-crutch (all JURY 2026-06-24)] · picked=[mic-onset/rhythm, three.js (GPU, scarce), onset-loop-back, kids-garden]`. **2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §538):** **`905-kids-water-bowl`** ⭐ (mic-hum · **three.js** · *Faraday* standing-wave water surface — the pattern you see is the sound you sing; **resurrect-first**, needs no motion sensor) + **`906-kids-tilt-fireflies`** (device-tilt · **three.js** GPU particles · gravity-herded firefly swarm whose density & flower-bumps drive a soft pulsing rhythm; ref Reynolds *Boids* 1986). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 85s`**, lint phase ran [only runs post-compile] with **zero warnings in the 907 folder**; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio in container — whether the looped beat reads as lock-onto-able, onset sensitivity across real claps vs room noise, and small-iPad garden legibility are unverified; ambient bed + auto-demo groove guarantee a sounding/moving glance).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/907-kids-stomp-garden/README.md`

## (Cycle 537 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/902-harmonic-mirror](/dream/902-harmonic-mirror)** — harmonic mirror: your chord, completed in just intonation 🎹✨. `demoable` · Cycle 537 (adult / harmony / **Canvas2D**). **For**: pianists & composers (Karel's exact audience) / harmony-curious / playable with zero hardware · **MIDI / on-screen-piano / computer-keyboard INPUT** (Web MIDI `requestMIDIAccess` + a clickable white/black one-octave piano + `a w s e d f t g y h u j k` map — all feed the identical pipeline; no-MIDI → `text-rose-300` notice + the on-screen paths still play) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (a circle-of-fifths constellation — bright white held nodes, soft violet **mirror** halos joined to the root by luminous completion lines carrying ratio labels `5/4`/`3/2`, the inferred chord name at center, 60fps amplitude pulse; deliberately the JURY-scarce Canvas2D surface, **no WebGL2/three.js/WebGPU/SVG**) · **just-intonation chord-completion TECHNIQUE** (deterministic Riemann-template inference + JI retuning — fresh for the lab) · **harmonic / luminous-clinical VIBE** (violet on near-black). **What if, as you play, your instrument completed the chord you imply — adding the 1–2 notes you DIDN'T play, tuned in pure JUST INTONATION to your root — so the harmony locks beat-lessly under your hands?** You play the keys you press in **equal temperament** (warm detuned saws); a **mirror** reduces your held notes to a pitch-class set, matches it against a small template bank (dom7 `0 4 7 10` · maj `0 4 7` · min `0 3 7` · sus4 `0 5 7` · bare-fifth `0 7`; anything else → a **cluster** heuristic completed with a safe fifth), and synthesizes the **missing** template tones in **just intonation** (small-integer ratios 9/8 · 6/5 · 5/4 · 4/3 · 3/2 · 5/3 · 9/5 · 15/8 over the inferred root's fundamental) so their partials align and the beating cancels — the just `5/4` third (~386¢) locks where the ET third (400¢) is restless. When the root shifts (C maj → A min) the persisting mirror voices **glide-retune** over ~180 ms rather than re-attack. **The deterministic, zero-latency cousin of a predictive AI accompanist** (RESEARCH §537 / **arXiv 2604.07612**, Apr 2026): it predicts nothing — it *finishes the chord you are already playing*. 2s-idle → a gentle **I–vi–IV–V** auto-demo arpeggiates through the identical path so a silent 06:30 glance is sounding + moving; first real note stops it. **Named refs:** Harry Partch & Ben Johnston (JI / small-integer ratios) · Hugo Riemann (functional-harmony templates) · arXiv 2604.07612. README cites **RESEARCH §537 (2026-06-24)** as the build hook — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) JURY flagged **0-for-15 across five windows**, now **3 cycles running** (535/536/537). **Clears 3/5 of the ambition floor** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MIDI/kbd input + chord-template inference + JI synth w/ glide-retune + Canvas2D constellation] · #3 named refs · #5 dated RESEARCH citation). `diversity: banned=[raw-WebGL2, data→roughness/detune, no-wrong-notes-safe-scale, 3rd-renderer-pass-on-847/872] · picked=[MIDI/keyboard, Canvas2D (scarce 2×), just-intonation-chord-completion, harmonic/clinical]`. **2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §537):** **`903-living-stave`** ⭐ (mic · **SVG** · live NSDF pitch+onset detection inks a self-drawing engraved treble staff — owns the 0× SVG lane, **resurrect-first**) + **`904-tide-organ`** (live **NOAA** tide data · **three.js** · the sea decides which organ pipes enter/leave & the arc's form — data→STRUCTURE, ref the Zadar Sea Organ). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 102s`**, lint phase ran [only runs post-compile] with **zero warnings in the 902 folder**; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio in container — whether the JI completion *audibly* locks vs the ET played notes, the glide-retune feel, and constellation legibility are unverified; the zero-hardware on-screen piano + auto-demo guarantee a sounding/moving glance).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/902-harmonic-mirror/README.md`

## (Cycle 536 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/899-kids-moire-beats](/dream/899-kids-moire-beats)** — moiré beats: roughness & calm 〰️. `demoable` · Cycle 536 (kids 4+ / **touch** / **Canvas2D**). **For**: a 4-year-old on an iPad / immediate-cause-and-effect / no-reading · **touch-drag INPUT** (one finger, Pointer Events → touch + mouse; ~280px swing covers the full range, huge & forgiving) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (two op-art grids — fixed concentric **rings** under twistable **stripes**, composited `multiply` so they physically interfere into a rolling moiré band; deliberately the JURY-reopened scarce Canvas2D surface, **no WebGL2/three.js/SVG**) · **moiré-interference TECHNIQUE** (grep-verified **0× in the lab**) · **op-art VIBE** (Bridget Riley cream/ink, fresh for the lab). **What if a 4-year-old could SEE and HEAR a sound's roughness at the same time — by sliding two striped patterns over each other so the moiré band they make IS the acoustic beating they hear?** A warm consonant **fifth drone (A2 + E3)** is always playing, each pitch built from two sine oscillators. The child's twist doesn't change the *pitch* — it changes the **detune** of the second oscillator (0→~9 Hz). Lined up: the moiré band slows to almost nothing AND the tones converge to **one clear calm tone** (a green ring shrinks to a dot); twisted apart: the band shimmers fast AND the sound throbs **WAH-WAH-WAH** (amber ring pulses). The coupling is literal — the visual band's roll speed and the audio beat rate are driven by the **same** `twist` value, so *moiré beat = acoustic beat*. **The clean template-break:** the child plays **roughness↔calm over a fixed consonant fifth — NOT a scale**, sidestepping the no-wrong-notes pentatonic crutch entirely; nothing can ever be "wrong." Kids-safe (master ≤ 0.24, 6 kHz lowpass, soft fades, never silent), 2s-idle auto-demo drifts the layer so a hands-off 06:30 glance is moving + sounding within ~1s, `text-rose-600` notice + live visuals if Web Audio is unavailable, full teardown. **Named refs:** Gerald Oster *Moiré Patterns* (Scientific American, 1963) · Bridget Riley op-art · acoustic beating. README cites **RESEARCH §536 (2026-06-24)** as the build hook — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) JURY flagged **0-for-15**, now 3 cycles running. **Clears 3/5 of the ambition floor** (#1 never-used technique [moiré 0×] · #3 named refs · #5 dated RESEARCH citation). `diversity: banned=[raw-WebGL2, three.js, data→roughness, no-wrong-notes-safe-scale] · picked=[touch, Canvas2D (scarce 2×), moiré-interference (0×), op-art]`. **2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §536):** **`900-kids-rope-singer`** ⭐ (touch · **SVG** · a *catenary* hanging-rope you hang beads on to sag→lower the pitch, pluck for a damped twang — owns the 0× SVG lane, resurrect-first) + **`901-kids-water-bowl`** (mic-hum · **three.js** · *Faraday* standing-wave patterns bloom on a vibrated water surface in time with the note you sing). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: lint phase ran [only runs post-compile] with **zero warnings in the 899 folder**; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio in container — whether the band reads as "the same wobble I hear," small-iPad op-art readability, and the twist feel are unverified; always-on drone + auto-demo guarantee a sounding/moving glance).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/899-kids-moire-beats/README.md`

## (Cycle 535 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/898-tremor-score](/dream/898-tremor-score)** — tremor score 🌍🎼. `demoable` · Cycle 535 (adult / data-sonification / **SVG**). **For**: adults / contemplative / data-curious · **live external-data INPUT** (USGS `all_day.geojson` earthquake feed, fetched **client-side**, public + CORS, no key, no API route → no guard needed; offline → synthetic ~30-quake fallback + `text-rose-300` notice) · **inline-SVG OUTPUT** (an Ikeda-restrained data-score — time axis, per-quake register marks/circles, a sweeping violet playhead, a lon/lat epicentre dot-field; **no canvas, no WebGL/WebGL2/WebGPU, no three.js** — deliberately the scarce SVG surface, answering JURY 2026-06-23 #1 "raw-WebGL2 is the new wall") · **data-driven event-scheduling TECHNIQUE** · **geologic / clinical / Ikeda VIBE**. **What if a *live* planetary earthquake feed *composed* a piece — not by fouling the harmony, but by deciding who plays, when, and in what register?** The last 24 h of global seismicity is compressed onto ~90 s: each quake becomes a **new voice entering** — **magnitude**→register/loudness/duration (big = low/long/loud; small = brief high glint), **depth**→timbre brightness (shallow = bright/more partials, deep = dark via lowpass), **longitude**→stereo pan, **latitude**→scale-degree, **temporal clustering**→density (an aftershock swarm = a dense overlapping flurry; a quiet stretch = a sparse solo). All pitches snap to a fixed consonant **Bb pentatonic/Dorian** mode across 3 octave bands, so **the data decides FORM while the mode keeps it in tune — data → structure, NEVER → roughness/detune.** This is the **direct answer to JURY #3** ("the next data piece must sonify STRUCTURE; `842`→`864` already cloned the detune move — retire it"). Per-voice chain: `OscillatorNode(triangle + sine partials) → Gain envelope → lowpass Biquad → StereoPanner → master → ConvolverNode(algorithmic IR) + dry → DynamicsCompressor limiter → destination` (shared `AudioContext` on the gesture; reverb IR synthesized in code; limiter keeps swarms un-clipped). README cites **RESEARCH §535 / OpenSeisML, arXiv 2605.20539 (May 2026)** (seismic catalogues as *generative compositional input*) + Ryoji Ikeda *data.path*/*datamatics* + Florian Dombois *Auditory Seismology* — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) that JURY flagged **0-for-15 across five windows**. 2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §535): **`899-harmonic-mirror`** ⭐ (the lab's FIRST Web MIDI — a JI chord-completion partner) + **`900-flow-grains`** (the lab's FIRST optical-flow input — camera motion → three.js particles + granular synth). **Clears 3/5 of the ambition floor** (#2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 named reference · #5 dated RESEARCH citation). `diversity: banned=[touch, raw-WebGL2] · picked=[external-data, SVG, data-driven-event-scheduling, geologic/Ikeda]`. **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: `✓ Compiled successfully` + lint/type passed); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio in container — the sound of a real USGS day + small-screen SVG crowding unverified; synthetic fallback guarantees a sounding/moving glance).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/898-tremor-score/README.md`

- **[/dream/896-tonnetz-loom](/dream/896-tonnetz-loom)** — tonnetz loom 🎵🪡. `demoable` · Cycle 533 (adult / geometric-music-theory / **SVG**). **For**: adults / music-theory-curious / contemplative-precise · **touch/pointer INPUT** (tap a triangle to strike its triad; P/L/R buttons to move) · **inline-SVG OUTPUT** (a 7×5 Tonnetz patch — 48 tappable triangles, note-labeled nodes, glowing path ribbon — built with `createElementNS`/React SVG; **no canvas, no webgl, no three.js, no GPU at all** — the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-23 #1 "raw-WebGL2 is the new wall, go deliberately non-WebGL2"; SVG is a scarce surface) · **neo-Riemannian Tonnetz + just-intonation TECHNIQUE** (NEW to the lab) · **geometric / music-theoretical VIBE**. **What if you could WALK the geometry of harmony?** The Euler/Riemann triangular pitch lattice rendered as crisp luminous SVG: every node a pitch class, every triangle a consonant triad, the three axes the perfect fifth (+7), major third (+4), minor third (+3). **Tap a triangle → hear that triad in just intonation** (major 4:5:6, minor 10:12:15; two detuned triangle oscillators per voice → envelope → procedural-IR ConvolverNode reverb + limiter). **P / L / R** apply the three classic neo-Riemannian transforms — each glides exactly ONE voice while the two common tones hold, so you both *hear* and *see* smooth voice-leading as the highlight hops to the adjacent triangle. Your path weaves a glowing ribbon over the lattice and **exports as a standalone `.svg`** (scarce vector output). A **drift auto-walk** runs a gentle hands-free P-L-R-L… loop so it sounds + moves on load with zero input (a guaranteed sounding 06:30 phone glance); touch-only, no mic/camera/network → bulletproof unattended. README cites the **freshest anchor of the cycle — RESEARCH §533 / arXiv 2606.11246** ("Nineteen to the Dozen: Embedding the Neo-Riemannian Tonnetz…", **June 2026**, last-30-days) plus Euler / Cohn / Lewin — landing the in-README dated citation (criterion #5) that JURY flagged 0-for-15. 2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §533): an audio-only "room-raga" where the room's ambient pitch tunes a JI drone ⭐ + a Canvas2D self-similarity-matrix "recurrence room" of Karel's piano. **Clears 4/5 of the ambition floor** (#1 never-used technique · #2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 named reference · #5 dated RESEARCH citation).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/896-tonnetz-loom/README.md`

## Newest (Cycle 532 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/891-kids-sing-a-path](/dream/891-kids-sing-a-path)** — sing a path 🎤🪰. `demoable` · Cycle 532 (kids / storybook-night / **SVG**). **For**: kids (iPad/phone, 4yo-usable, no reading) · **mic/voice INPUT** (`getUserMedia` → `AnalyserNode`; real-time fundamental-frequency estimate via a normalized-square-difference / YIN-lite `trackPitch` + RMS loudness gate, so quiet = no draw) · **animated SVG OUTPUT** (a scrolling glowing `<path>` extended point-by-point + a firefly riding the tip + trailing sparkles + gradient night sky/moon/stars, all driven by rAF mutating SVG attributes — **no canvas, no webgl, no three.js**; SVG is the freshest visual surface, 1×/10, dodging the jury #1 raw-WebGL2 HARD-ban AND Canvas2D's near-cap 3×) · **real-time pitch-contour TECHNIQUE** (NEW to the lab) · **warm storybook-night VIBE**. **What if a 4-year-old could SING and watch their own voice draw a glowing path a firefly flies along — and it always sounds beautiful because we follow the SHAPE of their voice, not whether they hit a "correct" note?** The key move (RESEARCH §532 / **PESTO**, arXiv 2508.01488 — *transposition-equivariance*): the robust, meaningful quantity in a sung signal is the **contour** (relative up/down/hold motion), not absolute Hz. A `ContourEngine` maps the pitch stream to relative motion against a slow-drifting baseline (the child can wander up forever and stay on screen) → path height; a **`HarmonyVoice`** sings a soft companion tone back, **quantized to C-major pentatonic** with portamento, over a gentle pad drone + sparkle twinkles, through a master limiter (kids-safe, never harsh). The result is a **no-wrong-notes piece that is NOT the banned snap-to-safe-scale template** — the visual follows the child's raw gesture (never "wrong"); only the audio is quantized. Hands-free auto-demo (~0.6s) drives the *same* pipeline so the page sings + animates with zero hardware; a giant 🎤 Sing! button requests the mic; `text-rose-300` notices for demo/denied. README cites the dated **RESEARCH §532 / PESTO**. 2 sibling explorers banked (IDEAS §532): a tilt+shake audio-led "listening jar" (the lab's first audio-only-led kids piece) ⭐ + a three.js 3D glass-harmonica "glass rain" tank.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/891-kids-sing-a-path/README.md`

## Newest (Cycle 531 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 parallel approaches to one concept, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/888-living-reverie](/dream/888-living-reverie)** — living reverie 🌌. `demoable` · Cycle 531 (adult / cinematic / long-form / **three.js**). **For**: adults / deep listening / live-ambient · **audio-file INPUT** (Karel's real "Welcome Home" piano streamed from the read-only `/api/audio/549fc519…` route, played quietly under the generative voices + chroma-folded to re-seed the melody; ~350ms non-zero analyser → **emerald "playing Karel's piano"** else **amber "generative piano — live recording unavailable"**; zero-network generative fallback starts in ~1.5s) · **three.js OUTPUT** (a single high-detail displaced membrane — vertex-shader FBM noise on an icosphere, additive glow shell + fog, drag-orbit; **non-particle on purpose**, dodging the `859-paths-compute-bloom` particle field; fresh three.js surface 2×/10 vs Canvas2D's near-cap 3×, and dodges the jury #1 raw-WebGL2 ban) · **long-form generative state-machine-with-memory TECHNIQUE** (NEW to the rolling window) · **cinematic / deep / ambient VIBE**. **What if Karel's own recorded piano became a 10-minute LIVING reverie — genuinely different at minute 8 than minute 1 — a generative engine with MEMORY that re-voices and re-orchestrates his motifs across an irreversible arc, never looping?** A **two-tier memory engine** (RESEARCH §531 / *Fusing Memory and Attention*, arXiv 2603.21282): a **LOCAL** bank of recent scale-degree pitch-cells (transposed/varied/re-quantized so each minute sounds like the last — continuity), coupled to a **GLOBAL** monotonic irreversible `age` (0→1 over 600s) + a four-section state machine (**sparse → blooming → dense → dissolving**) that owns density, note-rate, register spread, brightness and reverb depth, plus a **modal journey** (ionian→lydian→mixolydian→dorian→aeolian with a creeping tonic, every note quantized). The membrane IS the arc: flat/dark → warm rising ridges → turbulent folded terrain → collapse to deep-violet stillness. FM-piano + detuned-saw pad + granular shimmer → runtime-rendered ConvolverNode reverb → compressor. README cites the dated **RESEARCH §531** finding + **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports*/*Reflection*). A **multi-cycle commitment** (cycle 1 of N) — 2 sibling visual approaches banked (IDEAS §531): a three.js volumetric raymarch twin + a Canvas2D ink-strata twin (the future WebGL-unavailable fallback).

## Newest (Cycle 530 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 parallel approaches to one concept, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/886-kids-marble-music](/dream/886-kids-marble-music)** — marble music machine 🎢. `demoable` · Cycle 530 (kids / playful / **Canvas2D**). **For**: kids (iPad/phone, 4yo-usable, no reading) · **tap + tilt INPUT** (tap the top to drop a glowing marble / tap a stuck one to free it; device-orientation `gamma` tilt to steer, with **two big hold-to-tilt buttons** as a no-permission fallback — fully playable with zero sensors) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (`getContext("2d")` — scarce 2×/10 + jury-re-legitimized; **no WebGL/WebGL2/WebGPU/three.js**, dodging the jury #1 raw-WebGL2 HARD-ban AND a 4th GPU surface) · **material-based modal impact synthesis TECHNIQUE** (fresh mapping; tilt-input is 0×/10) · **warm playful self-playing-machine VIBE**. **What if a 4-year-old could conduct a self-playing music machine just by tilting — rolling glowing marbles through a track of materials where the SOUND of every collision is the object's MATERIAL (wood thuds, glass rings, metal shimmers, drums boom) and how hard it was hit?** Each of ~16 objects has a fixed pentatonic pitch + a material; a real **modal synthesizer** fires that material's bank of decaying sine partials per impact (slightly-stretched harmonics = wood, clean long partials = glass, inharmonic bar ratios 1/2.76/5.40/8.93 = metal, circular-membrane ratios 1/1.59/2.14/2.30 = drum), and **impact normal-speed scales both loudness AND brightness** so a soft graze and a hard slam on the same bell are audibly different. Self-contained 2D physics (circles + capsules, restitution + tangential friction, sub-stepped + clamped), motion trails, per-hit glow pulses, one gentle "sticky mud" stakes element (a trapped marble waits until the child taps it free). The machine never repeats — a generative instrument in the spirit of **Wintergatan's Marble Machine** + **Jem Finer's self-generative marble machine**. README cites the dated **RESEARCH §530** finding. 2 sibling approaches banked (IDEAS §530): a three.js 3D-gravity twin + a Canvas2D build-your-own machine.

## Newest (Cycle 529 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/883-negative-space](/dream/883-negative-space)** — negative space 🤫. `demoable` · Cycle 529 (adult / conceptual-critical / **Canvas2D**). **For**: adults · **microphone INPUT** (analysis-only — connected to an AnalyserNode and nothing else; never routed to output, never recorded, never sent anywhere; **no API route**) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (`getContext("2d")` — scarce 1×/10 + jury-re-legitimized; **no WebGL/WebGL2/WebGPU/three.js**, dodging the jury #1 raw-WebGL2 HARD-ban AND a 4th WebGPU) · **silence-reward / negative-space generative bloom TECHNIQUE** · **Cage-conceptual, meditative, critical VIBE**. **What if you played an instrument by being SILENT — a piece that blooms only in the GAPS of your sound, and ducks to nothing the instant you make noise, so you compose it by what you withhold?** The lab's **conceptual/critical** lane (a menu category the jury notes hadn't appeared in ~15 cycles) and the directest answer to Karel's "massively bigger concepts / surprise" ask: a true **inversion** — every mic instrument ever made maps loud→active; this one maps **silence→music**, so the performer's instrument is *restraint*. Short-term RMS feeds a **stillness timer**: while you stay below threshold the timer grows and a warm additive chord-bloom rises one consonant voice at a time (root→fifth→octave→third→twelfth→high color) toward a full chord; any sound above threshold rushes a fast **duck** (reverse-sidechain) and **freezes** the bloom where it is — it **resumes building from there** when quiet returns (no reset). Signal chain voices → master → `ConvolverNode` synthesized-impulse reverb → `DynamicsCompressor` limiter → destination; an always-on near-silent root means it's never fully dead. **Visual:** Canvas2D — silence blooms a luminous field outward from center (monochrome→violet as the chord fills + faint concentric "voice" rings per lit partial); sound floods dark grain/static inward from the edges + a closing vignette and the light recedes; sized via `ResizeObserver`, animated by a single rAF that runs energy→stillness→audio→draw in lockstep. iOS-safe: AudioContext + `getUserMedia` created inside the Begin tap. **Degrades fully:** mic denied/absent → a scripted **synthetic** energy envelope (silence→burst→silence) runs through the **identical** `stepStillness` pipeline so the inversion proves itself hands-free within ~1.5–4s + a `text-rose-300` "no mic — showing a demo" notice; a **"Make a sound"** button lets a no-mic reviewer trigger the duck/erode manually; live mic yields the demo the moment real audio arrives. Full teardown (cancel rAF, `track.stop()` the mic, stop/disconnect oscillators, `audioCtx.close()`, clear timers, remove listeners). **No API route → no `guard`**; **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **2–3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [mic RMS/stillness gate + silence-reward generative harmony engine + Canvas2D negative-space renderer + synthetic auto-demo = ≥4] · #3 named refs [**John Cage *4′33″*** — silence as the content · **Pauline Oliveros "Deep Listening"**] · **#5 — README cites the dated RESEARCH §529 (2026-06-23)** cross-modal sound↔perception thread, the jury's 0-for-15 criterion finally landed for the second straight cycle). **Source:** §529 dive (cross-modal "It Sounds Squishy" DIS 2025 + Janáček/Cage/Eno named anchors) → AGENT.md path (a) implement a dive hook + Karel's "surprise / bigger concepts" → adult **WIDE** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (jury 2026-06-23 #1 + audit, last 10 = 859,862,864,866,868,869,872,874,877,880)=[OUTPUT raw-WebGL2 (HARD jury — new 6-of-15 monoculture) · a 4th WebGPU (would hit 4×) · the "external-feed→roughness/detune" data mapping (jury #3) · kids vibe (5×, n/a this adult cycle)]` · `picked (winner 883)=[microphone INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT (scarce 1× + jury-re-legit, dodges both GPU walls AND the back-to-back SVG repeat 880 would cause) · silence-reward/negative-space-inversion TECH (lab-novel) · Cage-conceptual VIBE (the cold conceptual/critical menu lane)]` — clears every active ban. **WIDE forks (banked, IDEAS §529):** **`882-prosody-score`** ⭐ (type a sentence → a real functional-harmony phrase notated live in **inline SVG**: punctuation drives V→I cadences, keystroke timing→rhythm, word length→contour; keyboard input ≈0× + SVG = freshest surface/coldest input; **de-selected only because 880 was also SVG** — back-to-back surface repeat — NOT quality; Janáček *nápěvky mluvy* inverted) + **`884-living-reverie`** ⭐⭐ (**TOP adult RESURRECT-FIRST**: Karel's real "Welcome Home" piano → a 10-min **three.js** long-form generative reverie with a memory bank + four-section state machine + irreversible `age`/modal journey so minute 8 ≠ minute 1; the highest-ambition explorer at 3–4/5 [#2+#4+#3+#5] and executes TWO standing directives — the thin long-form-generative lane + "use his actual Paths music"; de-selected on a mild "too-similar" overlap with `859-paths-compute-bloom`'s additive-particle field + GPU/CORS runtime risk, NOT quality → **ship it on a dedicated DEEP long-form adult cycle**) — both built complete + self-reported clean (no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route), `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed), full briefs in IDEAS §529. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 37.8s` → Linting and checking validity of types PASS → reached `Collecting page data`**; 883 grep-clean of the build log — every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; the one initial TS-lib error [`getFloatTimeDomainData` wants `Float32Array<ArrayBuffer>`] fixed in the single allowed attempt by backing the analyser buffer with an explicit `ArrayBuffer`). **Env note:** fresh container `npm ci` aborted on `sharp`'s native libvips download (a `@capacitor/assets` transitive dep, unused by `next build`) → installed with `npm ci --ignore-scripts` (CLI flag only; no package.json/lockfile/.npmrc edits). **NOT browser/device-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at static-gen blocks page-data collection (infra; fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by ear/eye: whether the live-mic RMS threshold reads stillness cleanly on a real device, and whether the bloom↔erode contrast lands perceptually; the synthetic auto-demo + "Make a sound" button guarantee a sounding, blooming, eroding glance with zero hardware.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/883-negative-space/README.md`

## (Cycle 528 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/880-kids-rubber-band-band](/dream/880-kids-rubber-band-band)** — Rubber-Band Band 🎸🌈. `demoable` · Cycle 528 (kids / freedom-to-be-wrong / **SVG**). **For**: kids (4+) · **drag-pluck + drag-the-tuning-peg INPUT** (pointer/touch; off the touch-only rut by being a *drag-to-pluck + drag-to-retune* gesture) · **inline SVG/DOM OUTPUT** (animated `<path>`/`<rect>`/`<circle>` — **0× in the last 10 ships**, the single freshest surface, the jury's explicit "SVG and audio-only are 0×" target; clears the jury #1 WebGL2 HARD-ban — NOT canvas, NOT WebGL/WebGL2/WebGPU/three.js) · **damped-spring SVG string physics + Karplus–Strong pluck + a latched detune/retune state machine** technique · **warm playful colorful band-stage vibe**. **What if a kid's first instrument were a wall of stretchy glowing rubber-bands you pluck — and pulling one TOO FAR leaves it slack and out-of-tune until you earn the fix by turning its tuning peg?** **The third piece that breaks the always-consonant kids template (JURY 2026-06-23 #4** — "let the wrong choice *persist and matter* — no auto-resolve like 849 — the way 868 makes the child earn the calm; build the third one and the template is broken for good"). ~7 horizontal bands across a dark warm stage, each a color + a note in C major-pentatonic; **drag a band sideways and release** → it twangs a **Karplus–Strong**-style plucked tone (noise burst → tuned `DelayNode` feedback comb with in-loop low-pass) and visibly wobbles on an integrated **damped spring** (mid-point displacement, stiffness/damping scale with tension) before settling. Pluck gently → a sweet in-tune note. **The stakes:** over-stretch a band and it **latches `detuned`** — sags, glow dies, twangs ~2 semitones flat/dull/sub-buzzy, an amber halo pulses on the loose peg — and it **STAYS that way** (no timeout, no auto-resolve). To fix it the child **drags the tuning-peg knob back up** until `taut` crosses the threshold and the band snaps true + glows again. The wrong choice is visible, audible, lasting; the child earns the fix — mapped onto a real-world intuition (tension sets pitch; you tune a string with a peg). Kids-safe chain `master 0.26 → lowpass 7.2kHz → DynamicsCompressor → destination`, always-on soft warm pad (never silent), the out-of-tune note an **unmistakable LARGE contrast** but **never louder/harsher** (quieter decay, loop filter drops ~5.2→1.4kHz, soft sub-buzz — per the developmental finding that a 4yo registers dissonance only with a large contrast). Large hit areas (70px band strokes, 38–76px peg halos). iOS gesture-gated Start (AudioContext created/resumed inside the tap). **Degrades fully:** no AudioContext → `text-rose-300` notice, the SVG bands still play visually. **Auto-demo:** within ~1–2s of Start with no input, it plucks a couple of bands sweetly, over-stretches one into the slack/detuned state, then retunes it taut+true — a hands-free 06:30 glance SEES and HEARS the full stakes arc; stops on first real touch. Full teardown (cancel RAF, stop/disconnect nodes, `ctx.close()`). **No API route → no `guard`**; **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **2–3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [drag-pluck + drag-retune + damped-spring SVG string sim + Karplus–Strong synth + detune state machine = ≥4] · #3 named refs [**Karplus & Strong (1983)** "Digital Synthesis of Plucked-String and Drum Timbres" · the **monochord / rubber-band** as a child's first tunable string] · touches #5 [README cites the dated RESEARCH §528 consonance-contrast law]). **Source:** §528 dive (the consonance-development design law — a 4yo registers dissonance only with a LARGE unmistakable contrast → tunes the size of "wrong") → AGENT.md path (a) implement the dive hook + JURY #4 (break the kids template for good) → kids **WIDE** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (jury 2026-06-23 #1 + audit, last 10 = 877,874,872,869,866,864,862,859,856,853)=[OUTPUT raw-WebGL2 4×/10 (HARD jury — the new 6-of-15 monoculture) · always-consonant no-wrong-note kids template (jury #4) · auto-resolve-the-mistake (849 critique)]` · `picked (winner 880)=[drag-pluck + drag-retune INPUT · SVG/DOM OUTPUT (0×/10 — the single freshest surface, the jury's named SVG-is-0× target) · latched-detune/retune-by-peg TECH (genuine persistent wrong, child earns the fix — NOT the banned always-consonant or auto-resolve) · warm-playful band-stage vibe]` — clears every active ban; lands on the scarcest render surface in the lab. **WIDE forks (banked, IDEAS §528):** **`879-kids-grumble-garden`** ⭐ (tap a dark night-garden to plant C-pentatonic flowers; a clashing tap sprouts a **grumble-weed** that *spreads* via a Canvas2D diffusion growth sim and drones with **no auto-resolve** — the child "waters" it with consonant taps to re-bloom it; **another full template-break, de-selected on diversity** — Canvas2D was just shipped 877 + the garden/growth vibe is thick in the lab [248/322/799], NOT quality) + **`881-kids-thunder-marbles`** (TILT a glowing 3D **three.js** bowl to roll marbles that ring bells; a hard collision cracks a persistent **storm-marble** the child calms by rolling it slow into the center cradle — **love-aligned 169-kids-marble-run❤️ + 83-kids-tilt-rain❤️**, de-selected on three.js 2× vs SVG 0× + WebGL/DeviceOrientation-permission failure surface for an unattended glance, NOT quality) — both built complete + self-reported tsc/eslint clean (no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route), `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed), full briefs in IDEAS §528. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully` → Linting and checking validity of types PASS → reached `Collecting page data`**; `npx eslint src/app/dream/880-kids-rubber-band-band/` → 0; 880 grep-clean of the build log — every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/device-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at static-gen blocks page-data collection (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 471; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear/hand: the live SVG pluck-wobble feel, whether the out-of-tune contrast reads as "wrong" to a real 4yo, and the retune-the-peg gesture's discoverability; the always-on pad + the full pluck→detune→retune auto-demo guarantee a sounding, animating, story-telling glance with zero hardware.
