## (Cycle 515 — **adult** build · DEEP, 2 approaches to one concept, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/847-feedback-ecology-ii](/dream/847-feedback-ecology-ii)** — Feedback Ecology II 🌀. `demoable` · Cycle 515 (adult / dynamical-system emergent / long-form generative / live no-input). **For**: adults · **no-input / self-oscillating input** (it runs itself — the starved input category the jury asked for; dodges the touch+mic over-rep, 8× across the last 15) · **raw WebGL2 output** (the scarce GPU surface — 1× in the last 15; breaks the jury's HARD Canvas2D ban which had relapsed to 7-of-15) · **coupled-resonator feedback network whose topology evolves itself — a Lorenz attractor drifting the coupling + Hebbian edges that strengthen-or-die — rendered with ping-pong FBO feedback trails** technique · **dynamical-system / David-Tudor feedback-ecosystem vibe (active, not ambient)**. **What if the feedback-resonator network from `820` took its hands off the wheel — a chaotic attractor weathering the coupling through bifurcations while the graph rewires itself via Hebbian plasticity — so it's a long-form instrument that is genuinely different at minute 5 than at minute 0, and never loops?** This is **cycle-2 of [820 · Feedback Ecology](/dream/820-feedback-ecology)**, building exactly the deepening 820's own README queued: *"add Lorenz-attractor coupling weights that drift over time."* It inherits **820's ear-safe Web Audio engine wholesale** (eight high-Q `BiquadFilter → DelayNode → feedback GainNode` resonators on a small-world coupling graph, root 55 Hz, every path ending in a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter before a master gain ≤0.3). On top: **(1) Lorenz drift** — a classic σ=10/ρ=28/β=8/3 system (dt=0.005, 6 Euler sub-steps/frame) integrated each frame; its normalized x→global coupling (sweeps isolated-pings ↔ entrainment ↔ drone on its own), y→self-feedback "edge of chaos," z→slow timbre (filter-Q + gentle ±detune), every target passed through 820's existing hard clamps (`MAX_COUPLING=0.35`, `MAX_FB_GAIN=0.88`). Because the Lorenz trajectory is aperiodic it never settles — the instrument *weathers*. **(2) Hebbian self-rewiring edges** — each edge carries a live weight: when both endpoints are energetic the edge potentiates ("fire together, wire together"); idle edges decay toward a non-zero floor (`[0.12, 1.0]` clamp) — *seldom-used links die, heavily-used links strengthen* — and the live weights both drive the coupling gains and set the edge brightness on screen, so you **watch** the graph rewire. **(3) Autonomous seeding** — every ~5.5s a gentle impulse is fired into the lowest-energy node so the ecology never dies (tapping a node still perturbs it, but it is not the primary input). **GPU render (raw WebGL2):** additive point-sprite node blobs (size/brightness ∝ energy, 820's per-node palette), GPU `LINES` edges whose alpha ∝ live Hebbian weight × signal flow, a **ping-pong FBO feedback-trail accumulation** (fade-and-feed-back each frame so energy leaves luminous decaying trails — the "David Tudor feedback ecosystem" look Canvas2D can't do cheaply, and thematically apt for a feedback instrument), plus a faint drifting Lorenz "weather" point-cloud + background tint so the autonomous drift is visible as well as audible. Shaders compiled/linked with error checks; on teardown all programs/buffers/VAOs/textures/framebuffers deleted + `WEBGL_lose_context`. **Ear-safety inherited from 820 unchanged** (limiter, hard-clamped gains, gentle 2s start ramp, always-visible **panic mute**, gesture-gated `AudioContext`). **Degrades:** `webgl2` null → `text-rose-300` notice but **audio keeps running** (sound is the point); no Web Audio → `text-rose-300` notice. **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples/network/recording); **zero new npm deps** (hand-written WebGL2, NOT three.js, NOT Canvas2D). Ambition honest **3/5** (#4 **multi-cycle commitment** — explicit cycle-2 of 820, the deepening 820's README itself queued · #1 **first chaotic-attractor-driven self-rewiring network topology in the lab** — 820 had a *static* small-world graph + manual sliders; here the adjacency matrix is itself a dynamical system [Lorenz + Hebbian], and the ping-pong FBO feedback-trail is a GPU technique the lab is thin on · #3 named refs [**E. N. Lorenz, "Deterministic Nonperiodic Flow," J. Atmos. Sci. 20 (1963)** · adaptive/time-varying pulse-coupled oscillator networks (Hebbian plasticity) · inherited **David Tudor — *Rainforest* (1968) / *Pulsers* (1976)** + **Toshimaru Nakamura** no-input lineage from 820]). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-22 **#5** ("820-feedback-ecology is your only net-new high-ambition build — ship its cycle-2 [Lorenz-drifting coupling] instead of always opening a new tab; develop what you have") + **#1** (hard-ban Canvas2D → force a GPU surface) → AGENT.md path (b) "continue a multi-cycle ambitious build already underway" → adult **DEEP** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (audit, last 10 = 846,842,841,837,834,833,828,827,822,816)=[OUTPUT Canvas2D 7× (HARD jury ban) · INPUT touch 4× (+ touch/mic-only 8× over 15)]` · `picked (winner 847)=[no-input/self-oscillating INPUT (starved, 2×) · raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT (scarce GPU, 1×) · Lorenz+Hebbian self-rewiring TECH · dynamical-system feedback-ecosystem VIBE]` — clears both jury bans. **DEEP fork:** the parallel sibling **`848-feedback-ecology-ii`** (the SAME concept rendered as a luminous orbiting **three.js** 3D graph — emissive spheres + weight-colored `LineSegments` + a Lorenz `THREE.Line` weather-trail, additive-halo bloom fallback) was built complete + verified clean (tsc 0 / eslint --max-warnings=0) and **de-selected on diversity + ambition** — three.js is 2× in the last 15 (847's raw WebGL2 is the scarcer GPU surface) and 847's ping-pong FBO feedback-trail is the more substantive + thematically-apt GPU technique. 848 banked to IDEAS §515 (top adult RESURRECT-FIRST for a cycle where three.js isn't over-rep'd), saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-515/`, removed before the authoritative winner-only build (non-winner not committed). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 53s` → Linting + type-check PASS → reached `Collecting page data`**; **847 folder grep-clean of the build log** — zero references, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; `npx eslint src/app/dream/847-feedback-ecology-ii/` → 0; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 472; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether the Lorenz drift reads as a *coherent* hour-long weather rather than random wander, whether the Hebbian rewiring is visually legible as links brighten/die, and whether the bifurcation sweep stays musical across its whole range (the always-clamped limiter + gentle ramp + panic mute guarantee a safe, sounding, evolving glance regardless; honest caveat — GPU `lineWidth` is alpha-faked since most platforms clamp it to 1, so edge strength reads through brightness not thickness).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/847-feedback-ecology-ii/README.md`

## (Cycle 514 — **kids** build · DEEP, 2 approaches to one concept, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/846-kids-sing-garden](/dream/846-kids-sing-garden)** — Sing the Garden 🌻. `demoable` · Cycle 514 (kids 4+ / warm-daylight living garden / long-form generative). **For**: kids (4+) · **mic/voice input** (off the over-represented touch tag the audit banned this cycle) · **three.js WebGL `THREE.Points` + custom `ShaderMaterial` output** (the cold renderer — 0× in the last 10 shipped; a GPU point-field of thousands of pollen/petal points) · **voice-fed EMERGENT phyllotaxis (largest-gap + local inhibition on an expanding growth front) + long-form self-organizing/self-singing field** technique · **calm warm-daylight living-garden vibe**. **What if a 4-year-old's voice didn't just *place* flowers but GREW an organic garden whose spiral arrangement emerges on its own — the way a real sunflower head self-organizes — and the garden keeps breathing and growing even when the room is quiet?** The child hums/sings; a hand-rolled autocorrelation (Chris Wilson ACF) pitch detector with an RMS noise-gate snaps each note to a **C-major pentatonic** (no wrong notes) and deposits a new growth site **in the largest angular gap on the current front**, with an inhibition rule that relaxes as the head fills — so the angular increment self-organizes toward the golden angle (~137.5°) **without hardcoding it**, a lightweight Keller-Segel-flavoured phyllotaxis (arXiv 2509.06498, Sept 2025). Pitch → hue (low warm rose/gold → high cool sky/violet) + which chord voice it feeds; loudness → growth vigor + bloom brightness; a held note accelerates growth. **State + memory (long-form):** the field keeps seeding faint ambient sites and decaying bloom pulses during silence, and after ~2.5s quiet plays a soft generative bell arpeggio from already-grown sites — visibly and audibly richer at minute 5 than minute 1. Kid-safe `master gain 0.28 → lowpass 7000 → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)`; always-on C2+G2 pad (never silent); analyser never wired to output; iOS gesture-gated AudioContext in the first tap; **mic-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + ghost-hum auto-singer + tap-anywhere-to-grow** (fully demoable without a mic); WebGL-null → friendly notice; full teardown (rAF + `audioCtx.close()` + three.js geometry/material/renderer dispose + mic tracks stopped). **No API route → no `guard`** (no samples/network/recording — mic used only for live pitch/RMS, never stored or transmitted); **zero new npm deps** (three.js v0.182 already installed; NOT Canvas2D). Ambition honest **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [mic ACF pitch-detect + emergent largest-gap growth field + shader point-render + generative self-singing bell arpeggio = 4] · #3 named refs [**arXiv 2509.06498 "Phyllotaxis in a Keller-Segel model" (2025)** · **H. Vogel (1979)** sunflower golden-angle baseline · **Mort Garson *Mother Earth's Plantasia* (1976)** vibe · **Chris Wilson** autocorrelation] · #5 references RESEARCH §514, added this cycle <14 days). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 #5 (ship a banked sibling — this is the ⭐ top kids RESURRECT-FIRST `800-kids-sing-meadow`, elevated) → RESEARCH §514 (phyllotaxis is *emergent*, not just drawn) → kids **DEEP** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (audit, last 10)=[INPUT touch 4× · OUTPUT Canvas2D 6×]` · `picked (winner 846)=[mic/voice INPUT · three.js shader point-field OUTPUT (cold) · emergent-phyllotaxis TECH · warm-daylight-living-garden VIBE]` — clears both bans. **DEEP fork:** the discrete/legible sibling **`845-kids-sing-meadow`** (one sung note → exactly one flower on the literal Vogel spiral, three.js `InstancedMesh`, call-and-response replay — the *most 4yo-legible* take) was built complete + clean (tsc 0 / eslint 0) and **de-selected on ambition/research-fit** (846 realizes today's actual research finding and is long-form; 845 plots the static formula). 845 banked to IDEAS §514 (top kids RESURRECT-FIRST), saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-514/`, removed before the authoritative winner-only build (non-winner not committed). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: `✓ Compiled successfully` → Linting and checking validity of types → reached `Collecting page data`; **846 folder grep-clean of the build log** — zero references, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; `npx eslint src/app/dream/846-kids-sing-garden/` → 0; `tsc --noEmit` → 0 in folder; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 472; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether the emergent spiral reads as a recognizable sunflower head on a real GPU, whether a 4-year-old's hum reliably plants via ACF on a phone mic, and whether the abstract point-field reads as "I made that" the way discrete flowers (845) would (the ghost-hum + tap-to-grow fallback + always-on pad guarantee a sounding, growing glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/846-kids-sing-garden/README.md`

## (Cycle 511 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/837-quasicrystal](/dream/837-quasicrystal)** — Quasicrystal 🔷. `demoable` · Cycle 511 (adult / generative / long-form / clinical-crystalline). **For**: adults · **autonomous-generative input** (it plays itself; you tune tempo / φ-inflation / traversal / reseed — NOT a touch-performance, off the over-represented touch tag) · **Canvas2D pentagrid-tiling output** (deep-near-black ground, cyan fat-rhomb + violet thin-rhomb glowing edges, Ikeda crystalline) · **de Bruijn pentagrid → Penrose P3 aperiodic tiling → aperiodic-order music** technique (grep-0× — the lab's FIRST aperiodic/quasicrystal composition) · **geometric crystalline / Ryoji-Ikeda vibe**. **What if a piece of music had the structure of a quasicrystal — perfectly ordered, self-similar, yet NEVER exactly repeating — so it can play for an hour and stay coherent without ever looping?** A real **de Bruijn pentagrid** (5 line-families at 36° steps, offsets γ₀…γ₄) is dual-transformed into a true **Penrose P3 rhomb tiling** with exact 5-fold symmetry and provable aperiodicity — fat rhombs (|j−k|=1,4) and thin rhombs (|j−k|=2,3). A **traversal** (spiral / sweep / growth-front) visits each tile and sonifies it: the 7 Penrose **vertex configurations** (sun/star/ace/deuce/jack/king/queen) map to **just-intonation ratios** (sun→1/1, queen→3/2, king→4/3, jack→5/4, star→6/5, deuce→**7/4** harmonic seventh, ace→9/8), register rises with distance from center, fat rhombs use warm additive partials (1f·2f·3f) and thin rhombs bright partials + FM shimmer, over a slow drone that shifts as the traversal travels outward. Because the tiling is aperiodic but built from a finite local vocabulary, the line is **non-repeating yet self-similar** — motifs recur transposed and re-spaced but the exact sequence never returns, so it's genuinely different at minute 5 than minute 1 (the lab is thin on true **long-form generative**). **φ-inflation** zooms the world by powers of φ=(1+√5)/2 — the same patterns reappear at larger scale, a slow harmonic-rhythm change. An in-app **Design Notes** overlay explains the tiling, the mapping, and why it never repeats. Voices glide with `setTargetAtTime`/ramps (no clicks); master `gain → DynamicsCompressor → destination`, modest gain; gesture-gated AudioContext; full teardown (rAF + `audioCtx.dispose()`). **Degrades:** no Web Audio → `text-rose-300` notice, the crystal still grows silently. **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples/network/recording); **zero new npm deps** (Canvas2D, NOT three.js). Ambition honest **3/5** (#1 **first aperiodic-order/quasicrystal music in the lab** — builder grep of penrose/aperiodic/quasicrystal/de-bruijn/pentagrid found only 3 *incidental* mentions, no prior aperiodic-music · #2 ≥3 subsystems [de Bruijn pentagrid tiling generator + traversal→sequencer + additive/FM synth voices + drone + Canvas2D render = 4+] · #3 named refs [**Roger Penrose** P3 (1974) · **N.G. de Bruijn** pentagrid/"Algebraic theory of Penrose's non-periodic tilings", 1981 · **Dan Shechtman** quasicrystals, Nobel 2011 · **"Quasiperiodic Music"** arXiv 2009.04667; aesthetic kin Ryoji Ikeda]). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 #1 (rest his recording, end the answering-agent thread) → RESEARCH §511 (Skylight made the sky *visible* but silent → data/structure sonification; "Quasiperiodic Music" arXiv 2009.04667 → aperiodic order as musical structure) → this adult **WIDE** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21 + audit)=[his-recording-as-source · machine-listens-to-recording/answering-agent · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default · touch-input (5× in last 10)]` · `picked (winner 837)=[autonomous-generative INPUT (off touch) · Canvas2D crystalline-tiling OUTPUT · de-Bruijn-pentagrid/aperiodic-order TECH (grep-0×, NOT his-recording, NOT answer/follow, NOT pentatonic) · geometric-crystalline-Ikeda VIBE]` — clears every ban. **WIDE fan-out — 2 explored, banked to IDEAS §511:** `836-flightpaths` (sonify the live ADS-B sky as a clinical radar organ — each real aircraft a sustained spatial voice; the genuine real-world-data lab-first that answers JURY #3, de-selected because its live feed is often CORS-blocked → most visitors see the simulated sky; **top adult RESURRECT-FIRST**) + `838-hocket-loom` (play one live MIDI/keyboard line → it shatters across an interlocking hocket/kotekan/phase ensemble; de-selected because canon/echo/interlock is the lab's most-trodden ground). Both built complete + build-verified (tsc 0 / eslint 0), saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-511/`, removed before the authoritative winner-only build (non-winners not committed). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 42s` → Linting and checking validity of types → Collecting page data**; `npx eslint` on the folder → 0/0; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 472; cgroup-locked, unraisable as root; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether the aperiodic sequence reads as "coherent but never looping" to the ear and whether the vertex→JI mapping sounds as intended on a real device (the always-on drone + autonomous traversal guarantee a sounding, crystallizing glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/837-quasicrystal/README.md`

## (Cycle 510 — **kids** build · DEEP, 2 approaches to one concept, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/834-kids-paint-mixer](/dream/834-kids-paint-mixer)** — Paint Mixer 🎨. `demoable` · Cycle 510 (kids 4+ / bright painterly / color-as-language). **For**: kids (4+) · **touch drag-paint input** (no reading) · **raw WebGL2 output** (the lab's COLDEST renderer, 0× in the last 10 shipped) · **physically-correct Kubelka-Munk subtractive pigment mixing → GPU readPixels → gliding chord** technique · **bright cream finger-paint vibe**. **What if a 4-year-old finger-paints by MIXING REAL PAINT — and the color they mix IS the chord they hear?** Three big glowing pigment blobs (red/magenta, yellow, blue) drag around a cream paper canvas; where they overlap the paint mixes like REAL paint — **blue + yellow makes GREEN**, not the muddy gray that ordinary RGB mixing gives — because a GLSL ES 3.00 fragment shader runs full **36-sample Kubelka-Munk spectral mixing per pixel** (RGB→K/S absorption+scattering, mix in spectral space, integrate over CIE XYZ color-matching functions, back to sRGB), with a domain-warp smear for a wet-paint feel. Each ~20 Hz frame, `readPixels` samples the actual mixed pixels under the blob centroid → HSV → drives a live, always-in-tune chord over a fixed C+G pad: **warm/gold → bright major/add9 · green/teal → open sus · blue/violet → tender minor · magenta → dreamy maj7**, with saturation→richness and value→brightness, all gliding click-free via `setTargetAtTime` (voices never retrigger). So the heard chord **exactly equals the seen mixed color** — the child paints harmony, never a wrong note (the fixed center holds it in tune; color shapes only quality + richness). The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-21 #4** (kill the kids "pentatonic-never-wrong" crutch — let harmony be *shaped*) and **#5** (ship a banked sibling — this is the §508 ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST `829-kids-color-blend`, rebuilt and upgraded from approximate RGB blend to physically-correct pigment optics + real GPU pixel-readback). Kids-safe `masterGain 0.27 → lowpass ≤7 kHz → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)`; always-on pad (never silent); ≥64–72px tap targets; iOS gesture-gated AudioContext in the first tap; auto-drift after ~1.5s idle so an unattended phone keeps painting + singing; **WebGL2-null → CSS `mix-blend-mode:multiply` fallback that still drives the same audio** + `text-rose-300` notice; no Web Audio → visuals stay alive + notice; full teardown (program/buffer/VAO + `WEBGL_lose_context` + `audioCtx.close()`). **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples/network/recording); **zero new npm deps** (raw WebGL2, no three.js). Ambition honest **3/5** (#1 **first Kubelka-Munk physical pigment mixing in the lab** — grep-0×; every prior "pigment" is metaphorical Anadol data-as-pigment — and **first GPU `readPixels`→audio coupling** · #2 ≥3 subsystems [touch drag-physics + per-pixel Kubelka-Munk spectral mixing + readPixels color→chord-quality synth + WebGL2 render = 4] · #3 named refs [**Isaac Newton's 7-color↔7-note mapping** · **Scriabin's *clavier à lumières*** · **Mixbox / "Practical Pigment Mixing for Digital Painting," Sochorová & Jamriška, SIGGRAPH Asia 2021** + **Spectral.js** Kubelka-Munk]) · soft #5 (RESEARCH §510 — Mixbox/Spectral.js GLSL + CoolerSpace 2026 color tooling). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 #4 + #5 → RESEARCH §510 (RGB additive mixing is physically WRONG — blue+yellow=gray; Kubelka-Munk gives blue+yellow=green like real finger-paint) → kids **DEEP** fire (paint vs. light). **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21)=[pentatonic-never-wrong (kids crutch) · UPIC pitch-painter · his-recording-as-source · machine-listens-to-recording · adult-ambient-as-default]` · `picked (winner 834)=[touch drag-paint INPUT · raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT (cold, 0× last 10) · Kubelka-Munk-pigment-mix→chord-quality TECH (harmony genuinely shaped, NOT pentatonic, NOT UPIC) · bright cream finger-paint VIBE]` — clears every ban. **DEEP fork:** the physically-opposite sibling **`835-kids-light-mixer`** (additive LIGHT — red+green=yellow, all=white, on a near-black starfield) was built complete + clean and **de-selected on novelty** (additive RGB mixing is the *ordinary* mix the world already does — and the original 829 framing; the pigment approach is the lab-first + the fresher, more kid-native surprise). 835 banked to IDEAS §510, saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-510/`, removed before the authoritative build (non-winner not committed). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: `✓ Compiled successfully in 80s` → Linting + type-check PASS → reached `Collecting page data`; **834 folder grep-clean of the build log** — zero references, zero warnings/errors from it, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 472; cgroup-locked, unraisable as root; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether blue+yellow visibly reads as green on a real GPU and whether the heard chord tracks the mixed color tightly under a child's finger (the always-on pad + auto-drift + CSS fallback guarantee a sounding, painting glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/834-kids-paint-mixer/README.md`

## (Cycle 509 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/833-spectral-loom](/dream/833-spectral-loom)** — Spectral Loom ❄️🎨. `demoable` · Cycle 509 (adult / clinical-Ikeda / live-performance). **For**: adults · **live-mic input** (rests his recording — the material is the *visitor's* own sound; the visitor, not a stored file) · **Canvas2D spectrogram output** (cool monochrome→cyan/violet magnitude ramp, crisp grid, bright scrub-head) · **freeze-the-live-STFT → editable spectral-image buffer → additive resynth** technique · **clinical / data-instrument / Ryoji-Ikeda vibe — active not ambient**. **What if you could FREEZE your own live sound as a spectrogram *image* and then PAINT that frozen picture — smear energy sideways, brush it in and out, stretch it — and hear the picture you painted?** A live STFT (`AnalyserNode` fftSize 1024, dB→0..1, folded onto a perceptual log-frequency row grid) scrolls a rolling spectrogram; **❄ Freeze** copies the window into an editable magnitude matrix `buffer[TIME][FREQ]` — a held image. **Editing the picture edits the sound:** a soft-falloff Gaussian brush **raises/lowers** magnitude, **smear** drags spectral energy sideways in time (freeze-stretch), a **freeze-stretch slider** runs a per-frame IIR time-blur that melts transients into drones, and a **scrub head** sweeps the frozen frame with a **Loop** toggle for rhythmic re-triggering (active, never a static drone). Resynthesis = a capped **96-oscillator additive bank** reading the magnitude column under the scrub head each frame, the loudest rows mapped to oscillator freq+gain with `setTargetAtTime` smoothing (no zipper). Master `gain → lowpass ≤13.5 kHz → DynamicsCompressor → destination`. **Crucial mic-free path:** mic denied/absent → a visible `text-rose-300` notice **and** a "Use demo sound" button that routes an internally-generated evolving chord/whistle/noise through the **identical** capture→freeze→scrub pipeline, so the whole mechanic is fully demoable with no mic; the canvas animates a demo spectrogram on load (never blank, never silent). **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples, no network, no recording stored); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [live STFT capture + frozen editable spectral buffer + 96-osc additive resynth + spectrogram renderer = 4] · #3 named ref [**Jean-François Charles, "A Tutorial on Spectral Sound Processing Using Max/MSP and Jitter," Computer Music Journal 32:3 (2008)** — spectrogram-as-editable-image-buffer, the GRM/IRCAM spectral-freeze lineage; aesthetic kin **Ryoji Ikeda**]). **#1 NOT claimed (honesty):** spectral processing has lab ancestors (`34-spectral-morph`, `321-spectral-flight`) — but **none let you PAINT the frozen image and resynth it**, which is 833's genuine differentiator + high surprise ("the picture *is* the sound"). **Resurrects the §507-banked `826-spectral-loom`**, satisfying **JURY 2026-06-21 #5** ("stop banking DEEP losers — ship a banked sibling, clear the bank"). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 #1 (rest his recording, end the answering-agent thread) + #3 (real-world-data / non-music) + #5 (ship a banked sibling) → RESEARCH §509 (space-weather sonification is fresh-in-2026 but a grep proved it's owned by the lab — `314`/`471`; EDM-drop owned by `387`/`631` — so two of three briefs were re-treads, redirecting to the one un-owned framing) → this adult **WIDE** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21)=[his-recording-as-source · machine-listens-to-recording · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default]` · `picked (winner 833)=[live-mic INPUT (rests his recording) · Canvas2D spectrogram OUTPUT (≤3×, not banned) · spectral-freeze-and-PAINT TECH (NOT his-recording, NOT pentatonic, NOT UPIC, NOT machine-listens-to-HIS-recording) · clinical-Ikeda active VIBE (deliberately NOT ambient)]` — clears every ban. The 3 explorers spread inputs (mic / external-API / transport-touch) and techniques (spectral-paint / space-weather-sonification / EDM-arrangement); the two non-winners (`831-solar-wind-organ` ≈ existing `314`/`471`; `832-drop-engine` ≈ existing `387`/`631`) were caught redundant at the curate grep and **NOT committed or re-seeded as fresh** (logged honestly to STATE/IDEAS as a research-grep lesson; saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-509/`). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: TypeScript + ESLint + production webpack compile PASS, reached `Collecting page data`; **833 folder grep-clean** — zero warnings/errors from it, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; `npx eslint` on the folder → 0/0; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 471; cgroup-locked, unraisable as root; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether painting the frozen spectrogram audibly reworks the resynth and whether the additive-bank level is balanced on a real device (the demo-sound path + on-load animation guarantee a sounding, animated glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/833-spectral-loom/README.md`

## (Cycle 508 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/828-kids-feelings-sun](/dream/828-kids-feelings-sun)** — Feelings Sun 🌞💛. `demoable` · Cycle 508 (kids, 4+). **For**: a 4-year-old on iPad/mobile · **touch-DRAG input** (one big draggable sun; mouse too) · **animated SVG/DOM output** (glowing sun with `feGaussianBlur`, CSS-animated sky gradient + drifting motes — NOT Canvas2D, NOT three.js, NOT WebGL) · **2-D affect-field → bilinear chord-quality morph** technique (grep-thin — the kids lane's first *continuous emotional* harmony-shaper) · **warm emotional kids-sky vibe**. **What if a 4-year-old could SHAPE the FEELING of a chord — drag one friendly sun across a feelings-sky and the harmony morphs happy↔cozy↔floaty↔dreamy — instead of tapping a pre-approved pentatonic scale?** The sun's (x,y) **bilinearly blends four chord qualities** at the corners: up-left bright **major** (happy), down-left **minor** (cozy/wistful), down-right **sus** (floaty), up-right **add9/maj7** (dreamy). A fixed Web Audio pad always holds ONE tonal center (**root drone + perfect-fifth ALWAYS sounding**, so it is always "in tune"); two **color voices glide continuously** — the harmonic third morphs maj3↔min3↔P4, plus a high added-tone voice that fades in toward the dreamy corner — ALL via `setTargetAtTime`/ramps so the harmony morphs **click-free** (voices never re-triggered). Every position is consonant, but the child genuinely controls bright-vs-dark / open-vs-rich / plain-vs-dreamy. The sky gradient, the sun's **expressive face** (smile + sleepy-dreamy eyes), its glow color, and floating motes all shift with the current feeling; a soft bell sparkle trails the sun. **This is the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-21 #4** (kill "pentatonic-never-wrong"; let harmony be *shaped*) on the **emotion axis** — completing the kids harmony-shaping set: `816` shaped harmony by **interval-stacking**, `822` shaped **rhythm** by spinning polygons, `828` shapes the **feeling/quality** continuously. It also satisfies **JURY #5** ("stop banking DEEP losers — ship a banked sibling"): 828 is the cycle-506 ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST banked `823-kids-feelings-chords`, built and shipped. **Interaction (no reading):** drag the ≥200px sun anywhere; untouched for ~1.5s it gently auto-drifts on a slow Lissajous path so an unattended phone is always morphing + singing. Kids-safe `master gain ≈0.28 → lowpass ≈7000 → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; soft ~0.6s attacks; always-on pad (never silent); iOS gesture-gated AudioContext in the first tap; full rAF + `audioCtx.close()` teardown. **Degrades:** no Web Audio → friendly `text-rose-300` notice, visuals stay alive. **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples, no network, no data collection); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [touch affect-field mapping + bilinear chord-quality morph engine + always-on pad/color-voice synth + animated SVG render = 4] · #3 named refs [**Russell's circumplex model of affect** (valence×arousal) · **Hevner's affective tone/mode associations** · current touchstone **Synesthesia "Color to Sound" 2026 AURA mode** color→4-voice chord]). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 #4 (kill pentatonic; shape harmony) + #5 (ship a banked sibling — resurrects the §506 ⭐ `823`) → RESEARCH §508 (color/feeling→chord; Synesthesia AURA 2026 + Sound Color Project/Plutchik) → this kids **WIDE** fire. Winner of a 3-explorer fire; banked **`829-kids-color-blend`** ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (mix two glowing colored lights → the color you make IS the chord; **raw WebGL2** — the lab's COLDEST renderer, 0× in the last 10; color-as-language, love-aligned `82`❤️/`317`❤️) + **`830-kids-feelings-choir`** (tap to gather singing creatures into a circle → assemble a chord; Canvas2D, Toca-Band orchestration) as **IDEAS §508**. **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21)=[his-recording-as-source · machine-listens-to-recording · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default]` · `picked (winner 828)=[touch-DRAG INPUT · SVG/DOM OUTPUT (≤3×, not banned — jury said surfaces are spread, don't reflexively flee) · continuous-affect-field chord-quality-morph TECH (NOT pentatonic, NOT UPIC, NOT his-recording) · warm-emotional kids VIBE]` — clears every ban; the 3 explorers spread renderers SVG-DOM / WebGL2 / Canvas2D and shaping-mechanisms feeling-position / color-mix / gather-orchestration. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: TypeScript + ESLint + production webpack compile PASS, reached `Collecting page data`; **828 folder grep-clean** — zero warnings/errors from it, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (infra, fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 471; cgroup-locked, unraisable as root; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether a 4-year-old reads "drag up-left = happy, down-left = cozy" without instruction and whether the third audibly morphs major↔minor smoothly on a real device (the always-on pad + fifth + idle auto-drift guarantee a sounding, morphing glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/828-kids-feelings-sun/README.md`

## (Cycle 507 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/827-waveguide-mesh](/dream/827-waveguide-mesh)** — Waveguide Mesh 🪘🌊. `demoable` · Cycle 507 (adult / live-performance / installation). **For**: adults / stage · **Web MIDI + on-screen-pad + direct-membrane + keyboard input** (the most stage-playable in the window; MIDI is **0× in the last 10 shipped** — the starved input) · **Canvas2D displacement-field output** (coppery-violet heatmap that paints the *exact* field the audio reads) · **2-D digital-waveguide-mesh / FDTD wave-equation membrane run at AUDIO sample rate in an AudioWorklet** technique · **percussive, metallic, live-performance vibe — deliberately NOT ambient**. **What if a chord weren't synthesized but physically *travelled*?** You strike a point on a 2-D vibrating drumhead; a Gaussian energy bump spreads, reflects off the rigid rim, interferes with itself, and rings as a real plate/drum tone via the standard FDTD update `u_next = (2u − u_prev + C²∇²u)·damping` (fixed boundaries, C inside the 2-D CFL bound ≤1/√2). The mesh runs **at the sample rate inside a Blob-URL `AudioWorklet`** (`worklet-source.ts`); the **audio signal IS the displacement read at fixed pickup nodes** (DC-blocked + softly saturated), and the worklet posts a downsampled field ~45×/s so **the wavefronts you see are the waveform you hear**. If `AudioWorklet` is unavailable it degrades to a matched **bank of modal resonators** (circular-membrane eigenratios 1.00·1.59·2.14·2.30·2.65…) excited per strike + a control-rate FDTD mesh for the same visual — audio/visual stay coupled either way. **Play it** via a MIDI keyboard (note-on velocity = strike force, pitch = membrane tension = wave speed = strike position), two rows of on-screen pads (toms/plates — tap several for a chord), direct taps on the head, or the A–L computer-keyboard row; status line shows "MIDI connected ✓" or an amber "no MIDI — use the pads." Pitch is the **full chromatic scale** (NOT pentatonic); higher notes tighten the head (faster waves, shorter ring) for articulation; chords = multiple strikes summed into one membrane. Master `gain → lowpass ≤12 kHz → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; audio gesture-gated (iOS unlock); idle auto-strikes so the canvas is never dead. **No samples, no recordings, no network, no API route → no `guard`; zero new npm deps.** Ambition honest **2/5** (**#2** ≥3 subsystems [FDTD mesh DSP + Web MIDI + AudioWorklet audio-rate engine w/ modal fallback + Canvas2D renderer = 4] · **#3** named refs [**Van Duyne & Smith, "Physical Modeling with the 2-D Digital Waveguide Mesh," ICMC 1993**; Perry Cook STK modal synthesis for the fallback]). **#1 NOT claimed (honesty):** FDTD-wave-field-as-sound already exists as kids `478-kids-wave-pond` + `489-kids-pond-pair`; 827's genuine differentiators are the **adult MIDI live-performance register** of that family and the **audio-rate-in-AudioWorklet** mesh (vs. their control-rate visual + pickup). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 (rest his recording, end the answering-agent thread, stop defaulting to adult-ambient, chase a starved frontier) → RESEARCH §507 (GPU-compute-field→sound is *saturated* in the lab — `327`/`520`/`579`/`55` + ShaderVine 2026 — so redirect off it toward the starved live-performance/MIDI physical-model axis) → this adult **WIDE** fire. Winner of a 3-explorer fire; banked **`825-gravity-sequencer`** (pointer · symplectic N-body orbital integration as a *percussion sequencer* — perihelion crossings strike tuned bells, stable orbits lock a groove, chaos drifts it; Otomata + Kepler) + **`826-spectral-loom`** (live-mic STFT freeze → editable spectral-image buffer → additive resynth; smear/scrub/brush the frozen spectrum like wet paint, edit-the-picture-edits-the-sound, demoable mic-free; Jean-François Charles CMJ 2008) as **IDEAS §507**. **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21)=[his-recording-as-source · machine-listens-to-recording · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default]` · `picked (winner 827)=[Web-MIDI/pad INPUT (starved 0× in window) · Canvas2D displacement-field OUTPUT (≤3×, not banned) · 2-D-FDTD-waveguide-mesh-audio TECH (not in last 10) · percussive live-performance VIBE (NOT ambient)]` — clears every ban; the 3 explorers spread inputs MIDI/pointer/mic and techniques waveguide-mesh/orbital-sequencer/spectral-paint. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 42s` → Linting and checking validity of types → Collecting page data**; **827 folder grep-clean** — zero warnings/errors from it, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; `npx eslint` on the folder → 0/0; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (fails identically on pristine `main`; ceiling cgroup-locked, unraisable as root; Vercel deploys normally). Unverified by eye/ear: whether the audio-rate worklet mesh stays stable + musical across a real device and whether the visible field reads as tightly synced to the sound (idle auto-strikes guarantee a sounding, rippling glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/827-waveguide-mesh/README.md`

## Cycle 506 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1

- **[/dream/822-kids-shape-drums](/dream/822-kids-shape-drums)** — Shape Drums 🔺🥁. `demoable` · Cycle 506 (kids, 4+). **For**: a 4-year-old on iPad/mobile · **touch input** (tap to add/cycle shapes, big turtle/rabbit speed buttons) · **Canvas2D output** (full-bleed `requestAnimationFrame` rotating polygons — off the recent three.js 799/803/805 streak; jury says Canvas2D no longer banned at 3× and the concept reads cleanly in 2D) · **polyrhythm-as-rotating-polygons / vertex-crossing scheduler** technique (grep-thin — the kids lane's FIRST shapeable-rhythm piece) · **playful, bold-saturated-on-near-black, geometric, calm-but-lively vibe**. **What if a 4-year-old could BUILD a polyrhythm by spinning shapes — choosing how many sides each shape has and how fast it spins — instead of tapping a pre-approved pentatonic scale?** 1–5 big rotating polygons sit on a dark canvas, each a bold color with glowing vertex dots; a faint trigger line crosses the top of each shape, and every time a vertex sweeps past it the shape PINGS a warm note + flashes a ripple. A triangle pings **3×/rotation**, a hexagon **6×** — so the **number of sides the child chooses IS the rhythmic subdivision**, and the **spin speed IS the tempo**. Two shapes of different side-counts spinning at related speeds (×0.5/×1/×1.5/×2 of a base tempo, snapped so they stay clean) drift in and out of phase — the child has literally *built* a 3-against-2 or 4-against-3 polyrhythm. **This is the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-21 #4** — which banned the kids lane's "pentatonic-never-wrong" crutch (6×) and asked for a piece where **harmony OR rhythm can be *shaped***. Cycle 504 answered the *harmony* half (`816-kids-tone-tower`, JI chord-stacking); 822 answers the **rhythm** half (the grep-thin register), completing the pair. Pitch is NOT the child's variable — each shape is locked to one tone of a single warm **Dadd9 stack** (D3/A3/D4/F#4/B4) so *every* combination is consonant — the child shapes the **rhythmic interplay**, never a "wrong note." **Interaction (no reading):** big round **+** (80px) adds a shape (cycles triangle→square→pentagon→hexagon, cap 5); **tap a shape** cycles its sides 3→8; **tap its center** removes it (never empty); one **giant 64px speed button per shape** (turtle/rabbit, no words) cycles the musically-related ratios. Pings fire via a per-frame **vertex-crossing edge detector** in the rAF loop; soft marimba/bell synthesis (3 detuned partials, click-free `linearRamp`-attack/`setTargetAtTime`-decay, lowpass tail, light feedback delay) + a quiet sustained pad so it's never silent — levels low, safe for a sleeping toddler. **Audio gesture-gated** ("tap to start" overlay, iOS unlock), rAF cancelled + AudioContext closed on unmount. **Degrades:** no Web Audio → visuals stay alive + a small "sound is off" notice (no throw). **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples, no network, no data collection); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **2–3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [touch shape-builder + rotating-geometry vertex-crossing scheduler + chord-voiced marimba synth + Canvas2D render = 4] · #3 named refs [**"Polyrhythms in shapes"** / thekidshouldseethis · **Musical Toys "Polyrhythm"** · **Steve Reich phasing / Harvard pendulum-wave** lineage] · soft #5 RESEARCH §506 today). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 #4 (kill pentatonic; shape rhythm) + #5 (ship a banked sibling — this resurrects/de-pentatones the §504-banked `818-kids-pendulum-choir` as a rotating-polygon variant) → RESEARCH §506 (polyrhythm-as-rotating-polygons) → this kids **WIDE** fire. Winner of a 3-explorer fire; banked **`823-kids-feelings-chords`** ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (drag a sun across a feelings-sky → SHAPE the chord's emotion major↔minor↔sus↔add9, SVG/DOM — the renderer-diverse harmony pick) + **`824-kids-clap-loop`** (clap a rhythm → a glowing WebGL2 band loops it back, resurrects §504-banked 817) as **IDEAS §506**. **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21)=[his-recording-as-source · machine-listens-to-recording · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default]` · `picked (winner 822)=[touch INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT (off the three.js streak) · polyrhythm-rotating-polygon shapeable-RHYTHM TECH (NOT pentatonic, NOT UPIC) · playful-geometric kids VIBE]` — clears every ban; the 3 explorers spread renderers Canvas2D/SVG-DOM/WebGL2 (the "spread, don't flip" answer). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: all code gates PASS — TypeScript + ESLint + production webpack compile, reached `Collecting page data`; **822 folder grep-clean** — zero warnings/errors from it, every warning pre-existing `src/lib/**`; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container **~4096-fd `EMFILE`** at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen. *Nuance THIS cycle:* pristine `main` built **fully** (just under the fd ceiling), and adding 822 (one more of ~500 routes) tips static-gen over it — a **page-count fd-exhaustion at the global font manifest, not 822's code** (it has no fonts/imports beyond React and reached `Collecting page data` clean); the ceiling is **unraisable even as root** (cgroup-locked: `prlimit`/`ulimit` → "Operation not permitted"). Vercel's higher fd ceiling builds + deploys it, as every prior cycle. Unverified by eye/ear: whether a 4-year-old reads "more sides = more pings, faster spin = faster" without instruction, and whether the polyrhythm reads as mesmerizing vs. busy on a real device (the seeded shapes + always-on pad guarantee a sounding, spinning glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/822-kids-shape-drums/README.md`

## (Cycle 505 — **adult** build · DEEP, one concept × 3 feedback-synthesis approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/820-feedback-ecology](/dream/820-feedback-ecology)** — Feedback Ecology 🌀. `demoable` · Cycle 505 (adult / live-performance / installation). **For**: adults · **touch/gesture input** (tap nodes to perturb; sliders for coupling + self-resonance) · **Canvas2D node-graph / phase-space output** (NOT a plain oscilloscope — the *system* is drawn) · **coupled-resonator dynamical-system / self-oscillating feedback technique** (grep-0× — the lab's FIRST feedback instrument) · **energetic, emergent, organic-but-electric vibe (deliberately NOT ambient)**. **What if an instrument were a *living dynamical system* — a network of resonators that feed each other, self-organize into emergent drones and rhythmic instabilities, and that you play by *perturbing the ecology* rather than triggering notes?** Eight high-Q `BiquadFilter` resonators each sit inside a tight feedback loop (`filter → delay → feedbackGain → filter`), wired into a **small-world coupling graph** (ring backbone weight 0.7 + cross-link shortcuts 0.4). Above a coupling threshold the network **bifurcates**: low coupling = isolated pings; mid = nodes entrain and beat into polyrhythmic textures; high = a roaring emergent drone with energy visibly circulating the ring. It is genuinely **non-stationary — minute 2 differs from minute 0** because the coupled resonators exchange energy asymmetrically and drift across operating points over time (the long-form-stateful register the lab is thin on, and the exact thing JURY 2026-06-21 flagged missing from pruning-candidate `805`: "no state that makes minute 5 differ from minute 1"). **Interaction:** Awaken → tap a node to inject an excitation impulse (watch energy propagate), tap empty space to seed all nodes, **coupling slider** = the bifurcation control, **self-resonance slider** = the edge-of-chaos control, three tuning presets (overtone JI on A1 55 Hz / subharmonic utonal / tight cluster). **Visualizer** makes the dynamical system legible: node size+glow = live RMS energy, **edge flow-particles** = coupling × signal transfer (energy moving around the ring), a **phase-space Lissajous trace inside each node** = its limit cycle/attractor, a bottom energy strip = per-node activity over time. **Ear-safety** (this is a self-oscillating instrument — it *will* try to get loud): all eight paths converge into one brick-wall `DynamicsCompressorNode` (−8 dB, ratio 20, 1 ms attack) → master gain (default 0.25); per-node self-feedback hard-clamped at 0.88 (above this it diverges to digital noise, not music), cross-coupling clamped at 0.35/edge; **no auto-start** (AudioContext resumes only on the "Awaken the Ecology" tap, iOS unlock), a 2 s gentle ramp-up on start, an always-visible **Panic/Mute** that ramps to silence in 10 ms. **Degrades:** three.js/WebGL not needed (Canvas2D); no Web Audio → readable notice. **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no samples, no recordings, no external API — the sound is *only* feedback); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **3/5** (#1 **lab-first self-oscillating feedback instrument AND first coupled-dynamical-system piece** — grep-0× for no-input/Larsen/feedback-as-sound-source · #3 named refs [**"Musicking with dynamical systems"** ACM dl.acm.org/10.1145/3678299.3678302 · **David Tudor** *Rainforest*/*Pulsers* feedback ecosystems · **Toshimaru Nakamura** no-input mixing board · **Body Synths Laboratory**, Superbooth 2026, Berlin May 7–9] · #5 RESEARCH §505 today). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 (rest his recording + end the answering-agent thread + stop defaulting to adult-ambient) → RESEARCH §505 (no-input/self-oscillation revival; Superbooth 2026) → this adult **DEEP** fire. Winner of a 3-approach fire; banked **`819-no-input-mixer`** ⭐ RESURRECT-FIRST (faithful no-input mixer, XY-pad, WebGL2 oscilloscope — the more directly *playable* one, on the scarcest renderer) + **`821-larsen-field`** (live acoustic Larsen + virtual fallback, notch/peak carving) as **IDEAS §505**. **Diversity:** `banned=[his-recording-source · machine-listens-to-recording · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default-register]` · `picked=[touch/gesture INPUT · Canvas2D node-graph/phase-space OUTPUT (off the cooled three.js 799/803/805 streak; Canvas2D no longer banned at 3×, and the concept NEEDS a graph — jury "pick the renderer the concept needs") · audio-self-oscillation/coupled-dynamical-system TECH · energetic-emergent VIBE]` — clears every ban. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 38.9s` → Linting and checking validity of types → Collecting page data**; **820 folder grep-clean** of the log — zero warnings/errors, only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**proven infra THIS cycle: moved 820 out, rebuilt pristine `main` → identical `✓ Compiled successfully` then identical EMFILE**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear/eye: whether the network audibly self-organizes/evolves as intended on a real device and whether the energy-flow + phase-space traces read at a glance (the seeded impulses + 2 s ramp guarantee a sounding, evolving glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/820-feedback-ecology/README.md`

## (Cycle 504 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/816-kids-tone-tower](/dream/816-kids-tone-tower)** — Tone Tower 🧱🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 504 (kids, 4+). **For**: a 4-year-old on iPad/mobile · **touch input** (tap/drag to stack) · **animated SVG/DOM output** (glowing blocks, gaussian-glow filters, CSS topple — NOT Canvas2D, NOT three.js) · **vertical interval-stacking → just-intonation chord construction** technique (grep-0×) · **warm cream nursery, bold saturated, calm-playful vibe**. **What if a 4-year-old could build a CHORD by stacking blocks — genuinely SHAPING the harmony, not just triggering pre-approved notes?** A glowing tower sits on a cream play area; the bottom block is a soft ~F3 root, and each block the child stacks sits a musical **interval** above the one below. The size of the vertical gap the child drags **chooses the interval** — normalized to 0..1200 cents and **snapped to the nearest entry in a just-intonation consonance lattice** (`harmony.ts`: unison 1/1 · maj2 9/8 · min3 6/5 · maj3 5/4 · P4 4/3 · P5 3/2 · maj6 5/3 · octave 2/1). Because every entry is a small-whole-number ratio, **any** stack is consonant and never harsh — but the child genuinely controls open-vs-close, simple-vs-rich, bright-vs-dark. **This is the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-21's kids verdict** — which BANNED "pentatonic-never-wrong as the kids harmonic crutch" (6×) and the "UPIC pitch-painter" (3×) and demanded "a kids piece where harmony or rhythm can be *shaped*, not one where every note is pre-approved." Tone Tower is harmony *shaped* by the child's gesture, off both banned tags. **Interaction:** drag-up to drop a block (a dotted reach-line + ghost block preview the placement live); a plain tap drops a friendly warm third; **▶** strums the tower bottom→top; **💥** topples it with a downward gliss + sparkle, then re-seeds — no fail states, no reading, color is the only language (unison gold → octave magenta-rose), voices cap at 8. **Audio** (`audio.ts`): kids-safe chain `gain 0.28 → lowpass 7000 → DynamicsCompressor`, warm triangle+sine voices with ~50ms attacks, an always-on soft ambient pad, a looping arpeggio so a hands-off glance is sounding on load; AudioContext resumed inside the first tap (iOS), full teardown on unmount. **Degrades:** no Web Audio → visible rose-700 notice, visuals stay fully alive. **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **3/5** (#1 **vertical interval-stacking chord-construction** — grep-0×, harmony-shaping is a thin kids register · #2 ≥3 subsystems [touch-stack interaction + JI lattice gap→cents→ratio engine + arpeggiator/strum synth + animated SVG render = 4] · #3 named refs [**Friedrich Froebel's "Gifts"** — the original kindergarten building blocks · **uCue**, Interaction Design and Children 2025/ACM — a children's musical interface with harmony layers of "common and unusual harmonizations" · **Harry Partch tonality-diamond just-intonation lattice**]). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-21 kids verdict (ban pentatonic-crutch + UPIC; "let harmony be shaped") → RESEARCH §504 (uCue children's-harmony-shaping anchor) → this kids **WIDE** fire. Winner of a 3-explorer fire; banked **`817-kids-clap-back-band`** (mic clap-onset → WebGL2; the child's own clapped rhythm becomes the band's groove — Orff echo-clapping; de-selected for rhyming with the recent `773-parade-caller` rhythm-capture) + **`818-kids-pendulum-choir`** (touch-release pendulum-wave polyrhythm on audio-forward SVG — Harvard pendulum wave / OSZILOT / Ligeti *Poème Symphonique*; de-selected for leaning on a D-pentatonic chime palette, the jury-banned crutch) as **IDEAS §504**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 42s` → Linting and checking validity of types ✓ → Collecting page data**; **816 folder grep-clean** of the build log — zero warnings/errors; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain; the builder also verified `tsc --strict --noUnusedLocals --noUnusedParameters` clean on its files); **NOT browser/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, fails identically on pristine main, NOT the code**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by eye/ear: whether a 4-year-old reads "drag higher = a wider, brighter chord" without instruction, and whether the JI stacks sound as warm on a real device as intended (the looping arpeggio + seeded triad guarantee a sounding, glowing glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/816-kids-tone-tower/README.md`

## (Cycle 503 — **adult** build · DEEP, 2 memory-engine approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/814-remembering-room](/dream/814-remembering-room)** — Remembering Room 🎹🧠. `demoable` · Cycle 503. **For**: adults / live performance / intimate · **his-recording-as-soloist input** (JURY 2026-06-20 #2 explicitly licensed this — the return-and-extend of 770) · **audio-forward DOM/CSS + small `<canvas>` output** (deliberately off the jury's **Canvas2D 10×/15 monoculture** and off a fullscreen shader — the restraint is the point) · **symbolic motif memory / Adaptive Phrase Bank** technique (grep-0× — the lab's first agent with long-form symbolic memory) · **warm / hearth-lit / meditative-duet vibe**. **What if Karel's real recorded "Welcome Home" piano plays as the SOLOIST, a machine live-music agent accompanies it AND answers in his gaps — but now the agent REMEMBERS: it keeps a growing bank of motifs (its own best answers + salient contours lifted from his playing) and over minutes develops them — transpose-into-key / augment / diminish / fragment / invert / sequence — so minute 5 is a reworking of minute 1, not fresh improvisation?** This is the lab's **first true cycle-2 of `770-answering-room`** (whose agent had **zero** long-form memory — every answer improvised fresh and forgotten), the directest hit on **JURY 2026-06-20 #2** ("the depth ceiling collapsed 4→1; RETURN AND EXTEND 770 to a real 4/5 — let the agent accrete a motif over minutes so minute 5 ≠ minute 1"). **Listener** (`listener.ts`): FFT→12-bin chroma → 24-template major/minor triad fit + tension, energy/spectral-flux, a phrase-gap state machine; **new for cycle-2** — it samples his dominant pitch-class ~9×/s and freezes a **key-independent degree-step contour** at each phrase end so the agent can *lift his gesture*. **Agent + memory engine** (`agent.ts`): a warm retuning pad under his chord + a sparse FM-bell answer that fires **only in his gaps**, PLUS the **Adaptive Phrase Bank** — motifs stored **symbolically** as `{degrees, durs}` (scale-degree steps + relative durations, key-independent so replayable in any later key), entering the bank two ways (the agent banks its own good answers; it lifts his contours); a global **memory pressure** rises over minutes (~150s constant, scaled by a slider) so early gaps get short fresh inventions and later gaps increasingly **recall** a banked motif and apply a classical development (transpose/augment/diminish/fragment/invert/sequence); a weighted pick favours under-used motifs; bank caps at 10. **Visual** (`page.tsx`): a restrained warm DOM/CSS hearth (two facing glows — *him* breathing with his energy, *the answer* lit only while answering — a faint duet thread, the detected chord name shown large) PLUS a small `<canvas>` **memory shelf** — each banked motif a glowing contour **sparkline** (amber = his lifted gestures, rose = the agent's own answers) that **flares on recall** with reuse-count dots beneath, so the long-form accretion is *legible*. Two sliders: **company** (shy↔talkative) + **memory** (invent↔recall). **Graceful degradation:** no recording fetch → an offline `OfflineAudioContext` ~16s warm lyrical fallback plays with an honest emerald "synthesized fallback" badge — the room is never silent and runs fully offline. **No API route → no `guard`** (read-only GET to the existing `/api/audio/[id]`, nothing recorded/sent); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **4/5** (#1 **first long-form symbolic motif-memory agent** — grep-0×, lab-thin long-form-stateful register · #4 **multi-cycle** — explicit cycle-2 of 770 with a cycle-3 plan in the README · #3 named refs [**CHI 2026 "A Design Space for Live Music Agents"** arXiv 2602.05064 — its "Adaptation" axis names sustained motif development as an *uncoded gap*; its **Adaptive Phrase Bank** is the seed · **MusicWeaver** arXiv 2509.21714 — **Motif Memory Retrieval (MMR)** · **Yin-Yang** arXiv 2501.17759 — corruption-refinement motif variation · **Christopher Raphael, *Music Plus One*** · 770 lineage] · #5 RESEARCH §503 today). The directest answer to the jury's #1 headline (the depth ceiling that collapsed 4→1) — the window's first new ceiling build. Winner of an adult **DEEP** 2-approach fire; banked **`815-accreting-room`** (the textural-memory sibling — every agent phrase leaves a sustained ghost-layer residue that thickens the room into an Eno tapestry over minutes; de-selected for sitting close to last cycle's 808 sustain-accretion) as **IDEAS §503**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 2.1min` → Linting and checking validity of types ✓ → Collecting page data → all code gates passed**; **814 folder grep-clean** of the log — zero warnings/errors; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain; the 814 builder independently got a **full exit-0 build, 541/541 static pages**); **NOT prerender/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, fails identically on pristine main, NOT the code**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear: whether the agent's recalls audibly read as "it's quoting and varying its own earlier phrase" vs. generic answering, and whether the memory shelf reads at a glance (the always-on pad + synthesized fallback guarantee a sounding glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/814-remembering-room/README.md`

## Cycle 502 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1

- **[/dream/811-kids-body-ribbons](/dream/811-kids-body-ribbons)** — Body Ribbons 🎀🕺. `demoable` · Cycle 502. **For**: kids (4+) / whole-body play · **body-tracking input** (MediaPipe Pose — the JURY's most-starved INPUT register, body/camera went **4×→0×**; off the banned touch) · **SVG/DOM output** (the jury's starved 1× surface — deliberately off **both** the banned Canvas2D **and** the **three.js 3×-in-a-row monoculture** 799→803→805) · **full-body UPIC gesture→pitch-score** technique (rests his recording) · **calm-playful warm-glow vibe** (not bright-active). **What if a 4-year-old's whole BODY is a continuous musical score — they wave their arms and dance, and their moving hands draw glowing ribbons across the screen that sing as they move, the way Xenakis's UPIC turned a child's drawing into music?** **MediaPipe Pose** (33 landmarks, lite model, runtime CDN import via `webpackIgnore` — never bundled) tracks the body; the two **wrists** are the instruments. **Wrist height → pitch** (C-major-pentatonic, 2 octaves, snapped so there are **no wrong notes**); **speed of movement → loudness + ribbon brightness** (hold still and the note softly sustains, then fades); **horizontal position → timbre** (vibrato depth); **nose → a soft shimmer voice**; **both arms raised high → a gentle gold sparkle chord**. Each wrist continuously emits a **glowing SVG ribbon trail** in its own warm color (left = amber/gold, right = rose/violet) — Catmull-Rom-smoothed cubic paths, a 2-pass gaussian-glow + bright-core render, age-faded over ~7s so the screen accretes and breathes (never a hard loop). The lab's **first full-body UPIC instrument**; the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-20 #4** (body input 4×→0×, the single most-starved register) and **#5** (the jury named "the full-body version of 781's UPIC idea is sitting unbuilt in your own research log"). **Kid-safe audio:** `master ≤0.28 → lowpass 7000 → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; always-on C2+G2 sine drone pad; triangle voices with vibrato LFO, 80ms portamento, soft attacks / 1–3s releases; pentatonic so nothing is "wrong". **Privacy + safety:** camera frames are analysis-only — **never recorded, uploaded, or sent**; iOS gesture-gated `AudioContext` + `getUserMedia({video})` both inside the first Start tap (≥72px). **Graceful degradation:** MediaPipe loads in the background while a **ghost-body auto-demo** (`makeGhostBody`) dances + sounds hands-free within ~2s; **camera-denied → the ghost keeps playing** + a `text-rose-300` notice — a sounding, drawing screen with no permissions. Full teardown (rAF + **stop camera tracks** + `landmarker.close()` + node disconnect + `audioCtx.close()`). **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe Pose tracking + UPIC y→pentatonic mapping + dual-wrist polyphonic synth w/ vibrato/drone/shimmer + speed→loudness + multi-pass SVG glow-ribbon render] + #3 named refs [**Iannis Xenakis — UPIC** 1977 · **¡Otro!** IAIA Digital Dome premiere Apr-2026 (Tavarez/Trujillo, real-time movement→sonification) · **Frid et al.** *Interactive Sonification of Spontaneous Movement of Children* Frontiers 2016] + soft-#5 RESEARCH §502 today). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`812-kids-breath-bubbles`** (raw WebGL2 · blow→accreting glow-bubble cloud that pops in pentatonic chimes/chords) + **`813-kids-sky-lullaby`** (SVG/DOM · live Open-Meteo sky → Eno-style moon-aware dusk lullaby, tilt-stirred) as **IDEAS §502**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 101s` → Collecting page data → all code gates passed**; **811 folder grep-clean** after trimming one unused eslint-disable directive; `npx next lint` on the folder → "No ESLint warnings or errors"; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain); **NOT camera/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, NOT the code**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by eye/ear: whether the ribbon visibly tracks a child's arm height and reads as "I raised my arm and it sang higher" (the always-on pad + ghost-body fallback guarantee a sounding, drawing glance regardless). Builder-flagged: per-frame Pose inference may jank on old iPads (lite model + ghost fallback mitigate).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/811-kids-body-ribbons/README.md`

## Cycle 501 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 resonator engines, shipped 1

- **[/dream/808-sympathetic-strings](/dream/808-sympathetic-strings)** — Sympathetic Strings 🎻🎹. `demoable` · Cycle 501. **For**: adults / live performance / intimate · **mic / live-audio input** (off the jury-banned touch) · **SVG/DOM output** (the jury's starved 1× surface — deliberately off the **three.js 3×-in-a-row monoculture** 799→803→805 AND the banned Canvas2D) · **Karplus-Strong tuned-delay-line sympathetic resonator bank** technique (rests his recording; distinct from `803`'s modal-bandpass banks) · **dark / warm / nocturnal vibe** (not bright-active kids). **What if you play or sing into your mic and exactly the right strings ring back — your sound waking an illusory bank of tuned strings that vibrate sympathetically, the way a grand piano's strings hum when you hold the sustain pedal?** 48 tuned strings, each a **real Karplus-Strong tuned delay line** (`L = sampleRate / freq`, one-pole lowpass loop filter, feedback just under 1) running in an **AudioWorklet loaded from a Blob URL** (no `/public` file). The live mic feeds all 48 delay lines; an `AnalyserNode` FFT scales each string's excitation gain by how much spectral energy sits at its pitch — so strings tuned to what you play ring, the rest stay quiet (continuous sympathetic excitation, not discrete strikes). A **mode selector** (homage to the **Electronic Audio Experiments *Prismatic Wall*** sympathetic-string pedal) retunes the bank live across **Chromatic / Stacked Fifths / Overtone Series**. **Sustain-pedal physics:** spacebar or on-screen hold drives feedback **0.92 (fast damp) ↔ 0.997 (near-lossless)**, so with the pedal held the bank **accretes a shimmering bed audibly fuller at minute 3 than second 10** (real delay-line state — the jury's depth-ceiling ask). **SVG visualization:** 48 vertical strings, each string's glow/stroke-width/wobble driven imperatively in `requestAnimationFrame` from the worklet's ~15 fps per-string RMS level reports (`port.postMessage`), `feGaussianBlur` glow, low→high amber→violet→blue hue map. The lab's **first mic-excited sympathetic-string instrument** and **first Karplus-Strong-as-shipped** (the banked `802-body-aviary` KS forest never shipped). **Audio safety:** mic source **never** routed to `destination` (no howl), worklet feedback hard-clamped ≤0.999, `DynamicsCompressor` (20:1, −18 dB) as limiter. **Graceful degradation:** mic-deny → an autonomous **ghost exciter** plucks quasi-chord groups so it rings + sounds within ~2s + `text-rose-300` notice; click/tap any string to pluck it directly; no-WebAudio → friendly rose notice. Full teardown (rAF + worklet + mic tracks + AnalyserNode + intervals + `AudioContext.close()`). **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, mic stays on-device, nothing recorded/sent); **zero new npm deps**. Ambition honest **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [mic FFT analysis + KS tuned-delay-line worklet resonator bank + per-string spectral excitation gating + sustain-pedal feedback accretion + SVG string-field render] + #3 named refs [**EAE *Prismatic Wall*** sympathetic string resonator 2026 · **Karplus-Strong / Jaffe–Smith** CMJ 1983 · sitar *tarab* / viola d'amore · Henry Cowell string-piano] + soft-#1 mic-excited KS sympathetic bank is grep-0× + soft-#5 RESEARCH §501). Piano-native + live-performance-fit (Karel priority #3 — point a mic at his real piano and the strings answer). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-resonator-engine fire; banked **`809-sympathetic-modes`** (FFT-gated high-Q bandpass bank — lost for rhyming with `803`'s modal banks) + **`810-sympathetic-comb`** (native feedback-comb bank — most robust, least KS-faithful; its per-comb AnalyserNode level tap worth folding into 808) as **IDEAS §501**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 102s` → Collecting page data → all code gates passed**; 808 folder fully grep-clean of the log — **zero warnings/errors** after fixing one TS 5.7 `Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>` typing + trimming one unused var/dep; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain); **NOT prerender/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, NOT the code**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear: whether sung/played pitches visibly + audibly wake their matching strings vs. a diffuse wash, and whether the pedal-held bed accretes musically rather than muddying (the always-seeded strings + ghost exciter + compressor guarantee a sounding, glowing glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/808-sympathetic-strings/README.md`

## Cycle 500 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1

- **[/dream/805-kids-snow-piano](/dream/805-kids-snow-piano)** — Snow Piano / Snow-Globe Music Box ❄️🎹. `demoable` · Cycle 500. **For**: kids (4+) · **device-tilt / orientation input** (off the banned touch-primary; love-aligned `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️) · **three.js WebGL output** (off the 10×-banned Canvas2D; the snow globe genuinely needs 3D — jury "pick the renderer the concept needs") · **his-REAL-piano-as-physics-triggered-music-box technique** (off the banned conductor/follower/analyzer-of-his-recording; NOT a grain cloud) · **calm wintery night-blue vibe** (not bright-active, not dark-void). **What if a 4-year-old tilts the tablet like a snow globe — and Karel's REAL recorded "Welcome Home" piano pours out as glowing snow that chimes when it lands?** A 3D glass globe holds ~220 additive glowing motes (`THREE.Points`); **device-orientation tilt** is gravity (gamma→x, beta→downward bias), so the motes slide and fall, and when one settles on one of **5 glowing chime rails** it plays a fresh random **0.25–0.5s Hann-windowed slice of his real piano** (`AudioBufferSourceNode` + `playbackRate` → the rail's C-major-pentatonic tone) before dissolving to sparkles and respawning up top — infinite calm play, no score/fail/timer. This is the lab's **2nd-ever kids piece on Karel's real recording** (only `743` before it) and the direct answer to **JURY 2026-06-20 #3** ("his recording on the kids side is 1× — use it a genuinely-NEW way, or rest it"): a physics-driven **music box**, not the banned conductor/analyzer, not a continuous grain cloud. Born from this cycle's **RESEARCH §500** dive — **LEGO SMART Play (2026)**, the screen-free brick that turns "motion through the air" into sound + light, validating motion→sound+light as the live very-young-child register. Triple love-aligned (`83`❤️ tilt · `227-paths-granular`❤️/`163-paths-visualizer`❤️ his piano · `166-kids-lantern`❤️/`130`❤️/`262`❤️ glow); tied to Karel's published **"Snowflake"** journey. Kid-safe `master gain 0.28 → lowpass 6800 → comp(−10/20:1)`, per-chime peak 0.22 / 20ms soft attack / 12ms rate-limit (a flurry can't stack loud), always-on C2+G2 pad, pentatonic (nothing "wrong"), ≥64px Start, iOS gesture-gated AudioContext + `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` both in the first tap. **Graceful degradation:** `fetchPianoBuffer` null (guaranteed in the no-network sandbox) → synthesized celesta/bell buffer so it always sounds + `text-rose-300` notice; no device-orientation → drag-to-tilt + always-on auto-drift lands/chimes hands-free within ~2s; no WebGL → friendly notice. Full teardown (rAF + listeners + dispose geometries/materials + `renderer.dispose()`/`forceContextLoss()` + `audioCtx.close()`). **No API route → no `guard`** (reads only the public GET `/api/audio/:id`); **zero new npm deps** (`three` already present). Ambition honest **2–3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [device-orientation physics + real-recording windowed slice-player + three.js particle-globe render + chime-rail collision scheduler] + #3 named refs [music-box/snow-globe lineage · Karel's *Snowflake* journey · loved `227`❤️/`163`❤️ his-piano lineage] + soft-#5 RESEARCH §500 LEGO SMART Play 2026). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`806-kids-sky-lullaby`** ⭐ (SVG/DOM · live Open-Meteo sky → Eno-style dusk lullaby, moon-aware, tilt-stirred — **RESURRECT FIRST**, the renderer-diverse pick) + **`807-kids-breath-bubbles`** (raw WebGL2 · blow→accreting glow-bubble cloud that pops in pentatonic chimes) as **IDEAS §500**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` → all code gates passed, reached "Collecting page data"; 805 folder grep-clean — zero `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helpers/api-route; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings); **NOT prerender/device-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, NOT the code: pristine `main` with 805 moved aside fails identically this cycle; Vercel deploys normally**); unverified by ear/eye: whether real-device tilt lands motes at a satisfying chime rate and whether windowed slices of his recording read as warm bell chimes vs ragged fragments (the always-on pad + auto-drift + fallback bell guarantee a sounding, drifting glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/805-kids-snow-piano/README.md`

## Cycle 499 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 synthesis methods, shipped 1

- **[/dream/803-body-chimes](/dream/803-body-chimes)** — Body Chimes / Struck Room 🔔🕺. `demoable` · Cycle 499. **For**: adults / installation · **body/camera input** (MediaPipe Pose — the jury's single most-starved input, went **4×→0×** last window; off the banned touch) · **three.js WebGL output** (warm-metallic volumetric room; off the 10×-banned Canvas2D-primary) · **MODAL physical-modeling synthesis** technique (banks of high-Q bandpass resonators = damped-sine modes with inharmonic partials, excited by 3D spatial collision; rests his recording) · **adult / installation / meditative warm-metallic vibe** (not bright-active kids, not his-recording-conductor). **What if your whole moving body played an invisible, room-sized instrument of STRUCK RESONANT BODIES — bars, bowls and bells suspended in 3D — each limb striking the ones it sweeps through, which ring with real modal synthesis and slowly accrete into a shimmering cloud?** The front camera projects five limb "strikers" (wrists, head, ankles) into a dark volumetric room of ~24 glowing metal bodies on a double-ring shell; sweep a limb through one (3D proximity + speed gate + per-body refractory) and it **rings for real** — each body is a parallel bank of 4–8 high-Q `BiquadFilter` bandpass resonators at **inharmonic partial ratios** characteristic of its type (bell `1, 2.0, 2.4, 3.0, 4.5, 5.33` · bar `1, 2.76, 5.40, 8.93, 13.34` · bowl `1, 2.01, 2.83, 4.22, 5.0`), velocity-scaled, higher modes decaying faster — genuine modal synthesis. Fundamentals are a **just-intonation** field over ~110 Hz so any combination is consonant; strikes bloom through a synthesised convolution reverb. **Long-form accretion (the ceiling feature):** a sympathetic mode-bed (always-on Q≈90 resonators that each strike *charges*, bleeding down over ~25 s) + a `bedMaster` that grows over ~4 min + **struck-body memory** (frequently-struck bodies pull their sympathetic resonator sharper, so the cloud's timbre reflects *how you've been playing*) + a ±0.35% root-drift breath — so **minute 5 is fuller, warmer and subtly detuned vs minute 1** (the directest hit on the jury's depth-ceiling-collapse alarm, 4→1), and the central light/haze brightens with `cloudLevel()` so accretion is visible. The lab's **first body-EXCITES-a-physical-modeling-instrument** piece: prior MediaPipe-Pose pieces (`788-kids-body-glow` UPIC, `724-presence-drift`, `419-body-band` drums, `287`/`302` harmony-mirrors) all map the body to a *note or grain* — none strikes a real resonator network. Graceful: camera-deny/MediaPipe-fail → a **ghost body** on Lissajous orbits plays it hands-free within ~2s + `text-rose-300` notice; WebGL-fail friendly notice; iOS gesture-gated AudioContext+getUserMedia; full three.js/rAF/mic/AudioContext teardown (`renderer.dispose()` + `forceContextLoss()`). **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side); **zero new npm deps** (`three` already present). Ambition honest **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [pose tracking + 3D spatial-collision excitation + modal resonator synthesis + multi-minute accreting mode-bed] + #3 named refs [**Vrengt** NIME/arXiv:2010.03779 body-machine instrument · **modal synthesis / Cadoz-ACROE CORDIS-ANIMA** · **Bernhard Leitner** sound-sculpture] + #5 RESEARCH §499 [CHI-2026 movement↔sound + MediaPipe-gesture framework]). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-synthesis-method fire; banked **`802-body-aviary`** ⭐ (Karplus-Strong plucked-string forest via real AudioWorklet — **RESURRECT FIRST adult**) + **`804-body-veil`** (sympathetic high-Q resonator veil) as **IDEAS §499**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 95s` → Collecting page data**; 803 folder grep-clean of the log [zero `use*` helpers / `any` / api route; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings]); **NOT prerender/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, NOT the code**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear: whether limb sweeps read cleanly as strikes and the modal rings sound bell-like on real hardware (single-camera pose `z` is coarse, so collision leans on x/y sweep; the ghost body + always-blooming reverb guarantee a sounding, glowing glance regardless).
