## Cycle 498 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 growth grammars, shipped 1

- **[/dream/799-kids-sing-garden](/dream/799-kids-sing-garden)** — Singing Garden 🌱🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 498. **For**: kids (4+) · **mic/voice input** (off the banned touch; serves KIDS.md's vocalization goal) · **three.js WebGL output** (off the 10×-banned Canvas2D-primary monoculture; the renderer the concept needs) · **voice→L-system (Lindenmayer) growth + melody-memory call-response** technique (rests his recording entirely) · **calm warm daylight garden vibe** (the jury's reintroduced-calm "middle", not bright-active, not dark-glow). **What if a 4-year-old could SING a 3D garden into being — and the garden then sang their melody back?** The child sings and a branching plant GROWS in real time as a **true L-system grammar** (an `LSystem` class with `iterate()` + 3D turtle-graphics, the grammar generated per phrase from the sung pitch + loudness): **pitch** (octave-collapsed → C-major-pentatonic, never wrong) drives branch angle/reach, **loudness (RMS)** drives thickness + growth speed + glow, and a **held note** keeps a branch extending and blooms small glowing flower-spheres at its tips. Go quiet for ~1s and the garden **sings the contour of what you just sang back** on soft inharmonic bell voices while every plant pulses in warm light (Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening* call-and-response). New plants accrete along a **golden-angle spiral** so the garden is **long-form stateful — fuller at minute 5 than minute 1** (the directest hit on the jury's kids-depth-ceiling-collapse alarm, 4→1). The lab's **first voice→procedural-grammar plant** and **first three.js kids piece**: prior kids voice pieces (`158-kids-hum-paint`❤️ brush, `244-kids-sing-creature` loopback, `100`❤️ splat) are 2D per-stroke/splat/loopback — **none grows a persistent 3D structure from the voice via a generative grammar** (grep-0× / web-0×). It **inverts Mort Garson's *Mother Earth's Plantasia* (1976, "music FOR plants")** — here the child's voice MAKES the plants. Kid-safe `master ≤0.28 → lowpass 7000 → comp(−10/20:1)`, always-on C2+G2 pad (never silent), autocorrelation pitch (Chris Wilson ACF, analyser **never** wired to output — no feedback, never recorded/sent), iOS gesture-gated AudioContext+getUserMedia (AGC/NS/echo off for clean onsets), **ghost-hum hands-free fallback** on mic-deny (auto-grows + answers within ~2s + `text-rose-600` notice), no-WebGL friendly notice, full three.js/rAF/mic/AudioContext teardown (`renderer.forceContextLoss()`), 12-min soft goodnight fade, dark-on-light daylight typography. **No API route → no `guard`** (fully client-side, no network); **zero new npm deps** (`three` already present). Ambition honest **3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [autocorrelation pitch/RMS analysis + L-system grammar/turtle-graphics three.js growth + melody-memory call-response sequencer] + #3 named refs [**L-systems — Lindenmayer / Prusinkiewicz *The Algorithmic Beauty of Plants*** · **Mort Garson *Mother Earth's Plantasia*** · Chris Wilson autocorrelation · Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*] + soft-#1 voice→procedural-grammar plant is grep-0× + soft-#5 §498 negative-result gap). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-growth-grammar fire; banked **`800-kids-sing-meadow`** ⭐ (phyllotaxis golden-angle bloom-spiral — clearest one-flower-per-note cause-effect; **RESURRECT FIRST**) + **`801-kids-sing-grove`** (InstancedMesh reed-field with continuous wind-sway — the "never static" motion idea) as **IDEAS §498**. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 103s` → Collecting page data → all code gates passed**; 799 folder grep-clean of the log [self-reported ESLint 0/0]; `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 project-wide; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings); **NOT prerender/ear-verified** — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json` blocks static-gen (**infra, NOT the code: pristine `main` with 799 moved aside fails identically this cycle**; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear: whether the L-system growth visibly tracks a child's sung pitch and the bell reply reads as "the garden sang MY melody" (the always-on pad + ghost-hum fallback guarantee a sounding, growing glance regardless).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/799-kids-sing-garden/README.md`

## Cycle 497 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers, shipped 1

- **[/dream/797-tonnetz-walk](/dream/797-tonnetz-walk)** — Tonnetz Walk 🎼🧭. `demoable` · Cycle 497. **For**: adults / listening · **autonomous input** (light click-to-steer; off the banned touch) · **SVG/DOM output** (the jury's starved 1× surface — the literal #1 "spread, don't flip onto a new monoculture", off the 10×-banned Canvas2D) · **Neo-Riemannian P/L/R harmony-walk** technique (rests his recording — breaks the answering-room thread) · **clinical-elegant / meditative vibe**. **What if you could WATCH harmony take a walk — a chord gliding forever through the Tonnetz lattice by smooth voice-leading, never the same path twice?** A single triad walks the **Neo-Riemannian** lattice on its own, choosing **P** (parallel) / **L** (leading-tone) / **R** (relative) transforms over time; each holds two common tones and steps the one remaining voice, so a warm 3-voice pad (portamento on the moving voice) + sub-bass + feedback-delay reverb **glides** from chord to chord rather than jumping. The luminous **path through the lattice IS the form** — a weighted, anti-backtracking, home-biased stochastic chooser means **minute 5 never resembles minute 1** (the directest standing answer to the jury's depth-ceiling-collapse alarm). Visual is **pure inline SVG**: 24 triad-triangles (major up, minor down) on the fifth/major-third axes, current node glowing, a fading trail of recent triads, the active P/L/R edge color-lit, lit pitch-class dots, mono chord labels + a live readout (chord · last transform · steps · mm:ss). Controls: **wander↔home** bias, **step** tempo, **arpeggio** toggle, and **click any triad** to steer there (BFS over the 24-triad graph) then resume wandering. Pure-synthesis — no network, no sensors, no assets. Distinct from `37-ratio-lab` (a *static* just-intonation Tonnetz you click to hear): this is an **autonomous generative walker**, motion-through-the-lattice not click-to-sound. Refs: **Euler's Tonnetz (1739)** · **Neo-Riemannian theory — Riemann / David Lewin / Richard Cohn *Audacious Euphony* 2012**. ⚠️ Compile + lint + type-check verified clean (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` `✓ Compiled` → Linting + types PASS; 797 folder grep-clean of the log); NOT prerender/ear-verified — the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` blocks static-gen (pristine main fails identically; Vercel deploys normally). 2 more explored this fire (`798-sympathetic-strings` ⭐, `796-aeolian-sky`) — banked to IDEAS §497.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/797-tonnetz-walk/README.md`

## Cycle 496 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 divergent explorers, shipped 1

- **[/dream/795-kids-sound-hunt](/dream/795-kids-sound-hunt)** — Sound Hunt 🔦🐾. `demoable` · Cycle 496. **For**: kids (4+) · **device-orientation / compass input** (no touch, no mic, no camera) · **audio-forward / eyes-closed HRTF spatial output** (a minimal calm SVG compass — the *sound* carries it; NOT a Canvas2D-primary spectacle, off the 10×-banned monoculture) · **spatial 360° sound-hunt** technique · **calm bedtime "lantern" vibe** (the reintroduced calm). **What if a 4-year-old closed their eyes, held the tablet like a lantern, and turned their body to HUNT for gentle animal sounds hidden floating around them in 3D space?** Eight gentle voices — each a **C-major-pentatonic chord tone** so any subset is consonant — sit at fixed azimuths around a **360° ring** placed via Web Audio **`PannerNode` (HRTF)**; the child's **device-orientation/compass heading** rotates the `AudioListener` so turning their body sweeps a "listening beam." Angular proximity of the beam to a voice drives its gain via a **cosine lobe** (it swells as you near it), and crossing an inner threshold **latches a soft once-only chime + a dot-glow**; collecting all eight blooms a calm rising chord, then the piece **loops forever** — no score, no fail, no timer. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-20 #1** (Canvas2D is **10 of 15** — rotate onto the scarcest surface; **audio-only is the literal "spread, don't flip"**) + the verdict's structural alarm that **the kids side fled calm entirely** (only `743`/`747` left) — a deliberately calm, eyes-closed bedtime register. The lab's **first kids spatialized-listening piece**: the kids set is almost entirely **screen-bound** (instrument on-glass or in front of the camera) and the rare audio-first kids pieces (`346`/`417`) are entrainment toys; **none places sound *around* you to find by moving** (spatial sound-hunt grep-0×). Off **every** JURY ban at once: **device-orientation INPUT** (not touch) · **audio-only / HRTF OUTPUT** (not Canvas2D-primary; the scarcest surface) · **spatial sound-hunt TECH** (rests his recording — not a conductor/follower/analyzer) · **calm bedtime VIBE** (off bright-active — the reintroduced calm). **Ambition honest 2–3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (HRTF spatial-audio graph w/ per-voice proximity-swell + latched once-only reveal · device-orientation/compass input w/ functional auto-tour + drag fallback · synchronized SVG compass/ring + chord-completion logic + always-on drone bed) · **#3** named refs (**PlugSonic** — web/mobile binaural sonic narratives, Geronazzo et al. · **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*** · Web Audio **`PannerNode` HRTF**) · **#5** (RESEARCH §496 today — web-binaural / spatial-audio-2026). **#1 not claimed** (PannerNode/HRTF exists in the lab's adult spatial pieces; the *kids* spatial-listening-hunt mapping is the fresh move). **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master 0.28 → lowpass 7000 → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; always-on gentle sub-drone bed so it never feels broken; soft attacks/long decays; pentatonic so nothing is ever "wrong." **Graceful degradation — genuinely functional, not a notice:** no motion sensor (e.g. desktop/laptop) → a slow **auto-tour** sweeps the beam and reveals every voice hands-free within ~2s, **plus** a **drag-to-turn** control on the ring, **plus** a `text-rose-300` notice — fully playable + audible with no sensor; `PannerNode`/`AudioListener` position+orientation setters **feature-detected** for both modern (`positionX.value`) and legacy (`setPosition`/`setOrientation`) APIs; iOS `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` + `AudioContext` both created inside the Start tap; full teardown (rAF cancel + node stop/disconnect + `audioCtx.close()` + listener removal). **No API route → no guard** (fully client-side, no network); **no new npm dep** (pure Web Audio + SVG; package.json/lock untouched); headphones recommended for the 3D effect. ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached "Collecting page data" → all code gates passed; **zero `795`/`sound-hunt` mentions in the log** — folder grep-clean; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain); **NOT prerender/device/ear-verified** — container's ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`, the standing environmental failure (pristine `origin/main` fails identically; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear: iOS compass-heading axis feel + HRTF placement on a real iPad (the auto-tour + drag fallback + always-on drone guarantee a sounding, sweeping glance). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`794-kids-sing-garden`** ⭐ (three.js · sing→grow a 3D **L-system** garden w/ melody **call-and-response**, long-form stateful — the depth pick; **RESURRECT FIRST (kids)**) + **`793-kids-shadow-puppet`** (pure SVG · body→a singing cut-paper *wayang kulit* moth — overlaps `788`; resurrect with a swappable bestiary + formant voice) as **IDEAS §496**. ## (Cycle 495 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 memory mechanisms, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/792-answering-swarm](/dream/792-answering-swarm)** — Answering Swarm 🐝🎹. `demoable` · Cycle 495. **For**: adults / Karel-the-pianist / **his real recording as input** (no mic, no camera, no touch) · **audio-forward output** (a small warm canvas dot-field readout — the *sound* carries it; NOT a Canvas2D-primary spectacle, off the 10×-banned monoculture, the register the jury itself tagged `770` in) · **stigmergic swarm-intelligence motif-memory** technique (lab-first) · warm-hearth adult-meditative vibe. **Cycle-2 of `770-answering-room`. What if the duet partner were a SWARM of his own motifs that self-organizes — so minute 5 has settled themes minute 1 didn't?** His "Welcome Home" piano plays **whole** as the soloist (the verbatim 770 loader — never granulated). The listener folds the FFT into a 12-bin chroma to fit the **triad under his hands** + spectral-flux onsets + a phrase-gap machine, and on each gap **harvests** the run of pitch-events he just played (his PAST only — no lookahead, per LiveBand's causal constraint) as a **motif fragment**. Each fragment becomes a tiny agent in a **decentralized stigmergic swarm** (cap 12): every frame each agent measures how well its pitch-classes sit on the current chord/scale (`motifFit`) and **deposits pheromone** scaled by his energy if it fits, while a global **evaporation** fades the rest; a fresh fragment replaces the **weakest** agent when full. There is no central composer — over minutes the colony **consolidates** its pheromone onto a few emergent **"bridging motifs"** (a `consolidation` 0–100% readout makes minute-5≠minute-1 *audible-in-numbers*), and 770's soft **FM bell** answers in his gaps by sounding the **strongest** agent's motif, snapped onto the current key and resolved on a chord tone; the warm **pad** still voices the chord under him. `company` slider (shy↔talkative) + a **trading-fours** toggle (a longer doubled turn from the consolidated theme during quiet stretches). The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-20 #2** (the depth ceiling collapsed **4→1** — "RETURN AND EXTEND a gem to a real 4/5; `770` is the obvious target — let the agent ACCRETE a motif over minutes") + **#1** (off the 10× Canvas2D-primary monoculture — audio-forward, "spread don't flip") + **#3** (his recording is still the whole soloist but used a *genuinely-new way* — a self-organizing motif memory, NOT a conductor/follower/analyzer). **Ambition honest 4/5** (the restored ceiling): **#1** first swarm-intelligence / stigmergy audio piece in the lab (grep-checked — no prior swarm/pheromone prototype) · **#3** named refs (**Markus J. Buehler, *MusicSwarm*, Advanced Intelligent Systems Apr-2026, arXiv 2509.11973**; **George Lewis *Voyager***) · **#4** explicit cycle-2 of `770` (multi-cycle thread; next-cycle deepenings banked) · **#5** RESEARCH §495 today (**LiveBand**, arXiv 2606.03803, **June 2026, <30d** — causal past-only accompaniment; + MusicSwarm Apr-2026). **Bulletproof glance:** his recording via the public `/api/audio/[id]` GET, else a ~16s `OfflineAudioContext` synthesized warm phrase (honest emerald fallback badge) so the room **always sounds**; no API route → **no guard** (only READS the existing public audio route — rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep** (pure Web Audio + Canvas; package.json/lock untouched); no WebGL needed; AudioContext resumed inside the Begin tap; full teardown (rAF / source.stop+disconnect / agent.dispose / analyser.disconnect / ctx.close). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached "Collecting page data" → all code gates passed; **zero `792`/`answering-swarm` mentions in the log** — folder grep-clean; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings remain); **NOT prerender/device/ear-verified** — container's ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`, the standing environmental failure (pristine `origin/main` fails identically; Vercel deploys normally); unverified by ear: whether the swarm consolidates onto themes that read as coherent and whether the answers land against his pedal-heavy passages (the always-sound synth fallback + consolidation readout guarantee a sounding, self-organizing glance). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-mechanism fire; banked **`790-answering-memory`** ⭐ (symbolic motif bank + six classical-development transforms + SVG motif-shelf — the most *musically legible* quote-and-develop; **RESURRECT FIRST (adult)**) + **`791-answering-markov`** (variable-order Markov style model + live SVG transition-ring — lower priority, overlaps `778-markov-mirror`) as **IDEAS §495**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/792-answering-swarm/README.md`

## (Cycle 494 — **kids** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 renderers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/788-kids-body-glow](/dream/788-kids-body-glow)** — Body Glow 🕺✨. `demoable` · Cycle 494. **For**: kids (4+) · **body/camera (MediaPipe Pose) input** (no touch, no mic) · **raw WebGL2 GLSL feedback field** (Canvas2D only as fallback — off the jury-banned Canvas2D-primary) · the whole body read as a **continuous UPIC pitch-field** · warm-daylight painterly "middle" vibe. **What if a 4-year-old's whole moving body PAINTED a glowing UPIC score in the air — the higher a limb, the higher the note it sings, and the dance leaves luminous trails that keep singing and fill the sky?** The literal full-body version of **Xenakis's UPIC** (1977: "music becomes a game for children — they draw, they hear"): front camera → MediaPipe Pose (33 landmarks, mirrored); each tracked part (wrists/head/ankles/elbows, 7 voices) drives **ONE continuous sung voice** (triangle + vibrato + per-voice lowpass) whose **vertical position = pitch**, glided with `setTargetAtTime` (portamento, never retriggered) across a **C-major-pentatonic** ladder C3–A5 so **nothing is ever wrong**. Output is a hand-written **raw WebGL2 (GLSL ES 3.00) 3-pass ping-pong feedback field**: fade (×0.965 decay → trails persist) → ~6300 additive `gl_PointSize` glow points/frame seeded at the limbs → present over a slow warm **daylight→dusk** gradient + bloom + tonemap — **long-form stateful: minute 5 is visibly fuller than minute 1**, then self-limits. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-20 #4** (body/camera went **4×→0×**, the single most-starved input, off every over-represented tag) + **#5** (the full-body UPIC the verdict named "sitting unbuilt in your own research log") + **#1** (off Canvas2D-primary onto a genuinely-demanded scarce shader — "spread, don't flip"). **NOT 419-kids-body-band** (body→drums) nor the `287`/`302`/`234` harmony-mirror — a fresh *continuous-sung* mapping of the tracker. Off every JURY ban: **body/camera INPUT** (not touch) · **raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT** (not Canvas2D-primary; scarce shader the concept demands) · **continuous-body-UPIC TECH** (rests his recording — jury #3's clean option) · **warm-daylight VIBE** (off BOTH bright-active AND dark-glow — the literal "land one in the middle"). **Ambition honest 3/5**: **#2** ≥5 subsystems (camera+Pose · 7-voice continuous portamento choir · raw-WebGL2 3-pass feedback field · always-on pad · Canvas2D fallback) · **#3** named refs (Xenakis **UPIC** 1977 · MediaPipe Pose · **CHI 2026 "Designing Interactive Movement Sonification for Hip-Hop Dance"** · Refik Anadol) · **#5** (RESEARCH §494 today — CHI 2026 + ¡Otro! IAIA Digital Dome Apr-2026). **#1 honestly NOT claimed** (MediaPipe Pose/body-input exist — `419`/`287`/`302`/`724`; the fresh move is the mapping). **Bulletproof glance:** camera denied OR MediaPipe fails → `makeGhostBody(t)` keeps the body painting + the choir singing **hands-free within ~2s** + a `text-rose-300` notice; no WebGL2 → inline Canvas2D glow field, same mapping; no 2D either → audio-only loop still sings; kid-safe `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7500 → comp(−10/20:1)` + always-on C2+G2 pad; iOS AudioContext + getUserMedia inside the tap; full teardown (rAF / camera tracks / `landmarker.close()` / GL programs+buffers+textures+FBO + `loseContext()` / osc stop / `audioCtx.close()`). Camera **analysis-only** — never recorded/stored/sent; **no API route → no guard** (only network is the MediaPipe CDN + model GET); **zero new npm deps** (raw WebGL2, no three.js; package.json/lock untouched). ⚠️ **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached "Collecting page data" → all code gates passed; lone `788` warning fixed → folder grep-clean of the log); **NOT prerender/device-verified** — container's ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`, the standing environmental failure (pristine `origin/main` fails identically; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT device-verified** (no camera/WebGL2/audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: MediaPipe reading a real toddler's whole body across lighting, the feedback-trail look on a real iPad, whether the continuous choir reads as "my body is singing"; the ghost-body + Canvas2D + audio-only fallbacks guarantee a sounding, moving glance). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-renderer fire; banked **`789-kids-body-ribbons`** ⭐ (SVG/DOM · the scarcest, most iPad-bulletproof renderer — glowing bounded ribbons + cut-paper daylight; **RESURRECT FIRST (kids)**) + **`787-kids-body-aura`** (three.js · 3D choir-of-light + skeleton + accumulating chord-cloud — repalette to daylight off the dark-glow) as **IDEAS §494**. ## (Cycle 491 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/777-song-architecture](/dream/777-song-architecture)** — Song Architecture 🎹🔬. `demoable` · Cycle 491. **For**: adults / Karel-the-pianist / **his real recording as input** (no mic, no camera) / **bright analytical Canvas2D heatmap** (no GPU shader) / a chroma **Self-Similarity Matrix** of his own piece / **the architecture of his music made visible AND audible**. **What is the hidden ARCHITECTURE of Karel's real "Welcome Home" piano — where do its phrases repeat, where does the form turn?** His recording plays **whole** (never granulated) while the browser computes — in real time, from the decoded buffer — a **chroma-feature Self-Similarity Matrix (SSM)**: ~1s frames → Hann-windowed FFT → 12-bin pitch-class chroma → an N×N cosine-similarity grid. Rendered as a luminous violet→magenta→amber→white-hot Canvas2D heatmap, the hot diagonal is "now = now" and the **bright off-diagonal stripes are where a passage RECURS** — it literally draws the song's form. A **playhead crosshair** sweeps the matrix in sync as he plays; **click any bright off-diagonal cell** and the two similar moments play back-to-back (frame *i*'s ~2s then frame *j*'s ~2s, resynthesized via `AudioBufferSourceNode` offsets) so you HEAR why the machine drew them as a repeat. A thin emerald **novelty curve** (Foote's checkerboard-kernel) marks likely section boundaries. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19 #1** ("use his music a NEW way — not grains; let its STRUCTURE be read") AND **#2** ("rotate off GPU-shader fields entirely; build one piece with NO shader and prove the idea carries it — Canvas2D is scarce again at 3×"). Off every JURY ban: **his-recording INPUT** (not mic-hum/camera) · **Canvas2D heatmap OUTPUT** (not the 9×-banned GPU-shader field) · **structural-analysis TECH** (his recording plays whole — NOT a grain cloud) · **bright analytical VIBE** (off the 9× near-dark luminous-glow). **Ambition honest 2–3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (his-recording playback + click-to-resynthesize · chroma feature extraction · N×N SSM compute · Foote novelty curve · Canvas2D heatmap render + interaction = six) · **#3** named ref (**Jonathan Foote, *Visualizing Music and Audio using Self-Similarity*, ACM MM 1999/2000**; novelty score from Foote 2000; recent revival nodded: SSM-Net, *Generating Music with Structure Using Self-Similarity as Attention* arXiv 2024) · arguably **#1** (first SSM-of-his-recording — `545-motion-loom` uses only 1-D autocorrelation for tempo, `370-tonal-map` reads harmony as a comet; none ever drew the full 2-D structure map) · **#5** (RESEARCH §491 today — Foote SSM, 2024–2026 ML revival). **Bulletproof glance:** his recording via the public `/api/audio/[id]` GET, else a ~36s `OfflineAudioContext` synthesized **A·B·A** piano stand-in (honest amber notice) so the SSM still shows real recurrence stripes and the piece **always sounds**; no API route → **no guard** (only READS the existing public audio route — rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep** (pure Web Audio + Canvas; package.json/lock untouched). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT browser/ear-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached "Collecting page data" (so compile + TypeScript + ESLint all **passed**; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings, zero from the 777 folder), then the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`) — the standing environmental failure every fire hits (pristine `origin/main` fails identically; Vercel deploys it normally). **NOT auditioned** (no real `/api/audio` fetch or audio output in sandbox) — unverified by ear: chroma-SSM stripe legibility on his actual pedal-heavy passages and the click-to-hear-the-repeat segment timing; the synth A·B·A fallback + always-sound design guarantee a sounding, stripe-showing glance regardless. Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`778-markov-mirror`** ⭐ (SVG · teach a small keyboard your melodic style → a live variable-order Markov chain improvises back in it on a glowing transition graph; ref Cope *EMI* / Hiller *Illiac Suite* — **RESURRECT FIRST (adult)** when SVG/DOM is cold) + **`779-ink-score`** (SVG · a machine draws an abstract Cardew-*Treatise* graphic score and plays the marks it inks, long-form motif memory so minute 5 ≠ minute 1; ref Cardew/Brown) as **IDEAS §491**. ## (Cycle 489 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 approaches, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/770-answering-room](/dream/770-answering-room)** — Answering Room 🎹🕯️. `demoable` · Cycle 489. **For**: adults / Karel-the-pianist / **his real recording as input** (no mic, no camera) / **audio-forward / "eyes-closed" register** with a minimal warm hearth-lit Canvas duet visual (no GPU shader) / real-time chroma+onset score-following → a generative harmonizing ensemble / **warm, intimate companionship — a duet, not a solitary dark meditation**. **What if Karel's real "Welcome Home" piano kept playing as the SOLOIST, and a machine "live music agent" listened to it and answered in the gaps — like a duet partner who arrived to play along?** His recording plays **whole** (never chopped into grains, never resynthesized — it *is* the human in the room); a second, synthesized voice **listens** in real time (a 12-bin chroma vector folded from the FFT picks the triad under his hands; spectral flux + energy find the silences between his phrases) and **accompanies**: a warm lowpassed pad voices the detected chord *underneath* him, and a soft FM-bell **answers only in his phrase-gaps**, loosely inverting his last gesture and resolving on a chord tone. A "company" slider tunes it from shy to talkative; it mostly **rests** and supports, never fights. The literal answer to **JURY 2026-06-19 #1** ("use his music a way the lab has never used it — let its structure conduct a generative system; remix his phrasing, NOT his grains") AND **#2** ("build one piece with NO GPU shader at all and prove the idea carries the piece, not the renderer" — the lab's thin audio-forward / eyes-closed register). Off every JURY ban: **his-recording INPUT** (not mic/camera) · **audio-forward + minimal-Canvas OUTPUT** (not the banned GPU-shader-field — and the cleanest renderer-diversity pick, dodging the warming SVG-3× / Canvas2D-3× audit entirely) · **score-following + generative ensemble TECH** (his recording plays whole — NOT a grain cloud) · **warm hearth VIBE** (off the 9× near-dark luminous-glow). **Ambition honest 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (his-recording soloist playback + real-time chroma+onset listener/score-follower + two-layer generative agent [chord pad + gap-answering bell] + reactive Canvas visual = 4) · **#3** named refs (**CHI 2026 *A Design Space for Live Music Agents*** · **Christopher Raphael *Music Plus One*** score-following · **George Lewis *Voyager***) · **#5** (RESEARCH §489 today — the 2026 "live music agents" register); arguably **#1** (first real-time score-following of his actual recording — prior lab listen-pieces [`138`, `414`, `496`, `718`] all track *live mic* or are ensembles you conduct; none accompanies HIS recording as a live soloist). **Bulletproof glance:** his recording via the public `/api/audio/[id]` GET, else a ~16s OfflineAudioContext synthesized warm lyrical stand-in (honest emerald badge) so it **always sounds**; the analyser taps the solo path only, so the agent never analyzes its own output; `master 0.9` with the pad held gently under his line; full teardown (rAF cancel + source stop/disconnect + agent dispose + analyser disconnect + AudioContext close). **No API route → no guard** (only READS the existing public audio route; nothing sent/stored — rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep** (pure Web Audio + Canvas; package.json/lock untouched). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT browser/ear-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached "Collecting page data" (so compile + TypeScript + ESLint all **passed**; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` + other-folder warnings, **zero mentions of the 770 folder**, grep-confirmed), then the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`) — the standing environmental failure every fire since 472 (pristine `origin/main`, live on Vercel, fails identically at the identical path); Vercel deploys it normally. **NOT auditioned** (no real `/api/audio` fetch or audio output in sandbox) — unverified by ear: chord-detection accuracy on his actual dense/pedal-heavy passages and the pad-under-solo / answer-frequency balance (the gap thresholds were tuned blind); the synth-fallback path + always-sound design guarantee a sounding glance regardless. Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-approach fire; banked **`771-conducted-companion`** ⭐ (three.js · the same listener, but YOU conduct the agent's response by dragging a baton on a 3D geometry stage — consonance↔tension, sparse↔dense, low↔high; **RESURRECT FIRST (adult)** when a three.js slot is wanted) + **`772-reharmonized`** (Canvas2D · a machine agent **re-harmonizes** his recording in real time — jazz chord-substitution under his melody, labeled on a flowing "score river"; a jazz-responsive alternate arc — resurrect when Canvas2D ages out) as **IDEAS §489**. ## (Cycle 488 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 divergent bright/joyful explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/767-kids-kite-flyer](/dream/767-kids-kite-flyer)** — Kite Flyer 🪁☀️. `demoable` · Cycle 488. **For**: kids (4+) / **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientation`: gamma = L/R, beta = climb/dive) + pointer-drag / ghost-breeze fallback / **three.js real 3D scene** (gradient sky, smiling sun, fluffy clouds, green hills, glossy diamond kite + bow tail on a visible string) / tilt→spring kite-physics → altitude/wind pentatonic sonification + string-tension Aeolian drone / **bright sunny daylight, bold primaries, exuberant**. **What if a 4-year-old could TILT the tablet to fly a glowing kite across a sunny sky — and the flight PLAYED music?** The kite's **altitude is a melody** (snapped to a C-major pentatonic, so every height is a "right" note — higher is higher), **wind gusts are a rhythm** (soft sparkle chimes + particle bursts), and the **taut string hums a soft Aeolian-harp drone** that swells and brightens as the string tightens — the body's whole-tablet motion IS the instrument. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19 #4** (the un-built **bright/joyful/active-middle** kids register — exuberant without silly, bright without the 9× dark-glow), on the **only safe renderer** (three.js, off the banned GPU-shader-field AND dodging the warming SVG-3× / Canvas2D-3× audit watch), off the **mic/camera bans** (tilt input), off the **grain-cloud ban** (synth bells, no his-piano grains). **tilt INPUT** (scarce vs the worn touch default) · **three.js 3D-geometry OUTPUT** (the lab's only non-warming, non-banned renderer this window) · **tilt→kite-physics + altitude/wind pentatonic + string-tension Aeolian-sawtooth-drone TECH** · **bright sunny daylight VIBE**. **Ambition honest 2/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (device-tilt input w/ drag+auto-breeze fallback + three.js 3D spring kite-physics + altitude→pentatonic melody + string-tension→Aeolian sawtooth drone = 4) · **#3** named refs (the **Aeolian harp** [wind plays a taut string — the string drone] · **LocoRoco** [tilt the whole world to play] · kite-flying as embodied/whole-body play); arguably soft **#5** (RESEARCH §488 today — Frid et al. kids-movement-sonification, foundational). **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master ≤0.3 → lowpass ≤7500 → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; always-on warm pentatonic pad so it never feels broken; voice cap ~6 + refractory timing; **functional fallback, not a notice** — iOS `requestPermission()` requested inside the "Fly!" tap, and if tilt is absent/denied you steer by pointer-drag, with a ~2s-idle **ghost breeze** that keeps the kite swooping and singing hands-free (an unattended glance is always alive + sounding); WebGL-unavailable → friendly notice. AudioContext created inside the Start gesture (iOS); full teardown (rAF cancel + listeners removed + AudioContext close + three.js dispose + `forceContextLoss()`). **No API route → no guard** (fully client-side, no network); **no new npm dep** (`three` already in the repo; package.json/lock untouched). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender/device-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` reached "Collecting page data" (so compile + TypeScript + ESLint all **passed** — Next halts there only after the code gates; only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings, **zero issues / zero mentions of the 767 folder**, grep-confirmed), then the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`) — the standing environmental failure every fire since 472 (pristine `origin/main`, live on Vercel, fails identically at the identical path); Vercel deploys it normally. **NOT browser-verified** (no tilt/WebGL/audio in sandbox) — unverified by hand/ear: real-iOS `gamma`/`beta`→flight feel, three.js render on the review device, and audio balance; the pointer-drag + ghost-breeze fallback guarantees a sounding, flying glance regardless. Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`768-kids-balloon-band`** (three.js · press-and-hold to inflate balloons whose pitch glides up with size, released to accrete a poppable consonant pentatonic sky-chord — **now built WITH the real Canvas2D fallback** it was missing in §486; resurrect-ready) + **`769-kids-flower-duet`** ⭐ (Canvas2D · multi-touch: two kids plant a sunny flower meadow together, every flower sings, any two always harmonize — fills the KIDS.md **social-bonding / two-player** gap, the most robust of the three [pure Canvas2D, no sensor/permission/WebGL]; **RESURRECT FIRST** when Canvas2D ages out) as **IDEAS §488**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/767-kids-kite-flyer/README.md`

## (Cycle 487 — **adult** build · DEEP, 1 concept × 3 renderers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/766-sky-orrery](/dream/766-sky-orrery)** — Sky Orrery 🌌🎹. `demoable` · Cycle 487. **For**: adult / **live wall-clock + optional geolocation** (auto-evolving, zero required interaction; no mic, no camera) / **three.js 3D celestial dome** (Canvas2D fallback) / local astronomy → per-body phrase scheduler of his real recording / diurnal cosmic-but-warm, daylight-into-night. **What if you stood under a real 3D dome of the sky and the bodies overhead conducted Karel's piano?** The sun, the moon (correct current phase), and eight genuinely bright bodies (Sirius, Vega, Arcturus, Betelgeuse, Rigel, Altair, Jupiter, Venus) are placed at their **real computed altitude/azimuth** for the local time & place; the dome turns slowly with sidereal time; and **whichever bodies ride highest conduct a long-form, never-repeating arrangement of whole PHRASES of Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano** — Cage's *Atlas Eclipticalis* made literal: **sky position becomes score position.** Each above-horizon body owns a phrase-voice — its **altitude → gain & brightness**, its **azimuth → stereo pan** — so the audible arrangement is literally the sky overhead; scrub noon→midnight to hear it flip (sun-led major air at noon → a low star cluster at night). His recording is played as **whole continuous regions** (`bufferSource.start(when, offset, duration)` + equal-power crossfades), **not** a grain cloud — the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19 #1** ("use his music a way the lab has never used it — let its structure conduct a generative system; remix phrasing, not grains"), and a **return-and-deepen of `347-the-place`** (which scored the sky into *synthesized* sound — `766` is the first to let the real sky conduct his **real recorded piano**), so it also serves JURY **#3** (depth). **live-clock + optional-geolocation INPUT** (off banned mic-hum/camera) · **three.js 3D-geometry dome OUTPUT** (off the banned WebGPU/WebGL2 *shader-field*, and the only renderer in this fire's trio that dodges the warming SVG-3× / Canvas2D-3× audit watch) · **local-astronomy → whole-phrase scheduler TECH** (off banned grain-cloud) · **diurnal daylight-into-night VIBE** (off the 9× dark-glow). **Ambition honest 3/5**: **#1** (first celestial-position conducting of his **real recorded** piano — `347` did sky→*synthesis*, never his recording; grep-confirmed) · **#2** ≥4 subsystems (sun/moon/8-body local ephemeris + per-body phrase-voice scheduler w/ region playback + three.js dome render + Canvas2D fallback) · **#3** named refs (**John Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis*** · **Jem Finer *Longplayer*** · the planetarium/orrery · **John Luther Adams *The Place Where You Go to Listen*** — and the lab's own `347` predecessor) · **#5** (RESEARCH §487 today + §485 yesterday — the celestial / data-sonification register; Trayford "Unseen Astronomy" arXiv 2026). **Bulletproof glance:** Karel's real recording via the public `/api/audio/[id]` GET, else a ~16s OfflineAudioContext synthesized stand-in (honestly labeled) so it **always sounds**; voices primed at start so audio begins promptly; master chain `→ master 0.3 → analyser → lowpass → DynamicsCompressor → destination` with a night-deepening reverb send; **working non-WebGL fallback** — `createCanvas2D` draws the same day/night sky + pulsing bodies (never a notice-only dead state), audio path unchanged; AudioContext created inside the Start gesture (iOS); geolocation denied/slow → San Francisco (37.77, −122.42) + notice; full teardown (rAF cancel + audio close + three.js dispose + `forceContextLoss()`). **No API route → no guard** (only READS the existing public audio route; nothing sent/stored — rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep** (`three` already in the repo). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender/device-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` compiled + type-checked + ESLint-clean (only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings, **zero issues in the 766 folder**), but the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`) — the standing environmental failure every fire since 472 (pristine `origin/main`, live on Vercel, fails identically at the identical path); Vercel deploys it normally. **NOT browser-verified** (no WebGL/audio in sandbox) — unverified by hand/eye: real three.js render on Karel's review device (Canvas2D fallback guarantees a sounding, lit sky regardless), body-position plausibility (moon/star/planet positions are approximations, not an ephemeris — by design), and audio balance. Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire (one concept × 3 renderers); banked **`764-sky-almanac`** ⭐ (SVG sun-arc orrery — the canonical robust zero-interaction glance, sun/moon glyphs on a daylight dial; **RESURRECT FIRST** when SVG/DOM ages out of the window) + **`765-sky-spectra`** (Canvas2D atmospheric Rayleigh/Mie sky-color field — the *color* of the sky conducts density/harmony/reverb; resurrect when Canvas2D ages out or for a painterly/abstract slot) as **IDEAS §487**. ## (Cycle 486 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 divergent bright/joyful explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/761-kids-hill-roller](/dream/761-kids-hill-roller)** — Sunny Hill Roller ☀️⚽. `demoable` · Cycle 486. **For**: kids (4+) / **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientation.gamma`) + finger-drag to sculpt / **inline SVG/DOM, ZERO canvas/WebGL/WebGPU** / sculpt-a-heightfield-melody → ball-physics playback / bright sunny daylight (blue sky, smiling sun, green-gold rolling hills). **What if a 4-year-old could COMPOSE a melody by sculpting sunny hills with a finger, then TILT to roll a ball over their OWN shape — and the ball rolling over THEIR hills plays THEIR tune?** Nine rolling hills sit across a daylight scene; the child **drags any hill up/down** (≥64px `↕` handles) to set its **height = a C-major-pentatonic pitch** (taller = higher, nothing ever "wrong"; releasing previews the note), then **tilts the device** to roll a glossy ball along the surface under gravity (LocoRoco-style: the ball follows the slope they drew), and **each hill crest the ball crosses rings that hill's note** — so the shape the child sculpted IS the melody, looping as the ball re-enters from the left. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19**: **#4** ("the un-built bright/joyful/active-middle kids register — exuberant without silly, bright without dark-meditation" → sunny hills, not the 9× near-dark glow), **#2** ("rotate OFF GPU-shader fields [9/15] — build with no shader and prove the idea carries, not the renderer" → **pure inline SVG/DOM, zero canvas/WebGL/WebGPU**; ## (Cycle 485 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 divergent explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/760-wiki-carillon](/dream/760-wiki-carillon)** — Wiki Carillon 🔔🌍. `demoable` · Cycle 485. **For**: adult / **live external web event-stream** (Wikimedia EventStreams SSE) / **Canvas2D, ZERO GPU shader** / live-API data sonification → generative bell ensemble / civic / atlas / bright daylight (parchment, ink-and-gold). **What does the world sound like as it writes itself?** The live, public firehose of **Wikipedia edits** becomes a bright civic carillon over a pale atlas field: every edit anywhere on Wikimedia rings a struck bell — **edit size → pitch & decay** (big edits ring low and long, tweaks bright and short; pitches snap to a warm major-pentatonic so nothing is wrong), **each wiki keeps its place** (stereo pan + a vertical band hashed from the domain, so `en.wikipedia.org` always lands in the same spot), a **brand-new / anonymous editor → a soft violet swell** under the bell, a **bot edit → a muted grey woodblock**. Edits accrete into an ever-shifting, never-repeating composition — real-world **data sonification**, music *about* the world's collective writing, not about music. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19**: **#2** ("rotate OFF GPU-shader fields — WebGPU+WebGL2 are 9/15; Canvas2D is *scarce* again at 3×; build one piece with no shader and prove the idea carries, not the renderer" → `760` is **pure Canvas2D, zero WebGL/WebGPU/shader**), and the boldest **anti-monoculture** move of the fire — the **only** candidate that escapes the his-piano cluster the jury called "used exactly one way" (7×) **entirely**, on a **lab-first input** (live SSE / external event-stream, grep-0×), in a categorically new register (civic/atlas — Karel-cares #2 *surprise*). **live web event-stream INPUT** (off banned mic-hum/camera/touch) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (the jury-named scarce no-shader surface) · **live-API SSE data-sonification TECH** (off banned grain-cloud — synth FM bells, no his-piano grains) · **bright civic/atlas daylight VIBE** (off banned dark-glow). **Ambition honest 3/5**: **#1** (first live external SSE / event-stream input AND first live web-API data sonification in the lab — weather `502` / seismic `712` / solar-wind `314` exist but never a live edit-stream; grep-0×) · **#2** ≥3 subsystems (Wikimedia SSE feed w/ transparent synthetic fallback + FM-bell/woodblock/swell synth ensemble + Canvas2D atlas-bloom render + musical throttle/voice-cap) · **#3** named refs (**Hatnote *Listen to Wikipedia*** — Stephen LaPorte & Mahmoud Hashemi · **Brian Eno** generative ambient · **KLING KLANG KLONG *Sounds of the Unseen***) · **#5** (RESEARCH §485 today — the live data-sonification / never-repeating-stream register). **Bulletproof glance:** `master ≤0.3 → lowpass ≤8kHz → DynamicsCompressor(−12/12:1) → destination`; always-on open-fifth drone bed so silence between edits has a floor; **14-voice cap + ~55ms refractory + ~6 bells/sec throttle (queue cap 8, drop-oldest)** so a busy firehose stays musical not noise; **transparent offline fallback** — if the SSE errors / doesn't connect within ~3s / `EventSource` is unavailable, a Poisson-ish **synthetic edit generator** drives the piece (honest status badge: emerald `live: en.wikipedia + 40 wikis` vs amber `demo stream (offline)`), so it **always rings + blooms**; AudioContext created+resumed inside the Start gesture (iOS); full teardown (EventSource close + rAF + audio nodes + listeners + timers). **No API route → no guard** (SSE read directly client-side; nothing sent or stored — rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep** (Web Audio + Canvas2D + native `EventSource` only). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender/device-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` `✓ Compiled successfully in 34.3s` + type-check + ESLint clean (only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings, nothing in the `760` folder), but the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`); **proven environmental THIS cycle** — pristine `origin/main` (live on Vercel) `✓ Compiled successfully in 80s` then failed identically at the identical path; Vercel deploys it normally. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio/network/Canvas in sandbox) — unverified by ear/eye: whether `stream.wikimedia.org` connects from Karel's review device (the synthetic fallback guarantees a sounding, blooming glance either way, honestly badged), and whether the single FM-bell timbre family reads as a true carillon. Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`758-sky-almanac`** ⭐ (SVG sun-arc orrery · the local clock + computed sun/moon position conduct a long-form, non-looping arrangement of whole PHRASES of Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano — daylight palette, zero-interaction autoplay, the best unattended glance + highest ambition floor of the three; refs Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* / Finer *Longplayer*; **RESURRECT FIRST**) + **`759-phrase-loom`** (SVG · drag a luminous phrase-timeline to re-weave whole phrases of his recording via a Markov transition model — the literal "remix his phrasing, not his grains" answer; refs Eno / Burroughs-Bowie cut-up) as **IDEAS §485**. ## (Cycle 484 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 divergent bright/joyful explorers, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/755-kids-bounce-house](/dream/755-kids-bounce-house)** — Bounce House 🤸. `demoable` · Cycle 484. **For**: kids (4+) / **touch** (tap-sky-to-drop, drag-to-pluck) / **Canvas2D, ZERO GPU shader** / real **Verlet mass-spring cloth membrane** + tuned membrane-drum synthesis / bright sunny bounce-house daylight, joyful/active. **What if a 4-year-old could bounce bright balls on a giant stretchy TRAMPOLINE that SINGS — the sheet's wobble and each landing turning into warm tuned drum-and-bell notes?** A sunny sky scene: a big candy-purple trampoline stretched across the lower screen, drawn as a **real deforming 24×10 Verlet mass-spring cloth** (structural + shear springs, Jakobsen constraint relaxation, pinned rim). The child **taps the sky** to drop a fat glossy primary-colored ball — it falls, deforms the sheet for real, and **bounces**; each landing plays a tuned membrane-drum hit (center = low & round, edges = brighter), loudness from impact velocity, pitch quantized to C-major-pentatonic so nothing's ever "wrong." **Dragging the sheet** plucks a wobble that travels outward. Multiple balls pile up → a gentle emergent polyrhythm of warm hits; the cloth's averaged displacement modulates an always-on open-fifth pad. The deforming soft-body's motion *is* the score, not a faked wobble. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19**: **#2** ("rotate OFF GPU-shader fields — WebGPU+WebGL2 are 9/15; Canvas2D is *scarce* again at 3×; build one piece with no shader and prove the idea carries, not the renderer" → 755 is **pure Canvas2D, zero WebGL/WebGPU/shader**), **#4** ("the un-built bright/joyful/active-middle kids register — exuberant without silly, bright without dark-meditation" → sunny bounce-house, not the 9× near-dark glow), and off **#1/#3/mic-cam bans** (no grain-cloud, no GPU shader, touch not mic/camera). **touch INPUT** (off banned mic-hum/camera; touch is scarce again at ~2×) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (the jury-named scarce no-shader surface) · **Verlet cloth membrane + membrane-percussion-synthesis TECH** (the lab's FIRST structured mass-spring cloth — blobs exist at `286`/`451` but never a structured sheet; and the FIRST *sonified membrane*; deliberately OFF the saturated drop→collision→note register of `184`/`451`/`553`/`619`/`350`) · **bright joyful daylight VIBE** (off banned dark-glow). **Ambition honest 3–4/5**: **#1** (first structured Verlet cloth membrane + first membrane sonification in the lab — grep-verified trampoline/bounce-membrane 0×) · **#2** ≥3 subsystems (multi-touch input + Verlet mass-spring cloth solver w/ ball↔cloth collision + tuned membrane-drum/bell synth + Canvas2D render) · **#3** named refs (**Thomas Jakobsen** "Advanced Character Physics" GDC 2001 · **Xavier Provot** 1995 mass-spring cloth · **JellyCar Worlds** / **Toolkit for Verlet Motion 2026** soft-body lineage · membrane percussion synthesis) · **#5** (RESEARCH §484 today — the live soft-body/Verlet-physics 2026 creative-coding wave). **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7500 → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; always-on open-fifth pad that brightens with sheet activity; **3s-idle ghost auto-demo** drops a ball every ~1.5s + soft ping so a glancing reviewer sees+hears it bouncing and singing hands-free; canvas visuals run before audio (gesture-gated AudioContext, iOS); try/catch around audio + the step loop (never throws into rAF); full teardown (rAF + audio nodes + resize listener). **No API route → no guard** (fully client-side, no network); **no new npm dep** (Web Audio + Canvas2D only). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender/device-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` `✓ Compiled` + type-check + ESLint clean (only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings, nothing in the 755 folder), but the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`); the ghost auto-demo guarantees a sounding, bouncing glance regardless. Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`756-kids-hill-roller`** ⭐ (SVG/DOM · tilt: sculpt sunny hills then tilt to roll a ball over them = play the drawn melody — love-aligned `83`❤️, RESURRECT FIRST) + **`757-kids-balloon-band`** (three.js: hold-to-inflate balloons whose pitch rises with size, released to accrete a poppable sky-chord) as **IDEAS §484**. ## (Cycle 482 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 speech→music mappings, shipped 1)

- **[/dream/752-kids-word-band](/dream/752-kids-word-band)** — Word Band 🌈🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 482. **For**: kids (4+) / **live speech recognition** (Web Speech API) / **SVG/DOM, ZERO GPU shader** / phonetic word→pentatonic-xylophone riff + look-ahead groove / bright rainbow daylight, joyful. **What if a 4-year-old could SAY a word and HEAR its sounds turn into music — say "banana" and the rainbow xylophone bounces out ba-na-na as a happy climbing riff?** The child taps START, then speaks; the **Web Speech API** recognizes each word, maps its letters to an 8-bar **C-major-pentatonic rainbow xylophone** (vowels spread across the scale, consonants fill the gaps — a stable, deterministic riff, nothing ever "wrong"), plays it immediately, and tumbles the spoken letters in as big bouncing colored type. Each word lays a short looping ostinato that joins a gentle **Chris-Wilson look-ahead groove** (25ms/120ms, ≤4 simultaneous word-loops, oldest drops) so you build a little band. The lab's **first KIDS speech-recognition piece** (real `SpeechRecognition` exists only in adult `570`/`189`; voice-for-kids has always meant pitch/RMS `88`/`158`/`179`, never the *recognized word itself*) and the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19**: **#5** ("build the un-built in-browser-ASR finding — a 4yo says a word and hears it become a sound"), **#4** ("the un-built bright/joyful/active-middle kids register"), **#2** ("the lab is thin on SVG/DOM — build one piece with NO GPU shader and prove the idea carries, not the renderer" → 752 is **pure SVG/DOM, zero canvas/WebGL/WebGPU**), **#1** (off the banned grain-cloud — word→riff). **live-speech INPUT** (off banned mic-hum/camera/touch) · **SVG/DOM OUTPUT** (the jury-named scarce no-shader surface; off banned GPU-shader-field) · **phonetic word→xylophone-riff TECH** (off banned grain-cloud) · **bright rainbow daylight VIBE** (off banned dark-glow). **Ambition honest 2–3/5** (#2 ≥4 subsystems [Web-Speech recognition + word→letter→bar phonetic map + look-ahead groove scheduler + SVG/DOM render] + #5 [RESEARCH §411 in-browser ASR, 6 days old, in-window; reinforced by §482 today — Web Speech spec milestone 2026-05-21 + iOS-Safari 14.5+ support] + qualified-#1 [first kids speech piece; first word→xylophone mapping]). Refs: **Incredibox** (additive band-building) · **Toca Band / Sago Mini** (bigger-bar-lower-note, pentatonic-only safety) · **Web Speech API** · **Chris Wilson** look-ahead scheduler. **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master 0.28 → lowpass 7kHz → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; warm marimba timbre; always-on C2/G2 pad; **graceful fallback** — speech unsupported/denied (Firefox, incognito) → a big 26-letter tap keyboard (each tinted its bar color) + PLAY runs the identical riff + `text-rose-300` "Tap the letters to play!" notice; **3s auto-demo** cycles seeded happy words hands-free (resumes after 5s silence); AudioContext inside the START gesture (iOS); full teardown (recognition + scheduler timer + nodes + AudioContext). **No API route → no guard** (fully client-side, no network); **no new npm dep**. ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender/device-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` `✓ Compiled successfully in 2.2min` + type-check + ESLint clean (only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings), but the container's anomalous **~4096 fd ceiling** killed Next static-generation (`EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`); **proven environmental THIS cycle** — pristine `origin/main` (live on Vercel) `✓ Compiled` then failed identically at the identical path; Vercel deploys it normally. **NOT browser-verified** (no speech/audio in sandbox) — unverified by ear/eye: iOS-Safari `webkitSpeechRecognition` latency (0.5–2s typical, could feel laggy), `onend` auto-restart race, and whether spelling-driven riffs read as *musical* per-word (deterministic + pentatonic so never wrong); the tap-keyboard + auto-demo guarantee a sounding glance with zero mic. Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-mapping fire; banked **`751-kids-say-a-zoo`** ⭐ (semantic: spoken word → bouncing Canvas2D creature that sings, accreting chord-garden — most pure-4yo delight, RESURRECT FIRST) + **`753-kids-spell-parade`** (rhythmic/stateful: word letters → accumulating Canvas2D marching-band groove) as **IDEAS §482**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/752-kids-word-band/README.md`

## (Cycle 480 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 divergent explorers)

- **[/dream/747-kids-inkblot-bloom](/dream/747-kids-inkblot-bloom)** — Inkblot Bloom 🦋🖋️. `demoable` · Cycle 480. **For**: kids (4+) / **mic hum/blow** (analysis-only) / **WebGPU** Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion (Canvas2D-CPU fallback) / kaleidoscopic ink-bloom / contemplative, no-groove. **What if a 4-year-old could hum and watch living, mirror-symmetric inkblots bloom and unfold like a butterfly — and hear each fold sing?** A near-dark calm screen: the child **hums or blows** into the mic; soft ink blooms outward from the centre and unfolds in **kaleidoscopic mirror symmetry** (a living Rorschach butterfly/flower) — the ink is a **Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion** field run as a **WGSL compute shader** on a 256² ping-pong buffer (the lab's first *kaleidoscopic-symmetry* RD), folded with mirror symmetry to a full-screen quad. As the reacting fronts spread and split they ring soft consonant bells (each hum seeds a fresh drop of ink; pitch follows the bloom's radius + the hum's pitch). No beat, no loop, no "wrong" note — C-major-pentatonic snap. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-18 #1** ("give a kid the *scarce* renderer — ban the Canvas2D monoculture": WebGPU is primary, Canvas2D only as fallback) + **#2** ("texture/transformation, NOT a loop-groove, vary the register — not silly"). **mic INPUT** (off banned touch-primary; serves KIDS.md vocalization) · **WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (scarce — off the raw-WebGL2-4×-audit-ban AND banned Canvas2D-primary [fallback only]) · **kaleidoscopic Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion TECH** (NOT a step-sequencer groove) · **contemplative ink-on-light/Rorschach VIBE** (off banned silly). **Ambition honest 2–3/5** (`#2 + #3`, arguably `#1`): **#2** ≥4 subsystems (analysis-only mic [RMS breath + zero-crossing pitch] + WGSL Gray-Scott compute + kaleidoscope-fold render + RD-driven additive-bell sonification) · **#3** named refs (**Bileam Tschepe (elekktronaut)** feedback/inkblot TouchDesigner lineage · **Entagma "Easy Houdini: Inkblots — Steal from TouchDesigner" (May 2026)** · **Gray-Scott** / Pearson 1993 / Turing 1952) · arguably **#1** (first mirror/kaleidoscope-symmetric RD, and first mic-driven RD, in the lab — RD exists at `9` but never symmetric/voice-driven). **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master ≤0.26 → lowpass 7kHz → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; mic **analysis-only** (never wired to destination, never recorded/sent); always-on C2/G2/C3 drone; **ghost-hum auto-demo** keeps the ink blooming + singing hands-free on mic-deny/idle (zero permission); **WebGPU → Canvas2D-CPU fallback runs the identical Gray-Scott model** (96² grid, same kaleidoscope fold) so it blooms+sings end-to-end with no GPU; AudioContext + getUserMedia inside the Start gesture (iOS); full teardown (rAF / mic tracks / WebGPU device+buffers / AudioContext). **No API route → no guard** (no network at all — fully on-device; rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep** (WebGPU via `navigator.gpu`, usage bits hardcoded — no `@webgpu/types`). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender-verified** — authoritative winner-only `npm run build` `✓ Compiled successfully in 39.3s` + type-check + ESLint clean (only pre-existing `src/lib/**` warnings), but the container's anomalous **4096 fd soft limit** killed Next's static-generation worker (`EMFILE` at "Collecting page data"); **proven environmental THIS cycle** — pristine `origin/main` (live on Vercel) `✓ Compiled` then failed identically at the identical path; shipped on green code gates + prerender-safe `"use client"` structure (read the page.tsx: no module-top-level browser-API access) + the live-Vercel control (see STATE.md cycle 480). **NOT browser/device-verified** (no GPU/mic/audio in sandbox) — unverified by ear/eye: whether WGSL compiles first-run on a real iPad (Canvas2D fallback is the safety net), whether the RD regime reads as a *butterfly*, whether breath onsets seed cleanly; the ghost-hum + Canvas2D fallbacks guarantee a sounding, blooming glance regardless. Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`748-kids-aurora-silk`** ⭐ (tilt → three.js mass-spring CLOTH aurora veil — the iOS-bulletproof first-cloth sibling, RESURRECT FIRST) + **`749-kids-shadow-garden`** ⭐ (camera body-silhouette → WebGPU instanced flower-growth — the gentle-giant sibling) as **IDEAS §480**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/747-kids-inkblot-bloom/README.md`

## Cycle 478 — **kids** build · DEEP, ONE concept [cycle-2 of 738] via 3 musical models

- **[/dream/743-kids-song-family](/dream/743-kids-song-family)** — Song Family 🌟🎹. `demoable` · Cycle 478. **For**: kids (3+) / **mic voice/hum** (analysis-only) / **raw WebGL2** GLSL companions (Canvas2D fallback) / accreting **chord-harmony** memory voiced by CataRT grains of **Karel's real Welcome Home piano** / tender companion, no-groove. **What if a 4-year-old's hummed songs hatched a growing FAMILY of glowing companions who remembered the child's melodies and sang them back in stacked HARMONY — and their voices were made from grains of Karel's OWN real recorded piano?** The **cycle-2 of `738-kids-song-sprout`** (the lab's first long-form/stateful kids piece, which was *monophonic*): this builds the harmonic/multi-voice dimension 738 explicitly banked for cycle-2 — the directest hit on **JURY 2026-06-18 #3** ("reward DEPTH, RETURN and extend the ceiling, stop banking and walking away") AND simultaneously **#5** ("use Karel's real music — it's in 1 of the last 15; a kids one could use his piano grains"). Hum a phrase → a glowing friend hatches & remembers it; hum more → a whole family (cap 4) appears, each visibly growing (bigger/brighter/warmer) as it hears more; go quiet → the family sings the remembered melodies back as a **chord choir** (each companion on a different consonant chord tone, the stack deepening unison→octave→5th→3rd+9th with maturity), so a single line blooms into warm 3–4-part harmony and minute-5 differs from minute-1. Nothing's ever wrong — D-Dorian snap. **mic-voice INPUT** (off banned touch-primary) · **raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT** (the scarcest non-banned renderer — off banned Canvas2D-primary [fallback only], off WebGPU-4×-now-audit-banned, off three.js-3×-would-ban; iOS-bulletproof) · **accreting chord-harmony memory + CataRT grains of his real piano TECH** (free/rubato chord-choir, emphatically NOT a step-sequencer loop-groove) · **tender/wonder companion VIBE** (off banned silly). **Ambition honest 4–5/5** (`#1+#2+#3+#4+#5`): **#1** first multi-voice/harmonic memory in the lab (738 + all 700+ priors are monophonic) AND first kids piece voiced by concatenative grains of his real recording in a *harmony* context (721 used his grains as a single breath-bloom) · **#2** ≥5 subsystems (mic autocorrelation pitch/phrase capture + phrase-memory & growth store + harmony scheduler + CataRT grain corpus from his piano w/ offline-synth fallback + raw-WebGL2 GLSL renderer) · **#3** named refs (arXiv `2506.18143` *AI Harmonizer* [four-part harmony from one voice] · Diemo Schwarz *CataRT* · Brian Eno *Reflection*) · **#4** cycle-2 of 738 (cycle-3 = round⇄chord⇄phase toggle [merge 742/744], more voices, seasons) · **#5** RESEARCH §478 same-day. **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; mic **analysis-only** (never to destination); always-on warm drone; **ghost auto-demo** (a virtual child hums, hatching & growing the family hands-free, zero permission) on mic-deny/idle; his-piano loader (`fetch /api/audio/<id>`, 4s abort) → on ANY failure/offline a gentle offline-rendered synth voice so it ALWAYS sounds; AudioContext unlocked in the Start gesture (iOS); Canvas2D glow-dot fallback for no-WebGL2; full teardown (rAF/tracks/audio nodes/GL resources/listeners). **No API route → no guard** (only READS the existing public `/api/audio/<id>` GET; no secret, no DB write, no side effect — rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm dep**. ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender-verified** — authoritative `npm run build` compiled + type-checked + ESLint-passed cleanly, but the container's anomalous **4096 fd soft limit** killed Next's static-generation worker (`EMFILE` at "Collecting page data"); **proven environmental THIS cycle** — pristine `origin/main` (live on Vercel) fails identically at the identical path; shipped on green code gates + prerender-safe `"use client"` structure (grep-verified: no module-top-level browser-API access) + the live-Vercel control (see STATE.md cycle 478). **NOT browser/device-verified** (no GPU/mic/audio in sandbox) — unverified by ear/eye: whether autocorrelation tracks a small child's hum, whether the chord-harmony reads as *a family answering*, whether the real-piano grain voice resynthesizes cleanly (the synth fallback is the likely-reviewer path), growth/cadence tuning, iOS mic unlock; the ghost demo + synth fallback + Canvas2D fallback guarantee a sounding, growing glance regardless. Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-model fire; banked **`742-kids-song-round`** ⭐ (raw WebGL2 developing ROUND/canon, synth — the bulletproof zero-network sibling) + **`744-kids-song-phase`** ⭐ (raw WebGL2 Steve Reich PHASING, phase-rings visible) as **IDEAS §478**. ## ⭐ Earlier (Cycle 477 — **adult** build · DEEP, ONE cross-device concept via 3 network substrates)

- **[/dream/741-piano-room-pulse](/dream/741-piano-room-pulse)** — Piano Room Pulse 🎹🔗. `demoable` · Cycle 477. **For**: adult / **MIDI + computer-keyboard** (on-screen strip fallback) / **three.js** breathing pulse-ring (Canvas2D fallback) / **Supabase Realtime cross-device + NTP shared-clock ensemble-lock** / intimate connected duet. **What if you typed the same 4-letter room code on two phones, played Karel's piano together — and a shared NTP-style clock gently locked BOTH of you onto ONE drifting pulse so a real ensemble LOCK emerges across the room despite ~100ms of network latency?** The directest hit on **JURY 2026-06-18 #4** ("make ONE multi-user piece *actually* cross-device — retire the asterisk") AND the deepest answer to **#3** ("reward DEPTH, build the ceiling, stop banking and walking away"): the lab's one prior cross-device piece (`729-piano-portal-jam`) joined via a clunky manual SDP copy-paste; this joins by **typing a room code** (Supabase Realtime, frictionless) and adds the capability 729 never had — a **shared clock + ensemble lock**, the actual hard problem of networked music. Only tiny note-events travel (`{note,vel,who,beat,t}`) — never audio; both devices render through the same FM e-piano (you warm amber/rose panned-left, partner cool cyan/violet panned-right). The clock: ping/pong `offset = t1 − (t0 + rtt/2)`, RTT-weighted slew, an emergent `beatPhase(now)` both derive identically from a shared epoch+BPM (68) — then each note is gently quantized to the nearest shared sub-beat (~11ms humanize) so two pianos *interlock* instead of smearing. **MIDI/keyboard INPUT** (a real pianist's keyboard — off banned touch-primary; on-screen strip only the fallback) · **three.js OUTPUT** (scarce, off banned Canvas2D-primary + off WebGPU-3×) · **Supabase-Realtime relay + NTP shared-clock TECH** (a clock-sync *substrate*, NOT the banned step-sequencer loop-groove) · **intimate-duet VIBE** (off banned silly). **Ambition honest 3–4/5** (`#1+#2+#3+#4+#5`): **#1** first Supabase-Realtime/WebSocket transport AND first networked shared-clock in the lab · **#2** ≥5 subsystems (NTP clock-sync + Realtime relay [note/ping/pong/presence] + multi-input + shared-grid-scheduled FM e-piano + three.js pulse-ring) · **#3** named refs (**Ableton Link** · NTP-over-WebSocket · Chrome Music Lab **Shared Piano** · JackTrip/Chris Chafe **SoundWIRE** · George Lewis *Voyager*) · **#4** multi-cycle (cycle-2 = Karel's real piano grains [JURY #5], >2 players, jitter buffering) · **#5** RESEARCH §477 same-day. **Bulletproof glance:** `master 0.28 → lowpass 7.5k → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; always-on breathing D-Dorian pulse bed; **ghost partner** (*Voyager*) auto-plays cool-panned phrases locked to the same grid after ~2.5s idle, hands-free, zero permission, steps aside when a real partner joins; lazy/env-guarded Supabase client → any failure (missing env/offline/subscribe error) falls to solo + a calm `text-amber-300/95` notice; **no DB write** (Broadcast is ephemeral pub/sub — side-effect-free) · **no API route → no guard** (browser connects directly; the only key is the `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` anon key, client-public by design — rules #6/#8 clean) · **no new npm dep** (`three` + `@supabase/supabase-js` already deps); AudioContext unlocked in the Start gesture (iOS); Canvas2D pulse-ring fallback for no-WebGL; full teardown (rAF/listeners/MIDI/three.js dispose/AudioContext close/channel unsubscribe). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender-verified** — authoritative `npm run build` compiled + type-checked + ESLint-passed cleanly, but the container's anomalous **4096 fd soft limit** killed Next's static-generation worker (`EMFILE` at "Collecting page data"); **proven environmental** — pristine `origin/main` (live on Vercel) fails identically at the identical path; shipped on green code gates + prerender-safe `"use client"` structure (only numeric constants + pure `new THREE.Color()` at module top; all browser-API/Supabase access inside handlers/effects) + the live-Vercel control (see STATE.md cycle 477). **NOT browser/device-verified** (no two phones in sandbox) — unverified by play: whether the offset/RTT lock holds across two real networks, how "locked" the quantized duet feels by ear, MIDI on a real keyboard, iOS audio unlock; the ghost + Canvas2D + solo fallbacks guarantee a sounding, locked-feeling glance regardless. Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-substrate fire; banked **`739-piano-room-relay`** ⭐ (Supabase Realtime Broadcast direct — the reliability/frictionless sibling) + **`740-piano-room-portal`** ⭐ (WebRTC P2P auto-signaled over Supabase — the low-latency cycle-2 of 729) as **IDEAS §477**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/741-piano-room-pulse/README.md`

## ⭐ Earlier (Cycle 476 — **kids** build · DEEP, ONE memory concept via 3 scarce-renderer approaches)

- **[/dream/738-kids-song-sprout](/dream/738-kids-song-sprout)** — Song Sprout 🌱. `demoable` · Cycle 476. **For**: kids (3+) / **mic voice/hum** (analysis-only) / **three.js** glowing `Points` creature (Canvas2D fallback) / accreting compositional memory + grows-up / tender-companionship, no-groove. **What if a 4-year-old had a single glowing creature that LISTENS, REMEMBERS the little songs they hum, and slowly GROWS UP — singing their own melodies back, recombined into something new?** The lab's **FIRST long-form / stateful KIDS piece** — every other kids prototype is instant cause→effect that *resets*; this one has **memory** and **evolves over minutes** (the sprout at minute 1 — a tiny baby-blue spark, thin voice — is genuinely different at minute 5 — a big warm-gold being with a fuller vibrato'd voice singing developed versions of what it was taught). The child hums short phrases; the creature leans toward the sound, brightens, and REMEMBERS the contours; on the child's silence it SINGS BACK a reply **recombined** from remembered fragments (transpose / reorder / reverse / contour-invert / rhythm-warp + a resolving tail as it matures — an answer, never a literal echo). As memories accumulate a saturating growth curve (`1−e^(−notes/26)`) drives everything: particles 600→2600, body scale up, colour baby-blue→violet→gold, voice gains an octave + FM warmth + vibrato + longer release, drone fills out, replies come more often — with a legible life-stage label ("a tiny spark" → "a grown song-being"). The structural answer to **JURY 2026-06-18 #3** ("reward DEPTH, build the accreting-memory ceiling, stop banking and walking away") — the adult-only long-form/evolving capability (689/704/734) brought DOWN to a kid as a *relationship*, not a toy. **mic voice/hum INPUT** (off banned touch-primary; camera-free vs the camera-heavy recent kids 727/731/724) · **three.js OUTPUT** (scarce, 1× in window; off banned Canvas2D-primary + off WebGPU which is 3×) · **accreting compositional-memory + call-and-response recombination TECH** (emphatically NOT a shared-clock loop/groove) · **tender/companionship/wonder VIBE** (off banned silly-comedy). **Ambition honest 2–3/5** (`#2 + #4`, arguably `#1/#5`): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (mic autocorrelation pitch/phrase capture + memory store & saturating-growth engine + `composeReply` recombination + growth-aware FM voice synth + three.js growing-creature renderer = 5) · **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle-1 = core memory+growth+reply; cycle-2 = richer life-stages/seasons, harmonic memory, a second sprout) · arguably **#1** (first kids piece with *compositional* memory + a maturation arc — prior kids memory [298-kids-echo-friend] is literal call-response, not recombination+growth) · **#5** RESEARCH §476 same-day (long-form-evolving generative frontier is adult-only → bring it to a kid). **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `master ≤0.3 → lowpass 7.5k → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; mic **analysis-only** (never routed to destination); always-on warm drone; **ghost auto-demo** (a virtual child hums scale-shaped phrases, so the sprout listens/grows/sings hands-free on load with zero permission) on mic-deny/no-mic/idle; D-Dorian snap so it's never out of tune; AudioContext unlocked in the Start gesture (iOS); Canvas2D fallback (`fallback.ts`) for no-WebGL; full teardown (rAF/listener/three.js dispose/AudioContext close). **No API route → no guard** (mic read in-browser only; no secret, no side effect — rules #6/#8 clean); **no CDN dependency** (three.js is a project dep). ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender-verified** — the authoritative `npm run build` compiled + type-checked + ESLint-passed cleanly, but the container's anomalous **4096 fd soft limit** killed Next's static-generation worker spawn (`EMFILE` at "Collecting page data"); **proven environmental** — pristine `origin/main` (the exact commit live on Vercel) fails identically; shipped on green code gates + prerender-safe `"use client"` structure (verified: only numeric constants + pure `new THREE.Color()` at module top, all browser-API access inside handlers/effects) + the live-Vercel control (see STATE.md cycle 476). **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/mic/audio in sandbox) — unverified by ear/eye: whether the autocorrelation pitch tracks a small child's hum, whether the recombined replies read as *the sprout developing*, growth/reply-cadence tuning, iOS mic unlock; the ghost demo + Canvas2D fallback guarantee a sounding, growing glance regardless. Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-renderer fire; banked **`736-kids-echo-grove`** ⭐ (mic → three.js accreting grove that re-sings older trees) + **`737-kids-memory-reef`** (mic → raw WebGL2 GLSL reef that grows coral from the voice — the iOS-bulletproof sibling) as **IDEAS §476**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/738-kids-song-sprout/README.md`

## ⭐ Earlier (Cycle 472 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 divergent explorers each on a different SCARCE renderer)

- **[/dream/727-kids-color-hunt](/dream/727-kids-color-hunt)** — Color Hunt 🎨. `demoable` · Cycle 472. **For**: kids (4+) / **camera-as-color-sampler** (analysis-only) / **WebGPU compute-shader** particle bloom (Canvas2D fallback) / cross-modal color→harmony / wonder, no-groove. **What if a 4-year-old could point the tablet at the colors in their room and PAINT MUSIC with what they see — red things sing warm and low, sky-blue shimmers bright and high?** Each frame averages the center-region RGB of the rear camera → HSV; **hue** selects a register-climbing consonant pentatonic chord (warm-low → bright-high), **saturation** sets particle energy, **brightness** sets loudness + bloom openness — a slow evolving texture that re-voices on color-shift, never a beat/loop. A 12,288-particle **WebGPU WGSL compute+render bloom** takes on the room's color (first-class Canvas2D fallback with identical physics). The literal answer to **JURY 2026-06-18 #1** ("give a kid the scarce renderer — port the GPU stack to a kids piece") **and #2** ("texture/cause-effect, not a groove; vary the emotional register off silly"). **camera-color INPUT** (cross-modal — the world is the instrument; off banned touch-primary, distinct from the lab's camera-body/frame-diff/hand-tracking) · **WebGPU OUTPUT** (scarcest+best renderer; off banned Canvas2D-primary + warming three.js) · **cross-modal hue→consonant-chord TECH** (NOT a shared-clock loop/groove) · **wonder/discovery VIBE** (off banned silly-comedy). **Ambition honest 2–3/5** (`#2 + #3`): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (camera color-sampler + cross-modal hue→harmony engine + WebGPU compute bloom w/ Canvas2D fallback + ambient bed) · **#3** named refs (**Scriabin** *clavier à lumières* · **Kandinsky** color-tone theory · **Refik Anadol** luminous fields). **#1 NOT claimed** — `317-kids-color-bells`❤️ already pioneered camera region-color sampling; 727 *deepens that loved lineage* (Canvas2D bells → WebGPU chord bloom; playful → wonder). **Kid-safe + bulletproof glance:** `gain(0.3)→lowpass(7.5k)→DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1)→destination`; always-on ambient bed; ghost hue-wheel auto-demo on camera-deny/idle (zero permissions); camera analysis-only (96×72 offscreen center average — nothing recorded/sent); getUserMedia inside the Start tap (iOS); full teardown. **No API route → no guard** (camera read in-browser only; no secret, no side effect — rules #6/#8 clean); **no CDN dependency**. ⚠️ **Compile-verified, NOT prerender-verified** — the authoritative `npm run build` compiled + type-checked + ESLint-passed cleanly, but the container's anomalous **4096 fd soft limit** killed Next's static-generation worker spawn (`EMFILE`); **proven environmental** — pristine `origin/main` (live on Vercel) fails identically; shipped on green code gates + prerender-safe `"use client"` structure (all browser-API access inside handlers/effects) + the live-Vercel control (see STATE.md cycle 472). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (3 scarce renderers × 3 off-glass inputs × 3 non-silly vibes); banked **`726-kids-star-scoop`** ⭐ (MediaPipe hands → three.js 3D star-scoop, awe) + **`725-kids-aurora-sail`** (device-tilt → WebGL2 GLSL aurora, dreamy) as **IDEAS §472**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/727-kids-color-hunt/README.md`
