## (Cycle 415 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers all chasing the jury's WARMTH dare. Ships **`580-tide-breath`**: *the real ocean breathes a warm chord — a direct, keyless, client-side fetch of your coast's LIVE marine conditions makes the actual swell the breath of a sustained just-intonation drone, over a WebGPU water-surface that rises and falls with the live tide.* The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-13** on THREE axes at once: **#1** revive the starved WebGPU (1× across the last 15; the lab fled into a three.js monoculture) — 580 renders a WGSL fragment-shader water-surface with a Canvas2D hot-downgrade; **#2** real-world-data sonification (**0× the whole window**, named-starved every jury) — I curled the endpoint live (`marine-api.open-meteo.com/v1/marine`, no key, `access-control-allow-origin: *`, returned wave 1.28 m / swell 12.1 s); **#4** chase WARMTH, not a puzzle — no interaction after the play gesture, no melody, no beat, just a luminous sea and a chord the ocean is playing. On play: optional geolocation (3 s timeout → silent Monterey-Bay default) → keyless Open-Meteo Marine fetch (baked sample on offline/failure, shown amber **"Sample swell (feed offline)"** vs emerald **"Live · Open-Meteo Marine · {place}"**). Three live numbers drive the whole piece: **swell_wave_period** → a sine breath LFO on master gain + lowpass (one inhale/exhale per swell cycle, ~6–18 s), **wave_height** → breath depth, **sea_surface_temperature** → timbre warmth. Sound = six detuned oscillator pairs tuned to a **D2 just-intonation chord** (1, 3/2, 2, 5/2, 3, 7/4) through a warm `gain → lowpass → compressor → out`; **no note-tapping, no melody, no onset/beat — one continuous breathing chord.** Visual = a sum-of-sines water surface breathing in sync; WebGPU (WGSL: waterline + horizon glow + shimmer + temperature-tinted palette) with a Canvas2D hot-fallback, alive from frame one. **real-marine-data INPUT · WebGPU OUTPUT** (Canvas2D fallback — NOT three.js [jury 6×], NOT SVG [jury 5×], NOT the mic [jury 5×] — every ban dodged + revives the starved renderer) · **tidal-data sonification TECH** (NOT onset/beat/tempo) · **warm-oceanic-drone VIBE** (NOT a cerebral puzzle). **Ambition honest 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [marine fetch + marine→JI breath/harmony mapping + WebGPU/Canvas2D water render + breathing-drone synth = 4] + #3 named refs that DRIVE it [Andrea Polli — ocean/climate-data sonification; Éliane Radigue & La Monte Young — warm long-form just-intonation drone]; **#1 NOT claimed** — real-data sonification broadly exists [`314-solar-wind` space-weather, `437-wiki-pulse`, `575-kids-sky-song` weather, `355` seismic], so a live-MARINE breathing-drone is a fresh source + breath structure, not a fresh primitive; **#5 NOT claimed** — Open-Meteo Marine is infrastructure, not a <14-day result). Born from RESEARCH §415 (NOAA space-weather is keyless+live BUT `314-solar-wind` already owns it → grep saved a near-duplicate; the unmined warm real-data source is the OCEAN). **Resonates with Karel's loved ocean cluster** (`107-ocean-presence`❤️, `84-wave-fluid`❤️; additive). Degrades fully: no location → Monterey Bay; no/failed network → baked sample swell, always plays; no WebGPU → Canvas2D; autonomous so the silent 06:30 glance always shows a breathing sea that sings. **No API route, no `guard`** (public read-only no-key client fetch — no secret; CSP allows `https:` connect-src). No new npm deps. Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer fire; siblings **`581-still-bloom`** (off the glass: device-motion *stillness* → an audio-first warm Radigue/Oliveros drone that BLOOMS from silence only while you hold the phone still, motion scatters it; minimal Canvas2D bloom — the off-glass/audio-only/conceptual lane the jury keeps naming 0×; LOST: lowest ambition, no WebGPU, payoff needs a held-still device so a silent glance sees only the breathe-demo) + **`582-piano-aurora`** (a warm piano's *harmony* — 12-bin FFT chroma, NOT onset/beat — paints a WebGPU curl-noise aurora confined to a warm amber→rose→violet arc; built-in generative pad + drop-your-own file; Anadol/Akten/Bridson; LOST: a beautiful audio-reactive *visualizer* but the lab's flow-field/aurora lane is crowded [130❤️/262❤️/441] and it carries no real data — resurrect bound to Karel's actual `/api/audio/[id]` tracks) banked IDEAS §415. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, **451/451** pages, `/dream/580-tide-breath` 6.6 kB / 113 kB prerendered static), not browser-verified (no WebGPU/audio/geolocation/network in sandbox). · *(Cycle 414 shipped `579-silk-choir`; below.)*

- **[/dream/580-tide-breath](/dream/580-tide-breath)** — Tide Breath 🌊🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 415. **For**: adult / meditative / real-world-data. The real ocean breathes a warm chord: a keyless client-side fetch of your coast's LIVE marine conditions makes the actual swell the breath of a sustained just-intonation drone (swell period → breath rate, wave height → breath depth, sea-surface-temp → timbre), over a WebGPU water-surface that rises and falls with the live tide. Real-marine-data input (NOT the mic), WebGPU (NOT three.js/SVG — revives the jury-starved renderer), Canvas2D fallback; autonomous, no puzzle. The directest answer to the jury's #1 (revive WebGPU) + #2 (real-world-data, 0× the window) + #4 (warmth). Refs: Andrea Polli; Éliane Radigue; La Monte Young; Open-Meteo Marine. No API route / no guard (public no-key client fetch). Design notes: `src/app/dream/580-tide-breath/README.md`

## (Cycle 414 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 approaches on one concept. Ships **`579-silk-choir`**: *grab a luminous sheet of silk and PULL it — the membrane's tension bends a warm choir/bowed-string chord, so pulling it taut makes the voices rise and brighten and letting it billow lets them sigh and fall.* The pitch is **continuous and physical — you bend the chord with your hands, you never tap a discrete note** — the directest break from **JURY 2026-06-13**'s banned kids **pentatonic-tap-wash** (≈6 of 7 kids pieces are "tap → a discrete consonant note → pretty viz"), on the **jury-starved WebGPU** renderer it explicitly wanted back (starved 1×; the lab fled into a three.js monoculture). The sheet is a 2-D grid of point masses (34×24) integrated with **verlet / position-based dynamics** (velocity implied by `position − previousPosition`; relaxation passes satisfy structural + shear springs; top edge pinned so it hangs like a curtain). When `navigator.gpu` exists the whole solver runs in a **WebGPU compute shader (WGSL)** — integrate + relax passes over storage buffers, read back per frame — with a **runtime hot-downgrade** to a byte-equivalent CPU solver if WebGPU is absent/fails (so it looks + plays identically on an older iPad); both render the same glowing additive filaments (Canvas2D), violet at rest → gold where pulled taut. Multi-touch grabs the nearest node; the sheet is split into six regions and each region's **mean stretch past rest length** (a 0..1 tension) continuously **bends one voice of a just-intonation major-add9 over A2** (ratios 1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 3/2, 2/1, 9/4) up to ~5 semitones via `setTargetAtTime` portamento, opening a per-voice lowpass as you pull — the chord physically warps under your hands and resolves home on release; **there is no note-tapping anywhere.** Kids-safe master (`gain → lowpass ≤8 kHz → compressor → out`); AudioContext born in the first tap; the silk shimmers from frame one and a silent idle **auto-demo** sweeps a grab across it so the 06:30 phone glance sings with zero setup. **multi-touch-stretch INPUT · WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (Canvas2D fallback — NOT three.js, NOT SVG, NOT WebGL2-at-edge, NOT the mic — every jury ban dodged + revives the starved renderer) · **verlet-membrane tension→continuous-pitch TECH** (NOT onset/beat/tempo) · **warm/silky/continuous-expressive-pitch VIBE** (NOT a pentatonic-tap toy, NOT a creature). **Ambition honest 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [verlet/PBD solver + multi-touch grab + tension→continuous-bowed-chord synth + GPU/Canvas2D render = 4] + #3 named refs that DRIVE it [Thomas Jakobsen *Advanced Character Physics* (GDC 2001) — the silk *is* a Jakobsen verlet cloth; Ondes Martenot / Theremin — the continuous-hand-bent-pitch lineage, the reason there are no discrete taps]; **#1 NOT claimed** — verlet exists at `303-kids-wind-harp` as 1-D strings, so a 2-D grab-stretch *membrane* sounding continuous tension→pitch is a fresh interaction + musical structure, not a fresh primitive; **#5 NOT claimed** — no <14-day bind, WebGPU-production-ready is a months-old 2026 trend, said plainly). Born from RESEARCH §414 (WebGPU production-ready across all browsers in 2026 + verlet membrane → a stretchable singing sheet). Degrades fully: no WebGPU → CPU/Canvas2D; no touch → mouse drag; failures in `text-rose-300`. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + WebGPU/Canvas2D — no network, no secrets). No new npm deps. Winner of a DEEP 3-approach fire; siblings **`580-silk-veil`** (the same grab-stretch concept in pure **Canvas2D** — the bulletproof-iPad path; LOST: most reliable but Canvas2D-only so does NOT revive the jury-starved WebGPU; resurrect for a max-reliability fire) + **`581-silk-ripple`** (a different interaction — **flick/slap → traveling waves** that bend the chord in ripples; WebGPU + Canvas2D; LOST: most fun for a 4yo + also revives WebGPU but a *variant* of the headline with a subtler bend; resurrect for a playful fire / fold in as 579's cycle-2 gesture) banked IDEAS §414. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, **450/450** pages, `/dream/579-silk-choir` 5.83 kB / 109 kB prerendered static), not browser-verified (no WebGPU/touch/audio in sandbox). · *(Cycle 413 shipped `576-presence-room` — the lab's first head-tracked binaural, adult WIDE — not separately blocked in INDEX; doc drift. Cycle 412 below.)*

- **[/dream/579-silk-choir](/dream/579-silk-choir)** — Silk Choir 🪡🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 414. **For**: kids (4+). Grab a luminous sheet of silk and pull it — the membrane's tension bends a warm choir/bowed-string chord, so pulling it taut makes the voices rise and brighten and letting it billow lets them sigh and fall; the pitch is continuous and physical, you bend the chord with your hands and never tap a note. A 2-D verlet / position-based-dynamics membrane on a **WebGPU compute shader** (with a runtime hot-downgrade to a CPU/Canvas2D solver), six regions each bending one voice of a JI major-add9 by stretch via `setTargetAtTime` portamento. Multi-touch stretch input (NOT the mic), WebGPU (NOT three.js/SVG/WebGL2 — revives the jury-starved renderer), Canvas2D fallback. The directest break from the banned kids pentatonic-tap-wash. Refs: Thomas Jakobsen *Advanced Character Physics* (2001); Ondes Martenot / Theremin. No API route / no guard (pure client). Design notes: `src/app/dream/579-silk-choir/README.md`

## (Cycle 412 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 approaches on one concept. Ships **`575-kids-sky-song`**: *today's REAL current weather writes a child a little song — and the child plays it.* The piece does a direct client-side fetch of the child's **live local weather** (Open-Meteo — free, no-key, no-auth, CORS; geolocation with a silent default-coordinate fallback, a baked "example sky" if offline) and lets the **real weather COMPOSE an evolving generative piece**: WMO `weather_code`+`is_day` pick the key/mode/instruments (clear→bright major celesta · overcast→suspended pads · rain→minor-pentatonic plucks · snow→glassy maj7 bells · fog→low drone · thunder→hushed dorian · night→dropped an octave), `wind_speed`→tempo, `cloud_cover`→note density, `temperature`→timbral warmth, `precipitation`→an overlaid droplet voice. A Chris-Wilson **look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms pump, notes scheduled ~120 ms ahead) plays an 8-step ostinato that **mutates + re-seeds over time** (Eno-style recombination) so it's a moving piece, not a loop; the child **touches a raw-WebGL2 aurora field** to add their own glowing voice, always snapped to today's key (no wrong note, no fail state). The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-13 #2** (real-world-data sonification — **0× the whole window**) + **#4** (chase warmth, not a puzzle), and a grep-verified **kids-first real-data sonification** (the lab's prior weather toys `115`/`120`/`83` are all *synthetic*/"Zero API"; the only real-data pieces `355`/`337` are adult, out of frame). **live-weather-data + touch INPUT · raw-WebGL2 aurora OUTPUT** (Canvas2D fallback — NOT three.js, NOT SVG, NOT the mic — every jury ban dodged) · **real-data generative-sonification TECH** (NOT onset/beat/tempo *extraction* — this *generates*) · **warm/dreamy/wonder VIBE**. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 qualified kids-first real-external-data sonification + #2 ≥3 subsystems [live Open-Meteo fetch + weather→generative-song engine + look-ahead Web Audio scheduler + WebGL2 aurora render = 4] + #3 named refs [Andrea Polli climate-data sonification; Natalie Miebach woven weather-scores; Brian Eno generative recombination]; **#5 NOT claimed** — the sonification lineage is foundational, no <14-day bind, said plainly). Degrades fully: WebGL2 absent → Canvas2D field that still drifts + sings; geolocation denied → silent default city; fetch failed → baked example sky + `text-rose-300` note, always plays. **No API route, no `guard`** (Open-Meteo is a public read-only no-key client fetch — CSP-verified the enforced `connect-src … https:` allows it; no secret). No new npm deps. Winner of a DEEP 3-approach fire; siblings **`573-kids-sky-window`** (raw-WebGL2 luminous GLSL sky — fbm clouds/sun/rain/lightning; the richest atmosphere renderer — LOST: 575's generative engine is the bigger concept leap; resurrect for an atmosphere-forward fire) + **`574-kids-weather-today`** (Canvas2D cut-paper diorama with a smiling sun + paper critter — the warmest, most 4yo-legible, most iPad-safe — LOST: closest in *form* to the lab's existing synthetic weather toys) banked IDEAS §412. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, **448/448** pages, `/dream/575-kids-sky-song` 8.21 kB / 114 kB prerendered static), not browser-verified (no WebGL2/audio/geolocation in sandbox). · *(Cycle 411 shipped `570-spoken-spell`; below.)*

- **[/dream/575-kids-sky-song](/dream/575-kids-sky-song)** — Sky Song 🌦️🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 412. **For**: kids (4+). Today's REAL local weather writes a little song and the child plays it: the live Open-Meteo conditions compose an evolving generative piece (key/mode/tempo/density/timbre all from the real sky), a look-ahead scheduler plays an ostinato that mutates + re-seeds over time (Eno-style), and the child touches a WebGL2 aurora field to add their own voice in today's key — no wrong note, no fail state. Live-weather + touch input (NOT the mic), WebGL2 aurora (NOT three.js/SVG), Canvas2D fallback. The lab's first kids real-data sonification (prior weather toys are synthetic). Refs: Andrea Polli, Natalie Miebach, Brian Eno. No API route / no guard (public no-key client fetch). Design notes: `src/app/dream/575-kids-sky-song/README.md`

## Cycle 411 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal explorers. Ships **`570-spoken-spell`**: *speak, and your live words become a self-layering musical incantation — Resonance hears your speech (real Web Speech API recognition), maps each word's letters to a D-pentatonic pitch, vowel-ratio to duration, and freezes finished phrases into looping ostinati, so the longer you speak the denser the canon and you can hear your own sentence inside it.* The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-12 #2** ("find sound in **language**, not a simulated physical field") and its #5-revival ask: born from this cycle's dive into the **2026 in-browser ASR wave** (transformers.js running Whisper/LiteASR/Cohere Transcribe fully client-side via ONNX/WASM — RESEARCH §411). **live-speech-recognition INPUT** (voice — NOT the jury's 7× finger-on-glass default) **+ SVG word-river OUTPUT** (NOT three.js [rolling-10 4× → BANNED], NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU [jury-banned]) · **continuous-transcription→linguistic-music→accretive-loop TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics→drone — the banned meta-technique) · **ritual/incantatory/cerebral VIBE**. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 qualified lab-first [first piece where *continuous* speech transcription drives the composition itself — distinct from `189-voice-scene`'s keyword-switch and `345-speech-melody`'s *typed* text] + #2 ≥3 subsystems [Web Speech ASR + deterministic phoneme→pitch engine + sample-accurate look-ahead scheduler + accretive loop-voice memory + SVG render = 5] + #3 named refs that DRIVE it [Steve Reich *Different Trains*/*It's Gonna Rain* — speech-melody; Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* — language→resonance]; **#5 soft/honest** — the ASR-wave bind is ~3 months, not <14 days, so not claimed as a clean 5). Degrades fully: no Web Speech API (iOS/Firefox) → typed-text fallback runs the same mapping; an auto-demo "speaks" a seeded incantation from frame one so the silent 06:30 glance always shows + hears the accreting canon. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + SVG + Web Speech API — mic is analysis-only, no recording/upload/network, no secrets; imports the existing read-only `@/lib/browser/speech-recognition` util). No new npm deps. Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer fire; siblings **`571-the-withholding`** (music you can only hear by staying silent — mic as an *inverse* gate, a conceptual/critical piece on attention-as-cost; Cage *4'33"*/Christine Sun Kim — LOST: strongest pure concept but a quiet-room/"imagine the silence" demo is less glance-legible; resurrect to open the missing conceptual lane) + **`572-orrery-tunings`** (turn/tilt your phone to tune into a just-intonation harmonic sky — gyro → raw WebGL2 aurora; La Monte Young *Dream House*/Oliveros — LOST: lowest ambition 2/5, adjacent to existing soundfield/tilt/JI rooms + flagged FBM frame-drops on iPad) banked IDEAS §411. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, 447/447 pages, `/dream/570-spoken-spell` 5.68 kB / 109 kB prerendered static), not browser-verified (no mic/audio in sandbox). · *(Cycle 410 shipped `569-kids-ember-keeper`; below.)*

- **[/dream/570-spoken-spell](/dream/570-spoken-spell)** — Spoken Spell 🗣️✨. `demoable` · Cycle 411. **For**: adult / live-performance / language-as-music. Speak and your live words become a self-layering musical incantation — each word's letters map to a D-pentatonic pitch, finished phrases freeze into looping ostinati, so the canon thickens the longer you speak and you hear your own sentence inside it. Web Speech API (live ASR) → deterministic phoneme→pitch + look-ahead scheduler → SVG word-river that glows each word as it sounds. Voice input (NOT a finger), SVG (NOT the banned three.js/Canvas2D/WebGPU). Typed-text fallback + seeded auto-demo for iOS/Firefox/silent glances. Refs: Steve Reich *Different Trains*; Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room*; the 2026 in-browser ASR wave. Design notes: `src/app/dream/570-spoken-spell/README.md`

## (Cycle 410 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 approaches to one concept. Ships **`569-kids-ember-keeper`**: *a glowing creature that lives in the device, remembers every visit, and grows a genuinely bigger, differently-shaped body each day a child comes back — and never resets to zero.* The lab's **FIRST cross-session persistent state** in 569 prototypes (grep-verified: no prior dream piece writes a creature/world to `localStorage` and reloads it across visits/days) — the directest answer to the **JURY 2026-06-12 #6**: *"518-living-ember is the one kids piece with memory that rewards return across days — extend THAT: a creature a child finds genuinely changed tomorrow, instead of another 90-second glow toy."* The body is not stored as pixels or a model file — it is **regrown from a saved "genome"** (`genome.ts`, `localStorage` key `resonance.ember-keeper.v1`) every load. The genome **only ever accumulates**: `visits`, `distinctDays`, learned pentatonic `palette`, and an append-only list of growth `tokens`. **Day-aware growth** (the big visible change): on load it compares stored `lastVisitDay` to `new Date().toDateString()`; a new calendar day adds a big glowing **limb** placed on a golden-angle (phyllotaxis) spiral — so today's creature is visibly bigger than yesterday's. Within a day, humming/petting adds small permanent **fronds** + brightens the ember. **Deterministic regrowth**: each token seeds a `mulberry32` PRNG so the same genome always regrows the same body (limbs never jump between loads — D'Arcy Thompson made literal). **Voice feeding**: mic → autocorrelation pitch detection (Chris Wilson method, `pitch.ts`) → quantized to C-major pentatonic (nothing ever wrong) → the degree is echoed back softly + saved to the palette; the **mic is analysis-only** (dead-end `AnalyserNode`, never recorded/stored/transmitted — only the derived integer degree persists). **three.js** render; kids-safe `gain → lowpass(8 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−10, 20:1) → destination` brick-wall limiter; AudioContext in the Wake gesture (iOS unlock). **For the 06:30 hands-free review** (the reviewer can't wait a real day): a fresh device with empty storage SEEDS a believable history — **5 visits across 4 days, a 5-note palette, 10 growth tokens (4 day-limbs + 6 fronds)** — so the glance always shows a rich, many-limbed creature breathing from frame one and singing on wake; and a clearly-secondary **🌙 next-day** button TIME-TRAVELS so Karel can watch a brand-new limb sparkle into place on demand, in seconds. **mic-voice + tap INPUT** (off-glass core — NOT the jury-flagged finger-on-glass default) **+ three.js OUTPUT** (NOT SVG [3× in the rolling-10, nudging the ban], NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU [jury-banned]) · **cross-session-persistent procedural-morphology TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics→drone — the banned meta-technique) · **companion/relationship/growth VIBE** (NOT a calm-sensory 90-second glow toy — it rewards *returning*, the jury's exact #6 ask). **Ambition 4/5** (#1 first-ever cross-session persistent state [grep-verified lab-first] + #2 ≥3 subsystems [autocorrelation pitch + accumulating-genome persistence engine + deterministic phyllotaxis morphology + three.js render + Web Audio = 5] + #3 named refs that DRIVE it [Tamagotchi (Maita/Bandai 1996) — lives in the device & rewards returning; Steve Grand's *Creatures* (1996) — accumulating internal state drives outward form; D'Arcy Thompson *On Growth and Form* (1917) — golden-angle limb placement taken directly] + #4 multi-cycle Ember spine cycle 1; **#5 NOT claimed** — no <14-day bind, cs.SD still server-ML, said plainly; the dive confirmed the live thread [Niantic Peridot generative-AI creatures, the rctAI "AI pets" wave]). Graceful degradation on both axes: mic-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + tap-to-pet still grows; WebGL-fail → `text-rose-300` notice + a DOM/CSS orb with one petal per token. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client mic + Web Audio + three.js — no network, no secrets). No new npm deps (three already installed). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one concept — *a creature genuinely different each day you return* — via what evolves); siblings **`570-kids-ember-song`** (the song-memory take: hummed phrases append to a persistent library, woven into a longer/richer piece each return — WebGL2 luminous strands; LOST: payoff is audible/abstract, least glance-legible at a silent review — the biggest *concept*, resurrect for a music-forward fire) + **`571-kids-ember-nest`** (the accumulating-world take: each visit plants a persistent SVG cut-paper flower/star/friend → a fuller world + denser additive chord; LOST: closest to the existing `116-kids-bloom-garden` self-seeding garden + another SVG nudges the 4× ban) banked IDEAS §410. Extends Karel's loved companion/embodied lane (`234-kids-hand-creature`❤️, `217-dance-avatar`❤️). Build-verified (`npm run build`), not browser-verified (no audio/WebGL in sandbox). · *(Cycle 409 shipped `568-conductor-hands`; below.)*

- **[/dream/569-kids-ember-keeper](/dream/569-kids-ember-keeper)** — Ember Keeper 🔥🐣. `demoable` · Cycle 410. **For**: kids (4+) / companion / cross-day memory. A glowing creature that lives in the device, remembers every visit, and grows a genuinely bigger, differently-shaped body each day you come back — and never resets. The lab's first cross-session persistent state: the body is regrown from a saved `localStorage` genome that only accumulates; a new calendar day adds a phyllotaxis limb, humming/petting adds fronds. Hum to feed it (analysis-only mic → pentatonic), tap to pet, 🌙 time-travel to watch it grow. three.js (NOT the banned SVG/Canvas2D/WebGPU); off-glass voice input. The jury's #6 ("a creature genuinely changed tomorrow") answered literally. Refs: Tamagotchi (1996) / Steve Grand *Creatures* / D'Arcy Thompson *On Growth and Form*. Design notes: `src/app/dream/569-kids-ember-keeper/README.md`

## (Cycle 409 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 approaches to one concept. Ships **`568-conductor-hands`**: *raise both hands to the camera and conduct two incommensurable tempi — your **left hand beats one voice, right hand the other**; the system snaps the two tempi to the nearest irrational ratio (√2, φ, e/2, π/2) so the two consonant voices drift through every phase relationship and can **NEVER share a downbeat** — metric dissonance you feel in your own arms.* **Cycle 2 of the polytempo spine** (`514-polytempo-loom` = cycle 1, the autonomous 5-voice loom the **JURY 2026-06-12 #1 named: "take it to cycle 2 AND chase a 4/5"**, and #4: *"514 had no embodiment — build the SECOND time-tension piece where gestures set incommensurable tempi"*). Where 514 was set *for* you, 568 you *conduct*: **MediaPipe-Hands beat-extraction** (`tracking.ts`) tracks each wrist's vertical motion, fires a downbeat at the bottom of each conducting stroke (velocity zero-crossing down→up, 150 ms debounce), and averages the last 3 inter-beat intervals → that hand's tempo (clamped 37–300 BPM). The **incommensurability lock** (`snapToIrrational`): voice A = your gestured base period; voice B is forced to `periodA / ratio` where ratio is the irrational *nearest in log-space* to what you actually gestured — you choose roughly the spread, the math guarantees the pulses are non-periodic against each other. **Web Audio** (`audio.ts`): two warm pluck voices (triangle + octave sine, fast envelope) playing the same D-pentatonic cell (voice B up a perfect fifth — consonant, distinguishable), each on its OWN sample-accurate **"Tale of Two Clocks" look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms pump, `start(t)` 120 ms ahead); master `gain → DynamicsCompressor(−10 dB, 20:1) → destination` limiter; AudioContext in the Start click (iOS unlock). **three.js** (`render.ts`): a Nancarrow **phase-space** — two emissive markers orbit their rings (one revolution per beat-period) tracing a **Lissajous ribbon that NEVER closes** because the ratio is irrational (the visible proof they never realign); each marker flashes on its scheduled beat; violet=voice A, amber=voice B, dark indigo field. **camera hand-tracking INPUT** (off-glass — the directest dodge of the jury's 7× finger-on-glass ban, and the freshest input vs the last 4 cycles: mic @406, audio-file @405/407, shake @408, tilt @404) **+ three.js OUTPUT** (NOT SVG [rolling-10 4× → BANNED], NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU [jury-banned]) · **conducting-gesture→tempo→polytempo-scheduler TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics→drone — the banned meta-technique) · **embodied/cerebral-rhythmic/Nancarrow VIBE** (the directest literal answer to jury #4 "two gestures set incommensurable tempi"). **Ambition honest 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe beat-extraction + irrational-lock + dual look-ahead schedulers + three.js phase-space = 4] + #3 named refs that DRIVE it [Conlon Nancarrow *Studies for Player Piano* — the irrational-ratio canon IS the lock; Ligeti *Continuum/Désordre*; Reich phase pieces — but the irrational lock means resolution is mathematically impossible, "Reich phasing that can never close"] + #4 spine cycle 2 — the jury's exact ask; **#1 contestable** — MediaPipe Hands exists [234/258/287/302/419/524] but *gesture→tempo* is a new cross-modal input, discounted honestly; **#5 NOT claimed** — no <14-day finding to bind, cs.SD still server-ML [the jury's own #5 call-out], said plainly). From frame one a silent auto-demo orbits two synthetic conductors at φ, so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows the drift; camera-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + two tap pads (fully playable). Camera/landmark data is **local only** (no recording, no upload, no network); **no API route, no `guard`**. No new npm deps (three already installed; MediaPipe via CDN dynamic import). Winner of a DEEP 3-approach fire; siblings **`569-conductor-batons`** (two-thumb tap-tempo → WebGL2 scrolling Nancarrow piano-roll, the cleanest + most legible — LOST on finger-on-glass + least embodied) + **`570-conductor-baton-motion`** (DeviceMotion baton → three.js — LOST: device-motion repeats cycle 408's input) banked IDEAS §409. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, 445/445 pages, `/dream/568-conductor-hands` 5.5 kB / 297 kB First Load [three.js + MediaPipe] prerendered static), not browser-verified (no camera/audio/GPU in sandbox). · *(Cycle 408 shipped `566-kids-shake-band`; below.)*

- **[/dream/568-conductor-hands](/dream/568-conductor-hands)** — Conductor's Hands 🫱🎼. `demoable` · Cycle 409. **For**: adult / embodied / time-tension. Raise both hands to the camera and conduct two incommensurable tempi — left hand one voice, right hand another, each snapped to an irrational ratio so the two consonant pulses drift forever and never share a downbeat (metric dissonance in your own arms). Cycle 2 of the 514-polytempo-loom spine, made embodied. MediaPipe-Hands beat-extraction → dual look-ahead schedulers → three.js never-closing Lissajous. Off-glass camera input (NOT a finger), three.js (NOT the banned SVG/Canvas2D/WebGPU). Refs: Nancarrow *Studies for Player Piano* / Ligeti / Reich. Design notes: `src/app/dream/568-conductor-hands/README.md`

## (Cycle 408 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 orthogonal off-glass explorers. Ships **`566-kids-shake-band`**: *shake the tablet like a maraca and a whole Brazilian **batucada** band dances and plays back in time — gentle jiggles ring the chocalho shimmer, big arm-swings bring in the surdo bass drum; five glowing 3D characters bounce + flash on a darkened stage, one per instrument.* The child's **whole arm is the instrument** — off-glass, embodied, joyful. **DeviceMotion shake-onset detection** (`accelerationIncludingGravity` magnitude → adaptive threshold 1.4× rolling-avg, 3.5 m/s² floor, 80 ms refractory; iOS `requestPermission()` inside the Start tap) with intensity tiers (≈3/9/18 m/s²) selecting which of **five from-scratch synth percussion voices** fires (surdo sine 130→42 Hz pitch-drop · caixa bandpass-noise+triangle snap · repique sine+transient · agogô three sine partials · chocalho soft noise grain). A look-ahead **92-BPM groove bed** plays a real 16-step batucada pattern always-on so the child's shakes layer on a musical bed. **three.js stage** — five emissive `IcosahedronGeometry` characters with per-character `PointLight` flash + spring-physics bounce. Master `gain → lowpass(7.5 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−10 dB, 20:1) → destination` brick-wall limiter so even a hard slam can't spike a child's ears. Zero-permission CSS auto-demo bounces the orbs from frame one (audio waits for Start); motion-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + pointer-drag-velocity fallback. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [DeviceMotion onset + percussion pool + groove scheduler + three.js stage + drag fallback = 5] + #3 named ref that DRIVES it [Brazilian batucada bloco — shake-intensity tiers ARE the ensemble's dynamic hierarchy; also Evelyn Glennie's whole-body rhythm]; **#1 NOT claimed** — accelerometer input [417/553] and synth percussion both exist; **#5 NOT claimed** — the dive's bind was to the jury-flagged camera-body lane, deliberately avoided). **device-motion / shake INPUT** (off-glass — distinct from BOTH recent kids inputs: mic @406, tilt @404; the directest finger-reflex dodge) **+ three.js OUTPUT** (NOT SVG [rolling-10 4× → BANNED], NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU [jury-banned]) · **accelerometer-onset→percussion-pool TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics→drone — the banned meta-technique) · **energetic/celebratory/embodied VIBE** (NOT calm-sensory-kids — answers jury #6 directly). **Ideal for Karel's 06:30 mobile review — he can literally shake his phone to play.** Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer fire; siblings **`565-kids-sing-choir`** (mic-sing live pitch → WebGL2 particle creatures → Jacob Collier-style 4-voice live vocal harmonizer; the biggest *concept* — LOST on the diversity gate: mic repeats cycle 406's input) + **`567-kids-color-garden`** (rear-camera dominant-color → WebGL2 instanced glowing plants → Scriabin hue→pitch garden the real world grows; LOST: camera-color adjacent to `368-rainbow-quest` + the trodden camera lane + lighting-fragile) banked IDEAS §408. Extends Karel's loved rhythm/device-play lane (`98-kids-drum-circle`❤️, `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️, `169-kids-marble-run`❤️). Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, 444/444 pages, `/dream/566-kids-shake-band` 5.07 kB / 293 kB First Load [three.js] prerendered static), not browser-verified (no accelerometer/audio/WebGL in sandbox). · *(Cycle 407 shipped `564-piano-splat-cathedral`; below: cycle 405's `557-piano-splat-galaxy`.)*

- **[/dream/566-kids-shake-band](/dream/566-kids-shake-band)** — Kids Shake Band 🥁. `demoable` · Cycle 408. **For**: kids (4+) / off-glass / embodied rhythm. Shake the tablet like a maraca → a Brazilian batucada band of five glowing 3D characters dances and plays percussion in time; gentle jiggle = chocalho shimmer, hard swing = surdo bass drum. DeviceMotion shake-onset → synth percussion pool + 92-BPM batucada groove bed; three.js stage; brick-wall limiter (safe sounds). Off-glass input (NOT a finger), three.js (NOT the banned SVG/Canvas2D/WebGPU). Ref: Brazilian batucada bloco / Evelyn Glennie. Design notes: `src/app/dream/566-kids-shake-band/README.md`

## (Cycle 405 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 approaches to one concept. Ships **`557-piano-splat-galaxy`**: *every note Karel plays BIRTHS a bloom of glowing **Gaussian splats** — his real Welcome Home piano accretes into a living galaxy of light you orbit, where transients are bursts, sustained notes are slow drifting nebula, and pitch paints the color.* The lab's **FIRST Gaussian-splatting renderer** in 553 prototypes (grep-verified: the only prior "splat" is fluid-force injection in `119-poem-fluid`, a categorically different thing). Spectral-flux **onset detection** on his real piano (fetched read-only from `/api/audio/549fc519-…`, with an always-on synth-piano fallback) fires bursts of 80–300 camera-facing billboard quads with a radial Gaussian falloff `exp(-4·r²)`, additively blended (depth test off → order-independent, no sort → robust + glowing), spawned on a growing ring with outward shell velocities + curl-noise turbulence + galactic shear, so a ~13k-splat pool swirls into arms tracing the phrasing. **THE FIRST HONEST 4/5 IN ~8 JURIES** — `ambition: #1 never-used technique (Gaussian-splat / point-based radiance-field rasterization) + #2 ≥3 subsystems (real-audio fetch+FFT+onset/pitch detection / generative bloom particle system / WebGL2 additive splat rasterizer + orbit cam) + #3 named refs (Kerbl/Kopanas/Leimkühler/Drettakis "3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering," SIGGRAPH 2023; the 2026 in-browser-splatting wave; Refik Anadol volumetric data sculptures) + #5 binds a genuinely <14-day finding (BrightCoding "Run Gaussian Splatting in Your Browser with WebGPU," 2026-06-05, 7 days old; corroborated by arxiv cs.GR June 8–12 point-based-radiance-field papers + the DNE×Gracia 4DGS Amy May musical performance)`. Directly serves Karel's directive to use his **real Welcome Home recordings**. **audio-file INPUT** (off-glass — dodges the jury's 7× finger-on-glass ban) **+ WebGL2 splat-rasterization OUTPUT** (NOT Canvas2D, NOT WebGPU — both banned at 4×; a wholly new rendering paradigm for the lab) · **Gaussian-splat radiance-field TECH** (the sound is his RECORDED music driving a renderer — the exact inverse of the banned GPU-physics-sim→drone move) · **volumetric/immersive/Anadol VIBE**. Auto-rotates + auto-blooms hands-free pre-Begin, so the 06:30 glance shows a living galaxy with zero setup. Winner of a DEEP 3-approach fire; siblings **`556-piano-splat-spectrogram`** (3D scrolling spectrogram solid — the legible/analytical variant) + **`558-piano-splat-body`** (a breathing "body of light," tightest bind to the 4DGS Amy May piece — lost as conceptually adjacent to `283-piano-isosurface`) banked IDEAS §405 as the alternate renderers for the new splat spine. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, 441/441 pages, `/dream/557-piano-splat-galaxy` 5.96 kB / 112 kB prerendered), not browser-verified (no GPU/audio in sandbox). · *(Cycle 404 shipped `553-kids-tilt-fountain`; below: cycle 403's `552-dream-house`.)*

- **[/dream/557-piano-splat-galaxy](/dream/557-piano-splat-galaxy)** — Piano Splat Galaxy 🌌. `demoable` · Cycle 405. **For**: adult / immersive / his-real-piano. **The lab's first Gaussian-splatting renderer** — Karel's real piano accretes into a swirling galaxy of glowing Gaussian splats you orbit; onsets bloom, pitch colors, phrasing draws the arms. References: Kerbl et al. "3D Gaussian Splatting" (SIGGRAPH 2023); the 2026 in-browser-splatting wave; Refik Anadol. The first honest 4/5 in ~8 juries (#1+#2+#3+#5). Design notes: `src/app/dream/557-piano-splat-galaxy/README.md`

## (Cycle 403 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 approaches to one concept. Ships **`552-dream-house`**: *the xenharmonic lattice comes ALIVE — a self-composing La Monte Young "Dream House" that slowly walks the just-intonation / Bohlen–Pierce Tonnetz on its own, sustains a pure-ratio drone, drifts forever via the comma pump so it never returns home, and is audibly a different piece at minute 12 than minute 1.* **Cycle 2 of the Xenharmonic Drone spine** (`538-xenharmonic-lattice` = cycle 1, the static tap-a-Tonnetz piece the **JURY 2026-06-12 named: "make it a spine and it's the first 4"**). Where 538 was something you *tap*, 552 is a room you *leave running*: an autonomous walker steps the lattice every 4–8 s biased to unvisited nodes (the **syntonic comma 81/80** guarantees loops never reclose → permanent drift), sustaining the nearest 2–6 nodes as a slow-crossfading **pure-ratio drone** (sine + odd partials + shimmer LFO, multi-second `setTargetAtTime` attacks). The headline is a genuine **long-form (~14-min) arc**: an 8-phase cycle smoothstep-interpolates **density** (2↔6 voices), a **drifting master lowpass** (700↔4800 Hz), a **±0.4-octave register breath**, and a **4-stop tuning migration** (5-JI→Bohlen–Pierce→19-EDO→back). **SVG Tonnetz** with a glowing comet-trail + accumulating visited-ghost dots makes the pilgrimage visible; HUD shows elapsed/phase. **none/autonomous INPUT** (the purest dodge of the jury's 7× finger-on-glass ban, and its #3 "leave-it-running / off-the-glass" dare) **+ SVG OUTPUT** (NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU — both banned at 4×; NOT three.js which 545 just shipped) · **JI-lattice-walk→pure-ratio-drone TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics sim — the banned meta-technique) · **La-Monte-Young / hypnotic-cerebral VIBE** (NOT calm-sensory-kids). Zero permission, runs itself — the strongest 06:30 glance-safety of any recent piece; tap = optional "exhale." **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [walker + crossfading drone synth + long-form arc engine + SVG render = 4] + #3 named refs [La Monte Young & Zazeela *Dream House* / Éliane Radigue / Euler Tonnetz / Bohlen–Pierce / Partch / syntonic comma] + #4 spine cycle 2 — the jury's exact ask; **#1 contestable** [first deliberate long-form >5min stateful piece, but 514/526 arguably already long-form — discounts honestly]; **#5 NOT claimed** — arxiv cs.SD June 5–12 had zero JI/microtonal papers, still server-ML; LIMITER arXiv 2507.08675 is July-2025, used as a *named ref* not a <14-day bind). Winner of a DEEP 3-steering fire; siblings **`550-xenharmonic-pilgrimage`** (voice-steered: autocorrelation pitch → nearest-node pull — lost: detector flagged basic + less glance-robust) + **`551-tilt-pilgrimage`** (tilt-steered: iOS deviceorientation → fifths/thirds drift bias + desktop tilt-pad — lost: needs permission + a real device) banked IDEAS §403 as the cycle-3 grab-the-wheel layers. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, 439/439 pages, `.next/.../552-dream-house/page.js` prerendered), not browser-verified (no audio device in sandbox). · *(Cycles 402 & 401 shipped `549-kids-song-friend` and `545-motion-loom` — see entries below.)* · Earlier header (Cycle 400 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 approaches to one concept. Ships **`541-kids-liquid-light`**: *a 4-year-old finger-paints a dark pool of living liquid light, and the liquid SINGS as it swirls.* A **WebGPU stable-fluids** (Jos Stam, SIGGRAPH 1999) solver built as a **kids sensory instrument** — drag injects swirling dye into a real GPU fluid sim (192² `rgba16float` grid, 28-iter Jacobi pressure solve, divergence-free projection), and swirl-speed→lowpass brightness + vertical-position→**C-pentatonic** register (C3–C5) make it sing, nothing ever wrong. **touch/drag INPUT + WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (0× in the kids rolling-10 → the diversifying renderer; a Canvas2D curl-noise "lite fluid" fallback is baked in for WebGPU-absent iPads) · **luminous / sensory / calm VIBE** — distinct from the recent joyful-awe (537) / prosocial (505) / aurora (500) / feeling-map (493) kids cluster. Extends Karel's loved fluid/particle/glow lane (`84-wave-fluid`❤️, `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️, `236-particle-life-song`❤️, `262-aurora-particle`❤️). Always-on C2+G2 pad; kids-safe `gain → lowpass(≤8k) → DynamicsCompressor(−6 dB, 20:1) → destination`; ghost-finger auto-demo swirls + sings from frame one. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems + #3 Stam 1999 / Dobryakov WebGL-Fluid lineage + #4 "Liquid Light" spine cycle 1; #1 NOT claimed — GPU fluid exists; #5 NOT claimed — cs.SD is server-ML). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-renderer fire; siblings **`542-kids-liquid-light-gl`** (WebGL2 ping-pong stable-fluids w/ vorticity confinement — lost: WebGL2 over-represented) + **`543-kids-liquid-light-flow`** (Canvas2D ~6500-particle advection — lost: redundant with the winner's own fallback) banked IDEAS §400. Build-verified (`npm run build` exit 0, 436/436 pages, `/dream/541-kids-liquid-light` 7.13 kB / 110 kB), not browser-verified (no WebGPU/audio in sandbox). · *(Cycles 399 & 398 shipped `538-xenharmonic-lattice` and `537-kids-sky-murmuration` — see entries below.)* · Earlier header (Cycle 397 — **adult** build · WIDE, 3 divergent explorers. Ships **`532-vocoder-veil`**: *you sing or speak, and your words come out played by Karel's REAL Welcome Home piano — your voice as the shape, his recorded piano as the voice.* The lab's **FIRST channel vocoder** (grep-verified: `vocoder` 0× in built prototypes), a classic 16-band Homer-Dudley analysis/synthesis filterbank — the live mic (modulator) drives the per-band gains of Karel's actual piano recording (carrier, fetched read-only from `/api/audio/549fc519-…`), so the piano "talks" in your formants. **Directly serves Karel's standing directive to use his real Welcome Home recordings.** **mic-voice INPUT + WebGL2 OUTPUT** (the scarcest renderer at 1×→2×) · uncanny-intimate, responsive — dodges the rolling-10 banned "solitary-never-resolves" VIBE (the piano *responds* in real time) AND the 4× renderer trap (Canvas2D/WebGPU/SVG all 3×; ships on WebGL2). A no-permission vowel auto-demo (ah→ee→oh→oo→mm) makes the piano sing + the 16-rung ladder animate from frame one; degrades to a synth carrier if the fetch fails and a Canvas2D ladder if no WebGL2. The three WIDE explorers spanned WebGL2/three.js/audio-first, none pushing a 3× renderer to 4×. 2 siblings banked IDEAS §397: **`533-hollow`** (three.js comb-filter / phase-cancellation tension — "tension is *absence*", the jury-named untouched primitive; Lucier/Amacher) + **`534-third-ear`** (combination/difference *Tartini* tones — a melody that exists only inside your ear; audio-first; clean lab-first; Tartini/Amacher). · *Previous (Cycle 396 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 approaches to one concept. Ships **`529-kids-doodle-choir`**: *a 4-year-old draws a simple doodle; a real in-browser neural net (TensorFlow.js + DoodleNet) recognizes WHAT they drew; the drawing comes alive — animates and SINGS its own warm motif — and joins a growing choir of singing creatures.* The lab's **FIRST TensorFlow.js / ML-recognition piece** (grep-verified: 0× `@tensorflow`/`tfjs` across ~360 prototypes — the single cleanest unused *major* technique left), on Karel's **loved draw/paint lane** (100/104/152/153❤️), in a JOYFUL register that dodges the now-4× "solitary-never-resolves" VIBE AND the WebGPU 4× renderer trap. **finger-drawing INPUT + Canvas2D OUTPUT** · TF.js DoodleNet (QuickDraw 345-class CNN) → 8 storybook archetypes, each with its own animation + a C-pentatonic motif so nothing sounds wrong. A scripted ghost-finger **auto-demo bypasses the classifier** (draws + sings on load even if the model never loads); a no-ML **geometric heuristic fallback** keeps it playable offline. 2 DEEP siblings banked IDEAS §396: **`530-kids-sketch-garden`** (no-ML geometric stroke classifier → WebGL2 glowing particle creatures — bulletproof offline; fold its glow into 529) + **`531-kids-drawing-band`** (same DoodleNet, but each doodle joins a tempo-locked 96-BPM groove — tied on ambition, lost on the saturated kids loop/band lane). · *Previous (Cycle 395, adult WIDE):* Ships **`526-jazz-room`**: *a living, self-playing late-night jazz trio — walking upright bass, Bill Evans rootless piano comping, brushed drums — that performs a full arc (head → piano solo → bass solo → trade-fours → head-out) over an F Jazz Blues, with you free to "sit in."* The lab's **FIRST jazz piece** (grep-verified: zero jazz/swing/bebop/walking-bass folders in 350 prototypes) and an alternate dramatic arc to Resonance's psychedelic 6-phase journey engine — directly serving Karel's explicit **priority #4** (journey-engine alternatives: "jazz responsive"). **autonomous-arc INPUT + Canvas2D OUTPUT** · swinging-warm-noir — dodges BOTH rolling-10 banned VIBE poles (solitary-never-resolves 4× AND tension-resolved-through-action 4×: a jazz arc simply *plays*, neither an empty drone nor a tension-puzzle). The three WIDE explorers spanned Canvas2D/SVG/WebGL2, deliberately NONE on the already-3× WebGPU. The trio plays itself from the first frame, so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a swinging, glowing scene. 2 siblings banked IDEAS §395: **`527-cipher-score`** (SVG type-text→living-score, Messiaen letter-cipher) + **`528-market-veil`** (WebGL2 live-crypto→ambient-drone — lost on a `502-atmosphere-veil` overlap + Binance US geo-block).))

- **[/dream/552-dream-house](/dream/552-dream-house)** — Dream House 🛕🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 403. **For**: adult / leave-it-running / xenharmonic, hypnotic.

  **The xenharmonic lattice comes alive — a self-composing La Monte Young "Dream House" that walks the just-intonation Tonnetz on its own and is a different piece at minute 12 than minute 1.** Cycle 2 of the **Xenharmonic Drone** spine (extends `538-xenharmonic-lattice`; the JURY 2026-06-12 named 538: *"make it a spine and it's the first 4"*). An **autonomous walker** steps the 2-D lattice (u=fifths 3/2, v=thirds 5/4) every 4–8 s, biased to unvisited nodes; the **syntonic comma (81/80 ≈ 21.5¢)** means loops never reclose, so the pitch drifts forever ("never returns home"). Sound = the walker's node + its 2–5 nearest neighbors held as a **sustained pure-ratio chord** at EXACT rational ratios (sine + 3rd/5th odd partials + a ~0.07 Hz detune shimmer LFO), all attacks/releases via `setTargetAtTime` (3–4 s) so chords never hard-start. **The long-form arc** (the headline): an 8-phase ~14-min cycle (gathering→ascending→flowering→tritave→radiance→dissolving→receding→stillness) smoothstep-interpolates density (2↔6 voices), a drifting master lowpass (700↔4800 Hz), a ±0.4-octave register breath, and a 4-stop tuning migration (5-JI→Bohlen–Pierce→19-EDO→back) — so the harmonic world itself changes. **SVG Tonnetz** render: nodes glow via `feGaussianBlur`, a **comet-trail** polyline + accumulating dim visited-ghost dots make the journey visible; viewBox pans to follow the walker; rAF mutates SVG attributes directly (no per-frame React churn); HUD shows tuning, root ratio, voice count, **elapsed clock + phase word**. Optional **tap = "exhale"** (fade current chord, bloom a fresh region); doing nothing is the default. Master `gain → drifting lowpass → DynamicsCompressor(20:1, −10 dBFS) → destination`; per-voice gain `0.55/voiceCount`, 8-voice hard cap; AudioContext in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + SVG — no mic, camera, network, secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **La Monte Young & Marian Zazeela, *Dream House*** (sustained just-intonation drone installation, 1993–present, MELA Foundation NYC) — primary; **Éliane Radigue** (long-form drone); **Euler's *Tonnetz*** (1739); the **syntonic comma / comma pump**; **Bohlen–Pierce** (Bohlen 1972 / Pierce 1984); **Harry Partch**, *Genesis of a Music* (1949); LIMITER (arXiv 2507.08675, 2025) as a recent gamified-JI-interface reference. Born from RESEARCH §403 (the honest read — arxiv cs.SD June 5–12 2026 has zero JI/microtonal/pitch papers, still server-ML, so #5 is unclaimable; the jury named 538 to spine-ify and dared the "leave-it-running / off-the-glass" lane). **Ambition 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (walker + crossfading drone synth + long-form arc engine + SVG render = 4) + **#3** named refs above + **#4** multi-cycle (Xenharmonic Drone spine cycle 2 — the jury's exact ask). (**#1 contestable** — first deliberate long-form >5min stateful piece, but 514/526 arguably already long-form, so it discounts to 3/5 honestly. **#5 NOT claimed** — cs.SD is server-ML; LIMITER is July-2025; said plainly. No clean #1 in any steering approach — Web MIDI is in 12 files, tilt/voice well-used.) **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-12 rolling-15) = [WebGPU-compute OUTPUT 4× · Canvas2D OUTPUT 4× · GPU-physics-sim→drone TECH 4× · finger-on-glass INPUT 7× · calm-sensory-kids VIBE 4×] · picked(552) = [**none/autonomous INPUT** (off-glass — the purest dodge of the 7× finger reflex + the jury's #3 dare; "none" only 3× in window) · **SVG OUTPUT** (NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU; NOT three.js which 545 just shipped) · **JI-lattice-walk→pure-ratio-drone TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics sim) · **La-Monte-Young/hypnotic-cerebral VIBE** (NOT calm-sensory-kids)]` — clean on every jury ban; takes the jury's #3 "leave it running, off the glass" dare head-on. **Love signal:** none directly applicable (cerebral drone lane); the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. **Winner of a DEEP 3-steering fire** (one living-Dream-House concept via three off-glass steering attacks): siblings `550-xenharmonic-pilgrimage` (voice-steered — autocorrelation pitch → nearest-node pull; the most musical, lost: detector flagged basic + less glance-robust) + `551-tilt-pilgrimage` (tilt-steered — iOS deviceorientation → fifths/thirds bias + desktop tilt-pad; the most physical, lost: needs permission + a real device) banked IDEAS §403 as the cycle-3 grab-the-wheel layers. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` with only the winner — exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 29.5s`, `✓ Generating static pages (439/439)`, `.next/server/app/dream/552-dream-house/page.js` prerendered), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio device in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether minute 12 *perceptually* differs from minute 1 over a real 14-min listen (the arc is structurally sound; HUD phase-word is the short-glance crutch); long-run gain/limiter interaction over 20+ min (mathematically capped, not stress-tested); iOS AudioContext suspend on screen-lock (`ctx.resume()` tap-to-resume not yet wired — the builder's flagged risk); extreme octave-folded frequencies at far lattice positions (clamped, perceptual balance unauditioned). Because it's fully autonomous with zero permission, the 06:30 glance reliably shows a drifting, glowing, singing pilgrimage with no setup.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/552-dream-house/README.md`

- **[/dream/541-kids-liquid-light](/dream/541-kids-liquid-light)** — Liquid Light 💧✨. `demoable` · Cycle 400. **For**: kids (4+) / iPad / sensory, calm.

  **A 4-year-old finger-paints a dark pool of living liquid light — and the liquid SINGS as it swirls.** Drag a finger and you inject swirling colored dye into a *real fluid simulation*; the speed and direction of the swirl drive a warm, ever-evolving pentatonic wash. No goal, no words, no wrong move — infinite calm sensory play. The primary renderer is a real-time **stable-fluids (Jos Stam, SIGGRAPH 1999)** solver running entirely on the GPU via **WebGPU compute shaders (WGSL)**: a velocity field + colored dye field on a 192×192 `rgba16float` grid, stepped each frame through add-forces (finger impulse + Gaussian dye injection) → semi-Lagrangian advect velocity → divergence → **28-iter Jacobi pressure solve** (ping-pong) → subtract-gradient (divergence-free projection) → advect dye, then a full-screen-triangle render pipeline tone-maps the dye to luminous glow on dark. **Sonification:** swirl-speed → lowpass brightness + simultaneous voice count; vertical position → **C-major pentatonic** register (C3–C5, nothing ever wrong); additive triangle/sine wash with `setTargetAtTime` glides; always-on C2+G2 breathing pad (never silent); kids-safe master `gain → lowpass(≤8 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−6 dB, 20:1 brick-wall) → destination`; AudioContext built inside the Start gesture (iOS unlock; `window.AudioContext || webkitAudioContext`). **Always-on auto-demo:** a hands-free "ghost finger" wanders a Lissajous path injecting dye + sound from frame one (cancels on first pointer-down, resumes after ~4 s idle) so the 06:30 glance always shows swirling, singing light. **Graceful degradation:** `navigator.gpu` missing / adapter-device-context failure → a Canvas2D curl-noise **"lite fluid"** (a few thousand additively-blended dye particles over a fading dark trail) + a `text-rose-300` "Playing in lite mode ✨" notice — still swirls, still sings (this is the safety net for iPads where Safari WebGPU is recent/uneven). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + WebGPU/Canvas — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). **No new npm deps.**

  References: **Jos Stam, "Stable Fluids," SIGGRAPH 1999** (the advection + Jacobi pressure-projection method implemented here); lineage note to **Pavel Dobryakov's WebGL-Fluid-Simulation** (the luminous dye-on-dark interaction, reimplemented on raw WebGPU compute). Born from **RESEARCH §400** (the dive's honest read — cs.SD this week is server-side ML so #5 is unclaimable, and a grep audit confirmed tension/time/tuning + the easy kids #1s are spent at 540 prototypes; the actionable pivot is "the biggest love-aligned sensory lane on the scarcest kids renderer"). **Ambition 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (WebGPU stable-fluids solver + Canvas2D fallback fluid + swirl→pentatonic sonification engine + ghost-finger auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named ref (Stam 1999 / Dobryakov) + **#4** multi-cycle ("Liquid Light" spine cycle 1 — cycle 2: two-finger dye-mixing duet + a longer-form settling pool). (**#1 NOT cleanly claimed** — GPU fluid already exists in the lab; the fresh element is *a WebGPU-compute fluid as a kids sensory instrument* — a context/audience, not a 0× primitive — honest. **#5 NOT claimed** — cs.SD is server-ML; said plainly.) **Diversity** `banned/over-rep (kids rolling-10)=[Canvas2D OUTPUT trending toward 4× (505/493/489)] · picked=[**touch/drag INPUT** (the core kids verb) · **WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (0× in the kids window → the diversifying renderer; Canvas2D fallback only when WebGPU absent) · **GPU stable-fluids → swirl-driven-pentatonic TECH** (not a roughness/DSP-primitive farm) · **luminous/sensory/calm VIBE** — distinct from the recent joyful-awe/prosocial/aurora/feeling-map cluster]` — no over-represented tag in the picked set. **Love signal:** directly extends Karel's loved fluid/particle/glow lane (`84-wave-fluid`❤️, `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️, `236-particle-life-song`❤️, `262-aurora-particle`❤️); additive — the kids/ambition/diversity gates governed. **Winner of a kids DEEP 3-renderer fire** (one "singing liquid light" concept on three renderers): siblings `542-kids-liquid-light-gl` (WebGL2 ping-pong stable-fluids with vorticity confinement + cheap bloom — complete + clean, lost on the diversity audit: WebGL2 at 2× in the kids window) + `543-kids-liquid-light-flow` (Canvas2D 56×56 velocity grid + ~6500 additive particles + curl-noise ambient — best legibility/compatibility, lost as essentially the winner's own fallback) banked IDEAS §400. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` with only the winner — exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 36.6s`, `✓ Generating static pages (436/436)`, `/dream/541-kids-liquid-light 7.13 kB / 110 kB First Load` prerendered static; one real ESLint fix on build: a local named `module` → `shaderModule` per `@next/next/no-assign-module-variable`), **NOT browser-verified** (no WebGPU/audio device in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the WGSL compute/render bind-group layout compiles + runs on a real WebGPU adapter (binding validation is strict; the Canvas2D fallback covers WebGPU-absent AND any first-run WGSL hiccup); iOS Safari WebGPU presence/stability on Karel's iPad (fallback covers absent); audio balance (pad vs wash) + limiter feel behind the −6 dB brick-wall; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock; ghost-finger pacing on a real device. The ghost-finger auto-demo + Canvas2D fallback are the safety nets so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows swirling, singing light.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/541-kids-liquid-light/README.md`

- **[/dream/532-vocoder-veil](/dream/532-vocoder-veil)** — Vocoder Veil 🎙️🎹. `demoable` · Cycle 397. **For**: adult / live, intimate, voice-through-piano.

  **You sing or speak, and your words come out played by Karel's real Welcome Home piano — your voice as the shape, his recorded piano as the voice.** The lab's **first channel vocoder** (grep-verified: `vocoder` 0× in built prototypes — only an INDEX mention). A classic Homer-Dudley **16-band analysis/synthesis filterbank**: the live mic (modulator) is split into 16 log-spaced bandpass bands (120 Hz–7 kHz); each band's energy is followed (squared via a `WaveShaperNode` x² → smoothed by a ~20 Hz lowpass) and that audio-rate envelope is connected straight into the `.gain` AudioParam of the matching band on the **carrier — Karel's actual piano**, fetched read-only from `/api/audio/549fc519-f7fc-4c38-a771-adaad2edbc81` (the confirmed WH track id used by 283/165) and looped. Highpassed noise is injected into the carrier so sibilants read. The piano *talks in your formants.* **Serves Karel's standing directive to use his real Welcome Home recordings** (carried open for many cycles). A **WebGL2 band-ladder** (16 glowing rungs) animates from the envelopes. **No-permission auto-demo:** a built-in vowel-cycle modulator (saw + peaking formant filters, ah→ee→oh→oo→mm) makes the piano sing vowels + the ladder animate from frame one. **Three-layer degradation:** mic denied → `text-rose-300` note + auto-demo continues; piano fetch fails → `text-amber-300` note + a 6-voice synth carrier (still fully works); no WebGL2 → Canvas2D ladder. Master `gain → lowpass(7.5 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−6 dB, 16:1) → destination`; `setTargetAtTime` params; AudioContext in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). **No API route, no `guard`** (only network is the read-only same-origin `/api/audio/[id]` GET + the mic — no writes, no secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Homer Dudley** — the Bell Labs *Vocoder* (1939), the original channel vocoder · **Wendy Carlos & Robert Moog** — vocoder on *A Clockwork Orange* (1972) · **Imogen Heap** — "Hide and Seek" (2005), the intimate-vocoder model. Born from RESEARCH §397 (the dive's honest cs.SD meta-finding — this week's real-time music ML is server-side MAX/MSP + Python inference, so #5 is unclaimable — pivoted to the ambition manual's named-but-untouched, client-feasible DSP lane). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (channel vocoder — grep-verified lab-first, 0× built) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (mic analysis filterbank + Karel's-piano carrier bank + audio-rate envelope-follower modulation + WebGL2 ladder + vowel auto-demo = 5) + **#3** named refs above. (#4 NOT claimed — single-cycle, opens a "his-real-music / cross-synthesis" spine. **#5 NOT claimed** — cs.SD this week is server-ML; said plainly.) **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4×) = ["solitary-never-resolves" VIBE (520/518/514/513/502)] · also-avoid (would→4×) = [Canvas2D/WebGPU/SVG OUTPUT, all 3×] · picked=[**mic-voice INPUT** (fresh; not the touch/draw/tilt comfort reach) · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (the scarcest renderer, 1×→2×; the three explorers spanned WebGL2/three.js/audio-first, none pushing a 3× renderer to 4×) · **channel-vocoder TECH** (lab-first) · **uncanny-intimate / responsive-playful VIBE** (NOT the banned solitary drone — the piano *responds* to your voice in real time)]` — dodges the banned VIBE AND the 4× renderer trap. **Love signal:** aligned via Karel's repeated "use my real recordings" directive + paths interest; additive — the ambition/diversity gates governed. **Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer adult fire** (three orthogonal directions on three renderers): siblings **`533-hollow`** (three.js comb-filter / phase-cancellation tension — "tension is *absence*", the jury-named untouched primitive; a drone carved by two stacked feedforward combs, notches swept through a 2-D field, a cylindrical shell hollowing at analytic notch positions; Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* / Amacher; ambition 3/5 with a *scoped* #1) + **`534-third-ear`** (combination/difference *Tartini* tones — two sines whose difference traces a pentatonic melody that exists only inside the listener's ear; audio-first / minimal Canvas2D, with an honest phantom-only/reinforced A/B toggle; Tartini 1714 / Amacher; clean lab-first, ambition 2/5) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §397. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` with only the winner — exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 67s`, `/dream/532-vocoder-veil` prerendered static 6.48 kB / 109 kB; one TS fix on build: `Float32Array<ArrayBuffer>` generic for the `WaveShaperNode.curve`), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio/mic/WebGL2/network in this sandbox) — unverified surface: **envelope-follower scaling** (the x²-smoothed band envelopes may sit ~0–0.1 and not drive the carrier gains to unity — the vocoder could read too quiet on a real voice without a scale factor; the hand-tuned vowel auto-demo likely masks this; the single most important live check); the `/api/audio/[id]` `decodeAudioData` + CORS behind the CDN (any exception drops to the synth carrier, still playable); mic permission + iOS AudioContext-in-gesture + `webkitAudioContext`; WebGL2 on old Android WebView (Canvas2D ladder covers it). The vowel auto-demo + synth carrier + Canvas2D fallbacks are the safety nets so absent mic/piano/WebGL2 never blocks the 06:30 glance.

- **[/dream/529-kids-doodle-choir](/dream/529-kids-doodle-choir)** — Doodle Choir ✏️🎶. `demoable` · Cycle 396. **For**: kids / iPad / draw-and-sing.

  **A 4-year-old draws a simple doodle with their finger; a real in-browser neural net recognizes WHAT they drew; the drawing comes alive — animates and SINGS its own warm little motif — and stays on the page as part of a growing, gently-looping choir of singing creatures.** "Quick, Draw! that sings." The lab's **first use of TensorFlow.js / any ML recognition** in ~360 prototypes (grep-verified: zero `@tensorflow`/`tfjs`) — the single cleanest unused *major* technique left, and an explicit counts-as-#1 in the ambition manual. TF.js loads via **CDN dynamic import** (`@tensorflow/tfjs@4/+esm`, `webpackIgnore` so Next never bundles it — the `493`/MediaPipe pattern, no npm dep); the drawing is rasterized to 28×28 grayscale and run through **DoodleNet** (yining1023 — a CNN trained on Google's Quick, Draw! 345-category dataset, ported to TF.js), and the top-1 class maps to one of **8 storybook archetypes** — sun (rises & pulses), fish (wiggles), bird (flutters), plant (sways), cloud (drifts), star (twinkles), critter (bounces), home (breathes) — each with a distinct warm motif in **C major / C pentatonic** so no two creatures ever clash. Each keeps its faint original stroke and loops its motif into the choir. A scripted **ghost-finger auto-demo** traces sun→fish→bird→plant→star→critter→cloud→home on load, **bypassing the async classifier** (it assigns the archetype directly), so a hands-free 06:30 glance ALWAYS shows animated, singing creatures even if the model never loads. **Two-layer graceful degradation:** TF.js/model fail → a hand-coded **geometric heuristic** (bounding box / ink density / aspect) keeps it fully playable with a `text-rose-300` "◈ shape matching" badge; Canvas2D absent → a notice. Master `gain → lowpass(≤7.8 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−6 dB, 20:1)`; `setTargetAtTime` envelopes; AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + Canvas2D; only network is the public TF.js CDN + DoodleNet model — no mic, camera, secrets, writes). No new npm deps.

  References: **Google *Quick, Draw!*** (Jongejan, Ha, et al., 2016) — the dataset/game this classifier descends from · **DoodleNet** (yining1023) — the TF.js port of a QuickDraw-trained CNN. Born from RESEARCH §396 (the dive's honest cs.SD meta-finding — this week's audio ML is server-side diffusion only, so #5 is unclaimable — pivoted to the ambition manual's named-but-untouched **TF.js realtime-inference** lane, with DoodleNet supplying the model). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (TensorFlow.js realtime ML inference — grep-verified lab-first, 0× in ~360 prototypes) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (TF.js/DoodleNet classifier + heuristic fallback + Web-Audio choir + Canvas2D storybook + ghost-finger auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named refs above. (#4 NOT claimed — single-cycle, opens a "drawing-comes-alive / browser-ML" spine. **#5 NOT claimed** — DoodleNet is foundational ml5-era, not a <14-day finding; said plainly.) **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4×) = [WebGPU OUTPUT would→4× (520/518/502) · "solitary-never-resolves" VIBE 4× (520/518/514/513/502)] · picked=[**finger-drawing INPUT** (touch ~2×, not banned; the core kids verb + Karel's loved lane) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (2×→3×, not banned; not WebGPU, not the 3× SVG) · **TF.js semantic sketch-recognition TECH** (lab-first; adds *meaning*, not a roughness/DSP-primitive farm) · **joyful drawings-come-alive-and-sing VIBE** (NEITHER banned pole — pure accumulative delight, the deliberate JOYFUL swing like the loved 524)]` — dodges the banned never-resolves VIBE AND the WebGPU trap. **Love signal:** strongly aligned — Karel loves the draw/paint lane (`100-kids-paint-song`❤️, `104-kids-mirror-draw`❤️, `152-kids-star-paint`❤️, `153-paint-compose`❤️); 529 is "your drawing comes alive" + the lab's first ML; additive — the ambition/diversity/kids gates governed. **Winner of a DEEP 3-approach kids fire** (one concept — draw→recognized→comes-alive-and-sings — via three classifier×renderer×form attacks): siblings **`530-kids-sketch-garden`** (no-ML geometric stroke classifier → WebGL2 additive-glow particle creatures; Patatap / $1-Recognizer; ambition 2/5 — forgoes the TF.js #1; the bulletproof-offline approach, fold its glow into 529) + **`531-kids-drawing-band`** (same DoodleNet, but each doodle joins a tempo-locked 96-BPM groove on one shared lookahead scheduler; ambition 3/5 — **tied** with the winner, lost on the saturated kids loop/band lane) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §396. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` with only the winner — exit 0, `/dream/529-kids-doodle-choir` prerendered static; `webpackIgnore` confirmed on the TF.js CDN import, no `use*`-named helpers, `'use client'` present), **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/audio/network in this sandbox) — unverified surface: the DoodleNet model URL is community-documented / best-effort (if it 404s the piece runs in heuristic "◈ shape matching" mode — still playable; the auto-demo is unaffected); the 345-label list is from community docs not the model binary; whether a 4yo's rough 28×28 doodle classifies well enough that recognition feels *magic* vs *random* (all-pentatonic motifs mean a misread still sounds good, but the "it knew it was a bird!" delight is unverified); iOS AudioContext-in-gesture + `webkitAudioContext` on iOS <15; TF.js CDN reachability under the env network policy. The ghost-finger auto-demo + heuristic fallback are the safety nets so absent model/CDN never blocks the 06:30 glance.

- **[/dream/526-jazz-room](/dream/526-jazz-room)** — Jazz Room 🎷. `demoable` · Cycle 395. **For**: adult / warm, swinging, live.

  **A living, self-playing late-night jazz trio — walking upright bass, Bill Evans rootless piano comping, and brushed drums — that performs a complete jazz arc over an F Jazz Blues, with you free to "sit in."** The lab's **first jazz piece** and an alternate dramatic arc to Resonance's psychedelic 6-phase journey engine (Karel **priority #4**: journey-engine alternatives — "jazz responsive" is named in the directive). A real **generative jazz-theory engine** (`jazz.ts`, 391 lines): F Jazz Blues 12-bar changes (F7–B♭7–…–D7–Gm7–C7), **Bill Evans Type A/B rootless voicings** (3-7-9 / 7-9-3 shells) with distance-minimizing voice-leading, a four-beat **walking bass** (root→5th→3rd→chromatic-approach-on-beat-4), **2:1 swing**. A **lookahead scheduler** (25 ms interval, 120 ms horizon off `audioCtx.currentTime`) drives plucked-string bass, an FM Rhodes-ish comping voice, and brushed drums (filtered-noise brushes + sine-sweep kick + ride). An autonomous **arc state-machine** — head → piano solo → bass solo → trade-fours → head-out (~5–7 min), tempo/density shifting per phase so minute 3 ≠ minute 1. A **Canvas2D** smoky-club scene (deep brown-black, warm amber spotlight, three glowing bass/piano/drum presences that pulse + radiate rings on note onsets, drifting smoke, chord + phase HUD) plays from the first frame; optional **sit-in** keyboard (9 F-blues keys) adds a melody voice. AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock); `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−6 dB / 20:1). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + Canvas2D — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Bill Evans' rootless left-hand voicings** (the "Type A / Type B" 3-note shell voicings, documented by Mark Levine in *The Jazz Piano Book*) — the backbone of the piano comping · **Jamey Aebersold play-along tradition** — the autonomous rhythm section you solo over. **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (generative jazz combo / rootless-voicing + walking-bass engine — grep-verified lab-first across 350 prototypes) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (jazz-theory engine + multi-voice synth + arc state-machine + Canvas2D viz = 4) + **#3** named refs above. (#4 NOT claimed — single-cycle; seeds a "Genre Journey-Arc" spine. **#5 NOT claimed** — no <14-day bindable finding; frontier is server-ML + WebGPU, said plainly.) **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4×) = [WebGPU OUTPUT would→4× (520/518/502) · solitary-never-resolves VIBE 4× (520/518/514/513) · tension-resolved-through-action VIBE 4× (508/505/500/496)] · picked=[**autonomous-arc + optional-touch INPUT** · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (2× — not banned; the trio of explorers covered Canvas2D/SVG/WebGL2, none on the 3× WebGPU) · **generative-jazz-voice-leading TECH** (lab-first) · **swinging-warm-noir autonomous-genre-performance VIBE** (NEITHER banned pole) · **ONE living trio you drop into CONCEPT**]` — dodges both banned VIBE poles AND the WebGPU trap. (Honest note: the JURY 2026-06-11 *text* banned Canvas2D when it was 6× in its window; it has since fallen to 2× in the governing rolling-10 — one jazz piece after that drought is diversification, not the renderer-migration the jury decried.) **Love signal:** no jazz/genre loves exist; `106-beat-cut`❤️ + `172-loop-station`❤️ (groove/rhythm) softly back an autonomous groove performance; additive — the diversity + ambition gates + Karel's #4 governed. **Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer adult fire** (three orthogonal autonomous genre/narrative arcs on three renderers): siblings **`527-cipher-score`** (SVG type-text→a-living-performed-score via a Messiaen-style letter→pitch+duration cipher; the most delightful + the starved keyboard-text input, lost on ambition 2/5) + **`528-market-veil`** (WebGL2 live-crypto-markets→an-evolving-ambient-drone; lost because its live-data sustained-drone concept *and `-veil` name* overlap `502-atmosphere-veil`, and Binance geo-blocks US → would run SIMULATED for Karel) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §395. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 77s`, `/dream/526-jazz-room` prerendered static **7.19 kB / 110 kB First Load**, route in `app-path-routes-manifest.json`; one harmless `beatDur`-unused ESLint warning removed), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio/Canvas in this sandbox) — unverified surface: the FM Rhodes timbre + exact swing *feel* at 132 BPM (never heard — may need a `modGain`/swing micro-tune); the `chooseSmoothVoicing` metric uses note-index not true SATB voice assignment (voice-leading is approximately, not strictly, smooth); whether the 5-phase arc reads as a *performance* vs a loop on a phone glance; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock + a `ctx2d.scale()`-on-resize compounding the builder flagged. All-synthesis/no-network means it ALWAYS plays on load — the safety net for the 06:30 glance.
