## (Cycle 394 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 renderers / one concept. Ships **`524-kids-hand-firebird`**: *hold up your hand and it becomes a glowing firebird of light — ~2000 GPU particles gather around your fingers, bloom wide and SING when you open your hand, and gather into a quiet ember when you close it.* The lab's **first MediaPipe Hands** (21-landmark hand-tracking; Pose existed at `493`, Hands never did) driving a **raw WebGL2** additive particle creature. **hand-tracking INPUT + WebGL2 OUTPUT** · joyful embodied cause-effect (open=bloom=sing, close=ember) — deliberately swings OFF the never-resolves contemplative VIBE that hit 4× in the rolling-10, and off the last two kids cycles' hushed single-presences (513/518). WebGL2 is the scarcest screen renderer in the rolling-10 (1×), so this is the one pick that doesn't push an already-3× renderer to 4×; extends Karel's loved particle lane (`130`/`236`/`262`❤️). Scripted **virtual hand** auto-demo blooms + sings **on load** with no camera, and a Canvas2D glow fallback covers no-WebGL2/no-camera, so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a singing creature. 2 siblings banked IDEAS §394: **`523-kids-hand-puppet`** (Canvas2D shadow-dog — best literal-4yo legibility) + **`525-kids-hand-choir`** (SVG 5-fingertip choir — open=chord, close=unison-hug; cleverest concept, perf-flagged).)

- **[/dream/524-kids-hand-firebird](/dream/524-kids-hand-firebird)** — Hand Firebird 🔥🕊. `demoable` · Cycle 394. **For**: kids 4+ / joyful, embodied.

  **Hold up your hand and it becomes a glowing firebird of light — a creature of ~2000 additively-blended GPU particles that gathers around your fingers, blooms wide and SINGS when you open your hand, and gathers into a quiet ember when you close it.** The lab's **first MediaPipe Hands** (21-landmark hand-tracking — the lab used MediaPipe *Pose* once at `493-kids-feeling-forest`, but **never the Hands model**; grep-clean before this). A **raw WebGL2** particle system (2000 points, additive blending, comet trails via a fade-feedback pass) is attracted to the live hand landmarks: **openness** (avg fingertip distance from palm, normalized by hand span) → bloom width + sung-voice trigger + brightness; **wrist height** → pitch (up = higher, quantized to C-major **pentatonic** so nothing is wrong); **finger spread** → scatter width + harmonics; **movement speed** → sparkle/trail intensity. Warm pentatonic Web-Audio voice + an always-on detuned-sine ambient pad (never silent); master through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−6 dB / 20:1) + ≤8 kHz lowpass (kids-safe), all `setTargetAtTime` envelopes. A **scripted virtual hand** (fist→open-bloom→close→flutter, ~8 s loop) drives the firebird + voice **on load before any camera permission** and resumes after 3.5 s of no hand — so a 06:30 hands-free phone glance always shows a glowing, singing creature with zero interaction and zero camera. Camera denied / MediaPipe-CDN-fails / no WebGL2 → `text-rose-300` notice + a Canvas2D glow fallback that still sings + the auto-demo continues. AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + WebGL2; camera is local hand-landmark analysis only — never recorded/uploaded; no network beyond the MediaPipe model CDN, no secrets). **No new npm deps** — MediaPipe via CDN dynamic ESM import (the `493`/`444` pattern), raw WebGL2, no three.js.

  References: **Zhang, Bazarevsky, Vakunov, Tkachenka, Sung, Chang, Grundmann** — "MediaPipe Hands: On-device Real-time Hand Tracking" (arXiv 2006.10214; the 21-keypoint in-browser hand pipeline) · the **luminous particle-creature lineage** — thatgamecompany *Journey* (cloth-light beings, 2012) + Memo Akten's particle-field works. **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (**MediaPipe Hands / HandLandmarker** — grep-verified lab-first; Pose exists at 493, Hands does not) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (hand-landmark tracking + WebGL2 GPU particle field + gesture→pentatonic synth + virtual-hand auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named refs above. (#4 NOT claimed — single-cycle; seeds a "Hand-as-creature / embodied" spine. **#5 NOT claimed** — cs.SD this week is server-ML only, said plainly.) **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4×) = [one-solitary-presence / never-resolves contemplative VIBE (520/518/514/513)] · picked=[**hand-tracking INPUT** (lab-first, non-touch/non-tilt) · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (scarcest screen renderer in the rolling-10 at 1× — the only pick that doesn't inflate an already-3× Canvas2D/SVG to 4×) · **hand-landmark→GPU-particle TECH** (lab-first) · **JOYFUL embodied / immediate-cause-effect VIBE** (NOT the banned never-resolves tag; a 4yo wants agency, and 513/518 were both hushed single-presences) · **ONE creature that is YOURS / playful-agency CONCEPT**]` — dodges the banned VIBE. (Honest tension: the JURY 2026-06-11 *text* listed WebGL2-shader as banned, but that was when WebGL2 was 4× in its window; it has since fallen to 1× — one particle piece after that drought is diversification, not the renderer-migration the jury decried, and it's the loved-particle lane.) **Love signal:** **`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️ + `236-particle-life-song`❤️ + `262-aurora-particle`❤️** (loved GPU/particle fields) pull the firebird aesthetic directly; `234-kids-hand-creature`❤️ + `104-kids-mirror-draw`❤️ + `101-camera-song`❤️ back the embodied camera/hand lane; additive — the diversity + ambition gates + Karel's "massively bigger" steer governed. **Winner of a DEEP 3-renderer kids fire** (one concept "your hand becomes a singing creature", three renderers): siblings **`523-kids-hand-puppet`** (Canvas2D warm shadow-puppet DOG, thumb=jaw — the most legibly-a-creature for a literal 4yo; lost because Canvas2D was already 3× → would be 4×, and it's the least "massively bigger" render) + **`525-kids-hand-choir`** (SVG five glowing fingertip-singers — spread = 5-part pentatonic chord, close = unison hug; the cleverest concept; lost because SVG was already 3× and the builder self-flagged a 60fps-via-React-state perf edge) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §394. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 108s`, `/dream/524-kids-hand-firebird` prerendered static **6.09 kB / 109 kB First Load**; the winner builder self-reported its folder tsc+eslint-clean with WebGL2 nulls guarded — no fix iteration needed), **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/WebGL2/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: the MediaPipe-Hands CDN import + WebGL2 particle pipeline have **never run on a real device** (model-load timing / `mediapipeTasks` export shape / a WebGL2 program-attribute mismatch are the likely first-run issues — the Canvas2D glow fallback + virtual-hand auto-demo fully cover camera/MediaPipe/WebGL2-absent; note **iOS Safari mid-2026 has no WebGPU but DOES have WebGL2**, so the full firebird should run on iPad); whether the openness/spread heuristics (tuned for an adult hand) read on a small child's hand; whether the sung voice reads as "the creature is singing" behind the limiter on phone speakers; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture + camera-permission UX on a child's tablet. The Canvas2D fallback + on-load virtual-hand auto-demo are the safety nets so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a glowing, singing creature.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/524-kids-hand-firebird/README.md`

- **[/dream/520-singing-dune](/dream/520-singing-dune)** — Singing Dune ⛰. `demoable` · Cycle 393. **For**: adult / meditative, long-form, physical.

  **One living dune of sand, under its own gravity. You tip the world; it avalanches and finds a new angle of repose — forever, never the same. Its motion is the sound.** The lab's **first actual continuum simulation**: a **WebGPU compute MLS-MPM** (Moving Least Squares Material Point Method) granular solid — 6k particles on a 128² grid, 5 compute passes/substep (clearGrid → P2G → gridUpdate → G2P → render), **APIC** affine-momentum transfer + a simplified **Drucker-Prager** plasticity (friction angle ~30°) so the sand *piles and avalanches* instead of splashing like water. Gravity comes from **device-orientation tilt** (gamma→gravity-x, beta→gravity-y; pointer-drag + arrow-key fallbacks on desktop — the mental model is "tip the world," never tap/paint). The dune's aggregate physics drives a **booming-dune granular drone** — the acoustic physics of singing dunes (frequency scales with grain shear, not dune size): kinetic energy → drone density + filter cutoff; mean shear rate → a low fundamental (72→108 Hz, JI stack 1:1/5:4/3:2/9:5/2:1) that bends up during an avalanche and sinks back at repose; avalanche impulses → soft filtered grain bursts. **No harmonic progression, no goal, no win, no resolve button** — the drama is entirely in the sand's motion. `navigator.gpu` absent (incl. iOS Safari mid-2026) → a **CPU MLS-MPM** fallback (2k particles, 64² grid, mpm88-style quadratic B-spline weights + APIC C-matrix, Canvas2D point render in umber→amber→gold) that runs **on load** with no interaction, so a hands-free 06:30 phone glance always shows a settling, sounding dune. AudioContext built in the **wake the dune** gesture (iOS unlock); `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−6 dB / 20:1). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + WebGPU; no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps (`@webgpu/types` is existing dev-only typing).

  References: **Hu, Fang, Ge, Qu, Stomakhin, Jiang** — "A Moving Least Squares Material Point Method with Displacement Discontinuity and Fracture" (SIGGRAPH 2018; the canonical MLS-MPM: APIC C-matrix, quadratic B-splines, Drucker-Prager) · **Jiang/Schroeder/Teran/Stomakhin/Selle** — taichi `mpm88`/`mpm99` reference code (the compact MPM demos that distill the method) · **Andreotti et al.** — "The song of dunes as a wave-trapped acoustic resonance" (PNAS 2008; the booming-dune acoustic physics). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (**MLS-MPM granular continuum** — the lab's first actual continuum/material-point simulation; honest caveat: "MLS-MPM" *appears* in `84-wave-fluid`'s README as a deferred-upgrade note but was **never implemented** there — 84 shipped a height-field; the substance is genuinely lab-first, and 520 is the long-deferred follow-through) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (MLS-MPM physics {P2G/grid/G2P/plasticity} + booming-dune granular synth driven by sim stats + device-orientation gravity input/render = 3–4) + **#3** named refs above. (#4 NOT claimed — single-cycle; seeds a "Physical Matter / Granular" spine. **#5 NOT claimed** — cs.SD this week is server-ML only, said plainly.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-11:** **refuses resolution** — a state to inhabit, no solve button (provocation #2, the `490`/`513`/`514`/`518` un-resolving lineage); **extends the WebGPU pieces** (`486`/`502`) instead of migrating renderers again (provocation #3); is about **one presence** — one dune, never two negotiating parties (provocation #5). **Diversity** `banned=[Canvas2D + WebGL2-shader OUTPUT · Plomp–Levelt-roughness TECH · touch INPUT · resolve-through-action VIBE · two-parties CONCEPT] · picked=[**device-orientation/tilt INPUT** (fresh, non-touch — gravity, not tap/paint) · **WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (jury-blessed "extend these"; CPU Canvas2D is only the iOS fallback, not the chosen renderer) · **MLS-MPM granular-continuum TECH** (lab-first, NOT roughness) · **warm-desert-meditative / NEVER-resolves VIBE** · **ONE-presence CONCEPT**]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** **`84-wave-fluid`❤️** directly (its README deferred MLS-MPM as "a second-cycle upgrade" — 520 finally builds it) + `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️ / `236-particle-life-song`❤️ / `262-aurora-particle`❤️ (loved GPU/particle-field pieces); additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. **Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer adult fire** (three orthogonal "one presence" directions): siblings **`521-overpass`** (live ISS overhead-pass → a lone JI voice rising/peaking/setting on a three.js star-dome; real-world-data + synthetic fallback — the cleanest resurrect) + `522-chladni-voice` (hum→Chladni standing-wave 60k-grain point cloud — lost because grep at curation found it overlaps existing `19`/`165-cymatics`, a concept-repeat the WIDE fan-out caught) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §393. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 43s`, `/dream/520-singing-dune` prerendered static **9.6 kB / 116 kB First Load**; one trivial TS-only fix — a `canvas` non-null alias for the nested closure), **NOT browser-verified** (no WebGPU/audio/device-orientation in this sandbox) — unverified surface: the WebGPU pipelines have **never run on a real device** (shader-compile/bind-group errors are the likely first-run issue; the CPU MLS-MPM fallback fully covers WebGPU-absent/failed incl. iOS); P2G has racy concurrent f32 writes (no f32 atomics → possible mass undercount in dense cells); the Drucker-Prager projection is approximate (no true SVD) so the angle of repose may drift from ~30°; whether the shear-driven drone *audibly* reads as "booming" behind the limiter; iOS DeviceOrientation permission firing from the wake gesture. The CPU fallback + on-load auto-settle are the safety nets so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a settling, sounding dune.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/520-singing-dune/README.md`

- **[/dream/518-kids-living-ember](/dream/518-kids-living-ember)** — Living Ember 🔥. `demoable` · Cycle 392. **For**: kids 4+ / warm, contemplative companion.

  **A single small warm ember that is simply here with you — no win, no fail, no resolve; hum to it and over many minutes it slowly becomes something new, carrying a trace of everything you gave it.** A real **WebGPU compute Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion** field (512², ping-pong `r32float` storage textures, 3 WGSL steps/render frame, **core-only** features — `texture_2d<f32>` reads + `texture_storage_2d<r32float,write>` writes, no extensions) renders a warm ember (deep crimson → amber → gold → soft white core) breathing on its own forever. Humming (mic RMS envelope, ANY voice — no pitch matching) raises the feed rate + deposits **permanent V-seeds** that bloom warm Turing fingers; memory is **monotonic** (`cumulativeHum` never decremented). Two banks of 4 **incommensurate LFOs** (irrational-ratio frequencies π/e/√2/φ over a 300 s base period) drift the f/k rates through the Gray-Scott zoo so **minute 5 ≠ second 30** and it never loops back. A warm C3 pentatonic / just-intonation lullaby pad (C2 sub so it's never silent) mirrors the SAME drift on the CPU (audio params updated ~4×/s, **no GPU readback**); soft bell on each hum; **no cadence, no arrival, no resolve button.** `navigator.gpu` absent (incl. iOS Safari mid-2026) → a warm CSS `radial-gradient` breathing ember driven by the same drift; no/denied mic → `text-rose-300` notice + a **mic-independent auto-demo** envelope. AudioContext built in the **wake it** gesture (iOS unlock); `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter + ≤8 kHz lowpass (kids-safe). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + WebGPU; mic local-analysis only, never recorded/uploaded; no network, no secrets). No new npm deps (`@webgpu/types` is existing dev-only typings).

  References: **teamLab** — *Sketch Aquarium / Future Park* (a single creature you bring to life and keep company in a living world; the canonical kids-installation grammar — needs no explanation, rewards presence not solving — the dive's actionable read) · **Brian Eno** — *Ambient 1: Music for Airports* (1978; long-form, non-repeating, no cadence, no arrival) · **Pearson, J.E. (1993)** "Complex Patterns in a Simple System," *Science* 261 (Gray-Scott Turing patterns). Spiritual successor to **`490-disintegration`** — same "no solve button" courage, made warm. **Ambition 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (voice/mic analysis + long-form memory/evolution engine + WebGPU-compute RD renderer + evolving lullaby synth = 4) + **#3** named refs above + **#4** multi-cycle (Companion/Presence spine, cycle 1). (#1 NOT claimed — WebGPU/RD/long-form all exist in the lab individually; the fresh element is *a warm single-presence kids companion that never resolves*, a concept not a primitive — honest. **#5 NOT claimed** — cs.SD this week is server-ML only, said plainly.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-11:** **refuses resolution** — a presence to inhabit, no solve button (provocation #2, the `490`/`513`/`514` un-resolving lineage); **extends the WebGPU pieces** (`486`/`502`) instead of migrating renderers again (provocation #3, and dodges banned Canvas2D/WebGL2 AND the now-3×-and-climbing SVG); is about **one presence** — one creature, never two negotiating parties (provocation #5). **Diversity** `banned=[Canvas2D + WebGL2-shader OUTPUT · Plomp–Levelt-roughness TECH · touch INPUT · resolve-through-action VIBE · two-parties CONCEPT] · picked=[**voice/hum-mic INPUT** (fresh, non-touch) · **WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (2× under-represented + jury-blessed; dodges Canvas2D/WebGL2 + 3× SVG) · **long-form evolving single-presence-with-memory TECH** (NOT roughness) · **warm-companion / inhabit-a-presence / NEVER-resolves VIBE** · **ONE-presence CONCEPT**]` — no banned tag; **diversity (SVG would be 4×) was the deciding lens** vs. the SVG sibling. **Love signal:** `262-aurora-particle`❤️ (warm glow palette) + `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️ / `98-kids-drum-circle`❤️ (embodied kids play); additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. **Winner of a DEEP 3-renderer kids fire** (one companion concept, three renderers): siblings **`517-kids-glow-friend`** (three.js soft metaball/halo creature with orbiting petal particles for remembered hums — the most legibly-a-friend for a 4yo and safest on iOS; lost only on the jury's "extend WebGPU" steer) + `519-kids-quiet-bloom` (SVG differential-growth / space-colonization organism that never prunes — lovely, but SVG is already 3× so picking it would make it the new 4× monoculture) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §392. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 95s`, `/dream/518-kids-living-ember` prerendered static **7.68 kB / 114 kB First Load**; two trivial TS-only fixes in the new WebGPU file — `writeTexture` typed-array generic cast + a `gpuCtx` null-narrowing alias — plus one typography bump), **NOT browser-verified** (no WebGPU/audio/mic in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the WGSL pipelines compile + run on a real WebGPU device (`r32float` storage support / binding layouts may need a first-run tweak — CSS ember covers WebGPU-absent/failed, and **iOS Safari mid-2026 has no WebGPU so iPad shows the CSS ember**); the ~5–10 s RD warmup before Turing patterns appear; whether the slow drift *audibly* reads as evolution behind the −6 dB limiter on phone speakers; whether a 4-year-old perceives the minute-to-minute morphology change (the math delivers it; a cycle-2 visual-milestone note); iOS AudioContext-in-gesture. The CSS-ember fallback + mic-independent auto-demo are the safety nets so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a breathing, evolving ember with sound.

- **[/dream/514-polytempo-loom](/dream/514-polytempo-loom)** — Polytempo Loom ⧗. `demoable` · Cycle 391. **For**: adult / contemplative, structural.

  **Five voices on one pitch set, each running at a different irrational tempo ratio — they drift across one another forever and never land a shared downbeat; the only tension is metric.** All five play the same D-pentatonic melodic cell (across 2 octaves → **zero harmonic tension**) on a soft bell/pluck synth, each scheduled by its own "Tale of Two Clocks" lookahead loop (25 ms interval, 120 ms ahead, sample-accurate off `AudioContext.currentTime`) at a fixed period set by ratios **1, √2, φ, e/2, π/2** — mutually irrational, so no two voices ever simultaneously return to beat 1. **No resolution, no solve button:** the optional "stir the loom" spread slider widens/narrows the mutual-drift *velocity* but preserves irrationality at every setting, so it never aligns. The visual is **inline SVG only** (no Canvas2D, no WebGL): a Nancarrow piano-roll loom — five colored lanes whose note-onset marks scroll at one px/sec rate, so faster voices show denser marks and the lanes drift into transient **moiré near-alignments** that always disperse; a dashed "NOW" playhead, per-lane ratio tags + live BPM, marks dimming into the past, a brief glow on each fire. The loom animates from page-load via a silent simulated schedule, so a no-audio 06:30 phone glance sees the drift alive before any gesture; audio starts on **Begin** (AudioContext built in the gesture, iOS unlock). `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter; audio-fail → `text-rose-300` notice, loom keeps drifting. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + SVG — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Conlon Nancarrow** — *Studies for Player Piano*, esp. **Study No. 40 "Transcendental"** (a canon at the ratio **e:π**) and his **√2 canons**, where the layers share no common period so a structural downbeat can never align; this is the interactive, eternal-loop counterpart to Nancarrow's fixed convergence-point pieces. Also **Amanous** (arXiv 2603.16890, Feb 2026 — Nancarrow + Xenakis + L-systems on a Disklavier; the dive's recent anchor) and **Callender, "Performing the Irrational"** (MTO 20.1). **Ambition 2/5**: **#1** never-used technique (irrational-ratio polytempo / metric-dissonance lattice — grep-verified lab-first; `nancarrow`/`polytempo`/`metric dissonance`/`polymeter` all 0×) + **#3** named references above. (#2 NOT claimed — 1 audio source + 1 SVG renderer = 2 subsystems, honest. #4 NOT claimed — single cycle; could seed a "Metric Dissonance / Time" spine. #5 NOT claimed — no <14-day finding to bind; the metric-dissonance literature is mature, not freshly-published, said plainly.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-11:** puts tension in a **never-touched primitive — TIME** (provocation #1, explicitly NOT the banned 4× Plomp–Levelt roughness engine — "tension you COUNT, not tension you hear beating"); **refuses resolution** — a state to inhabit, no solve button (provocation #2, the `490`/`513` un-resolving lineage continued); ships on **SVG**, the explicit anti-Canvas2D renderer (provocation #3); is about **one presence** — one drifting mechanism, never two negotiating parties (provocation #5). **Diversity** `banned=[Canvas2D OUTPUT (6×) · WebGL2-shader OUTPUT (4×) · Plomp–Levelt-roughness TECH (4×) · touch INPUT (6×) · resolve-through-action VIBE (~9×) · two-parties CONCEPT] · picked=[generative/none INPUT (no touch) · SVG OUTPUT (dodges Canvas2D + WebGL2) · irrational-polytempo / metric-dissonance TECH (lab-first, NOT roughness) · hypnotic-eternal-NON-RESOLVING VIBE · ONE-presence CONCEPT]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** `267-spectral-drift`❤️ + `236-particle-life-song`❤️ (loved evolving-texture pieces) softly back the long-form-drift register; additive — the gates governed. **Winner of a DEEP—no, WIDE 3-explorer adult fire** (theme: one presence, never resolves, not on Canvas2D): siblings **`516-slow-presence`** (WebGPU Gray-Scott long-form one-presence field — the `490` successor, the lab's best #4+#5 5/5 candidate) + `515-two-tempos-one-body` (audio/haptic-only cross-modal polytempo) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §391. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 36.0s`, `/dream/514-polytempo-loom` prerendered **static 4.4 kB**), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the five irrational tempi *audibly* read as distinct drifting streams vs. mush behind the 1.6 s bell decay; whether the cross-lane moiré near-alignments are perceptible as the visual payoff (may want a denser mark window); a few-ms drift between the setTimeout visual flash and the AudioContext-scheduled note under load; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture. The silent-on-load SVG drift is the safety net so the 06:30 glance always sees the loom alive.

- **[/dream/513-kids-shadow-still](/dream/513-kids-shadow-still)** — Shadow Still 🌑. `demoable` · Cycle 390. **For**: kids 4+ / calm, contemplative.

  **Move in front of the camera to wake a sound; go *still* and the two copies of it line up and cancel into a hollow hush.** A continuous A2 (110 Hz) shimmer plays from Start, run through a feed-forward **comb filter** (the tone summed with a sub-millisecond `DelayNode` copy of itself, plus mild feedback). The child's **body-motion energy** — read by simple per-pixel **frame-difference** on an *offscreen* 64×48 canvas (NOT MediaPipe, NOT a visible canvas; the visible renderer is SVG only) — drives the comb's delay time: **moving** pushes the delay toward 10 ms (sparse notches, full flanging swoosh, the silhouette and its echo-shadow split apart) and **stillness** slides it to **1/(2·f₀) ≈ 4.5 ms**, the exact anti-phase null for 110 Hz, so the comb teeth bite the fundamental and the tone **hollows toward near-silence** as a serif "hush…" fades in. The tension is **spectral** (notches / absence), explicitly **not** the over-used Plomp–Levelt/Sethares beating-roughness, and it **resolves toward absence**, never a warm consonant bloom — a calm state to inhabit. The whole piece is **inline SVG**: a soft kid-silhouette + a blurred translucent **echo-shadow** that separates on motion and merges at stillness, a live **comb-teeth spectrum strip** (`|1 + g·e^(−j2πfτ)|` over 0–8 kHz) whose bars drop into deep notches as it hushes, an ambient glow that dims with stillness, and a motion meter. **No win, no score, no "wrong"** — just the space between moving and still. Always-on **auto-demo** (sinusoidal motion-energy, ~4.8 s) runs on load before camera permission resolves and resumes after 3.5 s idle, so a no-camera 06:30 phone glance still hears+sees the full move→hush arc; camera denied/absent → readable `text-rose-300` notice + the demo keeps running. `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter + ≤8 kHz lowpass (kids-safe); AudioContext created inside the Start gesture (iOS unlock). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + SVG; camera is local frame-diff only — never recorded or uploaded; no network, no secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Alvin Lucier** — *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969) and the standing-wave/interference works, where physical space acts as a filter that dissolves a sound; this piece inverts that logic so *stillness* collapses the tone to a hush. **Pauline Oliveros** — *Deep Listening*, stillness and the space between sounds as a musical state to inhabit (not absence). **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1974)** / **Golan Levin** — frame-difference as the classic, ML-free webcam-as-controller. **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (comb-filter / phase-cancellation tension — grep-verified lab-first; phasing is `302`, xenharmonic is `276`, polyrhythm is `426`+12 — comb-notch was 0×) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (offscreen frame-diff motion detector + comb-filter synth + live SVG comb-spectrum + shadow/echo renderer + auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named refs above. (#4 NOT claimed — single cycle; could seed an "Interference/Stillness" spine. #5 NOT claimed — no <14-day finding to bind; this week's cs.SD/NIME had nothing client-implementable on interference, said plainly.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-11:** picks a **never-touched provocation-#1 primitive** (comb-notch, not the banned 4× roughness engine); **refuses the warm bloom** — resolves to *absence*, a state to inhabit (provocation #2, the lone-standout `490`'s missing successor); takes the **calm / anti-screen-frenzy** lane and inverts every loud tap-toy so *stillness makes silence* (provocation #3); is about **one presence** — a child and their own shadow, never two negotiating parties (provocation #5). **Diversity** `banned=[Canvas2D OUTPUT (6×) · WebGL2-shader OUTPUT (4×) · Plomp–Levelt-roughness TECH (4×) · touch INPUT (6×) · resolve-through-action VIBE (~9×) · two-parties CONCEPT] · picked=[camera-frame-diff-motion INPUT (fresh, non-touch) · SVG OUTPUT (jury's fresh renderer; dodges Canvas2D + WebGL2) · comb-filter/phase-cancellation TECH (lab-first, NOT roughness) · stillness-hushes-to-absence VIBE (NOT warm-resolve) · one-presence CONCEPT]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** `101-camera-song`❤️ + `104-kids-mirror-draw`❤️ (camera-as-embodied-kids-input) + `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️ (embodied calm play); additive — the gates governed. **Winner of a DEEP 3-approach kids fire** (one "vanish into your shadow" concept, three inputs): siblings `511-kids-ghost-bell` (tilt slides a ghost-twin over a bell; richest visuals but its overlap→cancel mapping reinforces the fundamental at delay→0 — needs a mid-delay-null fix) + `512-kids-echo-shadow` (hum and your own voice combs against itself; the most magical premise but headphone-only / feedback-howl) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked IDEAS §390. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `/dream/513-kids-shadow-still` prerendered static **4.34 kB**, warning-clean), **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the frame-diff threshold reads real camera contrast (first-guess; lower the divisor if it never exits demo on motion); whether the hush is *perceptibly* deep behind the −6 dB limiter + 0.65 feedback floor (a comb null is partial — may want a wet-mix duck at stillness); the double-smoothing may feel sluggish though it suits a calm piece; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture; camera-permission UX on a child's tablet. The permission-free auto-demo is the safety net so the 06:30 glance always shows the full move→hush arc.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/513-kids-shadow-still/README.md`

---

## (Cycle 385 — **adult** build · DEEP, 3 agent-brains for one concept. Ships **`496-voyager-room`** (see bottom of file): *you don't play notes — you CONDUCT a room of 5 autonomous machine musicians who listen to each other and to you, leaning the collective harmony into deliberate dissonance and releasing it.* George Lewis's **_Voyager_** made playable; binds **StreamMUSE** (frame-synchronous LM jamming, arXiv 2606.11886, 2026-06-10 — the 2nd 5/5 in the window). Answers JURY 2026-06-10: tension-you-conduct (not warm-resolve), bans three.js, and rejects the WebRTC-multi-user + accompanist lanes as *occupied/saturated* "too similar" traps in favour of a genuine lab-first (multi-agent ensemble, grep-clean). 2 siblings banked IDEAS §385: `497-call-ring` (ring-canon of imitation) + `498-swarm-score` (swarm consensus + tritone dissidents, WebGL2).

Prior (Cycle 383 — adult · WIDE): `490-disintegration` — no resolution, only entropy; a piece that physically destroys itself as you listen (Basinski). Siblings `491-room-resonance` + `492-automata` banked IDEAS §383.)

- **[/dream/490-disintegration](/dream/490-disintegration)** — Disintegration 🜄. `demoable` · Cycle 383. **For**: adults / contemplative.

  **A warm modal loop that physically crumbles as you listen — minute five is not minute one, and the act of listening is what consumes it.** Press **start**: a slightly melancholy A-minor bell loop (an unresolved Aeolian fragment — root, minor sixth "ache," sinking back, never landing on a bright tonic) begins. It is held as a single **mutable `Float32Array` "tape"** of 256 regions, each with PERSISTENT, monotonically-dying state. Every pass the engine bakes a small **random, IRREVERSIBLE** round of damage straight into the sample buffer — survival bleeds, an accumulating one-pole lowpass darkens each region (highs die first, like iron-oxide shedding), and short windows are **permanently zeroed** (soft-edged, no clicks) — then re-renders the already-eroded buffer, so nothing ever recovers. Over ~35 passes the loop thins, gaps tear open, and it crumbles toward silence under a sub-bass room-tone floor that fades last. You can only shape **how it dies, never whether**: **hold to remember** slows the bite (never reverses); **tap the eroding tape-ring** to re-excite a faded region into a faint ghost — at the cost of taxing every other region (net entropy still rises). Canvas2D tape-ring: each arc's thickness/brightness = surviving content, hue cooling as it muffles, going dark+torn as it dies; violet playhead sweep; `pass N · M% remains` readout. **An homage to William Basinski, *The Disintegration Loops* (2002).**

  Subsystems (2): (1) **offline loop synthesis** (`audio.ts`) — a deterministic additive-bell A-minor phrase rendered to a `Float32Array` with a seam crossfade + safe-peak normalize, plus a separate always-on sub-bass room-tone (`makeRoomTone`, sine pair → 220 Hz lowpass) that fades up at start and down as the loop dies. (2) **the disintegration engine** (`decay.ts`, `DisintegrationTape`) — the heart: a working `samples` buffer + an immutable `pristine` seed (only for re-excitation), 256 region states (`survival`/`cutoff`/`torn`/`lpMem`), `step()` applying irreversible wounds + global darkening + age-rising dropouts then a per-region running lowpass baked into the live buffer, `setHold()` (decay×0.35), `reExcite(norm)` (capped faint revival + a tax on all other regions), `meanSurvival()`/`isNearlyGone()`. The Canvas2D tape-ring renderer + the look-ahead scheduler (re-render eroded buffer → seamless queue → amplitude tracks mean survival) live in `page.tsx`. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + Canvas2D — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **William Basinski, *The Disintegration Loops* (2002)** — the load-bearing idea: a loop whose physical medium decays *as it plays*, so the music crumbles over time; this is the digital realization (accumulating, irreversible sample destruction with persistent per-region state). **Born from RESEARCH §383** (the dive negatively-steered off server-heavy diffusion + already-used WebGPU, then **grep-killed the jury's "starved" real-world-data-sonification menu item** as a saturated rut — USGS done 3× [233/279/337], NOAA/sky 2× [314/347] — which positively steered onto the genuinely-untouched conceptual-critical + tension register). **Ambition 2/5:** **#1** never-used technique (persistent irreversible audio-buffer self-disintegration — grep-verified `basinski`/`disintegration`/`self-eras`/`progressive degrad` absent across 340 folders) + **#3** named reference (Basinski 2002). #2 NOT claimed (2 subsystems); #4 NOT claimed (single-cycle); **#5 NOT claimed** — the dive found no bindable <14-day finding and says so plainly (the reference is foundational, 2002). The ambition here is *conceptual* — the big swing is the idea (a piece that destroys itself), not a subsystem stack. **This directly answers the JURY (2026-06-10) #1** — genuine tension with NO pre-baked cadence; the only "resolution" is entropy, shaped-not-escaped by the listener. **Diversity** `picked=[touch/none INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT (the corrective humble tool — dodges every count-banned GPU renderer: three.js-5×, WebGL2-4×) · persistent-self-disintegration TECHNIQUE (a conceptual framework, NOT a textbook DSP primitive — dodges the "first-DSP-primitive treadmill") · melancholic-entropy-NO-resolution VIBE (the exact opposite of the 15× warm-consonant monoculture)]`. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire (three orthogonal answers to "bring tension back"): siblings `491-room-resonance` (Lucier iterative re-convolution dissolution; mic; lost on robustness) + `492-automata` (Wolfram elementary CA compositional engine; grep-verified lab-first; lost narrowly on the tension axis) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §383. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `/dream/490-disintegration` 4.48 kB prerendered; one TS typed-array generic error fixed via `getChannelData().set()`), NOT browser-verified** (no audio/Canvas in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the decay *sounds* like Basinski's haunted bloom vs. a plain muting loop behind the limiter; whether the loop seam survives dropouts tearing near it; the real-time pace of "minute 5 = skeletal" (design intent, untested); the iOS AudioContext-in-gesture path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/490-disintegration/README.md`

- **[/dream/489-kids-pond-pair](/dream/489-kids-pond-pair)** — Pond Pair 🪷. `demoable` · Cycle 382. **For**: kids (4+).

  **Two glowing musical ponds that secretly talk — splash one and a little lily pad rides the ripples, drifts across the channel, and drops your sound into the other pond so it sings.** Cycle 2 of the **Wave Field** kids spine (cycle 1 = `478-kids-wave-pond`, one pond). Each pond is a real **2-D FDTD wave field** (56×56, Van Duyne–Smith digital-waveguide mesh): tap → a Gaussian ripple spreads, reflects off the rim, interferes. The **new mechanic is coupling**: (1) an always-on **sympathetic whisper** across the facing edge columns (`K=0.08`) so a ring in pond A subtly excites pond B; (2) a tangible floating **lily pad carrier** that bobs on the local wave height, is pushed by the wave gradient, accumulates field energy (a golden glow-dot fills), and on crossing the channel **injects that energy into the other pond** (ring + sparkle chime) — a *visible sound-messenger* a 4-year-old reads instantly. Pond A = low pentatonic (C3 D3 E3 G3 A3), pond B = high pentatonic (C4 D4 E4 G4 A4) → always harmonize, no wrong notes. Multi-touch (two kids, one pond each). Always-on C3+G3 ambient pad; `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter; 3-s auto-demo shows the pad crossing hands-free; iOS AudioContext created in the first gesture. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 two FDTD solvers + cross-pond coupling + carrier physics + pickup synth ≥3 subsystems · #3 named ref Van Duyne & Smith ICMC 1993 + sympathetic resonator coupling · #4 spine cycle 2 — an *extension*, the jury's higher-value move, not a 5th primitive; #1/#5 honestly not claimed — coupling two fields is an extension not a new primitive, and the dive bound no <14-day finding). **Diversity** (last-10: WebGL2 4× count-ban, three.js 3× + jury-named ban): `picked=[multi-touch INPUT · Canvas2D OUTPUT (dodges banned three.js + the WebGL2 4× count-ban) · coupled-FDTD-fields / sympathetic-coupling + carrier-object TECHNIQUE (a spine extension) · warm cooperative water VIBE (kids parity-exempt)]`. **Love signal:** `133-kids-ripple-pond`❤️ + `84-wave-fluid`❤️ + `105-pluck-field`❤️ + `140-kids-string-bridge`❤️ all back a tap-the-water-it-sings cooperative pond — additive. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-builder fire (coupling-mechanic axis): siblings `488-kids-echo-ponds` (the coupling K is a child-draggable vine; WebGPU-compute FDTD + Canvas2D fallback) + `487-kids-two-ponds` (the clean cooperative "two kids each claim a pond" core; dual-palette ImageData) banked in IDEAS §382 as cycle-3 fold-ins. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0 with only the winner present, `/dream/489-kids-pond-pair` in `app-paths-manifest.json`; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean — fixed two `react/no-unescaped-entities` errors + two unused-symbol warnings as the single fix attempt), **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/audio/touch in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether two `putImageData` + two 3×(56×56) FDTD loops hold 60 fps on a low-end phone (builder flags `GRID=40` as the mitigation knob); whether the pad's drift/cross timing reads as a clear "messenger" gesture vs. random wander on a real touchscreen; whether the always-on `K=0.08` whisper is audible vs. masked behind the limiter; tap-to-sound <50 ms; the iOS AudioContext-in-gesture path. Typography-compliant (hero `text-2xl`, body ≥`text-base`).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/489-kids-pond-pair/README.md`

## (Cycle 380 — **kids** build · DEEP, 3 polytonality explorers. The jury's #1 ask answered for kids: the lab's FIRST **polytonality / bitonality** toy — two musical worlds (two whole keys) ringing AT ONCE, and the bittersweet, *unresolvable* place where they overlap. Grep-verified lab-first technique. Two siblings — a tritone-split "mood prism" + a two-rivers braid — banked to IDEAS §380.)

- **[/dream/484-kids-two-suns](/dream/484-kids-two-suns)** — Two Suns. `demoable` · Cycle 380.

  **A 4-year-old toy where two glowing suns each sing their OWN key at the same time — drag them apart for two clear worlds, drag them together for one shimmering, never-resolving bittersweet bloom.** Tap anywhere to wake the suns (lazy AudioContext, no text-gate, no spinner). Sun A = **C major** (warm gold), Sun B = **A major** (cool violet), played as slow soft sine+triangle bell-arpeggios — **two keys superimposed at once** (polytonality). Each sun is **panned by its horizontal position** (per-sun `StereoPannerNode`) and its voice grows more present as the suns approach; the arpeggio **thickens as they merge** so the two keys ring as a dense bitonal cluster at the eclipse. **Multi-touch:** each sun claims its own `pointerId`, so a parent and child can drag both at once (KIDS.md's high-value two-hand play). Rendered with a **raw WebGL2 fragment shader** — two additive radial light fields over a dusk sky with a soft white-gold **corona bloom** where they overlap and a faint shimmer ring at the unresolved seam — with a **Canvas2D radial-gradient fallback** (visible `text-rose-300` notice) when WebGL2 is absent. After ~3.5 s of stillness the suns **auto-drift** (soft edge-bounce) so the piece is alive on load. **It never resolves to one key** — the shared E keeps the blend from sounding broken, the C-natural-vs-C♯ rub keeps it from settling, and it rests wherever the child leaves it: the ambiguity *is* the toy. This is the lab's **first genuinely UNRESOLVED piece** — the direct antidote to JURY 2026-06-10's flagged warm-consonant-resolves monoculture (15/15 consonant, 10 resolving on purpose). Subsystems (4): (1) WebGL2 fragment-shader renderer + Canvas2D fallback; (2) dual-key arpeggio scheduler (look-ahead, density-on-overlap); (3) multi-touch pointer routing + auto-drift; (4) per-sun StereoPanner spatialization + soft limiter/low-pass for kid-safe sound. No API route, no secrets, no new npm deps. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 polytonality grep-verified lab-first + #2 4-subsystem + #3 named ref Stravinsky *Petrushka* chord / Milhaud bitonal études; #5 not claimed — no <14-day finding was bindable, the dive negatively-steered onto a foundational primitive). Typography-compliant (hero `text-2xl`, body ≥`text-base`, `text-white/95` / `/75` / `/55`, accent `text-violet-300`, error `text-rose-300`).

## (Cycle 379 — **adult** build · DEEP, Resonant Room **CYCLE 2**. The jury's #1 ask made literal: bring genuine harmonic *tension* back and let the listener resolve it *through action*. Play a live MIDI keyboard into TWO sympathetic resonator rooms tuned a tritone apart — every note beats against a shifted copy of itself — and the only way out of the dissonance is to drag the rooms back into unison with your own hand. A real-time Plomp–Levelt/Sethares roughness engine makes the tension a *measured* number you watch fall. Two siblings — a whole-tone single-room suspension + a WebGPU dissonance-field renderer — folded into the spine's cycle-3 plan.)

- **[/dream/483-two-rooms-apart](/dream/483-two-rooms-apart)** — Two Rooms Apart. `demoable` · Cycle 379.

  **Cycle 2 of the "Resonant Room" spine — the warm room that resolved to the tonic (cycle 1, `475`) is replaced by its opposite temperature: a room that will NOT resolve unless you resolve it.** Press **Tap to Begin**, then play any note (live **Web MIDI** controller, on-screen 2-octave keyboard, or QWERTY home row) — each note injects a velocity-scaled filtered-noise excitation into **two parallel banks of 8 high-Q biquad bandpass resonators**: **Room A** rings the played note's overtones at reference pitch (panned −35° L); **Room B** rings the same set shifted by a continuous **detune (0–600 ¢, default = 600 ¢ tritone, panned +35° R)**. At full tritone the two rooms maximally disagree — every partial beats against a shifted copy at |fA−fB| Hz, a dense suspended dissonance with no tonic, ringing ~5.5 s (T60). A **real-time sensory-dissonance engine** (Plomp–Levelt / Sethares dyad roughness, summed analytically over all cross-room partial pairs — no FFT, since the resonator frequencies are known) produces a live 0–1 **tension** scalar that labels the readout ("Resolved → Settling → Tense → Dissonant → Clashing"), drives a center heat-glow, and animates a Canvas2D field of two opposed particle arcs (violet Room A / sky-blue Room B) joined by **interference arcs that throb at the literal beat frequency** — rose and fast at the tritone, slowing and greening as you close the gap. **The resolution is the player's act:** no timer, no cadence, no auto-release — drag the detune slider toward 0, Room B's filter frequencies ramp (`setTargetAtTime`) to meet Room A, the beating audibly slows to stillness, the fringes lock, the roughness scalar falls. Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **live Web MIDI input** (`requestMIDIAccess` + `onstatechange`) with on-screen/QWERTY fallback; (2) **dual coupled resonator-room engine** (2×8 biquads, StereoPanner L/R, bus compressor → brick-wall limiter); (3) **real-time roughness/sensory-dissonance analysis**; (4) **Canvas2D interference-field viz**. Pure client-side — no API route, no secrets, no new npm deps. **Ambition 4/5** (#1 first-MIDI-input + #2 + #3 + #4 spine-cycle-2; #5 not claimed — refs are foundational). Folds two siblings into cycle 3: a steepest-descent *dissonance-gradient hint* (`481`) and a *WebGPU dissonance-vorticity field* renderer (`482`).

## ⭐ (Cycle 371 — **adult** build · DEEP, Latent Piano Room **CYCLE 3**. The spine's three-cycle thread finally closes its *other* half: where cycles 1–2 let the dreamed image only *filter* Karel's piano, this image *re-composes* it — a scan-line reads the AI image as a spectral score and a 24-partial additive bank, quantized to the detected key, *sings the picture* as a warm duet over the real recording. Two image→music siblings — an in-key counter-melody improviser + a granular cloud — banked for cycle 4.)

- **[/dream/457-piano-image-duet](/dream/457-piano-image-duet)** — Piano Image Duet. `demoable` · Cycle 371.

  **Cycle 3 of the "Latent Piano Room" spine — the dreamed image stops merely *filtering* Karel's real piano and starts *re-composing* it.** Press **Play** and his Ghost recording streams through a Web-Audio chain (or drop a *Welcome Home* track; or a warm C–Am–F–G7–Cmaj9 synth fallback that **resolves on purpose** if the recording can't load — never silent). A musically-aware analyser (FFT 2048) tracks RMS dynamics, a 12-bin chromagram → dominant pitch-class + major/minor key, spectral-flux onsets (Bello 2005), and phrase boundaries. Caption-built features POST to a **guarded** route → FAL `flux/schnell`, and the returning image cross-fades full-bleed with a slow Ken-Burns drift. **The new move (cycle 3):** a glowing vertical scan-line sweeps the dreamed image left→right, synced to the music's phrase period (~4–12 s/sweep), reading it as a **spectral score** — each image column's vertical brightness profile is sampled at 24 log-spaced Y positions and drives a **24-partial additive bank**: Y-position → frequency **quantized to the detected key** (diatonic degrees — so however abstract or dark the image, the voice can only sing in-key and **resolve consonantly** alongside the piano, never atonal), brightness → that partial's amplitude. The result is a shimmer/choir voice that **duets** the real piano — the picture is literally singing along, and you *see* which part of the image makes which pitch.

  The loop closes the *other* way from cycles 1–2: there the image bent the piano's filter (image→audio = *filtering*); here the image **generates new musical material** (image→audio = *composing*). Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **real-recorded-audio chain** — `<audio crossOrigin="anonymous">` default `/api/audio/549fc519…` (Ghost recording) / file-drop / warm resolving synth fallback → `createMediaElementSource` → AnalyserNode → reverb→lowpass→compressor→master, with a 350 ms all-zero CORS-taint check that auto-swaps to the synth. (2) **musically-aware analysis** (`analysis.ts`) — chromagram/key/modality + spectral-flux onset + phrase boundary. (3) **guarded FAL image pipeline** (`api/route.ts`) — POST starts `const blocked = await guard(req); if (blocked) return blocked;`, 501 when `FAL_KEY` absent. (4) **image-as-spectrogram additive resynthesis** (`scanner.ts`) — `buildFrequencyGrid(keyPc, modality)` (in-key, rebuilt on every phrase boundary) → 24 sine oscillators (±3 ¢ shimmer detune) → highpass → `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; `sampleColumn()` reads the live scan column; partials smoothed per-frame. (5) **synthesized latent field** (`field.ts`) — when no FAL_KEY (the in-sandbox case), a plasma/blob field is rendered AND scanned by the same `readImageData()` path, so the piece is complete and beautiful with zero API calls (amber "synthesized field (no image key)" badge; emerald "dreaming live" when real images arrive). 10 s hands-free auto-demo. `@fal-ai/client` is an existing repo dep — **no new npm deps**.

  References: **Iannis Xenakis, *UPIC*** (1977 — drawn images become waveforms/score; the original image-as-score) · **MetaSynth** (Wenger & Spiegel, 1997 — any picture as a playable spectrogram) · **Art2Mus** (arXiv 2602.17599, Feb 2026 — *direct visual→music conditioning, bypassing the image→text step*; RESEARCH §371, the thesis that justifies letting the image re-compose rather than only filter) · **Bello et al. (2005)** spectral-flux onset detection. **Ambition 3/5 (honest), with the EXPENSIVE criterion stacked:** **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5, above) + **#3** named references (UPIC / MetaSynth / Art2Mus / Bello) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 3 of the Latent Piano Room spine — the #4 axis the 2026-06-09 jury said died at 414 and named the single most Karel-aligned move to restart). **#1 NOT claimed** — image/drawing→music already exists in the lab (`153-paint-compose`, `223-fourier-paint`, `100-kids-paint-song`); the fresh move is the *closed-loop image-RE-COMPOSES-piano* framing + in-key additive resynthesis (application novelty, not a new primitive). **#5 NOT claimed** — Art2Mus is ~4 months, not <14 days (the dive *bound* the build, which is the jury's #4 ask, even though the strict freshness window isn't met). **Diversity** (vs jury 2026-06-09 bans Canvas2D-OUTPUT · drum-machine-TECH · refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE · calm-pentatonic-kids): **real-recorded-piano INPUT** (the jury's #5 "use his actual piano, ignored 15 cycles") · **AI-image OUTPUT** (the canvas composites the AI image + scan-line HUD, not Canvas2D-as-the-piece — the sanctioned `441`/`448`/`454` dodge, and the jury named this lineage the bright spot to extend; honest: AI-image is now 4× in the trailing window, flagged for the next audit) · **image-as-spectrogram additive-resynthesis TECHNIQUE** (not drum-machine) · **warm/expressive/in-key/resolves-on-purpose VIBE** (the jury's explicit "missing middle" — dodges BOTH refuse-to-resolve AND calm-pentatonic). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept — *let the dreamed image re-compose the piano* — three image→music attacks); siblings `458-piano-image-improviser` (in-key generative counter-melody from image hue/gradient/edge features, Markov anti-repeat, resolves to tonic — the agentic take) + `459-piano-image-grains` (image-sampled granular cloud, Roads/Xenakis — the textural take) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS §371, folders removed. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 110s`, prerendered `/dream/457-piano-image-duet 11.4 kB` + guarded `ƒ /api` in the manifest), NOT browser-verified** (no FAL_KEY / no audio / no GPU in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether `/api/audio/549fc519…` resolves + Supabase signed-URL CORS lets the analyser read non-zero from Karel's network (all-zero→synth fallback covers it); whether the additive shimmer reads as "the image singing the piano" vs. a generic pad on real expressive piano + phone speakers; whether ~1 image/7 s + the scan sweep feels like a living duet; the FAL CDN's CORS for `sampleColumn`/`readImageData` (the synthesized-field path covers a taint); 24-oscillator additive perf on a low-end phone.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/457-piano-image-duet/README.md`

- **[/dream/456-kids-ferro-magnet](/dream/456-kids-ferro-magnet)** — Kids Ferro Magnet. `demoable` · Cycle 370.

  **For kids (4+). Tilt the tablet to drag a glowing magnet beneath a pool of black ferrofluid — and the flat dark surface rises into a cluster of living spikes that reach toward the magnet and chime warmly whenever a spike brushes one of the rim-bells.** This is the lab's **first ferrofluid / Rosensweig-instability piece** (grep-clean across 350+ prototypes — the only prior "magnet" pieces, `192`/`222-kids-magnet-notes`, are spring-pulled note-bubbles, not a spiking fluid surface). It directly answers the 2026-06-09 jury's "the lab forgot how to go *weird*": a **singing ferrofluid** is the freshest image of the three explored — cymatics already exists at `165`, and pendulum-waves are a known classic, but the lab has never made a spiking magnetic fluid. Press **Start** (creates/resumes AudioContext + requests iOS tilt permission in-gesture); tilt to move the magnet, or drag on desktop; leave it alone and the magnet drifts a Lissajous figure-8 so the pool stays alive hands-free at the 06:30 review.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **`ferro.ts` WebGL2 fragment shader** (GLSL ES 3.00) — the fluid surface is a **signed-distance field of N metablobs fused with Inigo Quilez's polynomial smooth-min (`smin`)** into one gooey membrane, with a **hexagonal three-wave interference ripple** modulating the SDF near the magnet to approximate the **Rosensweig normal-field spike array** (above a field threshold the surface resolves into pointed peaks), tonemapped glossy-dark with a warm rim/spec highlight. (2) **CPU spring physics** — each blob is a spring-mass particle: a gravity spring pulling it to rest, magnet attraction ∝ 1/r² with a field-radius envelope, and a per-blob `spikeH` that rises toward the magnet and relaxes back. (3) **`audio.ts`** — five **rim-bells** on a **just-intonation D-major hexachord** (D E F♯ A B C♯, ratios 1·9/8·5/4·3/2·5/3·15/8 × D3=146.83 Hz — warm and resolving, **not** the banned C-pentatonic lullaby), each four inharmonic partials → ADSR (6 ms attack, ~1.1–1.8 s decay), bigger/lower bells ring longer; always-on D2+A2 drone with slow tremolo, all through a brick-wall `DynamicsCompressor` (never harsh for small ears). (4) **tilt + drag + auto-demo input** — `DeviceOrientationEvent` β/γ (iOS `requestPermission()` inside the Start tap) → pointer-drag fallback (`text-rose-300` notice) → figure-8 auto-demo after ~2 s idle.

  References: **Ronald E. Rosensweig**, *Ferrohydrodynamics* (Cambridge, 1985) + the normal-field (Rosensweig) instability (~1969) — the hexagonal spike array on a ferrofluid under a perpendicular field · **Inigo Quilez**, polynomial smooth-min / SDF metaballs (iquilezles.org) · **Andrejs Cēbers**, ferrofluid pattern formation. Born from RESEARCH §370 (the dive's GPU-material-sim register + a grep-audit that killed the saturated material sims and surfaced ferrofluid as a clean lab-first). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (first ferrofluid/Rosensweig in the lab, grep-verified) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (WebGL2 SDF spike renderer · CPU spring physics · JI bell synth · tilt/drag/auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named references (Rosensweig · IQ · Cēbers). **#5 NOT claimed** — the dive anchors (ShaderVine Apr 2026, MPM 2025) are >14 days. **Diversity** (vs the 2026-06-09 jury bans + the over-leaned touch): **tilt INPUT** (clean dodge of touch, 4× in the last-10) · **raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT** (dodges the banned **Canvas2D**) · **ferrofluid/Rosensweig-SDF TECHNIQUE** (dodges the banned **drum-machine**) · **warm resolving JI VIBE** (dodges both the banned **refuse-to-resolve** and the banned **calm-cosmic-pentatonic kids template**). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `455-kids-sing-cymatics` (SING and watch your note's grains migrate into its Chladni standing-wave figure — the namesake, voice-driven; near-ship) + `457-kids-pendulum-wave` (pull a rack of 15 glowing pendulums and release → a hypnotic length-graded traveling wave that re-converges into a resolving arpeggio; grep-clean lab-first, the safest hands-free read) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS §370, folders removed. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/456-kids-ferro-magnet/page.js` in the manifest, `npx eslint` on the folder exit 0 / zero warnings), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/sensor/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the hex-ripple + spring physics reads as a convincing spiking *ferrofluid* vs. generic blobs on a real GPU (the README honestly flags the Rosensweig is approximated, not an energy-minimization, and the shader normals are simplified); whether tilt-steering the magnet *feels* good on a real tablet; the iOS DeviceOrientation-permission + AudioContext-in-tap path; tip-vs-circle bell-collision timing. No API route, no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets → no `guard` needed. Zero new npm deps.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/456-kids-ferro-magnet/README.md`

## Cycle 368 — **kids** build · DEEP — the jury's #1 ask for the kids lane: build the **loud, fast, playfully chaotic** piece the bans were missing — NOT a 5th calm-pentatonic lullaby. A joyful jelly avalanche whose music *escalates with the chaos and resolves on purpose*, on real soft-body physics. Restarts a multi-cycle spine (#4, dead since 414) and **binds today's fresh research** (#5) — the two ambition criteria the jury said had collapsed to zero.

- **[/dream/451-kids-jelly-storm](/dream/451-kids-jelly-storm)** — Kids Jelly Storm. `demoable` · Cycle 368.

  **For kids (4+). A joyful jelly avalanche you conduct — tap, drag, and SHAKE to make squishy creatures RAIN, and the more chaos there is, the louder, faster, and more triumphantly the music climbs, then lands on a big happy chord when it settles.** This is the **missing kids middle** the 2026-06-09 jury asked for (provocation #1): the bans had split the lab into a cold-adult / sweet-kids binary, with the kids half stuck on a calm-pentatonic-lullaby template (417/433/440/444). Jelly Storm is the opposite — energetic, bright, and it **resolves on purpose**. Press **TAP TO PLAY!** (creates/resumes AudioContext + requests iOS shake permission in-gesture); tap anywhere to drop a squishy creature, drag to fling/poke, shake to rain a burst. A bright **energy "MORE!" bar** makes the build-and-resolve arc legible to a 4-year-old.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **`pbd.ts`** — a **Position-Based-Dynamics** soft-body engine: each creature is a ring of perimeter particles + a center, held by **distance constraints** (perimeter springs, center spokes, cross-braces) and an **area/volume constraint** projected over substeps + ~5 iterations, so each blob genuinely *squishes* on impact and puffs back to full volume; cheap blob-vs-blob + bouncy-floor collisions, capped at 36 for perf, with live kinetic-energy + impact accounting. (2) **Energy-driven music engine (`audio.ts`)** — physics energy (kinetic + spawn rate) drives the music: low energy → sparse single mallets; rising energy → faster, denser arpeggios climbing a **I–IV–V–I** in **G major**; peak chaos → a triumphant tutti; calming → a clear **resolve to the tonic G chord**. Pitched melodic mallets (triangle+sine + light FM) + an always-on warm pad, all through a brick-wall `DynamicsCompressor` — full and energetic but never harsh/scary (kids-safe). **No drum machine, no pentatonic drift.** (3) **three.js / WebGL render** — each creature re-triangulated from its particles every frame into an additively-glowing saturated blob (glow brightens with squish + energy) over a dark-purple backdrop that brightens with the chaos (energy-reactive shader). (4) **Multi-touch input** — PointerEvents tap/drag/poke + multi-finger. (5) **DeviceMotion** — iOS-permission-gated shake-to-rain, with tap fallback when motion is unavailable. Hands-free **auto-demo** runs the whole build-and-resolve loop on its own ~3.2s after the last touch, so it's alive in sight + sound at the 06:30 zero-interaction review.

  References: **Müller, Heidelberger, Hennix, Ratcliff — *Position Based Dynamics*** (J. Vis. Comm. & Image Rep., 2007), the constraint-projection method the engine is built on; and the binding **fresh** anchor (RESEARCH §368, today): browser soft-body physics is having a 2025–26 moment — the WebGPU **AVBD** (Augmented Vertex Block Descent, 2025) solver and **jure/webphysics** are pushing real-time deformable bodies onto the web; PBD is the friendly, runs-everywhere ancestor that a kids' toy actually needs. **Ambition 4/5** (the regression fix the jury demanded — zero pieces were at 4–5/5): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (PBD solver · energy-music engine · WebGL render · multi-touch · DeviceMotion = 5) + **#3** named ref (Müller 2007) + **#4** explicit cycle-1 of a multi-cycle **"Squish" spine** + **#5** research from the last 14 days that *binds* the build (the 2025 AVBD/webphysics trend → PBD soft-body kids toy). **Diversity** (vs 2026-06-09 bans): **touch + device-motion INPUT** (input was the healthy axis; touch not count-banned) · **three.js/WebGL OUTPUT** (dodges the banned **Canvas2D**) · **PBD soft-body + energy-driven melodic synthesis TECHNIQUE** (dodges the banned **drum-machine percussion-synth**) · **loud/fast/joyful G-major build-and-resolve VIBE** (the requested missing middle — dodges both banned **refuse-to-resolve/clinical** AND **calm-cosmic-pentatonic lullaby**). Pulled softly by Karel's loves of `169-kids-marble-run`❤️ (physics-toy) + `234-kids-hand-creature`❤️. **Build-verified (exit 0, prerendered static 6.87 kB, zero warnings from the folder), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/audio/touch sensor in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the PBD squish reads as delightfully bouncy vs. jittery on a real tablet at 36 creatures; whether the energy→arpeggio escalation feels musical (not frantic) on phone speakers behind the limiter; the iOS AudioContext-in-tap + DeviceMotion-permission path; per-frame re-triangulation + additive-glow perf on a low-end tablet as the pile fills. The auto-demo is the safety net so a denied sensor never blocks the 06:30 demo. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one concept, three soft-body engines); siblings `449-kids-squish-jam` (Verlet mass-spring jelly) + `450-kids-goo-band` (WebGL2 metaball-SDF merging goo) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS §368.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/451-kids-jelly-storm/README.md`

## Cycle 367 — **adult** build · DEEP (the jury's #1 ask executed: restart a multi-cycle spine off loved `441` by running its closed audio→image→audio loop on Karel's **actual recorded piano**)

- **[/dream/448-piano-phrase-painter](/dream/448-piano-phrase-painter)** — Piano Phrase Painter. `demoable` · Cycle 367.

  **Karel's actual recorded piano paints itself — driven by MUSICAL STRUCTURE, not raw spectrum.** Press **Begin** and his **Ghost-journey piano** loads (via `/api/audio/549fc519…`, the real recording; or **drop any Welcome Home track**; or a warm synth stand-in that resolves on purpose). As it plays, the piece *listens like a musician*: a **spectral-flux onset detector** (half-wave-rectified, adaptive-median threshold + 80 ms debounce — Bello et al. 2005) finds note attacks, a **phrase-boundary** detector finds the breaths between phrases, a 12-bin **chromagram** matched to major/minor triad templates estimates the **harmonic color + consonance**, and a smoothed-RMS **dynamics envelope** reads ppp→fff. A new image is dreamed **at phrase boundaries / onset clusters — not on a fixed timer** — and the prompt is built from the *music*: harmony → palette (bright major → warm gold, deep minor → indigo), dynamics → density (sparse mist ↔ roiling tempest), pitch-class → hue accent, over a fixed Anadol-data-pigment base style → guarded FAL `flux/schnell`. The returning image **cross-fades** (~2.5 s, Ken-Burns + vignette) and the loop closes: its average color bends the **real piano playback** back — brightness opens the master lowpass + shimmer, warmth lengthens the reverb tail — while each detected onset fires a bloom pulse synced to the attack. This **restarts a multi-cycle spine** (#4, dead since cycle 414) and answers JURY 2026-06-09 provocations #1 (the warm/structured/resolves-on-purpose middle), #2 (441's loop on real piano), and #5 (real music, ignored 15 cycles).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **`analysis.ts`** — spectral-flux onset detector + phrase-boundary detector + 12-bin chromagram + major/minor/consonance estimator + dynamics envelope + music-derived prompt builder (no ML libs). (2) **Guarded FAL pipeline** — `api/route.ts` starts POST with `const blocked = await guard(req); if (blocked) return blocked;`, FAL_KEY-absent → 501, else `fal.subscribe("fal-ai/flux/schnell")`; **musically-timed** requests (≤1 in flight, ≥7 s apart). (3) **`field.ts`** — synthesized plasma + orbiting volumetric blobs + curl-flow particles + onset-bloom pulses, driven by the same `MusicalFrame` (the complete experience when no key/image). (4) **Image→audio loop-back** — 8×6 `getImageData` avg-color → lowpass/shimmer/reverb bend (CORS-taint try/catch → image-only). (5) **Real-audio chain** — `<audio crossOrigin="anonymous">` → `createMediaElementSource` → AnalyserNode → reverb/lowpass/compressor → destination; **all-zero-analyser detection** auto-swaps to the warm **`synth.ts`** stand-in (V7→I cadence, resolves, never atonal) so it's never blank/silent.

  References: **Refik Anadol** — *Unsupervised* / *Machine Hallucinations* (the data-pigment aesthetic, named in the prompt) · **Memo Akten** — *Learning to See* (the audio→image→audio feedback ancestor) · **Bello, Daudet, Abdallah, Duxbury, Davies & Sandler (2005)**, *"A Tutorial on Onset Detection in Music Signals"*, IEEE TSAP 13(5) — the half-wave-rectified spectral-flux + adaptive-median onset detector. Born from RESEARCH §367 (arXiv 2512.23320 *music-derived captions refined by cooperating agents* validates the music→prompt premise and is logged as the spine's cycle-2 hook; ~5 mo → #5 not earned). Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept — *a latent room that dreams Karel's real piano* — three technical attacks); siblings `447-piano-latent-room` (the faithful `441`-port on real audio — safest read, least differentiated) + `449-piano-latent-grains` (the image *re-composes* the piano as **granular re-synthesis** — most aggressive loop-back, rides loved `227`❤️/`323`❤️) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS §367, folders removed. **Ambition 3/5** (honest): **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle-1-of-a-planned-spine, the jury's dead-since-414 axis restarted) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5) + **#3** named references (Anadol / Akten / Bello 2005). **#1 NOT claimed** — image-gen, FFT and chroma all exist; the fresh move is *musically-structured* (phrase/harmony-driven) prompting on *real audio*. **Diversity** (vs JURY 2026-06-09 bans): **audio-file / real-recording INPUT** (0× in last-10 — the clean dodge of over-leaned touch [4×] + banned mic) · **AI-image OUTPUT** (1× — fresh; the canvas is only the AI-image compositor, NOT the jury-banned Canvas2D-as-the-piece) · **music-derived-caption + onset/phrase + chroma-key + audio-loopback TECHNIQUE** (dodges banned drum-machine percussion + Kuramoto) · **warm/expressive/resolves-on-purpose VIBE** (the jury's explicit "missing middle"; not the banned refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda). **Build-verified (exit 0, prerendered, guarded `/api` in manifest, `npx eslint` on the folder zero warnings), NOT browser-verified** (no FAL_KEY / no audio / no GPU in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether `/api/audio/549fc519…` resolves + the Supabase signed-URL CORS lets the AnalyserNode read non-zero from Karel's network (all-zero → synth fallback), whether the onset/phrase detector fires at musically-right moments on real expressive piano, whether ~1 image per phrase reads as "painting the music," the FAL CDN CORS for the loop-back (try/catch → image-only), iOS AudioContext-in-tap.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/448-piano-phrase-painter/README.md`

## Cycle 366 — **kids** build · DEEP, one big concept attacked 3 ways: *a child paints a living singing GALAXY of light by moving in front of the camera*; the winner is a **bare-hands WebGPU-compute galaxy** — 60k particles a 4-year-old cups, swirls and scatters with their hands, and it sings. Biggest kid-facing GPU concept in the lab; breaks the kids-percussion rut; dodges the count-banned touch input via camera + the cleanest renderer dodge, WebGPU 0× in the last-10

- **[/dream/444-kids-aurora-hands](/dream/444-kids-aurora-hands)** — Aurora Hands 🌌. `demoable` · Cycle 366.

  **For kids 4+ — cup, swirl, and scatter a huge living galaxy of light with your bare HANDS in the air, and the galaxy sings.** Wave your hands in front of the camera: their positions (via **MediaPipe HandLandmarker**, up to 2 hands × 21 landmarks, on-device) become moving gravity-wells and repulsors in a massive particle galaxy — **open hand pushes** the light outward into a nebula, **closed/pinched hand pulls** it into a glowing ball, **bring both hands together** → the particles converge and a warm consonant swell rises, **fling them apart** → an aurora scatters across the sky. No screen-touching; the instrument is the child's hands in the air. This is the **lab's biggest kid-facing GPU concept** (60 000 particles on a **WebGPU compute shader**; 8 000 on the **WebGL2 fallback**) and a deliberate break from the recent kids-percussion lane (`419` pose-beat / `423` face-beat / `426` euclid) — calm, luminous, continuous, cosmic. It rides the loved hands lineage (`234-kids-hand-creature`❤️) and the loved particle-galaxy lineage (`262-aurora-particle`❤️ · `236-particle-life-song`❤️ · `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️ · `107-ocean-presence`❤️).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **`hands.ts`** — MediaPipe HandLandmarker loaded at runtime from CDN (no bundle), camera analysis-only; an **attractor model** (open hand, openness>0.5 → repulsor strength −0.6; closed/pinched → attractor +0.8; influence radius scales with openness; smooth force falloff, no discontinuities) + a **ghost-hands** generator (1–2 autonomously-wandering attractors) that runs with no camera. (2) **`gpu.ts`** — a `createGalaxyRenderer()` factory that tries **WebGPU** (60k particles in GPU storage buffers; a **WGSL `@compute` shader** integrates velocities each frame — ambient curl-noise drift + up to 4 hand force-wells; WGSL render shader draws additive aurora-HSL billboards), falls back transparently to **WebGL2** (8k instanced `TRIANGLE_STRIP` quads, identical CPU physics, GLSL-300-es additive glow), and if neither is present returns a no-op so the UI still shows. (3) **`audio.ts`** — a continuous, **non-percussion** D-major-pentatonic (D E F# A B, no wrong notes) **chord-cloud**: 12 oscillator voices across two hand pools + an always-on detuned root drone with a fifth overtone (never silent); hand height → register, openness → voice density, x-position → `StereoPannerNode` pan (NOT HRTF — reads on phone speakers), hands-together → low-pass swell, total motion → loudness; a ~37s filter LFO (itself drifting) + a ~7-min drone-root drift (D→E→G→A) make minute 5 ≠ minute 0; everything through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (threshold −6 dB, ratio 20:1, master ≤0.25) so it can never blast small ears. (4) **input/auto-demo/lifecycle** in `page.tsx` — a tap-to-start overlay creates/resumes the `AudioContext` in-gesture (iOS-safe) and **ghost-hands begin immediately** so the full galaxy + music is alive with zero interaction; a ≥64px "✋ Turn on camera" button upgrades to real hand tracking; camera denied / model timeout (~4s) → stays in the gorgeous ghost-hands demo with a `text-rose-300` notice (never blank); a small top-right badge shows **WebGPU ✦** vs **WebGL2 ◈**; full GPU/audio/camera teardown on unmount. **No API route, no `guard` needed** — the only network is loading the MediaPipe model/wasm from its CDN; no Next.js route, no mic, no fetch-to-backend, no secrets, no FAL_KEY. Camera is analysis-only, never stored/drawn/transmitted (KIDS privacy rule). Zero new npm deps (MediaPipe is CDN-loaded; `three` not used).

  References: **MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (Google on-device 21-keypoint hand tracking via WebAssembly) · **MediaPipe → GPU particle systems** (the MediaPipe→NVIDIA-Flex interactive-particle work + three.js+MediaPipe hand-particle demos, derivative.ca community, 2026) · **Refik Anadol** (latent/particle-flow aurora aesthetic). Born from RESEARCH §366. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach kids fire (one concept — *paint a singing galaxy of light by moving in front of the camera* — three attacks: optical-flow / whole-body pose / hand-tracked WebGPU; alternating off cycle 365's WIDE); siblings `445-kids-light-river` (model-free **optical-flow** → 40k-particle **WebGL2** aurora, the most robust take) + `446-kids-star-swarm` (**MediaPipe PoseLandmarker** whole-body → ~29k **three.js** singing stars) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS §366, folders removed. **Ambition 2/5** (honest, no stretch): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (hand-tracking + WebGPU-compute galaxy + WebGL2 fallback + chord-cloud + ghost-demo = 5) + **#3** named references (HandLandmarker / MediaPipe→Flex / Anadol). **#1 NOT claimed** — hand-tracking, WebGPU compute (15 prior lab files) and particle systems all exist; the fresh move is the **bare-hands continuous calm GPU-scale *singing galaxy for kids***. **#5 NOT claimed** — the dive hits community tutorials, not a <14-day paper. **Diversity** (vs cycle bans): **camera hand-tracking INPUT** (the clean dodge of the count-banned touch [5×] + jury-banned mic) · **WebGPU-compute-particles OUTPUT** (0× in last-10 — the cleanest renderer dodge vs three.js 3× & WebGL2 3×; WebGL2 fallback present) · hand-driven attractor/repulsor GPU-particle TECHNIQUE (dodges Kuramoto) · kids-cosmic-continuous-NON-percussion VIBE (breaks the kids-percussion rut). **Build-verified (exit 0, `npx eslint` on the folder exit 0 / zero warnings), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/camera/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether WebGPU initializes + the 60k galaxy reads as "massive and alive" on Karel's hardware (WebGL2 fallback covers absence), whether the HandLandmarker CDN load + open/closed-hand openness heuristic tracks a 4-year-old's small hands reliably, whether the gather/scatter gesture *feels* magical vs. fiddly, `StereoPanner` + 12-voice balance on phone speakers, iOS AudioContext-in-tap, and the WebGPU-on-mobile-Safari path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/444-kids-aurora-hands/README.md`

## Cycle 362 — **kids** build · WIDE, 3 unrelated NON-percussion registers; the winner is a real **GPU fluid finger-paint** — a 4-year-old smears glowing liquid that swirls like ink in water and *hums*, breaking the recent kids-percussion rut and riding the most-loved paint + `84-wave-fluid`❤️ lineage

- **[/dream/433-kids-fluid-paint](/dream/433-kids-fluid-paint)** — Fluid Paint 🎨. `demoable` · Cycle 362.

  **For kids 4+ — finger-paint where the paint is REAL flowing liquid, and the motion of the fluid IS the music.** Pick one of five big (≥72px) emoji color swatches — violet · teal · amber · rose · cyan — then drag anywhere: glowing color pours out and **keeps swirling and blending like ink in water long after you lift your finger**, and the painting **hums** a soft, sustained, consonant chord that swells when you paint and settles when you rest. It is a deliberately **calm, dreamy, NON-percussion** kids instrument — the answer to the recent kids rut (the last several kids ships, `419` pose-beat / `423` face-beat / `429` noise-foley, were all rhythm/percussion) and to KIDS.md's named market gap (the *contemplative, parent-tolerable* option in a space full of loud high-energy toys). Rides the lab's most-loved lineage in one move: the **paint** family (`100`❤️/`104`❤️/`152`❤️/`160`❤️/`223`❤️) **and** `84-wave-fluid`❤️ (GPU fluid).

  Three subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **`fluid.ts`** — a faithful **Jos Stam "Stable Fluids" (SIGGRAPH 1999)** incompressible Navier-Stokes solver on **WebGL2** ping-pong framebuffer textures (RGBA 16/32-bit float): semi-Lagrangian **advect velocity** → **splat** Gaussian velocity+dye on drag (force ∝ drag speed) → **divergence** → **20-iteration Jacobi pressure solve** (`∇²p=∇·u`) → **gradient-subtract** (enforce `∇·u=0` so it swirls rather than expands) → **advect the separate dye field** by the divergence-free velocity → tone-mapped display (`1−exp(−dye·2)` soft-HDR, dark-suppressed). 128² velocity/pressure grid, 512² dye grid (the standard Stam real-time tradeoff). (2) **`audio.ts`** — sustained/evolving, **never percussion**: a five-oscillator open major-9 pad (C2 G2 E3 B3 D4, sine+triangle, ±4¢ detune) whose gain tracks a **JS-side energy accumulator** (`energy*=0.985`/frame — no GPU readback to stall the pipeline), the active swatch's **hue → lowpass cutoff** (cool=dark ~350 Hz, warm=bright ~3200 Hz — cross-modal color↔timbre), **drag-speed → 0.3–4.3 Hz tremolo LFO** (shimmer on fast strokes), a near-silent bandpassed noise bed (never silent), all through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−8 dB / 20:1) → master 0.22 (can't blast small ears). (3) **input/auto-demo/lifecycle** in `page.tsx` — pointer splat injected synchronously (<50 ms), a hands-free **auto-demo** at 1.8 s that strokes all five colors on a loop (a phone reviewer sees swirling glowing color with zero taps), a **tap-to-start** overlay creating/resuming the `AudioContext` in-gesture (iOS-safe), DPR-aware (capped 2×) `ResizeObserver` sizing, full teardown (`cancelAnimationFrame`, `sim.destroy()` deletes all GL resources + `WEBGL_lose_context`, `audio.destroy()`). Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 → readable `text-rose-300` notice; autoplay blocked → tap prompt. Camera/mic: **none**. **No API route, no `guard` needed — no network at all** (no mic, camera, fetch, secrets, FAL_KEY). Zero new npm deps.

  References: **Jos Stam, "Stable Fluids"** (SIGGRAPH 1999) — the foundational semi-Lagrangian incompressible solver this follows · **Mark Harris, "Fast Fluid Dynamics Simulation on the GPU"** (GPU Gems Ch. 38, NVIDIA 2004) — the canonical GPU/ping-pong-FBO mapping · **Pavel Dobryakov, WebGL-Fluid-Simulation** — the definitive browser lineage. Born from RESEARCH §362.
