Born from RESEARCH §378 (the dive's grep-audit killed three would-be re-treads — bowed-string already exists at `320-kids-light-loom`, kids-weather at `293-kids-sky-band`, modal membrane-drum at `202`/`284` — and surfaced the genuinely-open FDTD time-domain field). **Ambition 4/5** (the jury's named regression fix — climb to 4–5/5): **#1** never-used technique (time-domain FDTD 2-D wave-equation / digital-waveguide-mesh — grep-verified: the lab's membranes `202`/`284` are *modal* Bessel-mode banks with a cosmetic ripple, never a solved finite-difference field) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (4+, above) + **#3** named reference (Van Duyne & Smith 1993) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 1 of the Wave Field spine). **#5 NOT claimed** — the reference is foundational (1993), not <14 days; honest. **Diversity** (vs jury 2026-06-09 bans Canvas2D-OUTPUT · drum-machine/percussion-synth-TECH · refuse-to-resolve/clinical-Ikeda-VIBE · calm-cosmic-pentatonic-kids-template · AND the count-banned **WebGL2 OUTPUT** [5× in last-10: 475/474/460/456/451]): **multi-touch INPUT** · **three.js vertex-color OUTPUT** (dodges the banned Canvas2D AND the count-banned raw-WebGL2; three.js is 2× — under the count line) · **FDTD wave-field TECHNIQUE** (a brand-new physical-modeling primitive — dodges drum-machine AND the recent kids GPU-physics-sim rut: this is a *solved acoustic field you hear*, not a physics toy you watch) · **warm self-resolving water VIBE** (ripples spread then settle — neither refuse-to-resolve nor pentatonic-lullaby). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire fired off three grep-verified-fresh registers: siblings `479-kids-duet-bloom` (first cooperative two-player kids instrument — the chord only blooms when *both* play together; dual-input synchrony engine, SVG) + `480-kids-sing-bird` (SING and your pitch flies a bird along a melody contour; real-time YIN voice-pitch, SVG) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §378. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/478-kids-wave-pond` prerendered in the manifest; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU / audio / touch in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether a 64×64 grid stepped at 44.1 kHz in an AudioWorklet holds real-time on a low-end phone (builder flags it ~180 MFLOP/s — a production version would sub-rate the stepping or shrink the grid; ScriptProcessor fallback is heavier); whether the raw single-cell pickup reads as a pleasing "water-strike" vs. a low rumble behind the limiter (the pentatonic tone-synth layer carries the pitched warmth); whether tap-to-sound lands <50 ms (injection bypasses React state via a ref); the iOS AudioWorklet Blob-URL + AudioContext-in-gesture path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/478-kids-wave-pond/README.md`

- **[/dream/486-third-room](/dream/486-third-room)** — Third Room 🏛️. `demoable` · Cycle 381. **For**: adults / live performance.

  **Cycle 3 of the Resonant Room spine: a live-MIDI room split into a tritone clash — but now with a THIRD room a just-fifth above that shares Room A's harmonics, so you can negotiate the tritone into rest by routing it through a consonant intermediary, not just collapsing it.** Every played note feeds three parallel banks of N=8 high-Q biquad bandpass resonators: **Room A** (reference, panned −20° L, violet), **Room B** (the tritone interloper, +detune 0–600 ¢, default 600 ¢, +35° R, rose), and **Room C** (a **just-fifth, 702 ¢ = 3:2** above A, −35° L, emerald — its 2nd/4th/6th partials lock onto A's 3rd/6th/9th, so A↔C is intrinsically consonant). A **Room C blend slider** brings the bridge in from silence; the resolution gesture is *blend C in → find the pivot note where all three rooms momentarily agree → then collapse the detune*. A real-time **Plomp–Levelt / Sethares roughness** engine (computed over all cross-room partial pairs, C weighted by its blend) drives a Tension bar (Resolved → Settling → Tense → Dissonant → Clashing) and a **steepest-descent pivot-note hint layer** that analytically scans MIDI 48–84 each ~18 frames and glows the 2–3 notes that most lower total three-room roughness emerald on the keyboard — a partner that whispers crossroads without forcing one. Rendered as a **WebGPU compute vorticity field** (the lab's starved renderer): ~4,000 particles in a WGSL ping-pong compute shader, three room-attractors, roughness → per-room vorticity (Room C calms to laminar emerald as you find consonance while A↔B stays turbulent at full tritone), with a real Canvas2D three-attractor fallback. Live Web MIDI + on-screen + QWERTY input; 3-s hands-free auto-demo (plays at full tritone then blends C in); brick-wall limiter; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client-side Web Audio + Web MIDI + WebGPU/Canvas2D — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **Plomp & Levelt 1965** / **Sethares 1993** / **MacCallum & Einbond, "Real-Time Analysis of Sensory Dissonance," CMMR 2008** (the roughness model) · **Stautner & Puckette 1982** / **Jot & Chaigne 1991** (FDN/tuned-resonator lineage) · **just intonation 3:2 = 702 ¢** (the Pythagorean fifth — the consonant bridge). Born from RESEARCH §381 (the dive confirmed no fresh <14-day sensory-dissonance paper — the foundational MacCallum–Einbond real-time roughness model stays the right tool — and positively bound the renderer: WebGPU compute particle fields with ping-pong buffers [ShaderVine's 16 compute sims, nibi's TSL flow patterns] are the live 2026 register). **Ambition 4/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (live Web MIDI + **three** coupled resonator rooms + real-time roughness analysis + steepest-descent pivot-note suggestion engine + WebGPU compute vorticity field = 5) + **#3** named refs (above) + **#4** multi-cycle commitment (cycle 3 of the Resonant Room spine — the truest *extension*: one room [475] → two [483] → three) + (#5 honestly NOT claimed — refs foundational, no <14-day binding; #1 NOT claimed — WebGPU compute exists at `444`, roughness at `483`). **This directly answers the JURY (2026-06-10): #1** bring genuine tension the listener resolves *through action* (blend → pivot → collapse, never auto-resolved), **#2** *deepen* an existing lab-first into cycle 3 by extension rather than grabbing a 5th DSP primitive, **#3** ban three.js + pick a starved renderer (**WebGPU**, 0× in the last 10; dodges the WebGL2 4× count-ban). **Diversity** `picked=[**live-MIDI INPUT** (starved, jury's pick, spine continuity) · **WebGPU compute OUTPUT** (0× starved, jury-flagged; dodges banned three.js + WebGL2-4× count-ban) · **three-room tritone-vs-just-fifth negotiation + real-time roughness TECHNIQUE** (a spine *extension*, not a new primitive — dodges the jury's "first-DSP-primitive" treadmill) · **cold/ambiguous-tension-you-resolve-through-action VIBE** (the jury's exact #1 ask — the opposite of the 15× warm-consonant monoculture)]`. **Love signal:** `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️ (a loved GPU-compute particle piece) softly backs the WebGPU vorticity field; `105-pluck-field`❤️ / `140-kids-string-bridge`❤️ back a resonator piece — additive, the jury + spine governed. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one cycle-3 concept — *deepen 483 with a steepest-descent partner + the starved WebGPU renderer* — three resolution mechanics): siblings `485-resolving-room` (the whisper-panel note-hint engine + detune gradient arrow) + `487-guided-descent` (a roughness *landscape* with a shared-control "descend toward rest" autopilot) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §381 as cycle-4 fold-ins. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/486-third-room/page` in `app-paths-manifest.json`; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean — one unused eslint-disable directive removed as the single fix), NOT browser-verified** (no MIDI device / GPU / audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the analytically-predicted pivot notes actually *sound* resolved to the ear (the Plomp–Levelt model is an approximation, not ground truth — players may explore a few candidates); whether 3×8 high-Q biquads per held note stays clean polyphonically behind the limiter; whether the just-fifth A↔C consonance reads as a genuine *bridge* vs. just more sound on phone speakers; `requestMIDIAccess` + hotplug on Karel's controller (on-screen/QWERTY fallback covers no-device); whether WebGPU initializes on Karel's review phone (Canvas2D fallback covers no-`navigator.gpu`); the iOS AudioContext-in-gesture path.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/486-third-room/README.md`

### [493-kids-feeling-forest](/dream/493-kids-feeling-forest) · kids · cycle 384 · demoable
  **Cross a glowing "feeling forest" with your BODY — every place sounds like a different feeling, and you find calm (or stay in the bittersweet middle) by *moving*, not by pressing a "resolve" button.** The lab's first **explorable continuous harmony-tension MAP**: the screen is a top-down world; a glowing spirit sits at the child's position; **6 mood anchors** — Storm (m2+tritone+m9 cluster, 22¢ beating, sawtooth, fast tremolo, 380 Hz LP), Electric (aug+dom7), Bittersweet sus2 center, Dream whole-tone, Sweet major, Calm (open fifth+octave, 0¢ detune, no tremolo, 2200 Hz) — are blended at every position by **inverse-distance-weighting (power 2)**. Chord voicing (fractional semitone offsets — no scale-snapping), detune, oscillator timbre, per-voice filter cutoff, and tremolo rate all morph **continuously**, so dissonance↔consonance is a smooth **spatial gradient the child drives by walking**. There is no pre-baked cadence — tension is the subject; you resolve it (or dwell in it) through navigation. **Navigation by BODY/camera (primary, the jury's starved depth-camera input):** MediaPipe PoseLandmarker (loaded from CDN at runtime, no npm dep) maps the child's mirrored body/nose position onto the canvas; **drag/pointer fallback** if camera denied (`text-rose-300` banner) or MediaPipe CDN fails; **always-on auto-demo** wanders prickly→electric→bittersweet→sweet→calm→dreamy (~14 s loop) from page load so the sandbox always makes evolving sound + visuals hands-free; any touch/camera input stops it. **Canvas2D only** (8×8 mood-tinted grid — cold/jittering where dissonant, warm/steady where calm — + glowing creature that vibrates & sprouts spiky rays in tense zones, trail, tension-keyed particles, tiny live camera-position indicator). DynamicsCompressor limiter (−6 dBFS) so it can never get harsh-loud; AudioContext created inside the Start gesture (iOS). **No API route, no `guard`** (Web Audio + Canvas2D + on-device camera; the only network is the MediaPipe CDN + model; no mic-audio leaves the browser, no secrets). No new npm deps.

  Reference: **Chu, A., Smith, J. B. & Pardo, B., *FXplorer: A Map-Based Interface for Exploratory Audio Effect Design*, arXiv 2606.08286 (NIME 2026, listed 2026-06-09)** — replace menus/sliders with a continuous 2-D map you *navigate*; discover sound by *wandering*. This adapts it for pre-readers: the map encodes harmony+tension (not effect params) and the **body** is the cursor. Born from RESEARCH §384 (the dive *implemented* a <14-day finding — the missing 5th criterion the jury said kept every recent piece at 4/5). **Ambition 5/5**: **#1** never-used technique (explorable continuous harmony-tension map navigated by movement — grep-verified lab-first; `148-spatial-palette` is a pan/pitch *arranger*, `410-conchordal-garden` is autonomous a-life, neither is a navigable mood field) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (MediaPipe pose camera input + IDW harmony-morph synth + Canvas2D field/creature renderer + auto-demo wanderer = 4) + **#3** named ref (FXplorer) + **#4** multi-cycle (cycle 1 of a new **Sound-Map / explorable-harmony kids spine**) + **#5** binds a <14-day finding (FXplorer, 2026-06-09 — implemented, not negatively-steered). **Answers the JURY (2026-06-10): #1** brings genuine tension the listener resolves *through action* (a dissonant region you walk out of, never a pre-baked V–I) — breaking the 15× warm-consonant monoculture; **#3** picks a *starved* input (**depth-camera, 0×**) and a fresh renderer (**Canvas2D**, 1× in last 10 — dodges banned three.js + WebGL2-4× count-ban) — and is the **first build to claim #5/hit 5/5** in the window (the jury: "no piece hit 5/5 because the dive never binds"). **Diversity** `banned=[touch INPUT (~5×), three.js + WebGL2-4× OUTPUT, FAL-latent + real-piano + DSP-physical-modeling-treadmill + physics-sim TECHNIQUE, warm-consonant-resolves VIBE] · picked=[**body/camera-pose INPUT** (starved depth-camera, jury's pick; love-aligned — 104❤️/101❤️/234❤️) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (fresh, dodges three.js + WebGL2) · **explorable-harmony-map / IDW-morph TECHNIQUE** (lab-first, not a DSP-primitive farm) · **tension-gradient-you-resolve-by-moving VIBE** (the jury's exact #1 ask)]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** camera-driven kids pieces are loved (`104-kids-mirror-draw`❤️, `101-camera-song`❤️, `234-kids-hand-creature`❤️) — additive backing for body-navigation; the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed the pick. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach kids fire (one Sound-Map concept, three navigation inputs): siblings `494-kids-weather-walk` (drag baseline — lost on the banned touch input) + `495-kids-singing-valley` (voice→XY via hand-written YIN — strong, banked) are complete build-reviewed implementations in IDEAS §384. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/493-kids-feeling-forest` 5.91 kB prerendered static), NOT browser-verified** (no camera/audio/GPU in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether MediaPipe pose tracks a small child held close (HandLandmarker may track better — flagged for cycle 2); whether the dissonant→consonant gradient *reads* on phone speakers behind the limiter; whether a 4yo connects body-motion to the sound; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture + MediaPipe Blob/WASM load. The always-on auto-demo + drag fallback are the safety nets so absent camera never blocks the 06:30 demo.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/493-kids-feeling-forest/README.md`

### [496-voyager-room](/dream/496-voyager-room) · adults / live performance · cycle 385 · demoable
  **You don't play notes — you CONDUCT a room of five autonomous machine musicians who listen to each other and to you, leaning the collective harmony into deliberate dissonance and releasing it back to consonance.** Press **Start / Conduct the room** and five named agents are already improvising together: **VEGA** (Leader — states the root motif), **LYRA** (Harmonist — roughness-*minimizing*), **DRACO** (Provocateur — roughness-*raising*, tritone/m2-biased, dial-weighted), **ECHO** (Follower — imitates the loudest peer transposed, drawing the lit answering edge), **NEBULA** (Drifter — whole-tone pad). You shape them with a **TENSION dial** (consonance⇄dissonance, the primary control), **TEMPO**, and **spotlight** (tap an orb to make it lead). All notes snap to **D dorian** so dissonance is always a *layered choice*, never noise. This is the lab's **first multi-agent autonomous machine-improviser ensemble** (every prior improviser — 251/256/375/380/391/210/298 — is a *single* partner that follows YOU; this is a room of agents with their own will that you conduct) and the new **Machine Ensemble / Voyager spine**, cycle 1.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5+): (1) **5 per-agent probabilistic generators** (`agents.ts`) — each with its own personality bias, timbre (oscillator + partial set), stereo pan, color. (2) **Shared listening buffer** — every due agent reads the *previous frame's* collective events. (3) **Real-time Plomp–Levelt/Sethares sensory-roughness steering** (`roughness.ts`) — each candidate note is scored by the predicted roughness *if it joined the currently-sounding partials*, then steered (softmax-sampled) toward the conductor's tension target; Harmonist pulls roughness down, Provocateur pushes it up. (4) **Frame-synchronous beat clock** — agents emit in lock-step (StreamMUSE's frame-synchronous streaming inference, on-device). (5) **Conducting UI** + (6) **Canvas2D constellation** — orbs size/brightness = activity, lit edges = imitation, center tension-ring (Resolved·Settling·Tense·Clashing) + live roughness, orbs sprout spikes when tense, beat pulse. Always-on **auto-conduct** runs a ~20 s settle→build→release arc so the room is alive hands-free on load. `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture; audio-only graceful degrade if Canvas2D null. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client synthesis — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **George Lewis, _Voyager_ (1980s–present)** — the canonical autonomous machine-improviser "virtual orchestra" that listens and responds with its own musical will (the machines have agency; the human conducts a collective, not a tool). **StreamMUSE — "Real-Time Language Model Jamming: A Case Study for Live Music Accompaniment Generation," Bowen Zheng et al., arXiv 2606.11886 (submitted 2026-06-10)** — *frame-synchronous streaming inference*, implemented on-device as the lock-step generation loop. **Plomp & Levelt 1965 / Sethares 1998** — the sensory-roughness model. Born from RESEARCH §385 (the dive *implemented* a <14-day finding and logged two negative results: the JURY-flagged WebRTC-multi-user lane is *occupied* by 319/334/341 BroadcastChannel collab, and the responsive-accompanist lane is *saturated* — both rejected as "too similar" traps, triangulating onto the genuine gap). **Ambition 5/5:** **#1** never-used technique (multi-agent autonomous machine-improviser ensemble you conduct — grep-clean) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5+) + **#3** named refs (Voyager / StreamMUSE / Plomp–Levelt–Sethares) + **#4** multi-cycle (Machine Ensemble spine cycle 1) + **#5** binds a <14-day finding (StreamMUSE 2026-06-10 — implemented, not cited). The window's 2nd 5/5. **Answers JURY 2026-06-10:** **#1** genuine tension the listener resolves *through action* (conduct the dial — never a pre-baked cadence; breaks the 15× warm-consonant monoculture) · **#3** bans three.js (Canvas2D) and picks a fresh non-saturated direction. **Diversity** `banned=[three.js / AI-image-FAL / real-piano / DSP-physical-modeling-first + physics-sim / warm-consonant-resolves] · picked=[**conductor-gesture INPUT** (dial/tap — fresh) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (dodges three.js + WebGL2-4× count) · **multi-agent-autonomous-improvisation TECH** (not a DSP-primitive farm) · **tension-you-conduct-and-resolve VIBE** (the JURY's exact #1 ask)]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** `236-particle-life-song`❤️ (a-life agents) + `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️ softly back an autonomous-agent ensemble; additive, the JURY + gates governed. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-agent-brain adult fire (one concept, three machine-ensemble brains): siblings `497-call-ring` (ring-canon of imitation, SPREAD knob, Canvas2D) + `498-swarm-score` (48-agent circle-of-fifths consensus + tritone dissidents, WebGL2) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §385 as the spine's cycle-2/3 candidates. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `/dream/496-voyager-room` in `app-paths-manifest.json`; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean), NOT browser-verified** (no audio/GPU in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the tension dial *audibly* swings consonance↔dissonance at typical chord density (roughness-normalization constants are empirical, may need a numeric tweak); the by-ear balance of the five voices (saw/square brightness, drifter pad level); `setTimeout`-chained clock jitter under load; phone tap-to-spotlight ergonomics; iOS audio-unlock. Auto-conduct + audio-only degrade are the safety nets so the 06:30 demo always makes evolving sound.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/496-voyager-room/README.md`

### [500-kids-aurora-tilt](/dream/500-kids-aurora-tilt) · kids (4+) · cycle 386 · demoable
  **Tilt a tablet to bend a glowing aurora — tilt it far and the sky goes tense, shimmery and electric-violet; hold it flat and still and the lights widen, warm, and the music resolves home. The child holds all the tension in their hands and chooses, at any moment, to let it go.** A kids piece whose whole subject is **tension the listener resolves *through action*** (the jury's exact #1 ask) — embodied as the simplest possible gesture: level the device. No reading, no menus, no "right answer"; just a beautiful sky that calms when you hold steady.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **device-tilt / gyro input** — `deviceorientation` (`beta`/`gamma`), one-pole smoothed (α=0.08), iOS 13+ `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` called inside the Start gesture; denied/missing → **pointer-drag fallback** (drag sets virtual β/γ). (2) **WebGL2 domain-warp aurora shader** — raw GLSL ES 3.00 full-screen fragment shader using Iñigo Quilez's recursive `fbm(p + fbm(p + fbm(p)))` domain-warp; three aurora layers + wisps whose fold sharpness, flow speed, fBm octave count (3→6), lacunarity and palette (warm-green/teal → electric violet/magenta) all scale with tension; deep-indigo sky + deterministic star field; rendered at 65% DPR for perf; WebGL2-absent → `text-rose-300` notice + audio continues. (3) **evolving modal pad synth** — four sine+triangle voice pairs that voice-lead from an open-fifth+octave home voicing (C3 G3 C4 G4) to a sus4/add9 "yearning but never ugly" tense voicing (C3 F3 D4 B♭4) via `setTargetAtTime`, with tilt-scaled tremolo depth/rate on the upper voices; DynamicsCompressor brick-wall limiter (−6 dB, 20:1) + 8.8 kHz lowpass (kids-safe); AudioContext created inside the Start gesture. (4) **tilt-tension engine** — `tension = clamp(√(β²+γ²)/45°, 0, 1)` from the smoothed 2-D tilt vector, fed every frame to BOTH the shader uniforms and the audio params so visual and harmonic tension move as one. (5) **always-on auto-demo** — a hands-free Lissajous wander (`sin(t·0.31)·28°`, `sin(t·0.19+1.2)·22°`) drives the aurora + music on load with **zero permissions**, cancelled on the first real tilt/drag — so a no-touch 06:30 phone glance always shows it singing and moving. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client-side Web Audio + WebGL2 — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  Reference: **Iñigo Quilez, "Domain Warping"** (iquilezles.org/articles/warp) — the recursive `fbm(p + fbm(p + fbm(p)))` technique that yields organic curtain/cloud structure; the visual metaphor echoes real aurora-borealis physics (charged particles folded along magnetic field lines). Born from RESEARCH §386 (the dive's honest meta-finding: at ~360 prototypes the lab has exhausted easy all-time kids #1-lab-firsts, so the floor is cleared via #2+#3 and the cycle competes on surprise + diversity + execution). **Ambition 2/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5, above) + **#3** named reference (iq Domain Warping). (#1 NOT claimed — domain warping / tilt / aurora all exist in the lab individually; the fresh element is the *tilt-as-tension-dial resolve-by-leveling* interaction, an interaction pattern, not a new primitive — honest. #5 NOT claimed — ref is foundational, no <14-day binding.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-10:** **#1** genuine tension the listener resolves *through action* (level the device — never a pre-baked cadence; opposite of the 15× warm-consonant monoculture) · **#3** bans three.js (raw WebGL2) and dodges the count-banned Canvas2D (4× last-10). **Diversity** `banned=[touch INPUT; Canvas2D-4× + three.js OUTPUT; AI-image/FAL + real-piano + DSP-physical-modeling-first + physics-sim TECH; warm-consonant-resolves VIBE] · picked=[**device-tilt/gyro INPUT** (fresh, 0× last-10) · **WebGL2-shader OUTPUT** (2× last-10, dodges Canvas2D + three.js) · **domain-warp-aurora + tilt-tension TECH** · **tension-by-tilt-resolved-by-leveling VIBE** (the jury's #1)]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** `262-aurora-particle`❤️ (aurora palette) + `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️ (tilt input) softly back the pick; additive — the gates governed. **Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer kids fire** (three orthogonal fresh-tag directions): siblings `499-kids-sky-bells` (the **real live sky singing** — Open-Meteo weather → WebGL2 soundscape; highest surprise + the starved real-world-data menu category, but observational tension → better as an adult/all-ages piece) + `501-kids-hum-garden` (bloom an SVG flower by humming **steadily**, not on-target — freshest resolution mechanic, but child-voice YIN unverified) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §386. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `/dream/500-kids-aurora-tilt` prerendered static 6.03 kB; `npx eslint --max-warnings=0` on the folder clean — one react-hooks/exhaustive-deps ref-cleanup warning fixed via `const live = liveRef.current`), NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/gyro/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the 3-layer domain-warp aurora (~72 noise evals/px, 65% DPR) holds ≥30 fps on an older mobile GPU; whether `deviceorientation` + iOS `requestPermission` works on Karel's review phone (pointer-drag + auto-demo cover no-gyro); whether the chord *audibly* swings home↔tense behind the limiter on phone speakers; the iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock. Auto-demo (no permission) + drag fallback + WebGL2-absent audio-only are the safety nets so the 06:30 demo always shows evolving sound + light.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/500-kids-aurora-tilt/README.md`

### [502-atmosphere-veil](/dream/502-atmosphere-veil) · adults / ambient / live data · cycle 387 · demoable
  **The entire planet's *live* weather, breathing as one sustained, unresolved chord — and the dissonance only relaxes when the real sky calms, never on a timer, never on a tap.** A long-form (8+ minute), ever-evolving ADULT ambient instrument. Press **Start** once (AudioContext gesture) and leave it: a sustained spectral drone breathes under a WebGPU compute-driven field of ~60,000 particles riding a global wind field, and **both** the harmonic tension and the visual turbulence track the same scalar — **global atmospheric instability** read live from the real weather. This is **cycle 3 of the Living Earth spine** (earthquakes → space-weather → atmosphere; after `463-terra-gamelan` and `471-helios-orbit`).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **live real-world-data engine** (`weather.ts`) — ONE batched Open-Meteo request for 12 globe-spanning cities (London/NY/Tokyo/Sydney/Moscow/Singapore/São Paulo/Mexico City/Delhi/Nairobi/Reykjavík/Ushuaia), polled every 75 s, fetched directly client-side (no API route, no secret), with previous-poll pressure memory so a **falling barometer** (the classic building-storm tell) adds tension; per-city instability = wind(0.40) + low/falling-pressure(0.35 +0.25 bonus) + cloud(0.25); global tension = 0.6·mean + 0.4·peak so one violent storm reads against a calm planet. (2) **sustained-tension drone synth** (`audio.ts`) — open fifths+octaves (just-intonation ratios) at rest; as tension rises, partials detune into audible **beating** (±14¢), **tension tones** (m2 16/15, tritone 45/32, b9) fade in on a quadratic curve, and a roughness tremolo deepens; relaxation is **asymmetric** (rises faster than it falls) and the smoother only ever moves toward the data's value — **no scheduled cadence anywhere**. (3) **WebGPU compute particle field** (`gpu.ts`) — a compute shader advects ~60k particles through 12 city "vortices" (swirl ∝ instability) + tension-scaled value-noise turbulence; speed/turbulence/color-temp (cool indigo → warm amber/rose) all climb with tension; `navigator.gpu` absent → `text-rose-300` notice + a Canvas2D dot-field fallback driven by the same wind model, audio always continues. (4) **long-form memory** — the tonal center drifts ±70¢ on a very slow sinusoid and a voicing-mutation phase slowly re-weights the partials, so **minute 8 differs from minute 1** even at identical tension; it never loops. Plus an **always-on synthetic-weather auto-demo** (per-city sinusoids + a low-pressure system that drifts across and wraps the planet) that drives sound + light from the first frame and on any fetch failure, with an honest `LIVE`↔`DEMO DATA` HUD — so a hands-free 06:30 phone glance always shows it evolving. `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (−6 dB / 12:1) + ≤0.5 master gain, 4 s fade-in (intentionally quiet). **No API route, no `guard`** (Open-Meteo fetched directly from the client; no mic, camera, or secrets). No new npm deps (`@webgpu/types` is existing dev-only typings).

  Reference: **Éliane Radigue** (ARP-2500 works — *Trilogie de la Mort*, *Adnos* — where slow sustained tones' beating interference patterns *are* the music; this borrows that beating-as-tension language) + spectral-tradition thinking after **Gérard Grisey** (*Partiels* — consonance/dissonance as a continuous instability-driven axis, not chord changes). **Ambition 2/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (4, above) + **#4** multi-cycle (Living Earth spine cycle 3). (#1 NOT claimed — WebGPU, long-form, and external-API sonification all already exist in the lab individually; the fresh element is *atmosphere-as-sustained-tension*, a concept not a new primitive — honest. #5 NOT claimed — today's arxiv cs.SD (Jun 11) is server-side ML only, nothing client-implementable; said plainly rather than gamed.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-10:** brings genuine **sustained, unresolved harmonic tension the data resolves** (breaks the 15× warm-consonant monoculture — provocation #1) · **deepens an existing spine** instead of farming a new DSP primitive (provocation #2) · picks the **starved real-world-data input** and the **starved WebGPU output** (1× in the window), explicitly dodging the banned three.js + the over-represented raw-WebGL2 (4×) + Canvas2D (4×) (provocation #3). **Diversity** `banned=[touch + real-piano INPUT; three.js + Canvas2D-4× + raw-WebGL2-4× OUTPUT; AI-image/FAL + physics-sim + DSP-physical-modeling-first TECH; warm-consonant-resolves VIBE] · picked=[**live-weather-API INPUT** (real-world-data, starved menu category) · **WebGPU-compute OUTPUT** (starved 1×, dodges three.js/WebGL2/Canvas2D) · **live-data-sonification + spectral-tension TECH** (not a DSP-primitive farm) · **sustained-unresolved-atmospheric-tension-the-data-resolves VIBE** (the jury's #1)]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** `262-aurora-particle`❤️ (atmospheric particle palette) + `233-earth-pulse`/`314-solar-wind` lineage softly back a planetary-data particle field; additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed the pick. **Winner of a DEEP 3-approach adult fire** (one atmosphere concept, three renderers): siblings `503-atmosphere-front` (WebGL2 raymarched **volumetric storm-clouds** + lightning-on-rise — lost on the diversity audit, WebGL2 over-represented) + `504-atmosphere-isobar` (Canvas2D **isobar/pressure-gradient instrument**, Ikeda data-grid — the most rigorous data→tension math but rendered in banned Canvas2D) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §387. **Build-verified (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `/dream/502-atmosphere-veil` prerendered; folder `tsc`+`eslint` clean per builder), NOT browser-verified** (no WebGPU/audio/network in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the WGSL compute/render pipelines compile + run on a real WebGPU device (binding layouts/vertex strides may need a first-run tweak; Canvas2D fallback covers WebGPU-absent); whether Open-Meteo's live payload shape + CORS resolve from Karel's browser (synthetic auto-demo covers fetch failure); whether the instability thresholds (tuned on m/s) read as a real calm↔storm arc against live values; whether the drone *audibly* swings consonance↔dissonance behind the limiter; the iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock. Synthetic auto-demo + Canvas2D fallback + audio-only degrade are the safety nets so the 06:30 demo always shows evolving sound + light.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/502-atmosphere-veil/README.md`

### [505-kids-pulse-bridge](/dream/505-kids-pulse-bridge) · kids (4+) · cycle 388 · demoable
  **The music can only resolve home when two children tap *together* — when their two separate pulses lock into the same shared rhythm, a bridge of light completes between them and the chord blooms warm.** The screen splits into two giant tap-pads (warm **gold** left, cool **teal** right), one child per side. Each taps a steady beat; a glowing ring blooms on every tap. Out of sync, the two voices hold a yearning major-2nd and the bridge between the halves is broken into separate sparks — *not wrong, just "not yet together."* As the two players lock into the same rhythm, the voices voice-lead home to a warm open fifth, the bridge completes into a continuous glowing arc, and fireflies stream across it. There is **no wrong move and no fail state** — out of sync is "not yet," in sync is "together ✨." This is the lab's **first piece where synchrony *between two humans* gates the harmonic resolution** (every prior two-finger kids piece — `484-two-suns` spatial blend, `489-pond-pair` energy transfer — maps the fingers independently; none measures whether the two players are *in time with each other*), and cycle 1 of the new **"Together" spine**.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **dual pointer tracking** — Pointer-Events `pointerId` + pointer-capture so two fingers on two halves register simultaneously; each side's recent inter-tap intervals → a mean tempo. (2) **synchrony engine** — each player is a phase oscillator `φ(t) = 2π·((t−lastTap) mod ITV)/ITV`; the **Kuramoto order parameter** `R = |Σ exp(i·(φ_L−φ_R))| / N` over a sliding window measures phase lock, gated by a tempo-similarity factor (penalised above 1.25×) and compressed `R^0.65` so partial sync still feels good, then **asymmetrically smoothed** (τ_rise 1.8 s / τ_fall 3.2 s) so a lock must be *sustained* but a single late tap doesn't collapse it. (3) **adaptive voice-leading synth** — two pluck voices `setTargetAtTime`-glide from a yearning A3/B3 major-2nd toward a warm C4/G4 open fifth **as synchrony rises**, with a C–E–G arrival shimmer on the lock-threshold crossing; a C2+G2 ambient pad always underneath so it's never silent. (4) **Canvas2D bridge + firefly renderer** — a cubic-bezier light bridge broken/dim at low sync → continuous glowing arc with particles flowing across + warm radial bloom at lock; expanding tap rings. (5) **26 s auto-demo** — two simulated tappers walk an apart→converging→locked→drifting arc driving all subsystems hands-free on load (cancels on first real tap), so a no-touch 06:30 glance always shows a completing bridge. `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter + ≤9 kHz lowpass (kids-safe); AudioContext in the Start gesture (iOS unlock); Canvas2D-null → audio continues + `text-rose-300` notice. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client synthesis — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). No new npm deps.

  References: **The Moving Mandala** (Carreras et al., *Int. J. of Clinical and Health Psychology*, 2025–26) — rhythmic music as a *temporal scaffold* for child interpersonal synchrony and prosocial closeness; the finding that *shared rhythmic entrainment promotes felt closeness between children* is exactly why lock-in (not a score, not a reward) is the resolution here. **"Finding Our Tempo: Exploring Embodied Synchrony Through Full-Body Play in Children"** (TEI 2026) — synchrony must be *felt, not read*. Born from RESEARCH §388 (the dive's convergent 2025–26 finding that two children coming into sync is the highest-value musical interaction for them). **Ambition 4/5**: **#1** never-used technique (interpersonal two-human synchrony / Kuramoto phase-coupling gating resolution — grep-clean) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5, above) + **#3** named refs (Moving Mandala / Finding Our Tempo) + **#4** multi-cycle ("Together" spine cycle 1). (#5 NOT claimed — the cited papers are weeks–months old, not <14 days; said plainly.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-10:** genuine tension the players resolve **through action** (playing in time together — never a pre-baked cadence; the resolution is withheld until synchrony, breaking the 15× warm-consonant monoculture) and dodges the banned three.js. **Diversity** `banned=[three.js OUTPUT · AI-image/FAL + DSP-physical-modeling-first + physics-sim TECH · real-piano INPUT · warm-consonant-resolves VIBE] · picked=[**two-child-multitouch-tap INPUT** (fresh, 0× last-10) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (not in the hard-ban list) · **interpersonal-synchrony/Kuramoto TECH** (fresh, not a DSP-primitive farm) · **prosocial-tension-resolved-by-playing-TOGETHER VIBE** (the jury's #1)]` — no hard-ban tag. **Love signal:** `83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️ + `98-kids-drum-circle`❤️ (embodied/rhythmic kids play) softly back a two-player rhythmic-synchrony piece; additive — the gates governed. **Winner of a DEEP 3-approach kids fire** (one "Together" concept, three synchrony inputs): siblings `506-kids-orbit-meet` (continuous **motion**-phase Kuramoto orbits → braided mandala) + `507-kids-hum-meet` (**voice**-pitch-lock to a virtual partner via child-tuned YIN, in SVG) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §388. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `/dream/505-kids-pulse-bridge` prerendered static 5.44 kB, in `app-paths-manifest`), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio/multitouch in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the 0.82 lock-threshold is reachable by a real 4-year-old's ±300 ms timing jitter (the 0.65 compressive curve + 25% tempo tolerance + 3-tap window may need loosening); by-ear balance of major-2nd tension vs open-fifth resolution; two-children-one-phone ergonomics (designed for iPad — each half ≈45 mm on a 6" phone); iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock. The 26 s permission-free auto-demo is the safety net so the 06:30 demo always shows a completing bridge + evolving sound.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/505-kids-pulse-bridge/README.md`

### [508-accord-call](/dream/508-accord-call) · adults / multi-user / live · cycle 389 · demoable
  **Two people in different places, each holding one voice — and the harmony resolves only when *both* move toward agreement, over a real peer-to-peer connection with no server in the middle.** Each browser is one continuous voice (vertical pointer-drag sets pitch in a ~130–520 Hz just-intonation field); each sends its pitch over an `RTCDataChannel` ~20×/s and synthesizes BOTH voices locally. The **Plomp–Levelt / Sethares sensory-roughness** between the two voices drives everything: far from a consonant interval = audible beating + a jagged red "tension cord"; cooperate into unison/fourth/fifth/third/octave = the roughness drops, a shimmer pad blooms, and the cord becomes a smooth golden arc. **Neither player can resolve it alone** — the resolution is literally inaccessible to one will. This is the lab's **first true WebRTC peer-to-peer piece** (grep-verified: `RTCPeerConnection`/`createOffer`/`setRemoteDescription` appear in zero prior prototypes — the existing "collab" pieces 319/334/341 are same-origin **BroadcastChannel**, not a real peer connection), and **cycle 2 of the "Together" spine** (388's two-children-in-sync, now crossed from one-device-two-fingers to two-people-two-cities).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **WebRTC P2P signaling** (`webrtc.ts`) — pure `RTCPeerConnection`, no server/relay/WebSocket: "Create room" → `createOffer` → wait for ICE-gathering-complete (8 s cap) → copyable SDP blob; peer `joinRoom(offer)` → `createAnswer` → copyable answer blob; creator `receiveAnswer` completes the handshake. STUN `stun:stun.l.google.com:19302`; an **unreliable, unordered** `RTCDataChannel` carries `{pitch:0..1}` at ~20 Hz (UDP-like, drops stale frames instead of queueing). (2) **dual-voice Web Audio synth** (`audio.ts`) — two 5-partial harmonic voices (1/k amplitudes) with stereo pan + slow tremolo; a 4-sine shimmer pad blooms via `shimmerGain` as consonance rises; `setVoicePitch` ramps via `setTargetAtTime`; `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (≈ −6 dB) on master; `AudioContext` created inside the Start gesture (iOS unlock). (3) **Plomp–Levelt/Sethares roughness model** (`roughness.ts`) — pairwise partial roughness `r = a₁a₂(e^(−b₁·s·df) − e^(−b₂·s·df))`, `s = 0.24/(0.0207·fMin + 18.96)`, summed over both harmonic series and normalized 0..1; physically grounded, not an aesthetic heuristic. (4) **SVG renderer + rAF loop** (`page.tsx`) — two glowing orbs (yours warm-gold, theirs cool-teal) positioned by pitch, a tension cord that morphs jagged-red↔smooth-gold, ambient glow filters; attributes updated directly in a requestAnimationFrame loop (no React re-render). **Always-on practice partner:** on load / whenever no peer is connected, a simulated bot voice wanders and drifts toward consonant JI ratios 60% of the time so a SOLO visitor (and the hands-free 06:30 glance) hears + sees the full dissonance→consonance arc with zero setup; labeled honestly ("practice partner — no one's connected yet") and yields the instant a real peer's data channel opens. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + SVG + P2P data channel — no mic, camera, server, or secrets; the only network is the STUN handshake + the direct peer link). No new npm deps.

  References: **Plomp, R. & Levelt, W. J. M. (1965)**, "Tonal consonance and critical bandwidth," *JASA* 38(4) · **Sethares, W. A. (1998)**, *Tuning, Timbre, Spectrum, Scale* (the roughness formulation) · **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening* (1988/2005)** — mutual, attentive sonic negotiation between players across space as the conceptual spine. Born from RESEARCH §389 (the dive's honest cs.SD meta-finding — this week's audio ML is server-side only, so #5 is unclaimable — pivoted to the platform read: WebRTC is now native across >85% of browsers, and the JURY 2026-06-10 named WebRTC-multi-user as the 0× starved input in both provocations #3 and #5). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (true WebRTC `RTCPeerConnection` P2P — grep-verified lab-first; BroadcastChannel ≠ peer connection) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (4, above) + **#4** multi-cycle ("Together" spine cycle 2). (#3 named refs are foundational not the claim's backbone here; #5 NOT claimed — no <14-day finding implemented, said plainly.) **Answers JURY 2026-06-10:** genuine **tension the players resolve through action** (cooperate into consonance over the network — never a pre-baked cadence; breaks the 15× warm-consonant monoculture) · **deepens an existing spine** instead of farming a new DSP primitive (provocation #2) · picks the **explicitly-named starved input** WebRTC-multi-user (provocations #3 + #5) · bans three.js (SVG output). **Diversity** `banned=[touch + real-piano INPUT; three.js + Canvas2D-4× + raw-WebGL2-4× OUTPUT; AI-image/FAL + DSP-physical-modeling-first + physics-sim TECH; warm-consonant-resolves VIBE] · picked=[**WebRTC-peer INPUT** (0× starved, jury-named) · **SVG OUTPUT** (fresh, dodges three.js/Canvas2D/WebGL2) · **P2P-negotiated-roughness TECH** (not a DSP-primitive farm) · **dissonant-until-you-both-agree VIBE** (the jury's #1)]` — no banned tag. **Love signal:** `236-particle-life-song`❤️ (multi-agent interaction) + the embodied/together kids loves (`83`/`98`) softly back a two-human cooperative piece; additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. **Winner of a WIDE 3-explorer adult fire** (three orthogonal directions, each hitting a *different* jury-named starved menu category): siblings `509-spectral-loom` (**Web MIDI** keyboard → a long-form **WebGPU** "loom" of harmonic threads that sour toward dissonance unless you keep tending them; Grisey *Partiels* — the long-form-evolving-adult + MIDI menu category) + `510-the-withholding` (a **conceptual/critical, non-screen** piece whose suspension resolves only when you set the phone down and give it sustained **stillness** — mic-quiet + motion stillness; Cage/Lucier/Radigue) are complete, build-reviewed implementations banked in IDEAS §389. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `/dream/508-accord-call` prerendered static 7.37 kB / 110 kB First Load; two trivial lint fixes applied — unescaped quotes → `&ldquo;`/`&rdquo;`, removed an unused `eslint-disable`), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio / second peer / network in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether STUN traversal opens the data channel on Karel's network (strict symmetric NAT would need a TURN server — the practice-partner bot covers the no-peer case for the demo); whether the dissonance→consonance arc *audibly* reads behind the −6 dB limiter on phone speakers; the fixed shimmer-pad frequencies (262/330/392/523) can mildly beat against non-C settling points; the SVG `preserveAspectRatio="none"` stretch on extreme aspect ratios; iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock. The always-on labeled practice partner is the safety net so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows + hears the full arc with no second device.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/508-accord-call/README.md`

### [537-kids-sky-murmuration](/dream/537-kids-sky-murmuration) · kids (4+) · cycle 398 · demoable
  **Look UP into a deep 3D dusk sky where a real murmuration of thousands of glowing starlings swirls in volumetric depth — and the murmuration SINGS; a finger gently shepherds it, and when the cloud splits into sub-flocks you HEAR it split into harmony, resolving back to a chord when it re-merges.** A living weather of birds you stand under — joyful, awe, no score, no wrong move. Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one singing-murmuration concept on three renderers).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **3D boids flock engine** (`flock.ts`) — Craig Reynolds separation/alignment/cohesion in 3D with spatial-hash neighbour lookups (capped neighbour checks) + boundary avoidance, ~2.5–4k agents in typed `Float32Array`s. (2) **spatial clustering** (`computeClusters`) — grid-bucket detection of up to 4 sub-flocks every ~200 ms. (3) **emergent-state → harmony sonification** (`audio.ts`) — cohesion/alignment-order → harmonic richness & consonance; centroid height (Y) → C-major-pentatonic register; each cluster sustains its OWN pentatonic voice so a **split is audible as a new interval and a merge resolves toward unison/octave**; mean speed/turbulence → gentle tremolo + airy wash; always-on C2+G2 pad; master `gain → lowpass(8 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−6 dB, 18:1) → destination`, all `setTargetAtTime`; AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). (4) **three.js volumetric render** — one `THREE.Points` cloud, additive blending + soft sprite, depth-coded colour (near-warm/far-violet), `ExpFog`, a slowly drifting camera so depth reads; geometry/material disposed on unmount. (5) **ghost-shepherd auto-demo** — a scripted 3D attractor wanders a Lissajous path and a second attractor phases in/out on a ~12 s cycle so the cloud **divides into harmonising flocks and re-merges from frame one with zero permission**; cancels on first pointer-down, resumes after ~4 s idle. Touch = a soft 3D attractor the murmuration swirls toward (single + multi-touch → split). **Degradation:** three.js/WebGL context fail → `text-rose-300` notice + a Canvas2D 2D dot-flock (CPU boids ~300) that still flocks + sings; audio always continues. **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + three.js — no mic, camera, network, or secrets). **No new npm deps** (three@0.182 is an existing installed dependency, imported normally — not via CDN, not added).

  References: **Craig Reynolds, "Flocks, Herds and Schools: A Distributed Behavioral Model," SIGGRAPH 1987** (the canonical boids rules); **starling-murmuration collective-motion science** (Cavagna et al., *Scale-free correlations in starling flocks*, PNAS 2010). Born from RESEARCH §398 (cs.SD this window = server-side ML → #5 unclaimable; a grep-audit confirmed the easy kids #1s are spent at ~360 prototypes — TF.js/Hands/FaceLandmarker/Pose/WebGPU/AudioWorklet/GPU-fluid/boids all taken — so the honest move is a *bigger, more diverse* concept on the *scarcest renderer*). **Ambition 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5, above) + **#3** named refs (Reynolds 1987 / Cavagna PNAS 2010) + **#4** multi-cycle ("living flock that sings" spine cycle 1). (#1 NOT cleanly claimed — flocking already exists in the lab; the fresh element is *emergent-collective-flock-state-as-a-harmony-instrument, at scale, for kids* — an interaction/sonification pattern, not a clean 0× primitive, said plainly. #5 NOT claimed — cs.SD is server-ML.) **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4×)=["solitary-never-resolves" VIBE] · also-avoid (would→4×)=[Canvas2D + SVG OUTPUT] · picked=[**touch/multi-touch INPUT** · **three.js OUTPUT** (the scarcest renderer at 0× → hardest diversification; iPad-safe where WebGPU isn't; dodges the Canvas2D/SVG 4× trap) · **emergent-3D-boids → collective-harmony TECH** (not a DSP-primitive farm) · **joyful/awe/wonder VIBE** — neither banned pole]` — no banned tag; deliberately steers OFF yesterday's drawing-ML theme (529) so the kids set isn't two "draw → magic ML" pieces back to back. **Love signal:** Karel's loved particle/glowing-swarm lane (`130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️, `236-particle-life-song`❤️, `262-aurora-particle`❤️) — a singing murmuration of light at scale; additive, the kids/ambition/diversity gates governed. Siblings `535-kids-starling-choir` (raw WebGL2 transform-feedback GPU boids ~2.5k — the technical-scale resurrect) + `536-kids-lantern-shoal` (Canvas2D friendly-character shoal — the best 4yo legibility, lost only on the Canvas2D-4× diversity gate) banked in IDEAS §398. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 83s`, `✓ Generating static pages (434/434)`, `/dream/537-kids-sky-murmuration 6.21 kB / 297 kB First Load` prerendered static — the First Load is the three.js bundle, in line with other three.js routes), **NOT browser-verified** (no three.js/WebGL/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether 2–4 simultaneous cluster voices read as an audibly distinct *split→merge* on a phone speaker behind the limiter (the cluster threshold may need tuning — the key thing to check live); ~2.5–4k `THREE.Points` + per-frame CPU boids holding ≥30 fps on an older iPad (Canvas2D ~300-agent fallback covers the worst case); iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock; whether the slow camera drift gives enough parallax for 3D depth on a small phone. The ghost-shepherd auto-demo + Canvas2D fallback are the safety nets so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a swirling, singing, splitting murmuration.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/537-kids-sky-murmuration/README.md`

### [538-xenharmonic-lattice](/dream/538-xenharmonic-lattice) · adults / microtonal / cerebral · cycle 399 · demoable
  **Wander a tuning system the piano can't play — a navigable harmonic lattice in 5-limit Just Intonation, Bohlen–Pierce (the non-octave 3:1 tritave), and 19-EDO, where the tension lives in the TUNING itself, not in beating partials.** Drag across an SVG Tonnetz; each node sounds an EXACT rational-ratio pitch (no 12-TET rounding) via a 4-partial luminous synth. JI chords lock in eerily pure; the syntonic-comma "pump" drifts the pitch around a loop; **Bohlen–Pierce refuses Western resolution entirely**; 19-EDO sits in the uncanny valley. Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire.

  Subsystems / tech: SVG vector Tonnetz (9×7 / 7×5 / 9×5 node grids, indigo/violet edges, active-node glow, draggable pan) · exact-ratio Web Audio synthesis (`freq = baseHz × uRatio^u × vRatio^v`; partials [1,2,3,5]×; master `gain → DynamicsCompressor(−6 dB, 16:1) → destination`) · three tuning systems in `tuning.ts` (5-JI on 3/2 × 5/4; BP on 7/3 × 5/3 over the 3:1 tritave; 19-EDO on 2^(3/19) × 2^(5/19)) · **ghost-finger auto-demo** walks a curated path (the 5-JI path is literally a comma loop) sounding nodes from first load with zero permission, cancels on interaction, resumes after ~4 s idle. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps, no permissions** (pure client SVG + Web Audio — no mic/camera/network/secrets).

  References: **Heinz Bohlen & John R. Pierce** (the non-octave Bohlen–Pierce scale, 3:1 tritave / odd-harmonic 3:5:7:9); **Erv Wilson** (pitch lattices); **Harry Partch** *Genesis of a Music* (43-tone JI, tonality diamond); **Leonhard Euler** (the original Tonnetz, 1739). Born from **RESEARCH §399** (the 2026 microtonal-tooling wave — Microtone Fox May-2026 gallery, Microtonal Fabric, Entonal Studio — a <30-day dive bound directly into the build). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** never-used technique (xenharmonic *tuning-space instrument* — Bohlen–Pierce / Tonnetz-lattice / 19-EDO all grep-verified 0× prior; JI *ratios* alone appeared only as a chord detail at 456/397, never as a navigable lattice — framed honestly) + **#3** named refs (Bohlen/Pierce/Wilson/Partch/Euler) + **#5** binds a <14-day RESEARCH §399 finding (the 2026 microtonal wave). Directly answers **JURY 2026-06-11 provocation #1 verbatim** ("tension that lives in TUNING, not beating partials — Bohlen–Pierce, 19-TET"), and also hits jury #2 (BP *refuses* resolution), #3 (SVG, not Canvas2D), #5 (one lattice you explore — not "two parties negotiating"). **Diversity** `banned/over-rep (rolling-10)=[kids-joyful VIBE ≥4 · Canvas2D & WebGL2 OUTPUT ~3 each · touch INPUT] · picked=[**pointer/drag INPUT** · **SVG OUTPUT** (fresh — dodges the Canvas2D/WebGL2 crowding) · **xenharmonic-exact-ratio-tuning TECH** (lab-first, not a roughness-engine reuse) · **cerebral/microtonal/luminous-restrained VIBE** — not kids, not warm-resolve, not two-party]` — no over-represented tag. **Love signal:** none directly applicable (microtonal is a new lane); the ambition/diversity/jury gates governed. Siblings `539-tremor-globe` (live USGS earthquake sonification on a three.js globe — lost: its "first data-sonification" #1 is false, 4+ priors incl. 337) + `540-slime-cantor` (WebGPU Physarum — lost: incomplete build + 327-physarum-choir already exists) banked in IDEAS §399. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 66s`, `✓ Generating static pages (435/435)`, `/dream/538-xenharmonic-lattice 5.8 kB / 109 kB First Load` prerendered static), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: whether the ~21.5¢ syntonic-comma drift is audible in a casual 10-s glance (it's genuine but subtle); whether BP's "alien" quality reads on a phone speaker behind the limiter; ghost-finger 1.4 s/step pacing (meditative vs sluggish); unlimited polyphony could click under stress (a max-8 voice cap is the suggested cycle-2 hardening); iOS AudioContext-in-gesture resume. The ghost-finger auto-demo is the safety net so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a lattice lighting up and sounding its alien intervals.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/538-xenharmonic-lattice/README.md`

### [545-motion-loom](/dream/545-motion-loom) · adults / polytempo / cerebral · cycle 401 · demoable
  **The MOTION IN A ROOM conducts a Nancarrow polytempo canon — the left and right halves of the camera each carry their own moving rhythm, extracted with NO machine learning (just optical motion energy), and because two real-world motions never lock to a clean tempo ratio, the canon drifts forever and cannot be made to agree.** Cycle 2 of `514-polytempo-loom` (which was 5 autonomous voices at fixed irrational ratios) — now the two master tempi are *conducted* by your body / the room, off the glass. Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-approach fire (ONE conducted-canon concept, three input/render attacks).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **no-ML motion extraction** — `getUserMedia` → hidden 64×48 offscreen canvas → per-pixel luma frame-difference, accumulated separately for the LEFT half and RIGHT half of the frame (two "conductors"); one motion-energy scalar per half per frame, zero neural net / MediaPipe / WASM. (2) **tempo by autocorrelation** — each half's motion-energy signal buffers ~6 s; mean-subtracted autocorrelation finds the dominant lag in the 40–170 BPM band → BPM, smoothed (τ≈1.5 s), **never quantized** so the two halves' tempi form an irrational ratio by default (the classic onset/tempo-induction self-similarity method, applied to optical motion rather than audio). (3) **polytempo canon engine** ("A Tale of Two Clocks," Chris Wilson 2013) — 5 voices: voices 0–1 locked to conductor A (×1, ×1/√2), voices 2–4 to conductor B (×1, ×1/φ, ×φ/2) — two conducted masters spawn a 5-voice Nancarrow canon; 25 ms `setInterval` + 120 ms lookahead into `AudioContext.currentTime`, sample-accurate `osc.start(t)`; D-pentatonic across 2 octaves (zero harmonic tension — all drama is metric); sine + 2.756× inharmonic partial bell timbre; master `gain → DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (20:1, −12 dBFS) → destination; AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). (4) **three.js loom renderer** — 5 glowing `THREE.Line` threads whose 201 vertices are sinusoidal waves phase-driven by each voice's running BPM (threads on different conductors visibly drift apart — the visual drift IS the tempo drift); 160 pooled `THREE.Mesh` node-pulse spheres on beat-onsets (rise 5 ms / decay 1.8 s, no GC churn); slow ~90 s camera orbit; Ikeda-clinical violet/blue/emerald/cyan/purple on near-black (#020208); HTML overlay readouts "Left motion → X BPM / Right motion → Y BPM / ratio Z… — never rational"; full dispose on unmount. **Ghost-motion auto-demo:** before any camera grant, two synthetic motion-energy signals at incommensurate rates (A≈72, B≈72√2≈101.8 BPM) feed the same autocorrelator so the threads weave + drift from frame one with zero permission; audio after Start, pre-Start animates on `performance.now()`; the room takes over when motion is detected; ghost resumes after ~4 s still.

  References: **Conlon Nancarrow**, *Studies for Player Piano* — tempo canons at irrational ratios; **Study No. 40 "Transcendental"** (the e:π canon Kyle Gann says "probably never aligns," ganntrax.com). **Ryoji Ikeda** (the near-monochrome data-grid visual register). **Autocorrelation tempo-induction by self-similarity** (the classic onset/feature-function method), here applied to optical motion. Grounded in **spontaneous-motor-tempo science** (biorxiv 2025 "From Individual to Shared Tempo" / "Similarity in Spontaneous Tempo Impacts Flow … Joint Performance") — each body's innate preferred tempo rarely matches another's, so the drift is a human condition, not just a math trick. Born from **RESEARCH §401**. **Ambition 3/5 solid (#2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 named refs · #4 multi-cycle — 514 polytempo spine, cycle 2), with an honest case for 4/5 via #1** — *first tempo/rhythm EXTRACTION from camera motion* (optical-flow energy → autocorrelation → BPM): the lab has audio-onset/beat detection from the MIC (106-beat-cut, 147-kids-beat-pulse) and pitch-autocorrelation/YIN (507), but has **never derived a beat-RATE from visual motion** — a genuine cross-modal first, flagged as application-level (not a brand-new primitive) so it discounts cleanly to 3/5 if the jury disagrees. **#5 NOT claimed** — the freshest *relevant* findings (the biorxiv SMT papers) are ~9 months old, not <14 days; said plainly even after heeding the jury's "mine beyond cs.SD" and searching the motor-tempo + conducting-perception literature. **Answers JURY 2026-06-12 directly:** provocation #1 (take 514 to cycle 2 — chase a 4/5 above the floor) + provocation #4 (a conducted tempo-canon where gestures set incommensurable tempi — the SECOND time-tension piece, now embodied). **Diversity** `banned (jury rolling-15)=[WebGPU-compute + Canvas2D OUTPUT (4×) · GPU-physics-sim→drone TECH (4×) · finger-on-glass INPUT (7×) · calm-sensory-kids VIBE (4×)] · picked=[**camera-motion INPUT** (off-glass, NOT a finger; NOT ML) · **three.js OUTPUT** (1×→2×, the scarcest non-banned renderer — and crucially NOT SVG, which a sibling would have pushed to 4×) · **motion→autocorr-tempo→polytempo-canon TECH** (NOT a simulated-physical-field/GPU-drone) · **cerebral / Ikeda-clinical / metric-tension VIBE** — NOT calm-sensory-kids]` — clean on every jury ban. **Love signal:** none directly (polytempo/time-tension is a cerebral lane); the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. Siblings `544-conductors-canon` (MediaPipe **Hands** two-hand conducting → **SVG** scrolling loom — the precision/continuity resurrect, complete + clean, lost on the diversity audit because SVG is already 3× and a ship would make it 4×) + `546-clock-canon` (MediaPipe **Pose** full-body two-arm conducting → **SVG orbital phase-clock** idiom, dots that sweep and never realign — complete, the fresh-visual-idiom resurrect) banked in IDEAS §401. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 105s`, `✓ Generating static pages (437/437)`, `/dream/545-motion-loom 5.71 kB / 294 kB First Load` prerendered static — the First Load is the three.js bundle, in line with other three.js routes; builder reported `tsc --noEmit` 0 errors + `eslint --max-warnings=0` clean on the folder), **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/WebGL/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: (a) **whether autocorrelation on REAL room motion yields a musically-meaningful tempo** — the autocorrelator finds *a* BPM even for near-random motion, so moderate noisy scenes may give erratic readings until the 6 s buffer fills (the single most important thing to check live; the core interaction's fidelity); (b) WebGL `linewidth>1` is not honored on most drivers, so the threads render ~1px rather than as glowing ribbons (the node-pulse spheres carry the glow; cycle-3 fix = `Line2`/`LineMaterial`); (c) the O(n²) autocorr over ~360 samples runs per video frame — fine on desktop, may need batching to every 5th frame on low-end mobile; (d) iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock; (e) the node-pool wraps at 160 simultaneous pulses (artifacts only at 170 BPM × 5 voices). The ghost-motion auto-demo is the safety net so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a weaving, drifting, (post-Start) singing canon with zero permission.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/545-motion-loom/README.md`

### [549-kids-song-friend](/dream/549-kids-song-friend) · kids (4+) / companion / voice · cycle 402 · demoable
  **A creature whose body is literally MADE of the songs a 4-year-old sings it — it remembers the actual melodies (not a vague energy, not a timer) forever, across calendar days, and sings them back, woven and changed, every visit.** Sing or hum; the friend grows a new petal shaped like your melody, keeps every song, and greets you bigger tomorrow. Cycle 2 of the kids **Companion / Presence** spine (`518-kids-living-ember` = cycle 1, which remembered only humming *energy* within one session). Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one "remembers your songs across days" concept on three renderers).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **forgiving voice→pitch capture** (`audio.ts`) — `getUserMedia` → `AnalyserNode` → autocorrelation pitch detector tuned for a 4-year-old's high/breathy/wandering voice (clarity threshold + **babble-note fallback** so any vocalization still teaches a note), snapped to **C-major pentatonic** ~2 octaves; a melody = note-index array (cap 12; a ~1.2 s pause commits the song). (2) **cross-day melodic-memory engine** (`memory.ts`) — `localStorage` (`resonance.dream.549`, try/catch): song library + visit count + monotonic `totalNotesEver` + `lastVisit` + simulated `day`; on load the friend GREETS, is visibly bigger, and surfaces a new-calendar-day return. (3) **recall re-synthesis** (`audio.ts`) — never raw playback: transpose + gently slow + **weave two different days' melodies** into soft counterpoint, the active petal pulsing + mouth opening on each recalled note; warm pentatonic voice; kid-safe master `gain → lowpass 8 kHz → DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (20:1, −10 dBFS) → destination; AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). (4) **the body IS the songs (SVG)** — each remembered song is a teardrop/petal path whose vertical shape traces that melody's pitch contour (pentatonic degree → hue), radiating around a friendly face (blinking eyes, a mouth that opens when it sings); idle breathing via direct rAF attribute mutation (no per-frame React re-render); `feGaussianBlur` glow. **Two demo affordances** so the 06:30 glance never has to wait a real day: pre-seeded "yesterday's songs" sing hands-free from frame one with zero permission, AND an honestly-labelled **"✨ pretend it's tomorrow"** button that bumps the visit, regrows the body, and plays a transposed/slowed/woven recall instantly. **Degradation:** mic denied → `text-rose-300` message + the friend keeps singing its remembered songs hands-free (SVG is universal; a cute base body guards the zero-songs case). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client: mic [local only, never recorded/uploaded/networked] + Web Audio + SVG — no network, no secrets). **No new npm deps.**

  References: **Tamagotchi** (Aki Maita / Bandai, 1996) — the original persistent virtual companion that *remembers* and rewards return; **Brian Eno** — long-form, non-repeating (the recall is never twice the same); and the design thesis that a young child's bond with a voice companion deepens through *memory of content* (it knows your songs), not a clock — explicitly transcending `322-kids-voice-garden` (a timer-aged plant) by changing because of *what was sung*. Born from **RESEARCH §402** (cs.SD still server-ML → #5 unclaimable, said plainly; the relevant fresh-ish finding is a 2026 AccScience study on children's developmental preference for known, responsive voice agents — backs the design but isn't verifiably <14 days). **Answers JURY 2026-06-12 provocation #6 directly** — extend `518-living-ember` into "a creature a child finds genuinely changed tomorrow," not another 90-second glow toy. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems · #3 named refs Tamagotchi/Eno · #4 multi-cycle Companion/Presence spine cycle 2; honest contestable #1 — remembering melodic *content* across days recalled as woven counterpoint with the body drawn from contours is an interaction-first, but localStorage persistence itself isn't lab-first [322 aged a creature; 71 persisted a gallery], so it discounts cleanly; #5 NOT claimed). **Diversity** `banned (jury rolling-15)=[WebGPU-compute + Canvas2D OUTPUT (4×) · GPU-physics-sim→drone TECH (4×) · finger-on-glass INPUT (7×) · calm-sensory-kids VIBE (4×)] · picked=[**voice/mic INPUT** (off-glass, NOT a finger — the 7× reflex) · **SVG OUTPUT** (NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU — both banned; and NOT three.js, which 545 just shipped) · **pitch→persistent-memory→recall TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics-sim drone) · **companion / it-remembers-you VIBE** (NOT calm-sensory-kids — the bond/memory IS the differentiator, jury #6's point)]` — clean on every jury ban. **Love signal:** Karel's loved kids voice/mark-making cluster (`158-kids-hum-paint`❤️, `100-kids-paint-song`❤️, `104-kids-mirror-draw`❤️ — a child's voice/mark becoming a visible kept thing) softly backs it; additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. Siblings `547-kids-songkeeper` (three.js glowing creature, each song an orbiting contour-strand — richest idle presence, lost: three.js 2-in-a-row + less legible than petals-with-a-face) + `548-kids-songbloom` (raw WebGL2 GLSL bioluminescent shader grown from memory-seed uniforms — the freshest generative-shader-from-memory idea, lost: memory→uniform-hash is the least legible "see your own songs," no face) banked in IDEAS §402. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully`, `✓ Generating static pages (438/438)`, `/dream/549-kids-song-friend 5.75 kB / 112 kB First Load` prerendered static; zero build warnings in the folder), **NOT browser-verified** (no mic/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: (a) **pitch accuracy on a REAL 4-year-old's voice** is the core unknown (autocorrelation is octave-error-prone, a child's voice is breathy; clarity threshold + babble fallback + pentatonic snapping hide a lot, but capture timings want field tuning); (b) **SVG perf with a large library** — unbounded petal count under a blur filter could stress an old iPad after weeks of songs (cycle-3 cap/LOD); (c) the README `<Link>` 404s as a route (cosmetic, a lab-wide pattern); (d) iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock; (e) the single shared `rafRef` (idle vs listening) is slightly fragile though only one is ever live. The pre-seeded memory + "pretend it's tomorrow" button are the safety nets so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a friend made of songs, greeting + singing + visibly growing, with zero permission and no 24h wait.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/549-kids-song-friend/README.md`

### [553-kids-tilt-fountain](/dream/553-kids-tilt-fountain) · kids (4+) / tilt / playful-constructive · cycle 404 · demoable
  **TIP the whole tablet to pour a stream of glowing orbs down through a 3D vertical garden of chimes, bells, and strings — composing a singing waterfall by where you AIM the flow.** Tilt left/right/forward steers the gravity that pulls the cascade; each instrument-zone an orb falls through rings a warm pentatonic note (different timbre per zone). Active, constructive play — you aim and BUILD a tune — not a calm staring-glow toy. Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (three orthogonal off-glass inputs × three non-banned renderers).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **tilt input + gravity model** (`page.tsx` + `physics.ts`) — `deviceorientation` (gamma=L/R, beta=front/back) → a smoothed (EMA α≈0.08) 2D gravity vector applied to every orb (gravity never reverses); iOS 13+ permission via `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` on the Start gesture; desktop fallback pointer X/Y → gravity + arrow-key steer; denied/absent → `text-rose-300` notice + pointer fallback auto-on. (2) **orb physics + emitter** (`physics.ts`) — continuous emitter drips one orb / 100 ms; Euler integration under tilt-gravity; per-orb **AABB collision** vs 5 instrument zones; **hard cap 180 orbs**, retire >12 s / below floor, pool recycled each frame, drag 0.985. *Discrete rigid orbs triggering notes on contact — NOT a simulated fluid/field whose statistics are sonified (the jury-banned GPU-physics-sim→drone move).* (3) **zone-based spatial pentatonic synth** (`audio.ts`) — 5 zones, distinct timbres (bell sine+partials / Karplus-ish string / triangle chime / marimba / low-bell), pitch by horizontal hit position, all C-major-pentatonic (always consonant), per-zone rate-limiting, master `gain → lowpass(8 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−10 dBFS, 20:1) → destination` brick-wall limiter, `setTargetAtTime` envelopes, AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). (4) **three.js render** — additive-emissive glowing orbs + 5 luminous zone-panels that flash on hit, dark background, additive glow (no postprocessing dep); full dispose on unmount. **Ghost-tilt auto-demo** — a figure-8 synthetic tilt pours + sings from frame one with zero permission, cancels on real tilt/pointer, resumes after ~4 s idle (the 06:30 hands-free safety net). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client Web Audio + three.js — no mic/camera/network/secrets). **No new npm deps** (three@0.182 is an existing installed dependency, imported normally).

  References: **Toshio Iwai** — *Electroplankton* / *SimTunes* (the playful music-toy lineage where moving a thing composes); **Pythagoras' monochord** (pitch by position); **Rube Goldberg** (the cascade/chain idea); **Harry Partch**. Born from **RESEARCH §404** (face/expression music is the kids autopilot the jury warned against — grep-verified at 258/423 — so it was dropped; the easy kids lab-firsts are spent at ~360+ prototypes → compete on ambition + diversity + execution, the 537/549 precedent). **Answers JURY 2026-06-12 provocation #3** (take the off-glass lane — TILT, not a finger on glass) **AND #6** (break the calm-sensory-kids autopilot — active/constructive play, not a 90-second glow toy). **Ambition 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (tilt+gravity + orb-physics/emitter + 5-timbre zone synth + three.js = 4) + **#3** named refs (Iwai / Pythagoras / Rube Goldberg / Partch). (**#1 contestable, discounted** — "first tilt-poured cascade instrument" is application-level but tilt-marbles exist [169/184], so it discounts cleanly to 3/5. **#5 NOT claimed** — no <14-day bind for a kids cycle; cs.SD still server-ML, said plainly, the ninth jury running.) **Diversity** `banned (jury rolling-15)=[WebGPU-compute + Canvas2D OUTPUT (4×) · GPU-physics-sim→drone TECH (4×) · finger-on-glass INPUT (7×) · calm-sensory-kids VIBE (4×)] · picked=[**device-TILT INPUT** (off-glass, NOT a finger — the scarcest off-glass kids input recently used: 549 voice / 537 camera / 541 finger) · **three.js OUTPUT** (NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU — both banned) · **discrete-orb-gravity-physics → spatial-chime TECH** (NOT a simulated-physical-field/GPU drone) · **playful/constructive/joyful-fountain VIBE** (NOT calm-sensory-kids — the directest break of jury #6)]` — clean on every jury ban. **Love signal:** the loved kids embodied cluster (`83-kids-tilt-rain`❤️ — tilt; `100-kids-paint-song`❤️ / `158-kids-hum-paint`❤️ — a child's action becoming an audible/visible thing) softly backs a tilt-driven constructive instrument; additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. Siblings `554-kids-giant-shadow` (camera full-body Pose → raw WebGL2 GLSL metaball aura → continuous choir; Krueger *Videoplace*/Theremin — the biggest visual/technical swing, lost: heaviest real-iPad risk [PoseLandmarker-CDN + 33-joint metaball shader loop + camera permission] + Pose is the most-used kids input lane) + `555-kids-echo-canyon` (voice → SVG canyon → staggered 4-voice canon/round, distinct from 549/142; round-form/Oliveros — the most musically refined + most reliable, lost: least fresh, 549 last cycle was also voice) banked in IDEAS §404. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 41s`, `✓ Generating static pages (440/440)`, `/dream/553-kids-tilt-fountain 5.08 kB / 296 kB First Load` prerendered static — the 296 kB is the three.js bundle, in line with other three.js routes; zero warnings in the folder), **NOT browser-verified** (no audio/tilt/WebGL in this sandbox) — unverified surface: (a) **iOS AudioContext unlock inside the async `onClick` after the `requestPermission()` await** (the builder's flagged risk — Safari may drop the gesture-association across the await; a tap-to-resume guard is the cycle-2 fix); (b) **180-orb smoothness on an older iPad** (`MAX_ORBS` is the knob; additive-emissive per-orb meshes are the cost); (c) **whether aiming the flow reads as deliberate melody-making vs pretty randomness** — the core unverified interaction claim (zone layout + pitch-by-position is designed for it; only a real child's hands confirm); (d) extreme tilt could starve top zones (gravity never reverses, so the top always feeds); (e) the README `<Link>` 404s as a route (cosmetic, lab-wide pattern). The ghost-tilt auto-demo + pointer fallback need zero permission, so the 06:30 glance reliably shows a pouring, glowing, singing fountain with no setup.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/553-kids-tilt-fountain/README.md`

### [559-kids-clap-canon](/dream/559-kids-clap-canon) · kids (4+) / rhythm / time-tension · cycle 406 · demoable
  **Steve Reich's phasing, for a 4-year-old: clap a rhythm into the mic, two friendly blobs clap it back, then one drifts ~1.8% faster so the patterns slide unison → shimmering interlock → unison over ~30–40 s — and a big phase-wheel with two chasing dots makes the drift VISIBLE.** Active rhythmic discovery, not a calm glow toy: the child performs a clap, then watches a minimalist *process* unfold. Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (three orthogonal off-glass inputs × three renderers).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **mic clap/onset detection** — `getUserMedia({audio})` → `AnalyserNode` (fftSize 2048); per-rAF time-domain RMS vs a fast-attack/slow-decay EMA background; onset fires when `rms > max(0.015, ema·2.5)` with a 120 ms refractory; 2–6 claps → an inter-onset-interval array (~2 s silence commits; <2 claps → a default Reich-style 6-clap pattern so it always works). (2) **phase-canon scheduler** — two voices share the IOI array; voice B's interval × `(1−0.018)` ≈ 1.8% shorter loop; a 25 ms `setInterval` schedules sample-accurate `start(t)` 120 ms ahead (look-ahead, no `setTimeout`-per-note); voice A = warm bandpass-noise clap + low sine body, voice B = bright highpass woodblock + triangle. (3) **SVG render via direct rAF attribute mutation** (no per-frame React `setState`) — phase-wheel dots, character body-scale, glow opacity, hand positions all `setAttribute`'d each frame; React state only for discrete UI. (4) **character animation** — two cute SVG blob-creatures (ellipses + eyes + hands) flash/scale on each scheduled clap (decay 6×/s), `feGaussianBlur` glow pulse. Master `gain(0.75) → DynamicsCompressor(−10 dBFS, 20:1) → destination` brick-wall limiter; AudioContext built in the Start gesture (iOS unlock). **Hands-free auto-demo:** phase-wheel + blobs animate from frame one with a built-in pattern at zero clicks (audio waits for the Start gesture), cancels on interaction, resumes after ~4 s idle. **Mic-denied → `text-rose-300` notice + tap-anywhere clap fallback** (fully playable). **No API route, no `guard`** (pure client mic [local only, never recorded/uploaded/networked] + Web Audio + SVG — no network, no secrets). **No new npm deps.**

  References: **Steve Reich** — *Clapping Music* (1972, two performers clap the same 12-note pattern; one shifts an eighth-note every 12 bars until back in unison) and *It's Gonna Rain* (1965, tape phasing — two identical loops drift and realign). The gradual phase-shift IS the prototype, not decoration ("music that is about process, that would make the process perceptible"). Born from **RESEARCH §406** (no kid-appropriate <14-day client-buildable bind surfaced — WebGPU-baseline-via-Interop-2026 is a platform note not a technique; cs.SD still server-ML, said plainly the 10th jury running; the structural read holds that at ~360+ kids prototypes the easy lab-firsts are spent → compete on ambition + diversity + a named concept new TO CHILDREN). **Answers JURY 2026-06-12 provocation #4 literally** — the jury mourned that the time-tension vocabulary (tempo canons, polymeter, Reich phase, Nancarrow) "had no children"; this is that piece, for children — **AND #3** (off-glass input — mic clap, not a finger) **AND #6** (break the calm-sensory-kids autopilot — active rhythmic discovery). **Ambition 3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (mic onset + phase-canon scheduler + SVG rAF render + character animation = 4) + **#3** named ref that drives the piece (Steve Reich).
