(**#1 contestable, discounted** — phasing was touched once in an alt-lineage cycle 305 + tempo-canon exists at 514/545, so "first phasing piece" discounts cleanly; the fresh element is *phasing made legible + joyful for a 4-year-old*, an audience-first, framed honestly. **#5 NOT claimed** — no <14-day kids bind.) **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4×)=[finger-on-glass INPUT (529/537/541/538)] · picked=[**microphone clap/onset INPUT** (off-glass — the directest dodge of the finger reflex; the child performs in space) · **SVG OUTPUT** (rAF attribute mutation; SVG sat at 3×, not banned; NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU [jury-flagged], NOT three.js [553/545/537 just used]) · **onset → looping phase-canon TECH** (NOT a GPU-physics-sim drone) · **rhythmic/playful/active-discovery VIBE** (NOT calm-sensory-kids — only 1× in the last 10)]` — clean on the live finger-on-glass ban and every standing jury ban. **Love signal:** the loved kids embodied/rhythm cluster (`98-kids-drum-circle`❤️ — rhythm play; `100-kids-paint-song`❤️ / `158-kids-hum-paint`❤️ — a child's physical action becoming an audible/visible thing) softly backs a clap-driven rhythm instrument; additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. Siblings `560-kids-shadow-band` (camera no-ML frame-diff silhouette → three.js ~4k-particle creature → 3 zone-gated instruments; Krueger *Videoplace*/Rozin — the most embodied/"wow", lost: the most-redundant camera-body input lane + most lighting-fragile for the hands-free glance) + `561-kids-rainmaker` (device SHAKE/`devicemotion` magnitude → Canvas2D rain/lightning → Xenakis stochastic grain-cloud percussion, Poisson λ = shake intensity²; *Achorripsis* 1957 — robust pooling + fresh off-glass shake input, lost: the least conceptually surprising, a sensory weather toy) banked in IDEAS §406. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 30.6s`, `✓ Generating static pages (442/442)`, `/dream/559-kids-clap-canon 6.04 kB / 112 kB First Load` prerendered static; clean first run, no fix attempt needed; zero warnings in the folder), **NOT browser-verified** (no mic/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: (a) **clap detection on a real 4-year-old in a real room** (standard EMA onset detector, claps are sharp transients = the easy case, but a noisy room / quiet claps may mis-fire; the default-pattern + tap-anywhere fallbacks guarantee it always plays); (b) **whether the ~1.8% drift reads as Reich phasing over a real listen** — the phase-wheel makes it visible immediately (the legibility crutch), but whether a child *hears* the slide as one evolving groove is the core unverified perceptual claim; (c) iOS AudioContext unlock + mic-permission timing (no `await` before `ctx.resume`, lower risk than 553); (d) the README `<Link>` 404s as a route (cosmetic, lab-wide). The zero-permission SVG auto-demo is the safety net so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows the wheel turning and the two blobs drifting in and out of sync with no setup.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/559-kids-clap-canon/README.md`

### [564-piano-splat-cathedral](/dream/564-piano-splat-cathedral) · adult / audio-file / architectural-volumetric · cycle 407 · demoable
  **Karel's real Welcome Home piano doesn't just make a cloud — it BUILDS A CATHEDRAL.** A luminous Gothic nave of anisotropic, depth-SORTED Gaussian splats — floor → columns → arches → transept → vault → rose window — raised element-by-element by the music's onsets (in architectural order), lit by pitch, that you fly down the centre of. Stateful, not a loop: a floor + one column pair at second 10; the full vault and a blooming rose window by minute 3. **Cycle 2 of the Gaussian-splat spine** (`557-piano-splat-galaxy` = cycle 1, the lab's first 4/5 — an isotropic additive *cloud*; 564 is the depth-sorted anisotropic *architecture*). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one "his real piano sculpts a real anisotropic sorted splat field" concept on three forms/renderers).

  Subsystems (≥3): (1) **audio fetch + FFT + onset/pitch** (`audio.ts`) — `AudioContext` + `AnalyserNode` (fftSize 2048) on the Begin gesture; fetches **Karel's real piano** `/api/audio/549fc519-f7fc-4c38-a771-adaad2edbc81` (dual-mode JSON `{url}` or raw bytes, 4 s timeout, looped) → always-on **synth-piano fallback** so it sounds offline; master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; spectral flux vs adaptive EMA → onset, spectral peak → pitch → hue, energy → loudness. (2) **procedural cathedral geometry + build sequencer** (`cathedral.ts`) — the nave pre-generated at startup as typed-array splat positions/axes/radii for an ordered element list (floor · 5 column pairs · 5 arches · 2 transept arms · 4 vault bays · rose window · ambient); onsets raise the next element in build order, permanent once raised, re-lit by later onsets → demonstrably different at minute 3 than second 10 (long-form state/memory, not a loop). (3) **anisotropic, depth-SORTED Gaussian-splat rasterizer (the cycle-2 technique leap)** — each splat an **oriented ellipse** (not 557's isotropic circle), per-splat world axes aligned to its surface (column shaft vertical / arch tangent / vault flat / rose radial); vertex shader projects axis tips to screen (EWA-style tip-projection, Zwicker 2001 — a two-axis approximation, not full covariance) → oriented stretched quad; fragment evaluates an elliptical Gaussian + small additive emissive core. **Every frame the live splats are depth-SORTED back-to-front by view-Z and composited with `SRC_ALPHA, ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA`, depth-test OFF** — real alpha-over occlusion (a near column hides a far one), the correctness leap 557's additive-no-sort renderer lacked. Camera flies forward down the nave + auto-rotate; orbit-on-drag + wheel zoom. **Pre-Begin auto-demo** builds + lights the cathedral from frame one, zero permission (the 06:30 hands-free safety net); audio waits for the Begin gesture. **No API route, no `guard`** (consumes the existing read-only `/api/audio/[id]` GET — no side effects, no secrets). **No new npm deps.**

  References: **Kerbl, Kopanas, Leimkühler, Drettakis** — "3D Gaussian Splatting for Real-Time Radiance Field Rendering" (SIGGRAPH 2023); **Zwicker, Pfister, van Baar, Gross** — "EWA Volume Splatting" (IEEE Vis 2001, the anisotropic-projection algebra); **antimatter15/splat** (the canonical CPU per-frame depth-sort WebGL viewer); **James Turrell** (architectural light — being *inside* the light, not lit by it); **Refik Anadol** (volumetric data architecture). Born from **RESEARCH §407** — the 2026 in-browser splatting wave (SparseStreet arXiv 2606.03909 June 2026; MOBILE-GS ICLR 2026, 116 FPS sorted 3DGS on a phone; WebSplatter arXiv 2602.03207) establishes real-time sorted anisotropic 3DGS as a browser commodity, so cycle 2 leaps from 557's cheap isotropic+additive+unsorted path to the honest sorted-anisotropic renderer. **Answers JURY 2026-06-12 #1** (chase the ceiling — take the splat lineage that landed §405's first 4/5 to cycle 2 as a *massively bigger concept* with an added long-form-stateful axis) **AND #5** (mine a non-cs.SD well at current-month freshness). **Ambition 3/5, 4/5 arguable**: **#1** anisotropic + depth-sorted alpha-over splat rendering (a technique the lab has never rendered — 557 was explicitly isotropic + additive + unsorted; contestable only because the anisotropy is a two-axis approximation, discounted honestly) + **#4** multi-cycle splat spine (cycle 2) + **#3** named refs that drive it (Kerbl / Zwicker / antimatter15 / Turrell / Anadol); plus a real extra signal the sibling clouds lacked — **long-form STATEFUL build** (the jury's "long-form generative with state/memory" menu). **#5 soft/contestable** (the dive is genuinely current-month and motivates the leap, but the rendering math is foundational — not hard-claimed; honestly 3/5, not a gamed 4/5). **Diversity** `banned (rolling-10 ≥4× = SVG OUTPUT [559/552/549/538] + standing jury set: WebGPU-compute / Canvas2D / GPU-physics-sim→drone / finger-on-glass-default / calm-sensory-kids) · picked=[**audio-file INPUT** (his real piano — off-glass, the inverse of every banned input) · **raw WebGL2 splat-rasterization OUTPUT** (NOT SVG [4×], NOT Canvas2D/WebGPU [banned], NOT three.js; WebGL2 sat at 2×) · **anisotropic depth-sorted Gaussian-splat TECH** (renders his RECORDED music — the inverse of the banned "simulate a medium, sonify its statistics" drone) · **architectural/sacred/Turrell-Anadol VIBE** (a built spectacle you fly through, NOT calm-sensory-kids, NOT the meditative-drone cluster)]` — clean on the live SVG ban and every standing jury ban. **Love signal:** the loved piano-visualizer cluster (`163-paths-visualizer`❤️, pattern reused by 283/532/557) softly backs feeding his real Welcome Home piano; additive — the jury + ambition/diversity gates governed. Siblings `563-piano-splat-ewa` (full EWA covariance projection — quaternion→Σ→Jacobian→conic→eigenvalue-bounded quad + premultiplied alpha-over; the reconstruction-grade renderer, the cleanest #1-technique — lost: another orbitable cloud adjacent to 557; **the cycle-3 path is to swap 564's two-axis approx for this full covariance**) + `562-piano-splat-aurora` (anisotropic splats stretched into flowing aurora ribbons via axis→screen θ + σMajor/σMinor; Anadol palette — lost: also a cloud-you-orbit; the anisotropy-from-flow trick is the keeper) banked in IDEAS §407. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 45s`, `✓ Generating static pages (443/443)`, `/dream/564-piano-splat-cathedral 8.48 kB / 115 kB First Load` prerendered static; clean first run, no fix attempt; zero warnings in the folder), **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/audio in this sandbox) — unverified surface: (a) the real-piano fetch JSON-vs-bytes shape / CORS / `decodeAudioData` codec (synth fallback is the guaranteed path); (b) onset thresholds drive the *build sequence* — dense passages may race the cathedral up or stall (ear-estimated, untuned to the recording); (c) the anisotropy is a **two-axis approximation, not full covariance** — non-orthogonal axes get slight ellipse shear (563's full-EWA is the cycle-3 fix); (d) ~10k CPU-sorted splats + per-frame `bufferSubData` target 60 fps on a laptop, may dip on weak GPUs / high-DPI phones (JS `Array.sort`, not radix); (e) iOS AudioContext-in-gesture unlock; the README `<Link>` 404s as a route (cosmetic, lab-wide). The pre-Begin auto-build + forward-fly need zero permission, so the 06:30 hands-free glance always shows a living cathedral raising itself with no setup.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/564-piano-splat-cathedral/README.md`

### [576-presence-room](/dream/576-presence-room) · adults / embodied-spatial / meditative · cycle 413 · demoable
  **Your own face is the listener.** A webcam tracks your head pose (MediaPipe FaceLandmarker, CDN runtime load) and feeds it straight into the Web Audio **`AudioListener`** — lean and turn your head and a warm just-intonation Dream-House chord physically re-spatialises around you; lean toward a voice and it blooms. The lab's **first head-tracked binaural** piece and its first genuinely *embodied*-spatial one — the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-13 provocation #1 ("zero embodied-spatial prototypes after 570 builds / get off the glass") and #4 (warmth, not a puzzle). Winner of an adult **WIDE** fire (3 explorers).

  Subsystems (≥3): (1) **face tracking** (`face.ts`) — MediaPipe FaceLandmarker dynamically imported from jsDelivr CDN (no npm dep); one face/frame → heavily EMA-smoothed (α≈0.15) head pose: planar position from eye centroid, depth from inter-ocular scale, yaw from eye asymmetry, pitch from eye-to-nose span. (2) **spatial audio** (`audio.ts`) — 6 soft drone voices at JI ratios (1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 3/2, 15/8, 2/1) over a warm A2 (110 Hz) root, each = sine + triangle sub + detuned partial → its own **HRTF `PannerNode`** on a ring; every frame the pose drives `audioCtx.listener` position + forward orientation (modern `positionX/forwardX.setTargetAtTime`, `setPosition`/`setOrientation` fallback); multi-second swells; master `gain → lowpass → DynamicsCompressor (limiter) → destination`. (3) **visuals** (`render.ts`) — hand-written GLSL ES 3.00 fullscreen-triangle fragment shader (NOT three.js): warm amber/rose/violet volumetric haze tracking where you look + one glowing orb per voice that brightens with its bloom. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps** (camera frames analysed in-browser only — never recorded/sent). Fallbacks for the 06:30 phone glance: camera denied / FaceLandmarker fails / no webcam → `text-rose-300` notice + **pointer-drag "look around"** + a **silent Lissajous auto-demo** that orbits the field within seconds (cancels on real input, resumes after ~5s idle); WebGL2 absent → audio-only; audio blocked → visuals-only.

  References: **Maryanne Amacher** (perceived sound changes as you move your head — the body is part of the instrument); **La Monte Young & Marian Zazeela** *Dream House* (a sustained-JI environment you physically move through); **Pauline Oliveros** *Deep Listening* (attention/presence over performance). Born from **RESEARCH §413** (webcam head-tracking → Web Audio binaural listener — open-source Spatial-Head-Tracking-Audio + webcam-auralization research, an actively-developing 2026 browser technique). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** first head-pose→`AudioListener` (head-tracked binaural) in the lab — the lab's many HRTF pieces (`7-spatial`/`29`/`148`/`394`/`400`) all moved the listener by dragging a dot or singing, never by the visitor's own body, so this is a genuine interaction-first + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (above) + **#3** named refs; **#5 soft/qualified** (the webcam-auralization dive is genuinely 2026-current and motivates the build, but HRTF + landmark→Euler math is foundational — held as a soft bind, not hard-claimed). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-13)=[three.js OUTPUT 6× · SVG OUTPUT 5× · mic/voice INPUT 5× · onset/beat/tempo TECH 6× · pentatonic-wash + adult-cerebral-puzzle VIBE] · picked=[**camera/head-pose INPUT** (off-glass, not mic, not a finger) · **raw WebGL2 OUTPUT** (not three.js/SVG) · **head-pose→AudioListener HRTF-binaural TECH** (not onset extraction) · **warm meditative Dream-House VIBE** (feel, not solve)]` — clean on every ban. Winner of the adult WIDE fire; banked `577-still-bloom` (device-motion stillness → audio-first Radigue drone) + `578-magnetosphere` (live NOAA space-weather → WebGPU geomagnetic choir) as IDEAS §413. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 34.1s`, `/dream/576-presence-room 6.48 kB / 113 kB First Load` prerendered static), **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/GPU/audio in sandbox) — the silent auto-demo + pointer-drag mean the field always moves on a glance with zero permission.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/576-presence-room/README.md`

### [587-kids-button-garden](/dream/587-kids-button-garden) · kids (4+) / game-controller / off-the-glass · cycle 418 · demoable
  **A 4-year-old's first instrument is a GAME CONTROLLER, not a screen — mash the big buttons and a garden of warm light blooms and sings.** The lab's **first game-controller (Gamepad API) instrument** and its **first haptic-rumble output** — the directest answer yet to the standing jury #1 ("get off the glass / off the touchscreen / mic over-used"). The child grips a physical pad in two hands and watches the wall bloom; no analog-stick coordination, no reading, just the most toddler-native gesture there is — button-mashing — turned into music. Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one "a controller becomes a 4-year-old's instrument" concept on three interaction models).

  Subsystems (≥3 → 5): (1) **Gamepad input** (`page.tsx`) — `navigator.getGamepads()` polled inside the rAF loop with `gamepadconnected`/`disconnected` listeners; **edge-triggered** button presses (rising-edge detect → each press fires once, holds sustain cleanly); W3C "standard gamepad" mapping (face 0–3, d-pad 12–15, triggers 6–7, left-stick axes 0–1) with deadzone. (2) **warm just-intonation synth** (Web Audio only) — the four face buttons are voices at ratios **1/1, 5/4, 3/2, 15/8** (root / major third / fifth / major seventh) over a low root+fifth sine drone + band-passed "wind" noise; each voice = triangle + gently-detuned sine, soft attack + ~1.6 s release; **deliberately NOT the C-major-pentatonic "nothing-is-wrong" wash**. The **d-pad shifts the SEASON** — spring→summer→autumn→winter transposes the root (F3/G3/D3/C3) AND swaps the palette, so the whole world changes mood. Kid-safe master chain `gain → lowpass(8 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor (brick-wall limiter 20:1, −10 dBFS) → destination`; `AudioContext` built in the first gesture (iOS unlock) + `webkitAudioContext` fallback. (3) **Canvas2D render only** — additive (`lighter`) glowing flowers (radial halo + six rotating petal lobes + white core) bloom and breathe over a dark season sky with motion-blur trails; the whole field parallax-sways with the left stick. No three.js/WebGL/WebGPU/SVG. (4) **triggers** swell the wind/pad drone; (5) **dual-rumble haptics** — `vibrationActuator.playEffect("dual-rumble", …)` thumps on each bloom + a quickening heartbeat as more flowers live, feature-detected + try/catch-wrapped (absent on Safari/many pads → the piece works fully without it). **No API route, no guard, no npm deps** (no network I/O). Fallbacks for the 06:30 phone glance: no pad → friendly **amber** prompt + **A/S/D/F (or J/K/L/;) keyboard** voices + **arrow-key seasons** + big **on-screen tappable** buttons; **idle auto-demo** blooms flowers + drifts seasons on its own after ~2 s (and on first load) so a muted glance shows a living, singing garden — any real input takes over instantly.

  References: **Toshio Iwai** — *Electroplankton* (Nintendo DS, 2005) / *TENORI-ON* (Yamaha, 2007), the playful "anyone can make beautiful music with a controller" lineage; **the May 2026 Steam Controller (2nd gen)** haptic-song demos (a game controller that plays whole songs through its rumble) — the direct inspiration for routing blooms into sympathetic haptics. Born from **RESEARCH §418** (a controller is a 2026 music-and-haptics instrument + the browser Gamepad API gives it to us free + grep-verified `gamepad` = 0 hits in the lab → a genuine never-used INPUT and never-used haptic OUTPUT). **Ambition 3/5**: **#1** first game-controller input AND first haptic-rumble output in the lab (grep-verified) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (5, above) + **#3** named refs (Iwai / Steam Controller); **#5 soft** (the Steam Controller news is ~5–6 weeks, just past the strict 30-day line, and the Gamepad/haptic primitive is foundational — held soft, not hard-claimed). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-13 + rolling-10 saturation)=[three.js OUTPUT · SVG OUTPUT · mic/voice INPUT · onset/beat/tempo TECH · pentatonic-wash VIBE · growing-creature SPINE · camera INPUT (3× last 10) · WebGL2 OUTPUT (3× last 10)] · picked=[**game-controller/Gamepad INPUT** (lab-first, off-glass — not camera/mic/touch) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (off three.js/SVG/WebGL2/WebGPU) · **controller→warm-JI-synth + dual-rumble-haptic TECH** (not onset/beat/tempo, not data-sonification 2×) · **kids playful-warm tactile VIBE** (not a creature, not a puzzle, not the pentatonic wash)]` — clean on every banned tag. Winner of the kids DEEP fire; banked `586-kids-light-painter` (two-stick continuous chord-bend "light painter") + `588-kids-glow-drive` (steer-a-comet / wake-singing-stars spatial field) as IDEAS §418. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 110s`, `✓ Generating static pages (454/454)`, `/dream/587-kids-button-garden 4.67 kB / 111 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no physical gamepad/audio/iOS in sandbox) — the idle auto-demo + keyboard + on-screen-button fallbacks mean the garden always blooms and sings on a glance with zero hardware.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/587-kids-button-garden/README.md`

### [588-tidal-organ](/dream/588-tidal-organ) · adults / live-ocean-data / oceanic-warm · cycle 419 · demoable
  **A warm breathing instrument tuned by the LIVE state of a real ocean right now.** Pick (or geolocate) a coastline and the piece fetches the real current marine swell (Open-Meteo Marine API, no key, CORS-direct) and lets it *play* a slow just-intonation **sea-organ** drone over a luminous WebGPU ocean-energy field (Canvas2D fallback): wave-period = the breath of the whole texture, wave-height = how many stacked JI voices + brightness, swell-period = root pitch, swell-direction = stereo pan. The directest hit on the 2026-06-13 jury's loudest, repeatedly-named, every-jury-starved adult ask — **real-world-data sonification, "music *about* something other than music," tension in the data not a synth knob** — and it's warm, not a puzzle. Refs: Zadar Sea Organ / Annea Lockwood / "Pulse of an Ocean" buoy lineage / arXiv 2602.14560 (ENSO→gamelan, Feb 2026). ~3s auto-start on a drifting demo-swell so a silent 06:30 glance still breathes. Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (banked `589-still-bloom` device-motion-stillness→Radigue-drone WebGPU reaction-diffusion + `590-star-atlas` aim-the-phone-at-the-real-sky Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* — IDEAS §419).

### [594-disintegration-loop](/dream/594-disintegration-loop) · adults / long-form-generative / elegiac · cycle 421 · demoable
  **A recording that crumbles as it plays.** A warm just-intonation lullaby fragment is rendered once into a "tape" (a single mutable `Float32Array`), then **re-recorded onto itself pass after pass** through a wear chain — a downward-creeping one-pole lowpass (highs flake first, ~9 kHz → ~420 Hz floor), accumulating **oxide dropouts** (a persistent `dropoutMap` that only ever deepens, punching permanent holes), wow/flutter resampling jitter, and `tanh` tape saturation — so the erosion is **genuine compounding state, never reset and never an LFO**. The music at minute 5 is materially hollower than at minute 0; it decays into a ghost. The **only** interaction is one vertical lever: top = *hold on* (slows the decay, mixes a whisper of the pristine seed back in — buys time, never undoes), bottom = *let go* (accelerates the crumble) — a moral choice about letting a recording disappear, **tension you feel, not a puzzle you solve.** The Canvas2D waveform strip is driven by the *actual* degradation state (live peak envelope + dropout map + lowpass cutoff): bars wear down, holes punch through, warm amber desaturates to ash, a violet playhead sweeps each pass. The lab's **first piece built on audible decay/disintegration as accumulating state** — distinct from its many spectral (`243`/`267`/`321`) and granular (`227`) treatments, which visualize or scatter sound but never temporally self-degrade. A genuinely different **register** from the four warm reactive screen-toys before it (576/580/583/588): long-form, witnessed, about memory and loss. Refs: **William Basinski — *The Disintegration Loops* (2002)** + **Music Thing Modular "Degenerator"** self-overwriting looper (Synthtopia, 2026-05-28). ~2.5s idle auto-start so a silent 06:30 glance shows the tape visibly crumbling and (un-muted) hear it hollow. Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire — one concept attacked by 3 techniques; banked `595-disintegration-spectral` (frequency-domain erosion) + `596-disintegration-dust` (granular WebGPU crumble) as IDEAS §421.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/594-disintegration-loop/README.md`

### [597-kids-water-bowls](/dream/597-kids-water-bowls) · kids / friction-resonator-synthesis / serene-watery · cycle 422 · demoable
  **Six glowing water bowls a 4-year-old can play with no reading — TAP to ring them like bells, RUB a finger round the rim to make them SING.** The heart is the lab's **first friction-excited resonator / modal-synthesis primitive** (grep-verified 0× across 594 prior prototypes): each bowl is one resonator excited two ways. **Rub = a sustained glass-armonica friction tone** — looping pink-ish noise through a **high-Q bandpass** (Q 14→26) tuned to the bowl's pitch + 3 quiet inharmonic shimmer sines, where **drag speed** (smoothed px/ms) drives amplitude + brightness (bandpass opens upward) + Q in real time, so rub faster → louder/brighter/more focused, stop → it decays over ~0.6s. **Tap = a struck inharmonic bell** (additive sines at 1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2×, ~4ms attack, higher partials decay faster). Tuning is deliberately **not** pentatonic: just-intonation Lydian-ish `1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 11/8, 3/2, 5/3` over ~F3 (the 11/8 raised-4th = a watery floating color). Higher water = lower pitch (real physics, drawn so it teaches itself). Canvas2D glowing vessels with bold per-bowl colors, tap ripples + brightness bloom, rub standing-wave caustic shimmer, finger-glow trail, ambient water bed (never silent). Engineered for kids: ≥64px well-spaced bowls, pre-created nodes for <50ms response, multi-touch (two bowls sing at once), no fail states, kid-safe master chain (lowpass 7200 + compressor −18/6/12, gains capped), and a clever **audio-rate smoothing loop decoupled from the visual rAF** so the rub stays smooth even if the canvas hitches. ~2.5s idle auto-rub demo so a silent 06:30 glance sees + hears a bowl singing. Refs: Indian **Jal Tarang** (struck water bowls) + Benjamin Franklin's **glass armonica** (1761, rubbed glass). Winner of a kids **DEEP** fire — one instrument, 3 synthesis cores; banked `598-kids-water-bowls` (modal additive) + `599-kids-water-bowls` (bowed-waveguide AudioWorklet) as IDEAS §422. Resurrects the §420-banked seed `592`.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/597-kids-water-bowls/README.md`

### [601-feedback-howl](/dream/601-feedback-howl) · adults / no-input-feedback-synthesis · cycle 423 · demoable
  **An instrument that is nothing but its own scream.** No oscillator, no sample, no microphone — the sound IS a Web Audio graph fed back into itself, self-oscillating on the edge of runaway, and you sculpt the howl OFF the glass: keyboard and (on a phone) device-tilt bend, choke, and spike it without ever touching the screen. The lab's **first no-input self-oscillating feedback synthesis** (grep-verified: many oscillator/sampler/granular synths, but nothing whose sound is its own feedback loop), and the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-14's demand to swing OUT of the cozy attractor (warm-JI bed ~7×/15, warm-serene VIBE ~9×/15, Canvas2D 6×/15) and build something with **edges** — abrasive, on-the-brink, felt-tension-not-a-puzzle — that renders **off Canvas2D** and takes input from **something other than a fingertip**. Winner of an adult **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal abrasive explorers).

  Signal path (no external source): a single ring — `feedbackGain → delay → bandpass → peaking → waveShaper(soft saturation) → dcBlock(highpass) → feedbackGain` — kicked into self-oscillation by one short decaying noise seed at start, loop gain sitting just below 1.0 so it howls without running away. A post-shaper tap → `masterGain → DynamicsCompressor(brick-wall limiter) → AnalyserNode → destination`. **Safety (non-negotiable for feedback):** hard loop-gain cap `LOOP_GAIN_MAX = 0.985` (never true runaway), master brick-wall limiter (−8 dB, knee 0, ratio 20, 2 ms attack), a ~90 Hz DC-blocking highpass (kills the sub-pile-up that wrecks feedback loops), an instant **PANIC** that ramps loop gain + master to 0, sane start volume (~0.16 auto-demo / ~0.32 play) + always-visible mute — abrasive, never literally painful or speaker-destroying.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **feedback DSP network** (`audio.ts`) — the self-oscillating ring + every safety clamp + `applyParams`/`applyStab`/`applyPanic`/`readAnalyser`, all param changes glided via `setTargetAtTime`. (2) **keyboard control mapping** (`page.tsx`) — ↑/↓ loop gain (tension toward the edge), ←/→ delay time (pitch of the howl), `[`/`]` cutoff, `-`/`=` resonance Q, `1`…`9` jump cutoff across the band, space = a "stab" gain-spike screech, `P`/Escape = PANIC. (3) **tilt control mapping** — `deviceorientation` (iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` inside the start gesture) → forward/back bends cutoff, left/right bends delay/pitch; falls back silently to keyboard-only. (4) **WGSL reactive spectrum render** (`gpu.ts`) — a violent full-screen frequency lattice that smears and whites out at resonant peaks, driven by the live `AnalyserNode`, with a real Canvas2D fallback (writhing lattice + tearing scanlines + waveform ghost). **No API route, no guard, no npm deps** (pure client Web Audio + WebGPU; nothing recorded or sent). Fallbacks, each with a visible `text-rose-300` notice: tilt denied/unavailable → keyboard-only; WebGPU absent → Canvas2D; audio gated → the Start button is the gesture; ~2.5 s idle **auto-demo** self-starts a safe low-level feedback so a muted 06:30 glance already shows a living, writhing field, and the demo "wander" resumes after ~5 s idle.

  References: **Toshimaru Nakamura** — *no-input mixing board* (an instrument that is nothing but its own feedback); **David Tudor** — live-electronic feedback works (*Rainforest* / *Pulsers* lineage). Born from **RESEARCH §423** (the abrasive register is 0× in the lab; GPU-compute AV spectacle is a current 2026 technique — nibi/ShaderVine; and a grep caught that the obvious edgy ideas were already shipped — `387-drop-engine` EDM build-drop, `422-shatter-piano` granular destruction — so this swung to a genuinely un-built synthesis paradigm instead). **Ambition honest 3/5**: **#1** first no-input self-oscillating feedback synthesis in the lab (grep-verified) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (4, above) + **#3** named refs (Nakamura / Tudor). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-14)=[Canvas2D OUTPUT 6× · touch INPUT 4× · warm-JI-drone/chord bed ~7× · warm/serene/cozy VIBE ~9× · third real-ocean-data] · picked=[**keyboard + device-tilt INPUT** (off-glass, not a fingertip, not mic) · **WebGPU OUTPUT** (off Canvas2D; Canvas2D only as fallback) · **no-input feedback self-oscillation TECH** (not warm-JI, not onset/beat) · **abrasive / on-the-brink VIBE** (felt tension, the opposite of cozy)]` — clean on every banned tag. Winner of the adult WIDE fire; banked `600-data-scream` (voice → WebGPU Ikeda test-pattern data-strobe) + `602-motion-thrash` (camera optical-flow → violent industrial noise + WebGPU strobe) as IDEAS §423. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 44s`, `✓ Generating static pages (459/459)`, `/dream/601-feedback-howl 6.66 kB / 110 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no real GPU/audio/tilt in sandbox) — the auto-demo + keyboard + Canvas2D/keyboard-only fallbacks mean the field writhes on a glance with zero hardware, and Karel can ride the brink himself at his desk.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/601-feedback-howl/README.md`

### [603-kids-yell-blob](/dream/603-kids-yell-blob) · kids / comic-vocal-resynthesis / silly-monster · cycle 424 · demoable
  **The lab's FIRST funny prototype — a giant wobbly googly blob you YELL at, and it squashes, inflates, and HONKS your own voice back like a kazoo.** After 600+ pieces not one was comedic (every kids toy warm/gentle/awe — the exact cozy autopilot JURY 2026-06-14 banned). The kid-safe translation of the jury's demand for "edges" (fast/loud/abrasive/**funny**/unsettling) is the one register a 4-year-old can take loud: **silly.** Roar/sing/shout into the mic → a 40-point spring-mass **soft-body blob** (raw WebGL2, hand-written GLSL ES 3.00, big googly eyes) inflates, jiggles, and overshoots with squash-and-stretch (loudness raises the rest-radius + `popBlob` kicks the rim so it bounces back, a per-axis squash spring flattens-then-stretches on a loud onset), tinted cyan (high voice) → pink (low voice) by spectral centroid — and the kid's voice is **honked back, exaggerated**: detuned saw+square → **ring modulator** → **WaveShaper** (cartoon clip) → **swept bandpass**, pitched/bent by their voice with a drooping "HONK… honk…" envelope (rate-limited so it's comedic, not a buzz), plus a quiet distorted live-voice echo. It's a real **instrument** (the kid's yell IS transformed into the sound — the paradigm that won `601-feedback-howl`), not a reactive visualizer. Kid-safe master chain `gain → lowpass 7kHz → DynamicsCompressor (brick-wall, −10/0/20, 3ms/250ms) → destination`, pre-created nodes for <50ms response, no painful transients. Graceful: mic denied → visible `text-rose-300` notice + **hold-the-screen / mash-any-key fake-yell** (still bounces + honks); WebGL2 absent → notice. ~2.5s idle **auto-demo** yells at itself so a silent 06:30 glance shows it alive; real input preempts instantly, demo resumes after ~5s quiet. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps, no CDN, no three.js** (pure client Web Audio + raw WebGL2; nothing recorded or sent). References: **Disney squash-and-stretch** (Preston Blair, *Cartoon Animation*); **holtsetio (Niklas Niehus) WebGPU `softbodies` / Softbody Tetris (2026-03-13)** for the gooey soft-body feel; classic cartoon foley (**Carl Stalling / Treg Brown** honks). Born from **RESEARCH §424** (soft-body squish is a current 2026 WebGPU technique; the lab is 0× comedic — "funny" is the kid-native form of the jury's "edges"). **Ambition honest 2/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (mic RMS+centroid analysis · spring-mass soft-body · raw-WebGL2 render · comic ring-mod/waveshaper honk DSP = 4) + **#3** named refs (Blair / holtsetio / Stalling-Brown). #1 NOT claimed (grep: Verlet [303] / ball-pit physics [460] / "jelly" toys [125/286] pre-exist — squish isn't a clean lab-first; the genuine novelty is the comedic REGISTER, a diversity win not an ambition criterion); #5 NOT claimed (Softbody Tetris ~3 mo). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-14)=[Canvas2D OUTPUT 6× · touch INPUT 4× · warm-JI-drone/chord bed ~7× · warm/serene/cozy VIBE ~9× · camera over-mined · gamepad just-used · third-ocean-data] · picked=[**mic-voice INPUT** (off-glass, not a fingertip, not camera/gamepad) · **raw-WebGL2 OUTPUT** (off Canvas2D) · **comic-vocal-resynthesis + soft-body-squish TECH** (not warm-JI, not onset/beat) · **silly-monster VIBE** (loud + funny, the opposite of cozy)]` — clean on every banned tag, and the directest fill of the lab's 0×-comedic gap. Winner of a kids **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal silly explorers); banked `604-kids-stomp-band` (shake → three.js cartoon-foley junk pile) + `605-kids-blow-parade` (blow → WebGPU balloon raspberry parade) as IDEAS §424. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 45s`, `✓ Generating static pages (460/460)`, `/dream/603-kids-yell-blob 5.7 kB / 109 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no real mic/audio/GPU in sandbox) — the auto-demo + tap/keyboard fake-yell mean it wobbles + honks on a glance with zero hardware, and Karel can yell at it himself.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/603-kids-yell-blob/README.md`

### [619-kids-plinko-bells](/dream/619-kids-plinko-bells) · kids / Galton-quincunx-stochastic-physics / emergent-STEM-wonder · cycle 430 · demoable
  **"Marble Bells" — a 4-year-old drops a glowing marble into a peg field and watches *chance itself* play a chord.** Tap anywhere up top to release a little cluster of marbles; they cascade through a triangular **Galton / quincunx** peg lattice (~12 rows), bouncing left or right at every row with a slight center-pull plus real collision jitter (so the binomial → normal distribution emerges and no two runs look alike), and land in one of **9 just-intonation bins**. Each landing rings that bin's warm **struck bell** (additive sines at ~1, 2.0, 3.0, 4.2×, ~4ms attack, higher partials decaying faster). Because more marbles end up in the middle (the bell curve), the **center bins fill fastest** — and the bins are tuned symmetrically so the center IS the root/fifth/octave: the emergent chord is rooted and warm, richest at the middle, gentle color at the edges. The histogram bars ARE the live distribution; counts slowly bleed off so it can play forever. Nine JI bins over F3 ≈ 174.6 Hz (`1/2, 2/3, 3/4, 5/6, 1/1, 5/4, 3/2, 5/3, 2/1`), each a bold saturated hue — color is the language, nothing is ever "wrong." The lab's **first Galton-board / quincunx stochastic-physics instrument** (grep-verified 0× across 618 prior prototypes), and the directest break from the kids **silly-googly-creature 3-peat** (603/609/617): a mesmerizing STEM-wonder register — chance, made audible — not a creature, not mic, not cozy. Renders on **WebGL2** (raw GLSL ES 3.00, instanced glowing marbles/pegs/bins — the deliberately *under*-used renderer, off both the over-corrected WebGPU and jury-flagged Canvas2D), with a full **Canvas2D fallback** + a live backend badge. Kid-safe master chain (`gain → lowpass 7.5kHz → DynamicsCompressor −16/6/12 → destination`, gains capped, bells micro-rate-limited), gesture-gated AudioContext, always-on root+fifth+octave pad so it's never silent. ~2.2s idle **auto-rain** so a silent 06:30 glance is already cascading + singing; real taps preempt instantly. Refs: **Sir Francis Galton's quincunx / "bean machine" (1894)** (central-limit-theorem, binomial→normal); **Wintergatan's Marble Machine** (marbles-as-instrument); **Insook Choi, "Interactive Sonification Exploring Emergent Behavior" (2018)**. Love-aligned to Karel's emergent-music cluster (`236-particle-life-song`❤️, `130-tsl-particle-compute`❤️). Winner of a kids **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal explorers); banked `620-kids-music-mill` (rotating music-box peg sequencer, self-playing) + `621-kids-duet-bloom` (two-finger cooperative consonance, the social lane) as IDEAS §430. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 40s`, `✓ Generating static pages (467/467)`, `/dream/619-kids-plinko-bells 5.36 kB / 112 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/audio/pointer in sandbox) — the auto-rain + Canvas2D fallback mean it cascades and chimes on a glance with zero hardware.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/619-kids-plinko-bells/README.md`

### [622-wolfram-rhythm](/dream/622-wolfram-rhythm) · adults / 1D-elementary-CA-Wolfram-rule / austere-mechanical · cycle 431 · demoable
  **"Wolfram Rhythm" — a single integer composes the music.** A 1D **elementary cellular automaton** unfolds row by row on a musical clock: each cell's next state comes from its left/self/right neighbourhood via an 8-bit **rule number** (0–255), and every newly-born row's live cells fire short, edged percussion panned by column over a whole-tone set. The **rule number IS the composition** — `30` chaotic/noisy, `90` a self-similar Sierpinski fractal, `110` complex/Turing-complete (gliders collide), `184` traffic, etc. — change it (the one lever: a ±stepper, a 0–255 slider, and six labeled presets) and the entire character transforms. The lab's **first 1D elementary-CA / Wolfram-rule instrument** (grep-verified 0× — the existing CA pieces are 2D: `25`/`180` Conway Life, `264` Lenia, `360`/`377` sandpile), and a deliberate swing OFF the adult **edges/real-data autopilot** (601 howl · 606 vivisection · 613 seismic · 618 solar) into an austere, rule-driven register — edged (Rule 30 is literally chaos) but neither loud-abrasive nor cozy.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 4): (1) **1D-CA rule engine** (`engine.ts`) — toroidal `stepRow`, centered/soup seeds, the 256-rule lookup, `PRESETS`. (2) **long-form watchdog** (`StagnationDetector`) — detects dead / frozen / short-cycle states and `perturb`s (flips cells) or re-seeds so the piece keeps evolving for minutes rather than locking up. (3) **CA→percussion synth** (`audio.ts`) — metal-FM / band-passed-noise-click / snappy-pluck voices, column→whole-tone pitch + column→pan, 24-voice steal-oldest cap, master → brick-wall limiter (no warm drone anywhere). (4) **WebGL2 render** — the iconic scrolling Wolfram diagram via a texture-uploaded grid + fragment shader (newest row flashes at a bright playhead line, old rows fade as they scroll), with a real **Canvas2D fallback** + visible notice. Idle visual auto-start on mount (audio gated behind **Begin** per autoplay), so a silent glance is already a self-drawing diagram. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps** (pure client Web Audio + WebGL2; nothing recorded or sent).

  References: **Stephen Wolfram, _A New Kind of Science_ (2002)** (elementary CA, rules 30/90/110); **Matthew Cook** (Rule-110 Turing-completeness, 2004); **Iannis Xenakis** (cellular automata in composition — the severe/architectural register). Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire (3 CA paradigms — 2D Conway Life, 1D Wolfram, continuous Lenia); the **only duplication-clean** candidate (`620-life-engine` duplicated `25`/`180`, `622-quickening` duplicated `264`), banked both as cautionary IDEAS §431. **Ambition honest 3/5**: #1 first 1D-elementary-CA/Wolfram instrument (grep-0×) + #2 ≥3 subsystems (4) + #3 named refs (Wolfram / Cook / Xenakis); #5 not claimed (foundational). **Diversity** `banned=[WGSL-WebGPU 5× over-corrected · edges/real-data adult autopilot · JURY-14 Canvas2D/touch/warm-JI/cozy/ocean] · picked=[rule-number-stepper + sparse-perturb INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · 1D-elementary-CA TECH · austere-mechanical VIBE]` — clean on every banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 73s`, `✓ Generating static pages (467/467)`, `/dream/622-wolfram-rhythm 6.15 kB / 109 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/audio in sandbox) — the on-mount auto-running diagram + Canvas2D fallback mean it draws on a glance with zero hardware; unverified by ear: that rules 30/90/110 are audibly distinct and the metallic voices read as edged-not-harsh.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/622-wolfram-rhythm/README.md`

### [630-piano-refract](/dream/630-piano-refract) · adults / NMF-spectral-factorization / dissective-prismatic · cycle 434 · demoable
  **"Piano Refract" — the lab's FIRST genuine cycle-2 deepening: Karel's own solo piano refracted like light through a prism into five voices you solo, mute & re-mix.** This is the deepening of the loved standout `606-piano-vivisection` the jury begged for by hand ("606 is the model — it just needed a cycle 2 to actually reach 4/5") two juries running. Stage 1 keeps 606's bulletproof median-filter **HPSS** split (strings vs. hammers) as the legible *ground*; stage 2 is the new refraction — the harmonic (strings) layer is re-analyzed and fanned by **NMF** (non-negative matrix factorization, the lab's first — grep-0× across 600+ prototypes) into **four pitched register voices** (Low / Low-mid / High-mid / High). Five independently-mixable looping voices in total (Hammers + the 4 registers): solo isolates one, mute drops it, faders re-balance — all **instant & click-free** (every buffer plays continuously, sample-aligned; only gain nodes ramp). The technique register the jury praised in 606 ("clinical not cozy, his own music") — analytical and prismatic, neither doom nor warm.

  **The legibility fix (what makes this a real cycle-2, not a restart):** plain unsupervised NMF returns abstract components that are unstable run-to-run and impossible to name — the exact weakness that lost the *original* §425-banked `607-piano-prism` seed to 606. This build **warm-starts** the NMF basis `W`: each of the four columns is seeded as a Gaussian-in-log-frequency bump centered at **~150 / 350 / 800 / 1800 Hz** (with a positive floor so updates still adapt to Karel's real partials), biasing every component to settle into a **stable, musically-meaningful register regardless of the run**. The seeded-basis idea is Smaragdis & Brown (2003) applied for *legibility* rather than transcription.

  Subsystems (≥3 → 6): (1) **audio loader** (`audio.ts`, copied verbatim from 606) — fetches Karel's real solo-piano recording via the read-only `/api/audio/<id>` route, synthesized piano fallback. (2) **median-filter HPSS** (`hpss.ts`, from 606) — STFT/ISTFT, per-bin/per-frame medians, soft Wiener masks (phase kept). (3) **register-seeded KL-NMF** (`nmf.ts`) — warm-started multiplicative updates (~70 iters), chunked with `await` yields + progress bar. (4) **per-component Wiener resynthesis** — `Mk = Vk/ΣVj` applied to the original complex harmonic STFT → ISTFT → four register PCM buffers. (5) **5-voice mixer** — `BufferSource → GainNode → AnalyserNode → master → DynamicsCompressor → destination`, sample-aligned, instant solo/mute/gain. (6) **WebGL2 "prism" render** (`gl.ts`) — an incoming white beam splits into five colored bands (steel hammers + violet→rose→amber→emerald registers), each driven by its live level, soloed glows / muted dims, with a real **Canvas2D fallback** + backend badge; idle animation on mount + ~2.5s auto-demo cycles the solo so a silent 06:30 glance is already alive. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps** (READ-only of the existing audio route; nothing recorded or sent).

  Off-glass controls: keyboard `1`–`5` solo / `0` reset / `m` mute selected / `←→` select / `↑↓` gain / space play-pause; device-tilt "spectral spotlight" (iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` inside the Begin gesture, silent keyboard fallback + `text-rose-300` notice if denied). References: **Lee & Seung, _Nature_ 1999** (NMF + multiplicative updates); **Smaragdis & Brown, WASPAA/ISMIR 2003** (NMF on music spectrograms; seeded basis); **Fitzgerald, DAFx 2010** (the HPSS ground). Born from **RESEARCH §434** (NMF source-separation is a live 2026 front — Deep Non-Negative Autoencoders, arXiv 2510.08816; piano is "particularly amenable, beautiful key-hammer extraction" — Collins, eContact). Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire (one concept, 2 legibility approaches): beat `629-piano-prism` (random-init NMF + post-hoc centroid pitch-SORT) on **reliability + legibility** — the warm start is deterministic where the sort still rode the random-init lottery; banked 629 as IDEAS §434. **Ambition honest 4/5** — the lab's first honest #4 in two juries: **#1** first NMF in the lab (grep-0×) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (6) + **#3** named refs (Lee&Seung / Smaragdis&Brown / Fitzgerald) + **#4** genuine multi-cycle commitment (cycle 2 of the 606 decomposition spine, claimed honestly for the first time). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-15)=[touch INPUT 5× · WebGPU OUTPUT 6× · adult ominous/dark/austere "edges" reflex 5× · 4th real-data sonification on 588/613/618 template] · picked=[**his-piano-file + keyboard/tilt INPUT** (off-glass, not touch) · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (not WebGPU; Canvas2D fallback) · **NMF spectral-factorization TECH** (grep-0×, not real-data) · **dissective/prismatic VIBE** (analytical, neither doom nor cozy — the "his own material, clinical" register the jury praised in 606)]` — clean on every banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 37.3s`, `✓ Generating static pages (470/470)`, `/dream/630-piano-refract 10.1 kB / 113 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no real GPU/audio in sandbox) — the idle prism animation + auto-demo + Canvas2D/keyboard fallbacks mean it reads as alive on a glance with zero hardware; unverified by ear: that the warm-started registers separate cleanly on Karel's actual recording and that solo/mute is as click-free as designed.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/630-piano-refract/README.md`

### `632-polymeter-loom` — `demoable` · adult · WIDE-winner (cycle 435, 2026-06-15)
[/dream/632-polymeter-loom](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/632-polymeter-loom) — **"Polymeter Loom": interlocking Euclidean rhythms woven into a hypnotic groove you watch being woven.** Five pitched-percussion voices (kick · bass · marimba · bell · shaker) each play a **Euclidean rhythm** E(k,n) — k onsets spread maximally-evenly over n steps via the **Bjorklund** algorithm — on *different* step-counts (16/8/16/16/12), so the combined groove is **polymetric** and only realigns after the LCM of all step-counts (a long, evolving loop). The marimba+bell share a pattern but one runs **1.2% faster** — **Steve Reich phasing** — sliding out of unison and slowly back, so minute 2 sounds unlike minute 0 (long-form state without a loop). A **three.js loom** renders each voice as a concentric bead ring; beads flash (emissive + scale pop) on each onset, and the phasing rings visibly counter-rotate — the weave tightening and loosening. Off-glass controls: **Weave/Pause**, keys `1`–`5` toggle voices, `q/a w/s e/d r/f t/g` nudge each voice's onset count k, `space` toggles phasing, tempo slider 70–150 BPM. Idle auto-demo (loom rotates/breathes + rings drift before any audio); AudioContext created/resumed inside the first gesture (iOS unlock); graceful `text-rose-300` fallbacks for missing WebGL/AudioContext. References: **Steve Reich** (*Music for 18 Musicians*, *Clapping Music* — phasing); **Godfried Toussaint**, *The Euclidean Algorithm Generates Traditional Musical Rhythms* (2005). Born from **RESEARCH §435** (groove = microtiming/interlocking-meter science; JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #2 "build the missing middle — danceable/groove"). Winner of an adult **WIDE** fire (3 missing-middle registers): beat `631-drop-engine` (euphoric EDM build-and-drop + lab-first sidechain pump) and `633-slow-burn` (tender neo-soul microtiming) on honest ambition (two canonical refs that drive it) + output diversity (three.js, 0× in last 10) + cleanest vibe-threading (groove that dodges BOTH banned poles — doom AND cozy/warm); both losers banked as strong **IDEAS §435** seeds. **Ambition honest 3/5** — **#2** ≥3 subsystems (Euclidean generator + phasing clock/lookahead scheduler + multi-voice FM/pluck synth + three.js loom = 4) + **#3** named refs (Reich + Toussaint). #1 not claimed (euclidean/polymeter/Reich all referenced earlier in the corpus — only `sidechain`, which was 631's, was grep-0×). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-15)=[touch INPUT 5× · WebGPU OUTPUT 6× · adult ominous/dark/austere "edges" reflex 5× · 4th real-data sonification on 588/613/618 template] + rolling-10 over-rep=[WebGL2 OUTPUT 4×] · picked=[**keyboard + voice-toggle INPUT** (off-glass, not touch) · **three.js OUTPUT** (0× in last 10 — off banned WebGPU AND over-rep WebGL2) · **Euclidean-polymeter + Reich-phasing TECH** · **hypnotic/groove VIBE** (danceable, neither doom nor cozy)]` — clean on every banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, 471/471 static pages, `/dream/632-polymeter-loom` prerendered static ○, in `app-path-routes-manifest.json`, `.html` emitted), **NOT browser-verified** (no real GPU/audio in sandbox) — the idle loom rotation + bead pulse + WebGL/audio fallbacks mean it reads as alive on a glance with zero hardware; unverified by ear: that the Reich phasing pair audibly drifts and the polymetric weave grooves on-device.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/632-polymeter-loom/README.md`

### `636-kids-star-pilot` — `demoable` · kids · WIDE-winner (cycle 436, 2026-06-15)
[/dream/636-kids-star-pilot](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/636-kids-star-pilot) — **"Star Pilot": a 4-year-old flies a glowing comet with a game controller through a field of musical star-gates — and the gates they pass keep echoing back, so a melody builds itself.** Push the **left stick** (or **arrow keys / WASD** — automatic fallback, live `gamepadconnected` switch) to fly a momentum-driven ship through six **just-intonation-tuned star-gates** that bloom and ring soft additive bell-tones; face buttons (or **space / 1–4**) drop sparkly mallet+shimmer percussion. The heart is a **lookahead-scheduled loop-memory sequencer**: the last ~8 gates you visited are remembered and echo back on a slow pulse, so the music *accumulates and evolves* — minute 2 sounds unlike minute 0 (genuine state, not a loop). **No score, no fail state, no reading** — the ship softly bounces off the edges, it can't crash. Idle ~5s and a gentle auto-flight resumes so the scene is never silent and a glance always shows it alive. Renders on **Canvas2D** (a glowing additive-trail starfield — the deliberately under-used renderer, 0× in the last 10, off both the jury-banned WebGPU and the over-rep WebGL2). Kid-safe master chain (`gain → lowpass 7.5kHz → fast-attack DynamicsCompressor → destination`, gains capped, triggers rate-limited), Start-gesture AudioContext unlock, `text-violet-300` "connect a controller or use arrow keys" hint / `text-rose-300` canvas-fail notice. References: **Toshio Iwai, _Electroplankton_ (Nintendo DS, 2005)** + **Tetsuya Mizuguchi, _Rez_ / _Child of Eden_** (flying-through-a-synaesthetic-music-space). Born from **RESEARCH §436** (kid-native NON-touch inputs are under-used — the kids side keeps reaching for mic + camera-optical-flow; gamepad is 0× recently + jury-blessed via 587; and the active-wonder *middle* register is what the kids side lacks per JURY 2026-06-15). Winner of a kids **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal NON-touch explorers, honoring JURY provocation #4's touch ban): beat `634-kids-tilt-river` (tilt → flow-field light river, three.js) and `635-kids-sing-kite` (voice-pitch → kite altitude, Canvas2D) on diversity (gamepad is the rarest lab input) + genuine evolving state (loop-memory) + cleanest build; both banked as strong **IDEAS §436** seeds. **Ambition honest 2/5** (the kids norm): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (gamepad-poll + keyboard fallback · flight physics + gate collision · bell/percussion + loop-memory sequencer w/ lookahead clock · Canvas2D starfield = 4) + **#3** named refs (Iwai / Mizuguchi). #1 not claimed (gamepad used by 587; sequencer not lab-first). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-15)=[touch INPUT · WebGPU OUTPUT · adult "edges" reflex · 4th real-data] + rolling-10=[WebGL2 OUTPUT 4×] · picked=[**gamepad+keyboard INPUT** (off-glass; rarest in lab) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** (0× in last 10) · **gamepad-flight + loop-memory-sequencer TECH** · **playful arcade-wonder VIBE** (the missing middle — neither warm-cozy nor silly-funny)]` — clean on every banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 33.9s`, `✓ Generating static pages (472/472)`, `/dream/636-kids-star-pilot 4.54 kB / 111 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified** (no real gamepad/audio in sandbox) — the auto-demo + keyboard fallback mean it flies + sings on a glance with zero hardware, and Karel can fly it with arrow keys; unverified by ear/hand: that the JI gates read as a melody and the loop-memory echo audibly evolves on-device.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/636-kids-star-pilot/README.md`

### `649-chess-duet` — `demoable` · adult · WIDE-winner (cycle 441, 2026-06-16)
[/dream/649-chess-duet](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/649-chess-duet) — **"Chess Duet": a famous chess game played as a slow, inevitable TWO-VOICE counterpoint — White and Black are two instruments, and you hear the whole drama (quiet opening → escalating sacrifices → the sudden-quiet → the inevitable mate) as a piece that composes itself move by move.** The lab's FIRST piece that sonifies a *structured real-world game* as music — not a physical sensor feed, not an FFT of a sound: the combinatorial structure of a played game **is** the score (the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #5, "music about something other than music — a live chess game as a duet"). Bundled game: **Anderssen vs Kieseritzky, "The Immortal Game," London 1851** (45 plies, ending `23. Be7#`), parsed by a from-scratch SAN parser (no npm dep, no network). Two alternating voices read as call-and-response counterpoint — **White** = warm triangle+sine ("left hand", upper staff), **Black** = a darker FM/reedy saw ("right hand", lower staff); file a–h → scale degree in **D harmonic minor** (dramatic mode), rank 1–8 → register, piece type → articulation (pawn short · queen long & rich · knight grace-note leap · bishop gliding · rook firm). Game *events* drive the drama: a **capture** = a dissonant clash that resolves; a **check** = a held low tension pedal tone; **castling** = a settled perfect-fifth cadence; the final **checkmate** = a darkly resolving held chord; a running **material-balance** signal biases the timbre brighter/darker so the arc is *felt*. Renders on **inline SVG** (the scarcest renderer — last SVG was 637; off the jury-banned WebGPU, off the rolling-hot three.js [3×], off the over-rep WebGL2): an 8×8 board lights each move's from/to squares (captures glow red, mate glows indigo) beside an unfolding two-staff score with a tracking playhead, plus the legible SAN of the current move. Ear-safe master chain (`gain 0.42 → DynamicsCompressor → destination`); AudioContext created inside the Play gesture (iOS); **auto-starts the game ~2.5s after load** so a silent 06:30 glance is fully alive (board lighting up move by move + the duet playing, zero interaction/hardware). Tempo slider 1.0–3.5 s/move. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps** (pure client SVG + Web Audio — no network/mic/camera/secret). References: **Anderssen vs Kieseritzky, "The Immortal Game," 1851** (the bundled game); the informal lineage of chess-as-music / game-sonification, reframed here as strict two-voice counterpoint over a real master game with *events* (not just squares) driving the harmony. Born from **RESEARCH §441** (a corpus check killed two "0×" jury claims — MIDI-OUT already exists in `291-harmonograph`, WebRTC in `508-accord-call` — steering ambition toward the genuinely-0× lanes: structured-data-as-counterpoint and AI-pipeline chains; browser generative-composition is a live 2026 front — Loopstate/Riffer/KOSMOS sequencers, GEDMAS/GESMI algorithmic-EDM lineage, SMDIM arXiv 2603.00576 Feb-2026). Winner of an adult **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal explorers mapping 3 jury-named open lanes): beat `648-latent-chain` (the lab's first 2-model AI pipeline chain, audio→image→words→image — bolder #1 but budget-gated + invisible/unverifiable at a silent glance) and `650-endless-lift` (an ecstatic Shepard-fold gospel key-lift — the bulletproof ecstatic-register fill); both banked as strong **IDEAS §441** seeds. **Ambition honest 3/5**: **#1** first structured-game / counterpoint sonification in the lab (a genuinely new "machine," not the banned 588/613/618 physical-feed template) + **#2** ≥3 subsystems (from-scratch SAN parser + board model · two-voice contrapuntal mapper + Web Audio scheduler · event-driven harmony/drama state · SVG board+score renderer = 4) + **#3** the named game. #4 not claimed (single cycle); #5 not hard-claimed (SMDIM ~3.5mo, server-side — grounding only). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-15)=[touch INPUT 5× · WebGPU OUTPUT 6× · adult ominous/dark/austere "edges" reflex 5× · 4th real-data sonification on 588/613/618 template] + renderer-watch=[three.js hot 3× (632/643/645)] · picked=[**structured-game-data INPUT** (off touch, off recent mic/audio-file/physical-feed) · **inline-SVG OUTPUT** (the scarcest renderer — off banned WebGPU AND off hot three.js AND off over-rep WebGL2) · **game-structure→two-voice-counterpoint sonification TECH** (a new "machine," not the banned physical-feed template) · **cinematic / narrative-tension VIBE** (off BOTH banned poles — dramatic, not the ominous-austere autopilot, not cozy)]` — clean on every binding banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 91s`, `✓ Generating static pages (477/477)` [476 from cycle 440 + 649 = 477, confirming 648/650 absent], `/dream/649-chess-duet 6.93 kB / 113 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified by ear** (no audio in sandbox) — but uniquely low-risk: pure SVG + Web Audio with zero GPU/network/key/sensor surface, the 45 plies were verified against the historical game, and the 2.5s auto-start means the board + duet are alive on a glance with zero hardware; unverified: whether the D-harmonic-minor counterpoint + event-drama actually *feel* like the game's arc by ear (the one tuning surface).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/649-chess-duet/README.md`

### `652-kids-dance-band` — `demoable` · kids · WIDE-winner (cycle 442, 2026-06-16)
[/dream/652-kids-dance-band](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/652-kids-dance-band) — **"Dance Band": a 4-year-old makes a whole band play by DANCING in front of the camera — move more and more layers stack and the lights erupt; stand still and it eases back to a gentle pad.** The directest hit on JURY 2026-06-15's standing #1 kids critique ("warm-or-silly with nothing between — build the danceable/groovy register with *body* that nobody is building"). The webcam frame is drawn mirrored into a tiny `32×24` offscreen canvas; each rAF reads that grid, takes the **per-cell absolute frame-difference** from the previous frame (noise floor zeroed), and sums to a **global motion-energy** `0..1` plus a diff-weighted **spatial centroid** — **no ML model, no skeleton, pure pixels** (the ISMM "movement *energy* not pose" insight, RESEARCH §442). Energy passes an asymmetric low-pass (fast attack / slow release) and selects one of **5 groove tiers with hysteresis** (separate UP/DOWN thresholds so layers don't chatter): pad → +kick → +bass+hats → +snare backbeat → FULL-PARTY syncopated melody. A **112 BPM, 16-step Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** (`setInterval(25ms)` pump, ~120ms ahead of `audioCtx.currentTime`, **never** rAF) clocks every layer change and hit to the step grid, so intensity changes feel musical not twitchy; everything is **A-minor-pentatonic** so all movement is in-key (no wrong). The horizontal centroid pans the melody; energy raises filter/gain and brightness. **Output:** a Canvas2D glowing particle/energy field that erupts from the motion centroid (spark count/speed/hue scale with energy — violet calm → gold full-party), a coarse motion-heat overlay where the body moved, and a ring that flashes on every downbeat. **Privacy:** camera is `getUserMedia` on-device only, **no frames stored or sent** (zero network), with a reassuring note. Audio starts before the camera so the band is **never silent/broken**; soft attack envelopes + 1.2s master fade-in, no harsh transients. **Graceful degrade:** camera denied/unavailable → `text-rose-300` message + large (≥64px) 🌙-calm / **DANCE!** / 🔥-more fallback buttons and Arrow Up/Down, all driving the same energy signal so it fully demos with audio+visuals. References: **Golan Levin & Zachary Lieberman** — *Messa di Voce* / *Manual Input Sessions* (body as continuous reactive AV input); **IRCAM ISMM** research on sonifying movement *qualities* (energy/fluidity/impulsivity) + children's-spontaneous-movement sonification (RESEARCH §442). Born from RESEARCH §442 (movement-energy → groove; the visible research→build chain). Winner of a kids **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal NON-touch explorers in the missing-middle register, honoring the jury touch ban): beat `651-kids-choir-leader` (mic voice-pitch → in-key choir, tender — the realization of banked seed `635-kids-sing-kite`, love-aligned `158-kids-hum-paint`❤️) and `653-kids-bounce-band` (tilt → collision-quantized beat, bouncy); both banked as built-and-proven **IDEAS §442** seeds. **Ambition honest 2–3/5** (the kids norm): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (camera frame-diff motion-energy + centroid · 5-tier hysteresis mapper · clocked layered look-ahead groove engine · Canvas2D particle/heat field = 4) + **#3** named refs (Levin/Lieberman · ISMM). #1 contestable (optical-flow exists in the corpus; whole-body-motion-energy→layered-groove is application-level, discounted). **Love-aligned** with `234-kids-hand-creature`❤️ (camera/body) + the embodied kids cluster. **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-15)=[touch INPUT 5× · WebGPU OUTPUT 6× · adult "edges" reflex · 4th real-data template] · picked=[**webcam whole-body-motion-energy INPUT** (off-glass, NOT touch; camera absent from last 10) · **Canvas2D particle-field OUTPUT** (NOT WebGPU) · **frame-diff motion-energy → hysteresis-tiered layered groove TECH** · **ecstatic/danceable VIBE** (the jury's exact missing middle — NOT warm-glow, NOT silly-foley)]` — clean on every banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 47s`, `✓ Generating static pages (478/478)`, `/dream/652-kids-dance-band 4.54 kB / 111 kB First Load` prerendered static ○, `.html` emitted, `package-lock.json` restored so nothing outside the dream zone is committed), **NOT browser-verified** (no real camera/audio in sandbox) — the always-on soft groove + the DANCE! fallback button mean it plays + erupts on a glance with zero hardware (Karel can drive it with the on-screen buttons / arrow keys even with no camera); unverified by body: that the motion-energy thresholds + `6×` normalization feel right for a real child in a real room (lighting/distance will shift them — needs live calibration), and that the layered groove actually grooves on-device.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/652-kids-dance-band/README.md`

### `659-pulse-cathedral` — `demoable` · adult · WIDE-winner (cycle 445, 2026-06-16)
[/dream/659-pulse-cathedral](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/659-pulse-cathedral) — **"Pulse Cathedral": the lab's FIRST shipped ecstatic/euphoric adult piece — a euphoric EDM build-and-drop journey arc whose energy NEVER lands (riser → impact → DROP → climb → riser), with a sidechain pump that is both audible AND visible, and a Shepard-folded bass so the key reads as forever-rising.** The directest hit on JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #2 ("build the missing middle — ecstatic/danceable/groovy; the lab banks every ecstatic candidate [650/653-endless-lift] and never ships it"), and a journey-engine alternative Karel explicitly asked for (care #4: EDM build-and-drop as an alternate arc — still 0×). Audio: a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** (`setInterval(25ms)`, ~120ms ahead of `audioCtx.currentTime`, **never** rAF) clocks a layered Web-Audio bed — four-on-the-floor kick (sine pitch-drop + click), an offbeat rolling bass that is **sidechain-ducked by the kick** (a "pump bus" gain ramped down on each kick and released — the audible French-house pump), a detuned **supersaw** chord stack + pad, a bandpassed plucky arp, a filtered **noise riser** that sweeps a lowpass up through the BUILD, and a tasteful **impact** at the DROP — all in a bright Lydian-leaning mode. A **BUILD(8 bars)→DROP→SUSTAIN(4 bars)→BUILD arc state machine** (`arc.ts`) raises density/cutoff/intensity through the build and **Shepard-folds the bass octave** each cycle so the energy reads as a forever-climb (the "never-landing causal stream" from RESEARCH §445's LiveBand, made literal). **Output:** a raw **WebGL2** fragment-shader **cathedral nave** — brightness pulses with the kick and the **sidechain duck is *visible*** as a brightness dip-and-bloom, light-shafts/sparks erupt at the DROP, color sweeps violet (BUILD) → gold/white (DROP); animates before audio unlocks, with a **Canvas2D fallback** if WebGL2 is missing. Controls: **Start** (creates/resumes AudioContext in-gesture — iOS unlock + ~0.8s master fade-in), **SPACE / DROP** forces the next drop early, **↑/↓ ENERGY** adds/removes layers, **TEMPO** 120–130 BPM; ~2.5s idle → the arc runs itself (autonomous BUILD→DROP→SUSTAIN) so a silent glance is alive. Ear-safe master `gain ≤0.45 → DynamicsCompressor → destination`. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps, no network/key/mic/camera** (pure client Web Audio + WebGL2). References: the **EDM build-and-drop form** (riser → impact → drop); **sidechain "pumping" compression** (French-house / Eric Prydz lineage), modeled here as scheduled gain automation; **Roger Shepard**, *"Circularity in Judgments of Relative Pitch"* (1964) — the endless-climb illusion, folded into the bass; and the real-time *continuous causal music stream* research it echoes (Google DeepMind **Lyria RealTime** / **"LiveBand," arXiv 2606.03803, June 2026**) — cited as honest conceptual lineage, NOT implemented with ML. Born from **RESEARCH §445** (the reactive-accompaniment headline was killed by a corpus grep [251/256 already own that lane]; its surviving value — LiveBand's never-landing causal stream — bent the flagship into a euphoric arc that climbs forever). Winner of an adult **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal explorers across genuinely-open adult registers): beat `660-glasswork` (a tender 3am music-box, inline-SVG, Eno/Budd/Satie — the lab's intimacy gap; lower-leverage register + lower ambition) and `661-murmur-engine` (a boids murmuration AS the music, Canvas2D, Reynolds/Vicsek — but Canvas2D is HOT 3×→4× and flocking is not lab-first, so no #1); both banked as strong **IDEAS §445** seeds. **Ambition honest 2–3/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (look-ahead scheduler · sidechain-pumped layered bed · build-drop arc state machine · WebGL2 nave + Canvas2D fallback = 4) + **#3** named refs (EDM build-drop form · Prydz sidechain-pumping · Shepard 1964 · LiveBand/Lyria frame). **#1 SOFT** (Shepard foundational [40/132/187]; the audible+visible sidechain pump is plausibly lab-first but not hard-claimed); **#5 NOT hard-claimed** (LiveBand within-window but server-side ML — lineage only). The real value is the **0×-shipped ecstatic register fill** + a Karel-care-#4 journey-engine alternative. **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-15)=[touch INPUT 5× · WebGPU OUTPUT 6× · adult ominous/dark/austere "edges" reflex 5× · 4th real-data sonification on the 588/613/618 physical-feed template] + renderer-watch=[three.js HOT 3× · Canvas2D HOT 3×] · picked=[**keyboard + autonomous INPUT** (off banned touch; off recent mic/camera) · **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (the SCARCEST screen renderer — off banned WebGPU AND off BOTH hot three.js & Canvas2D; Canvas2D fallback only) · **EDM build-drop-arc + sidechain-pump + Shepard-fold TECH** · **ecstatic/euphoric VIBE** (the jury's exact 0×-shipped missing middle — dodges BOTH banned poles: NOT doom, NOT cozy)]` — clean on every binding banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 77s`, `✓ Generating static pages (481/481)` [480 from cycle 444 + 659 = 481, confirming 660/661 absent], `/dream/659-pulse-cathedral 7.65 kB / 110 kB First Load` prerendered static ○), **NOT browser-verified by ear** (no real audio/GPU in sandbox) — the ~2.5s idle auto-demo runs the BUILD→DROP→SUSTAIN arc and the WebGL2 nave animates before audio unlocks (Canvas2D fallback), so a silent 06:30 glance shows the cathedral pulsing + dropping with zero hardware; unverified: whether the pump *feels* like a pump, the drop *hits*, and the Shepard-fold reads as a forever-climb on real speakers (mix tuned by reasoning, not heard); the autonomous-demo visual kick is an approximation so its beat may drift slightly from the shader pulse.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/659-pulse-cathedral/README.md`

### `669-kids-hum-answer` — `demoable` · kids · WIDE-winner (cycle 448, 2026-06-16)
[/dream/669-kids-hum-answer](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/669-kids-hum-answer) — **"Hum & Answer": a child hums one note, closes their eyes, and an unseen friend answers — not by resolving sweetly, but by LEANING on a tense note and then SIGHING down to rest, so the child discovers tension→release with no screen at all.** The lab's **FIRST off-the-glass KIDS piece** — the directest hit on the jury's (2026-06-16) loudest, most-repeated, still-unaddressed directive ("get at least one builder off the glass; 13 of 15 render to a glass screen; the 2 that aren't are the best things here" + the Karel-facing line) — and it shipped the ⭐ PRIORITY off-glass banked seed (`664-kids-hum-answer`, §446) while pushing it past the banked single 4–3 into a full chain. **Off-the-glass / audio-first:** near-black screen, ONE soft CSS radial glow that breathes with post-limiter amplitude and shifts hue (violet/blue while a suspension is TENSE → warm amber as it RESOLVES); the whole experience is meant to work eyes-closed, the glow is there only for a glancing eye. **Input:** mic (`getUserMedia({audio})`); real-time monophonic pitch detection by **autocorrelation (Chris-Wilson approach)** — RMS gate + clarity gate + parabolic-interpolation refine, ~75–1000 Hz, **octave-collapsed** into a child-voice band (~G3–G4) to defend against octave errors; a short stability window means it answers a *deliberate* hum, not a passing slide. **The harmonic event (NOT pentatonic / NOT scale-snap):** the detected hum becomes a tonic and an unseen synthesized choir walks a genuine **suspension CHAIN** of three textbook common-practice figures that all resolve **DOWN by step** — **4–3** (P4 sighing to the M3), then **7–6** (m7 leaning to the M6), then **2–1** (M2 settling to the unison) — each tension held ~1.1 s, a ~0.3 s downward portamento "sigh," then ~0.9 s of rest, over a tonic drone an octave below. The child meets a tense note and hears it resolve: the denial of closure, then closure. **Synthesis (NO samples, NO AI voice):** formant-bandpassed sawtooth "ah" voices (three parallel bandpass resonators ~800/1150/2900 Hz). Safe master chain `gain (0.34) → DynamicsCompressor → destination`, capped per-voice gain 0.16, soft envelopes, no harsh transients. **Privacy:** no API route, no network — the mic stream is connected only to an analyser, never routed to output, never recorded or sent (stated on screen). **Idle / no-mic auto-demo:** mic denied/silent → a self-playing loop fires a synthesized "child" note that triggers the suspension-chain answer every several seconds with a `text-rose-300` notice, so a silent 06:30 glance still hears tension→resolution with zero hardware; full cleanup of MediaStream tracks / AudioContext / rAF / timers on unmount. References: the **4–3 / 7–6 / 2–1 suspension figures** of common-practice voice leading; **tonal closure and its denial as perceived emotional cues in children** (developmental music-cognition literature, RESEARCH §448 — children read closure/its-denial before they can label major/minor, making tension→release the most age-robust harmonic event here); **Bobby McFerrin's "Circlesong"** call-and-response model. Born from **RESEARCH §448** (which harmonic event a 4yo can actually perceive: *change* + *closure* before *mode*) → the dive's finding + the jury's off-glass directive jointly selected this from a kids **WIDE** fire of three orthogonal harmonic-event explorers: beat **`668-kids-two-suns`** (continuous major↔minor third-glide, tilt → three.js, cosmic-dramatic — strongest renderer-diversity, but the mode-flip premise is developmentally shaky for a 4yo and 669 was the only off-glass sibling) and **`670-kids-drone-cave`** (shifting tanpura drone under fixed bells, gamepad → WebGL2, mysterious — fresh moving-pedal mechanism, but WebGL2 is over-represented 4× and gamepad-fly overlaps 636); both banked as strong **IDEAS §448** seeds. **Ambition honest 2/5** (the kids norm): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (autocorrelation pitch-detect w/ gates+octave-collapse · suspension-chain voice-leading engine · formant "ah" synth + tonic drone · amplitude/tension-driven glow + demo fallback = 4) + **#3** named refs (the suspension figures · McFerrin · developmental tonal-closure literature). **#1 NOT claimed** — honest: the mic→harmonized-answer core has lab precedent (`280-kids-echo-canyon`); the fresh layer is the suspension *chain* pedagogy (tension→release walked across several figures), which is why this lost the §446 tie-break to 662 but wins now on the binding off-glass directive. **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-16)=[pentatonic/scale-snap "no-wrong-notes" TECH 6× · Canvas2D OUTPUT 4× · look-ahead-scheduler-generative-bed as the only idea 7× · warm/cozy kids VIBE 4× · glass-screen as the primary deliverable (13/15)] · picked=[**mic/voice INPUT** (love-aligned `158-kids-hum-paint`❤️) · **audio-first / OFF-GLASS OUTPUT** (the rarest+best-rated output in the lab; the kids side's first ever) · **autocorrelation pitch-detect → suspension-chain voice-leading TECH** (genuine dissonance→resolution, explicitly NOT pentatonic, NOT a scheduler-bed) · **hushed/sacred, tender-curious VIBE** (NOT warm-cozy, NOT silly)]` — clean on every banned tag, and the only sibling that breaks the glass-screen monoculture. **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 37.0s`, `✓ Generating static pages (484/484)`, `.next/server/app/dream/669-kids-hum-answer.html` emitted — prerendered static ○; losers `668`/`670` confirmed absent), **NOT browser-verified by ear** (no audio/mic in sandbox) — the no-mic demo loop means the suspension→resolution plays on a glance with zero hardware; unverified: pitch-detection accuracy on a real child's breathy voice, and the felt musicality/timing of the leans and sighs (the one tuning surface, on a real device).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/669-kids-hum-answer/README.md`
