### `671-glasswork` — `demoable` · adult · WIDE-winner (cycle 449, 2026-06-16)
[/dream/671-glasswork](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/671-glasswork) — **"Glasswork": a tender, intimate, 3am generative music-box whose harmony *breathes* through REAL four-part voice-leading — on every chord change each voice glides (audible portamento) to its NEAREST chord tone, so you both SEE the threads cross and HEAR the pad slide rather than jump.** The directest hit on JURY 2026-06-16 provocation #5 ("declare the missing middle solved and freeze groove/EDM for a week — the un-built adult register is now **tender / intimate** (the banked `660-glasswork`) — go there") — the explicit, named adult ask for this window, and the realization of the long-banked tender seed. **Audio (all Web Audio, no samples, no deps):** key is **D Dorian** (the natural-6 gives the wistful-not-sad colour, deliberately NOT C-major-pentatonic) with a rare Lydian-#11 tint; harmony is a **tension/resolution-weighted random walk** over a small diatonic palette (Dm7 · G · C · Am7 · Em · F · IVadd9 · a rare tint) with hand-tuned transition weights giving a homeward gravity, a new chord every ~6–10 s, never a fixed loop (`compose.ts`). The technique anchor: an **exhaustive 4-voice nearest-tone assignment** (`voiceleading.ts`) that minimizes total semitone motion with doubling/unison penalties so the chord stays open, then each pad voice **portamentos ~0.4–0.8 s** to its target — the glide is the point. A separate **never-repeating weighted melody** arches over each phrase (sine register-arch + woven rests, `compose.ts`). Synth (`synth.ts`): FM music-box bell (melody) · 4 portamento glass-pad voices · soft sub following the root · ConvolverNode reverb (synthetic generated impulse) · filtered feedback-delay shimmer · master ends in a **`DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter** (−6 dB / ratio 12 / master 0.34) so it can never clip or blast. **Output: inline SVG** (the scarcest renderer — last SVG was `649`; off the jury-banned **Canvas2D**, off the hot three.js/WebGL2): a dark constellation of soft `<radialGradient>`+`<feGaussianBlur>` nodes (one per pad voice, y = pitch) that bloom on each onset, **faint threads tracing the voice-leading** (a fading `<line>` from each voice's old tone to its new one — you watch the harmony move), and brief brighter sparks for melody bells; React re-render throttled to ~24 fps with all per-frame state in refs. **Interaction is gentle and optional** — pointer height = brighter register + more shimmer, pointer-right = busier (shorter chords/faster melody); a tap "drops a stone" pulling harmony toward rest on the next change; ~1.5 s idle eases back to autonomous. **It plays itself** — Begin and the pad swells in, the melody enters, chords change on their own, so a silent 06:30 glance sees blooming nodes + crossing threads and hears the piece with zero interaction or hardware. **No API route, no guard, no npm deps, no network/mic/camera/GPU/secret** (pure client Web Audio + inline SVG — the lowest failure surface in the lab; AudioContext created inside the Begin gesture for iOS, full teardown on unmount). References: **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports*, *Reflection* — generative ambient) · **Harold Budd** (*The Pearl*) · **Erik Satie** (*Gymnopédies* — intimate melancholy); technique anchor: classical four-part **voice-leading**. Born from **RESEARCH §449** (generative-ambient that obeys voice-leading rules — minimize each voice's motion, omit minor-9ths — is a live browser lineage [Alex Bainter / generative.fm], anchored in Eno's *Music for Airports*; tagged `[older, foundational]` honestly — the build is jury-directive + IDEAS-seed driven, path (c)). Winner of an adult **WIDE** fire (3 orthogonal explorers across under-built adult registers, each a different input × output × technique × vibe, none touching a banned tag): beat **`672-cinema-swell`** (a cinematic orchestral-swell instrument — hold chords → film-score string divisi blooms with crescendo swells + a tension arc, MIDI/keyboard → three.js, Zimmer/Richter/Jóhannsson; Karel care #4 journey-engine alt) and **`673-kepler-chimes`** (the music of the spheres — planets whose orbital periods snap to small-integer mean-motion resonance become an audible consonant polyrhythm that locks from phasing, tilt → raw WebGL2, Kepler/Matt-Russo-System-Sounds); both banked as strong **IDEAS §449** seeds. **Ambition honest 2/5** (the lab norm): **#2** ≥3 subsystems (harmonic random-walk · exhaustive nearest-tone voice-leading engine · weighted never-repeating melody + scheduler · 4-voice glass synth w/ reverb + feedback-delay shimmer · inline-SVG renderer = 5) + **#3** named refs (Eno/Budd/Satie + voice-leading). **#1 NOT claimed** — generative-ambient voice-leading is worked territory; the value is the explicitly-named **tender register fill** + the scarcest renderer + the lowest-risk glance candidate. **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-16, rolling-15)=[pentatonic/scale-snap "no-wrong-notes" TECH 6× · Canvas2D OUTPUT 4× · look-ahead-scheduler-generative-bed as the only idea 7× · warm/cozy kids VIBE 4× · glass-screen as the sole deliverable] · picked=[**pointer (gentle/optional) INPUT** · **inline-SVG OUTPUT** (the scarcest renderer — off banned Canvas2D AND off hot three.js/WebGL2) · **tension-weighted harmonic walk + exhaustive nearest-tone voice-leading TECH** (explicitly NOT pentatonic, NOT a scheduler-bed-as-only-idea — it has 5 distinct ideas) · **intimate / wistful / melancholy VIBE** (the jury's named open adult register; NOT warm-cozy, NOT ecstatic/groove — which the jury froze this week)]` — clean on every binding banned tag. **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 117s`, `✓ Generating static pages (485/485)`, `.next/server/app/dream/671-glasswork.html` emitted — prerendered static ○, `/dream/671-glasswork 5.43 kB / 108 kB First Load`; losers `672`/`673` confirmed absent), **NOT browser-verified by ear** (no audio in sandbox) — but uniquely low-risk (pure SVG + Web Audio, zero GPU/sensor/network/key surface) and the piece is fully autonomous on Begin so a silent 06:30 glance is alive with zero hardware; unverified: the felt glide timing, the chord-walk weights, melody rest rates, and reverb/shimmer balance (reasoned defaults — the one tuning surface, on a real device).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/671-glasswork/README.md`

### `677-presence-field` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 451, 2026-06-17)
[/dream/677-presence-field](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/677-presence-field) — **"Presence Field": step back from the screen and your whole body, tracked as a luminous skeleton, conducts a SPATIAL ensemble — each limb is a sustained voice placed in 3D around you, so spreading and raising your arms sweeps the music through the room.** The directest hit on the lab's single biggest untouched first, named across THREE consecutive juries (JURY 2026-06-16 #2 + standing: "depth-camera / room-presence / projection installation — zero embodied-spatial builds still") — the first ADULT installation/room-presence piece, and Karel's care #3 (live-performance) + #5 (installation mode). **The core idea — spatialization IS the instrument:** one Web-Audio `AudioListener` at the origin, and **seven sustained voices (head, both wrists, both elbows, torso, hips) each routed through its OWN `PannerNode` with `panningModel="HRTF"`** placed at the live 3D position of its tracked joint (screen-x → left/right, height → register + y, implied depth → z). Moving the body audibly moves the voices around the listener — that sweep is the payload (the realization of RESEARCH §451's spatialization→presence finding and "Sounding Bodies" pose→spatial-sound). **Harmony (NOT pentatonic — banned this cycle):** a slow drifting D-Dorian chord cycle (Dm7→G7→Am7→Fmaj7→Dm7, ~9–13s, 2.2s glides), adult/meditative, not a no-wrong-notes pad and not a groove look-ahead scheduler; ear-safe master gain (≤0.32) → `DynamicsCompressor` → destination, dry + convolution-reverb wet send, ~0.7s fade-in; stillness eases to a soft enveloping drone (`settle()`). **Input:** webcam **full-body pose** via **MediaPipe Pose** (Tasks-Vision `PoseLandmarker`), loaded from the jsDelivr **CDN at runtime** via an indirect `new Function('return import(...)')` so the bundler never resolves it (no npm dep, build never depends on it). **Output: three.js** (the scarcest renderer — 0× in the last 10 ships; off the jury-banned Canvas2D, off the hotter WebGL2) — a dark foggy room with a faint grid floor, glowing additive joint spheres + skeleton bone-lines, and a hued glowing marker at each voice's live spatial position; slow auto-rotate for installation feel; animates BEFORE audio unlocks. **Privacy:** camera is on-device only, analysed in-browser, never stored or sent (stated on screen); no API route, no guard needed (no network/server/secret — the only network is the public MediaPipe model/wasm CDN). **Graceful degrade (the 06:30-glance path is MediaPipe-independent):** an **idle preview** runs a *synthetic* drifting body through the three.js room + spatial ensemble before Start is even pressed, and any failure (camera denied, MediaPipe/CDN/WASM load error, WebGL loss, no pose, or ~2.5s idle) shows a `text-rose-300` notice and runs that same self-playing virtual body sweeping the full ensemble — so a silent glance hears + sees the spatial room with zero hardware and zero CDN dependency. Full teardown on unmount (MediaStream tracks stopped, landmarker closed, three.js renderer/geometries/materials disposed, AudioContext closed, rAF cancelled, resize listener removed). References: **arXiv:2601.22082** (Jan 2026 — spatialization + situated audio raise the felt *sense of presence*) · **"Sounding Bodies," arXiv:2311.06285** (3D spatial sound from body pose) · **Myron Krueger, *Videoplace* (1974)** (the body as the interface, no wearables) · **Refik Anadol** room-scale presence. Born from **RESEARCH §451** (the embodied-spatial room is the lab's biggest untouched first; the research front says the payload is the *spatialization itself*, not the viz) and realizes the long-banked **`442-body-orchestra`** seed. Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire (ONE concept — body-presence → spatial-audio — via three body-sensing approaches): beat **`678-presence-flow`** (the BULLETPROOF no-dependency twin — webcam frame-differencing → the same HRTF ensemble, raw WebGL2; banked ⭐ as the safest spatial-presence resurrect) and **`679-presence-veil`** (the ENVELOPMENT take — selfie-segmentation silhouette MASS widens the per-voice azimuth fan from a frontal arc to a surround RING as you approach, raw WebGL2 veil; banked); both **IDEAS §451**. **Ambition honest 2/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (MediaPipe-Pose body tracking · 7-voice HRTF spatial-panner ensemble + drifting D-Dorian harmony · three.js luminous-room renderer + synthetic-body auto-demo = 4) + **#3** named refs (arXiv:2601.22082 · "Sounding Bodies" · Krueger · Anadol). **#1 NOT claimed** — MediaPipe pose/hands/segmentation is used across the KIDS side (217/287/295/419/444/582/617); the genuinely-open frontier is the *register* (an ADULT installation/room-presence piece with spatialization-as-instrument = 0× builds), not the sensor. **#5 NOT hard-claimed** — the spatialization-presence paper is Jan 2026 (~5 months, not a <30-day drop). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-16)=[pentatonic/scale-snap TECH · Canvas2D OUTPUT · look-ahead-scheduler-generative-bed as the only idea · warm/cozy kids VIBE · glass-screen as the primary/default-reflex deliverable] · picked=[**full-body-pose / room-presence INPUT** (embodied, off the glass-control reflex — you play it from across the room with your body, not a finger on the screen) · **three.js OUTPUT** (scarcest renderer, 0× in last 10; NOT Canvas2D) · **HRTF spatialization-as-instrument + drifting modal voice-leading TECH** (explicitly NOT pentatonic, NOT a scheduler-groove-bed) · **adult meditative / installation / awe VIBE** (NOT warm-cozy kids, NOT the frozen ecstatic/groove, NOT doom)]` — clean on every banned tag; addresses the *embodied-spatial / get-off-the-couch* reading of the off-glass ask (vs the audio-first reading 669/666/655 already serve). **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` exit 0, `✓ Compiled successfully in 48s`, `✓ Generating static pages (487/487)` [486 from cycle 450 + 677 = 487, confirming 678/679 absent], `/dream/677-presence-field 6.36 kB / 298 kB First Load` prerendered static ○, `.next/server/app/dream/677-presence-field.html` emitted), **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/audio/GPU in sandbox) — the idle-preview + auto-demo guarantee a live glance with zero hardware; unverified by body/ear: whether MediaPipe Pose loads + tracks well on Karel's phone, whether the joint→HRTF placement reads as voices genuinely moving *around* you (HRTF front/back/elevation cues are weaker on speakers than headphones), and the felt latency/smoothing of the skeleton on a real device.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/677-presence-field/README.md`

### `702-kids-monster-band` — `demoable` · kids · WIDE-winner (cycle 460, 2026-06-17)
[/dream/702-kids-monster-band](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/702-kids-monster-band) — **"Monster Band": two little kids on two phones build ONE goofy 16-step monster groove neither could make alone.** The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-17 on **three** provocations at once: **#3 "ship a multi-user / two-player piece — still 0× across the entire window"** (the genuinely unbuilt register; *breaks the standing solo monoculture* — every one of the last 15 was one person alone), **#1 "groove, not a cadence"** (a beat built by tapping, emphatically NOT the harmonic-event/voice-leading template banned on the kids side), and **#5 "make a kid laugh"** (googly monsters that squash and BOING). Two browser tabs/phones open the same URL → join the same room over same-origin **`BroadcastChannel`** (no network, no server, **no api route, no guard, no secret**) → both phase-lock to **one shared 16-step ~96 BPM loop** via a broadcast beat-zero **epoch** (first player claims epoch 0; joiners adopt it; ties resolve last-write-wins toward the earliest). Each kid taps four giant ≥96px buttons — **🥁 BOOM** (low sine thump) · **📯 HONK** (triangle+FM down-glide) · **🫧 POP** (band-passed noise blip) · **🌀 BOING** (rubbery saw up-glide) — that drop sounds onto the nearest of the 16 steps and **loop forever**; the two kids' patterns overlay into one emergent groove. **Each monster squashes and bounces on EVERY hit — including the friend's hits** (instant `hit` messages + last-write-wins per-player 16-step pattern beads), so each kid *sees* the other playing in real time. **Input:** multi-user (same-origin BroadcastChannel) + big-button tap — the social co-play is the mechanic (off the jury-banned touch-as-primary reflex). **Output: three.js via @react-three/fiber** — bouncy googly 3D monsters with per-hit squash-and-stretch envelopes (off the banned WebGL2 + audio-first-SVG; three.js 1×→2× in the rolling-10, well under the 4× ban line). **Technique:** Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler (~25ms tick / ~120ms look-ahead) scheduling audio LOCALLY against the shared epoch (only the epoch + patterns cross the channel — audio is never streamed, which is what keeps the loops phase-locked); presence via hello/welcome/bye + a ~1.6s silence timeout; the two monsters detuned ±6% so they sound like siblings. **A real tapped GROOVE, emphatically NOT a harmonic event/cadence.** **Solo glance covered:** if no second player is present a friendly 👻 ghost-friend monster auto-plays a complementary pattern, and a ~2.5s idle auto-demo seeds a starter beat — so it sounds and bounces from the first second on one device; the ghost steps aside the instant a real friend joins and returns if they leave. Kids-safe by construction: ≥96px targets, <100ms response, master gain ~0.3 → lowpass → `DynamicsCompressor` (nothing harsh), iOS AudioContext unlock in the Start gesture, full teardown (close BroadcastChannel, cancel rAF, close AudioContext, clear timers). References: **Toca Band** (Toca Boca — the no-reading tap-a-character-to-add-a-part toy this is modelled on) · **Yamaha Tenori-on** (the lit 16-step grid sequencer) · **The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers** (1970s–80s networked ensembles — *send control data, not audio*; "an ensemble nobody could play alone"). Born from **RESEARCH §460** (the multi-user register is the jury's 0× ask; The Hub's control-signal architecture ported to a kid-legible Toca-Band interface). Winner of a kids **WIDE** fire (3 divergent silly/groove explorers on different embodied inputs): beat **`703-kids-boing-zoo`** ⭐ (tilt → jelly-animal physics bonks → emergent slapstick rhythm; Canvas2D, zero deps/GPU/permission — the most bulletproof + funniest, banked ⭐ as the safest silly-kids resurrect) and **`704-kids-funny-face-party`** (front-camera motion → googly creature + a FREEZE-dance game; Canvas2D — the directest off-glass/embodied take with a fresh freeze-dance mechanic, banked); both **IDEAS §460**. **Ambition honest 2/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (BroadcastChannel presence + last-write-wins beads sync · shared-epoch look-ahead scheduler · local Web Audio FM/noise monster synth · three.js/r3f bouncy-monster viz = 4) + **#3** named refs (Toca Band · Tenori-on · The Hub/League of Automatic Music Composers). **#1 NOT claimed** — BroadcastChannel multi-user already exists in the lab (319/334/341); this is a fresh *framing/recency* win (multi-user is 0× in the whole window) + breaks the solo monoculture, not a technique-first. **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-17)=[harmonic-event/voice-leading/tension-resolution TECH (esp. kids) · audio-first+minimal-SVG/glow OUTPUT · touch/on-glass-primary INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · hushed/tender VIBE · standing single-user/solo] · picked=[**multi-user / BroadcastChannel INPUT** (two players are the input; secondary tap; *breaks the solo ban*) · **three.js/r3f OUTPUT** (off WebGL2 + audio-first-SVG) · **shared-clock 16-step tapped-groove TECH** (NOT a harmonic event) · **silly/friendly monster-band VIBE** (off hushed/tender — laugh-first)]` — clears EVERY ban and actively *breaks the standing solo monoculture*. **Build-verified** (full `npm run build` with all 3 candidates exit 0; then authoritative winner-only `npm run build` **EXIT 0, `Generating static pages (497/497)`** [+1 route = the winner], `/dream/702-kids-monster-band` present), **NOT browser/two-device-verified** (no second device/audio/GPU in sandbox) — the ghost-friend + idle auto-demo guarantee a live sounding glance on one device; unverified by hand: true two-device sync convergence under contention (epoch-adoption + presence-timeout logic is in place and degrades to the ghost), and whether the monster voices + squash read as "a band two kids built together" on a real phone.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/702-kids-monster-band/README.md`

### `704-spectral-seance` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 461, 2026-06-17)
[/dream/704-spectral-seance](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/704-spectral-seance) — **"Spectral Séance": a generative sound dreams an austere data-image — and then the image *becomes the next instrument you hear*, re-sonified column-by-column as a spectrogram while a cyan scan-line sweeps it in lock-step. You literally see AND hear the picture being played.** The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-17 #4** ("extend the two ceiling builds [689, 666] instead of starting a sixth tonal-tension toy") — the lab's first true **closed audio→image→AUDIO loop**, where ceiling-build `689-dream-chapters` only went one-way (audio→image). **The closed loop, four subsystems:** (1) a generative **inharmonic spectral bed** — 8 partials at NON-integer ratios (1, 2.07, 3.31, 4.62…) so it stays a *texture* and never collapses into a chord/cadence (the harmonic-event/voice-leading template the jury banned this cycle); (2) an **AnalyserNode spectral descriptor** (low/mid/high band split, centroid brightness, flatness/roughness, density) → a **controlled-vocabulary prompt** (bright+sparse → "high thin filaments of white light on black, austere data grid"; dark+rough → "dense low charcoal static"); (3) the dreamed image — **guarded `fal-ai/flux/schnell`** when a key exists, else a **deterministic descriptor-seeded procedural data-image** (domain-warped interference/line-field + grid); (4) **the return trip** — the image is downsampled to a 128×48 offscreen buffer and read as a **spectrogram/score**: each column = a moment, each row = a log-spaced frequency bin (~80Hz–6kHz, top = high pitch), and a bank of **48 oscillators** has its per-row gains driven directly by per-column pixel brightness as the scan-line sweeps (~75ms/column, ~9.6s per 128-column pass); the re-sonified spectrum then feeds the *next* descriptor, closing the loop. **Input:** autonomous/generative + AI-image (NO mic, NO touch-as-primary — it runs itself; a single "Begin" gesture unlocks audio for iOS, ~2.5s idle auto-start). **Output: raw WebGL2** — the dreamed image + sweeping cyan scan-line + Ikeda grid/vignette rendered as a textured quad in a fragment shader (Canvas2D used ONLY for the tiny offscreen pixel-sampler, never for output). **Technique:** image-as-spectrogram cross-modal re-sonification — a genuinely never-used mechanic in the lab (689/441 were one-way audio→image; this is the literal *return trip*). **Vibe:** Ryoji Ikeda austere data-spectacle (black ground, white/cyan, precise) — deliberately far from the jury-banned hushed/tender pole. **Bulletproof keyless glance:** any non-200 / network error / CORS-taint silently falls through to the procedural data-image, re-sonified through the *identical* column-by-column path — so with no `FAL_KEY` (how the public preview usually runs) it still dreams an image AND plays it back as sound; the status line shows "dreaming (procedural)" in `text-emerald-300/95` so it reads as fine-without-a-key. WebGL2 absence shows a graceful notice and still runs audio. Ear-safe: 48 osc summed with tiny per-osc gains → lowpass → `DynamicsCompressor` → master hard-capped at 0.34, smooth gain ramps (no zipper clicks); full teardown on unmount (stop all oscillators, close audioCtx, clear timers, cancel rAF). References: **Ryoji Ikeda** (data-spectacle) · **Iannis Xenakis *UPIC*** (1977, draw-the-sound) · **the *ANS synthesizer*** (1958, image-as-spectrogram) · the spectrogram↔waveform synthesis lineage. Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire (ONE concept — *the music dreams a picture and the dream changes the music* — via 2 approaches to **how the image returns to audio**): beat **`703-latent-oracle`** ⭐ (the SOFT-closure twin — the loop closes via *dominant color*→synth [hue→cutoff, brightness→shimmer, saturation→dissonance], WebGL2 ping-pong feedback smear, Anadol/Memo Akten *Learning to See*; the long-banked 687 seed, fully built — banked ⭐ **IDEAS §461** as the resurrect for a key-awake night, since its color-drift narrows keyless). **Ambition honest 4/5**: **#1** never-used technique (image-as-spectrogram literal re-sonification — the closed *return trip*; 689/441 were one-way) · **#2** ≥3 subsystems (inharmonic generative bed + FFT descriptor→prompt + guarded-flux/procedural image gen + 48-osc re-sonifier = 4) · **#3** named refs (Ikeda · Xenakis UPIC · ANS) · **#5** recent research (RESEARCH §461 dive + the §455-banked seed it consumes). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-17)=[harmonic-event/voice-leading/tension-resolution TECH · audio-first+minimal-SVG/glow OUTPUT · touch/on-glass-primary INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · hushed/tender VIBE · standing single-user/solo] · picked=[**autonomous/generative + AI-image INPUT** (it dreams itself; off the touch reflex) · **WebGL2 raster OUTPUT** · **image-as-spectrogram cross-modal re-sonification TECH** (emphatically NOT a harmonic event) · **Ikeda austere data-spectacle VIBE** (off hushed/tender)]`. *Note on the two flagged tensions:* (a) **WebGL2** — the jury banned it on its rolling-**15** (4×: 658/659/662/675), but the diversity-audit's hard gate is the rolling-**10**, where WebGL2 is **cold** (last use 675, 9 ships back; 0× in the last 10) — so reviving it is legitimately diverse by the binding mechanic; flagged honestly. (b) **solo** — this is single-user, but the standing solo monoculture was *just broken* by 694 (cycle 457) + 702 (cycle 460), and JURY #4 (extend the inherently-contemplative ceiling builds) explicitly trumps for an adult cycle. **Build-verified** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build` **EXIT 0**, `✓ Compiled successfully in 34.7s`, `✓ Generating static pages (499/499)`, `/dream/704-spectral-seance 6.9 kB / 113 kB First Load` prerendered **static ○** + its `/api` route), **NOT browser-verified by ear** (no audio/GPU in sandbox) — the ~2.5s auto-start + procedural fallback guarantee a live, sounding glance with zero key/hardware; unverified on a real device: whether the 48-osc spectrogram playback reads as "the picture is the instrument," whether the inharmonic bed stays pleasant over minutes, and the felt latency of scan-line↔audio lock.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/704-spectral-seance/README.md`

### `710-presence-bloom` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 463, 2026-06-18)
[/dream/710-presence-bloom](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/710-presence-bloom) — **"Presence Bloom": the genuine cycle-2 of `677-presence-field`. Where 677 placed STATIC point-voices at your joints, here a deliberate wrist gesture (reach → dwell ~0.65s → outward flick) LEAVES BEHIND a persistent HRTF voice fixed in 3D that keeps singing — so over minutes you build a spatial chord-architecture around your own ears, rendered as a 60k-particle WebGPU storm where each voice is an attractor.** Two new capabilities over 677: **(1) accretion / long-form memory** — voices persist and accrete (cap ~24, oldest fades), each taking the next pitch in a slowly drifting **D-Dorian** modal field (Dm9→Gmaj9→Em11→Dadd9 region, homeward gravity on D/A), so the set is an evolving spatial chord and the field is genuinely *different at minute 5 than minute 1* (the 689/701-coral accreting-memory ceiling the jury asked the lab to extend); **(2) distance + room reverberation as a felt axis** — each voice's `PannerNode` (HRTF, inverse distance) has a reverb send into a shared `ConvolverNode` that GROWS with distance, so near voices are present+dry, far voices quiet+wet (the literal realization of RESEARCH §463's **arXiv:2505.18020** distance-cues+reverb finding). **The core idea — spatialization IS the instrument:** one Web-Audio `AudioListener` at the origin (your ears); harmony is the *material*, the *subject* is building luminous spatial architecture (emphatically NOT a harmonic-event/cadence — the jury's banned template). **Input:** webcam **full-body pose** via **MediaPipe Tasks-Vision `PoseLandmarker`** loaded from CDN at runtime via `import(/* webpackIgnore: true */ …)` (bundler never resolves it; no npm dep; build never depends on it). **Output: WebGPU compute** (`render.ts → makeRenderer`) — tens of thousands of GPU particles advected by a WGSL compute shader, each persistent voice an **attractor** particles gather/orbit/bloom around (nearer = brighter/larger), additive instanced point-sprite render; **with a first-class Canvas2D fallback** (~3600 particles, the SAME accreting-attractor behavior, additive trails + glow haloes) that is the EXPECTED path on most devices, so a WGSL failure *degrades* rather than breaks. **Vibe:** luminous architectural **AWE**, Anadol-scale — deliberately pushed away from 677's meditative-hush (the jury-banned tender/hushed pole, the explicit risk of a 677 cycle-2). **Privacy/safety:** camera analysed on-device only, never stored/sent; no API route, no guard needed (no server/secret; only network is the public MediaPipe CDN). Ear-safe: per-voice `GainNode` → HRTF `PannerNode` → (dry + distance-scaled reverb send) → master ≤0.32 (scaled down as voice count grows) → `DynamicsCompressor` → destination; soft attacks, ~0.7s fade-in, AudioContext created/resumed inside the Start gesture (iOS). **Graceful degrade (the 06:30-glance path is MediaPipe/GPU-independent):** visuals animate on mount (idle preview) before audio unlocks, and any failure (camera denied, MediaPipe/CDN load error, no pose, ~2.5s idle, or no WebGPU) runs a **synthetic ghost body** that drifts and places voices on its own — building the architecture hands-free — and falls to the Canvas2D particle field if WebGPU is absent, all with visible `text-rose-300` error notices; full teardown on unmount (cancel rAF, destroy GPU buffers, close AudioContext, stop camera tracks, remove listeners). References: **Refik Anadol** (*Latent City* / *Machine Hallucinations* — GPU particle data-architecture awe) · **arXiv:2505.18020** ("Effects of auditory distance cues and reverberation on spatial perception" — distance+reverb as the spatial signal, the cycle-2 deepening) · **arXiv:2407.13083** ("Modeling and Driving Human Body Soundfields through Acoustic Primitives" — body movement shapes spatial sound). Born from **RESEARCH §463** (the embodied-spatial cycle-2's real upgrade is persistence + distance/reverb, not more joints). Winner of an adult **DEEP** fire (ONE concept — *body leaves behind persistent accreting HRTF voices → a resonant architecture* — via 3 renderers): beat **`708-presence-cathedral`** (the most literal 677 cycle-2 — MediaPipe-Pose + three.js cathedral of emissive light-pillars; banked **IDEAS §463**, lost on renderer over-use [three.js → 4×] + CDN risk without a GPU-fallback safety net) and **`709-presence-tide`** ⭐ (the BULLETPROOF zero-dependency twin — webcam frame-diff [no ML/CDN/network] + Canvas2D top-down room-map; banked ⭐ **IDEAS §463** as the safest spatial-presence resurrect, realizing the long-banked 678-presence-flow lane). **Ambition honest 3–4/5**: **#2** ≥3 subsystems (MediaPipe-Pose + gesture detector · persistent-accreting HRTF spatial-voice engine w/ distance attenuation + distance-scaled reverb · drifting D-Dorian field w/ accretion + chord-drift · WebGPU WGSL particle storm + Canvas2D fallback = 4–5) · **#3** named refs (Anadol · arXiv:2505.18020 · arXiv:2407.13083) · **#4** genuine **cycle-2 of 677-presence-field** (the persistence + distance-reverb capability 677 lacked) · **#5** RESEARCH §463 same-day. **#1 NOT hard-claimed** — HRTF-spatialization-as-instrument + MediaPipe Pose both exist at 677; the fresh capability is *accretion + distance/reverb* (a cycle-2 deepening), not a lab-first technique. **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-17)=[harmonic-event/voice-leading TECH · audio-first+SVG/glow OUTPUT · touch/on-glass-primary INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · hushed/tender VIBE · standing solo] · picked=[**full-body-pose / embodied-spatial INPUT** (off the touch reflex — played from across the room) · **WebGPU OUTPUT** (scarcest non-banned renderer, 1× in window; off banned WebGL2, off warming three.js [677/689/699=3×], off hot Canvas2D) · **persistent-accreting HRTF-spatialization + distance/reverb TECH** (NOT a harmonic event) · **luminous architectural AWE / Anadol VIBE** (NOT hushed/tender)]` — clears every banned tag; WebGPU is the renderer-diverse choice. **Build-verified** (all-3-present `npm run build` exit 0 / 503 pages; then authoritative winner-only `npm run build` **EXIT 0**, `✓ Generating static pages (501/501)`, `/dream/710-presence-bloom 9.41 kB / 116 kB First Load` prerendered **static ○**), **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/camera/audio in sandbox) — idle preview + ghost-body auto-demo + Canvas2D fallback guarantee a live, sounding glance with zero hardware/CDN; unverified on a real device: the WGSL compute path on real hardware (degrades to Canvas2D, not breaks), whether placed voices read as moving *around* you (HRTF cues weaker on speakers than headphones), the dwell-then-flick thresholds, MediaPipe load on Karel's phone, iOS audio unlock.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/710-presence-bloom/README.md`

### `711-kids-beat-buddies` — `demoable` · kids · WIDE-winner (cycle 464, 2026-06-18)
[/dream/711-kids-beat-buddies](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/711-kids-beat-buddies) — **"Beat Buddies": the lab's FIRST two-player / multi-user kids piece. Two little kids sit on opposite sides of ONE tablet and build a silly band together — every pad they tap snaps onto ONE shared looping groove, so the result is always a beat, never noise.** The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-17 #3** ("ship a multi-user / two-player piece — still 0× across the entire window, the genuinely unbuilt register") at the same time as **#1** ("groove/rhythm, not a cadence") and **#5** ("MAKE A KID LAUGH") — and it was the only one of this cycle's three explorers to hit the multi-user gap. **Interaction:** the screen splits into two stacked player zones; the **top zone is rotated 180°** (`rotate-180`) so the kid across the table reads it right-way-up — Player A warm/orange (top), Player B cool/teal (bottom). Each kid has **four big silly-sound pads** (`clamp(64px,18vh,150px)`, emoji glyphs, no reading): drum, boing, honk, slide-whistle. **The core mechanic — shared-clock loop-stacking (Incredibox/Sprunki):** a single Web-Audio **look-ahead scheduler** (~25 ms `setInterval`, scheduling ~104 BPM 16th-notes ~120 ms ahead) runs ONE clock for BOTH players; a tap doesn't fire raw — it registers a *looping layer* with a fixed 16th-note pattern, quantized onto the next step, so adding pads builds a thickening shared groove and tapping again removes the layer. A soft synth kick + shaker always keep the pulse, so there's a beat to lock onto from the first tap. **The togetherness payload:** when BOTH players are active at once the two googly conductor blobs **high-five** with a confetti burst + spark line — pure co-play, no winning, no competition (the entrainment science of RESEARCH §464's **arXiv:2504.03958**: quantize both kids to one clock so locking-in is engineered, not demanded). **Input:** two-player same-device touch (each zone its own pad-state — emphatically NOT the jury-banned lone-finger-on-glass reflex; the second person IS the novelty). **Output: Canvas2D** — two squash-stretch googly conductor creatures that bop on every beat, grow brighter/bigger as layers stack, flash on each scheduled hit (splashes synced to `ctx.currentTime`), plus confetti. **Technique:** shared-clock quantized loop-stacking groove (a never-before-built mechanic in the lab — the lab's first *multi-user* anything on the kids side). **Vibe:** silly co-op band/dance-floor — groove + laughter, deliberately NOT a harmonic-event/tension-resolution machine (the jury's banned kids template). **Bulletproof glance (the most robust of the three explorers):** pure tap + synthesis, **no sensors, no camera, no permissions, no network, no CDN, no samples** — works instantly for any reviewer on any device, even solo (one person tapping both halves); a ~2.5 s-idle **auto-demo** gently toggles pads itself so a passing glance sees+hears the groove build and thin on its own. Ear-safe: all voices through `gain(0.28) → lowpass(7500Hz) → DynamicsCompressor → destination`; full teardown on unmount (cancel rAF, stop scheduler interval, close/disconnect AudioContext). If AudioContext can't start, a `text-rose-300` notice asks for one more tap. References: **Incredibox / Sprunki** (stackable looping beat layers locked to a shared clock — the dominant 2026-live kids beat-making form, RESEARCH §464) · **Toshio Iwai's Tenori-on** (shared lit grid-instrument played by touch). Born from **RESEARCH §464** (Incredibox-2026 mechanic + ensemble-entrainment science → a two-player shared-clock groove). Winner of a kids **WIDE** fire (3 divergent groove+laugh explorers on 3 inputs): beat **`712-kids-roll-a-beat`** (tilt-physics slapstick → bonks quantized onto a groove, velocity→comedic-pitch; banked **IDEAS §464**, lost on tilt-physics over-use [286/451/603/703]) and **`713-kids-dance-drum`** ⭐ (off-glass webcam-motion freeze-dance groove + spring-physics mirror creature; banked ⭐ **IDEAS §464** as the directest "get off the glass" / revives the abandoned dance register). **Ambition honest 4/5**: **#1** never-used technique (first multi-user / two-player piece in the lab; shared-clock loop-*stacking* quantizer driving two co-located players on a split+rotated screen) · **#2** ≥3 subsystems (shared look-ahead groove scheduler + 6 synth voices · split/rotated two-player tap UI w/ per-zone state · Canvas2D reactive creatures/splashes/confetti · idle auto-demo driver = 4) · **#3** named refs (Incredibox/Sprunki · Tenori-on) · **#4** multi-cycle (a reusable two-player co-op kids line — clean teardown + idle-demo; deepen with two-marble co-op, more pads, streak meter). **#5 NOT claimed** (a play piece about shared groove, not grounded in a specific algorithm-paper — though the design *choice* is motivated by §464's entrainment finding). **Diversity** `banned (JURY 2026-06-17)=[harmonic-event/voice-leading/tension-resolution TECH · audio-first+minimal-SVG/glow OUTPUT · touch/on-glass-primary INPUT · WebGL2 OUTPUT · hushed/tender VIBE · standing single-user/solo] · picked=[**two-player same-device INPUT** (the breaks-the-solo-monoculture move — the second person is the point; off the lone-finger reflex) · **Canvas2D OUTPUT** · **shared-clock quantized loop-stacking groove TECH** (emphatically NOT a harmonic event) · **silly co-op band VIBE** (NOT hushed/tender)]` — clears every banned tag, and is the rare build that *breaks the standing solo monoculture* (every one of the prior 15 was one person alone). Love-signal: backed by Karel's loves of `98-kids-drum-circle` (rhythm), `217-dance-avatar`, `234-kids-hand-creature`, `169-kids-marble-run`, `83-kids-tilt-rain`. **Build-verified** (two losers `rm -rf`'d, then authoritative winner-only `npm run build` **EXIT 0**, `✓ Compiled successfully in 46s`, `/dream/711-kids-beat-buddies 4.8 kB / 111 kB First Load` prerendered **static ○**), **NOT browser-verified by ear/touch** (no audio in sandbox) — pure-tap + auto-demo guarantee a live, sounding glance with zero hardware; unverified on a real device: whether two 4-year-olds *feel* the shared lock, the rotated-split ergonomics on a real tablet, and whether the quantize-on-tap latency reads as responsive to a kid.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/711-kids-beat-buddies/README.md`
