[/dream/712-seismic-room](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/712-seismic-room) — **"Tremor": the world's earthquakes, live — the moment each one is detected by the global seismic network, it becomes a struck resonant tone and an expanding ripple on a Canvas2D world map; you sit inside the seismic present of the whole planet and hear it tick.** The lab's FIRST real-world EXTERNAL-live-data instrument (683 sonified market data; this is the planet itself) and the literal realization of JURY 2026-06-17 provocation #4 ("sonify a non-market live stream — seismic — for free"). **Input:** the free, no-auth **EMSC / SeismicPortal real-time WebSocket** (`wss://www.seismicportal.eu/standing_order/websocket`, GeoJSON FDSN events). **Always sounds even with no network:** a Gutenberg–Richter **Poisson synthetic-swarm** fallback (small quakes vastly outnumber large), so a glance is always live and moving; a badge flips emerald on live connect, amber-calm on fallback, and a toggle forces sim/live. **Sonification (all Web Audio, no samples, no deps):** magnitude→fundamental (140→38 Hz, bigger=lower) + loudness + decay; depth→timbre (shallow=bright triangle + a band-passed noise "crack"; deep=pure low sine, no crack); longitude→`StereoPanner`; a procedural `ConvolverNode` reverb + a low detuned room-tone drone; master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so a magnitude-7 can never blast. **Visual:** Canvas2D equirectangular world map (faint 30° graticule + dotted equator/meridian, no raster assets), each event an expanding ring + bright core colored by depth (shallow=amber, mid=violet, deep=blue) with a lingering glow for large quakes, plus a monospace event log. **Named refs:** Florian Dombois — *Auditory Seismology* · EMSC real-time feed · Ryoji Ikeda data aesthetics. Adult · WIDE-fire winner (cycle 465; banked 713-shadow-duet ⭐ first-WebMIDI + 714-physarum-choir — see IDEAS §465). Status: **demoable** (build-verified, not browser-verified — live-feed schema + by-ear seismic reading untested in sandbox; the synthetic swarm covers any no-network glance).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/712-seismic-room/README.md`

---

[/dream/713-kids-rubber-face](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/713-kids-rubber-face) — **"Rubber Face Sing-Along": a 4-year-old grabs a giant googly face and SCULPTS a silly voice with their fingers — no microphone, the voice is shaped entirely by how the face is squished.** The lab's first **gestural-deformation → formant ("vowel") synthesis** (grep-confirmed: every prior vowel/formant kids piece — `393`, `413` — drove the synth from a real microphone; this one is sculpted by touch). **Mapping:** pull the **nose** up/down → fundamental pitch slides like a slide-whistle (~120–500 Hz, loosely snapped to a friendly set so there's never a harsh note); stretch the **mouth** wide vs. tall → morph three parallel bandpass formants between vowels **ooo↔aaa↔eee**; squish the **cheeks** inward → a wah lowpass-cutoff wobble; pull the **ears/eyes** → vibrato depth + goofy warble; **release → the feature springs back (boing)** and the voice glides to a whisper. **Input:** single/multi-finger touch-deformation (NOT a mic, NOT a button-grid, NOT two-player — deliberately breaks the kids side's just-formed two-player-groove streak `702`/`711`). **Output:** Canvas2D rubbery googly face (spring-loaded handles, rosy cheeks, big tongue, eyes that track the nose). **Audio:** sawtooth glottal source + vibrato LFO → 3-formant bandpass bank → wah lowpass → the kids-safe chain `masterGain(0.3) → lowpass(7500) → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`, always-on soft ambient pad, AudioContext unlocked in the Start gesture (iOS), `text-rose-300` retry-tap fallback, full teardown (rAF/osc/AudioContext). **Bulletproof glance:** a ~2.5s-idle **ghost-finger auto-demo** pulls the face around and sings on its own with zero permission/camera/CDN — yields instantly to a real touch. **Named refs:** Klatt formant synthesis / source-filter vocal model · Mr. Potato Head & stretchy-face toys · Sesame Street puppet mouths. Born from **RESEARCH §466** (density grep → deformation→synthesis is the lab's open laugh-mechanic). Kids 4+ · WIDE-fire winner (cycle 466; banked **`715-kids-stomp-zoo`** ⭐ off-glass webcam-stomp parade + **`714-kids-feed-monster`** drag-feed groove — see IDEAS §466). **Ambition 2/5** (#1 never-used technique [deformation→formant] + #3 named refs). Status: **demoable** (build-verified `npm run build` exit 0 / 504 pages; NOT browser-verified — the cartoon-vowel timbre + sub-50ms touch latency + multitouch need a real iPad; the idle auto-demo + friendly pitch-snap cover any silent/harsh glance).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/713-kids-rubber-face/README.md`

---

[/dream/718-duet-paths](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/718-duet-paths) — **"Duet with the Paths": play a phrase (MIDI / keyboard / on-screen keys) and a machine improviser ANSWERS IN THE GAPS — trading fours with grains pulled from Karel's OWN recorded *Welcome Home* piano (concatenative/corpus-based, MACataRT/CataRT), not an echo.** `demoable` · Cycle 467. **For**: adults / Karel-the-pianist / live-performance-fit / jazz-responsive alternate journey-arc. The lab's first **call-and-response duet partner whose voice is Karel's actual recording**: on Begin it fetches his solo-piano buffer via the read-only `/api/audio/<id>`, slices it into a CataRT-style grain corpus (each grain tagged by autocorrelation pitch + RMS + zero-crossing brightness), and when your phrase closes (~750 ms silence after ≥2 notes) it derives an *answering* line (complementary register · inverted contour · density-matched · resolving in D Dorian) and realizes each note from the **closest-pitch grain of his recording**, pitch-nudged via `playbackRate` — so the reply is built from *his* piano sound. Turn-taking ducking (play over it → it yields), idle auto-demo (call-and-response loops on load with zero hardware), Canvas2D piano-roll "river" (your notes warm amber/rose, the shadow's grains cool cyan/violet, flowing past a now-line). **Offline synth fallback** (OfflineAudioContext piano corpus) keeps it fully alive keyless/offline with amber provenance; emerald "Karel's piano 🎹" when his buffer loads. Three inputs: Web MIDI (hot-plug, device badge), computer keyboard (`asdfghjkl` + `wetyuo` + `z/x` octave), on-screen ≥44px keys. Pure client Web Audio + Web MIDI + Canvas — no API route, no new deps. Realizes Karel's MORNING ask ("Shadow Duet … jazz call-and-response wired to your Paths piano") + the banked ⭐ seed's own "strongest next step." **Source:** RESEARCH §467 (MACataRT arXiv:2502.00023, Feb 2025 · Diemo Schwarz CataRT · IRCAM Somax2 / Pachet Continuator lineage).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/718-duet-paths/README.md`

---

[/dream/719-kids-stomp-zoo](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/719-kids-stomp-zoo) — **"Stomp Zoo": a 4-year-old JUMPS and STAMPS in front of the camera and a whole parade of googly creatures launches into the air in a ripple, each stomp dropping a big comical tuba BONK.** `demoable` · Cycle 468 (kids, WIDE fire). **For**: kids 4+ / **off-glass whole-body** input / Canvas2D parade / silly marching-groove. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-17 #2** ("the next off-glass swing should be embodied, not another glow-screen") *and* **#5** ("MAKE A KID LAUGH — the kids side has gone uniformly solemn") at once. **Input (off-glass):** front webcam → tiny 64×48 offscreen **frame-difference**, motion energy measured ONLY in the lower ~45% band (feet/legs) → sharp **rise** above an adaptive baseline w/ ~180 ms refractory = a STOMP onset (one stomp = one bonk). **Output (Canvas2D):** six saturated googly creatures do a squash-then-launch gravity arc, **staggered into a Mexican-wave ripple** across the parade (rewards repeated stomping — the deepening over the original seed), with dust puffs, screen-shake, warm bonk-flash; a ~20 s **freeze-dance** giggle beat (bed drops, creatures freeze with ❄, then kicks back in). **Audio:** a friendly tuba-honk BONK (filtered sawtooth + sine sub) walks a low marching **bassline** `[55,49,62,44,58]` Hz — rhythm/GROOVE, emphatically **not** a cadence/voice-leading (the jury-banned kids template) — over an always-on gentle oom-pah + shaker bed. **Bulletproof glance:** a **ghost auto-stomper** fires the parade on a groovy rhythm whenever the camera is unavailable/denied OR there's no stomp for ~2.5 s, so a silent 06:30 glance is alive; `text-rose-300` notice on camera-denied. Kid-safe `master(≤0.3) → lowpass(7000) → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; AudioContext + camera both unlocked in one Start gesture (iOS); full teardown stops the camera tracks. **On-device only — nothing recorded or sent; no API route, no guard, no new deps.** Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (three *embodied non-glass inputs*: SEE/SHAKE/BLOW); banked **`720-kids-shake-pop`** ⭐ (device-SHAKE → fizzy-bottle build-and-erupt; possibly a lab-first shake input) + **`721-kids-blow-balloon`** (mic-breath → balloon hum + raspberry) as **IDEAS §468**. **Named refs:** *BodyBeats* (whole-body musical interfaces for children via Laban's Theory of Effort) · VTech Toy Fair 2026 "your movement is the controller." **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ≥4 subsystems [frame-diff motion engine + stomp-onset detector + tuba-bonk synth + Canvas2D parade physics] + #5 recent research [RESEARCH §466 VTech 2026, consumed via the banked seed]). **Source:** RESEARCH §466→§468 chain. **Build-verified** (losers removed, authoritative winner-only `npm run build` EXIT 0, ✓ Compiled successfully, 506/506 static, `/dream/719-kids-stomp-zoo 4.19 kB / 110 kB First Load` prerendered **static ○**); **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: whether the lower-band frame-diff reliably catches a real toddler's stomp without false-firing on hand-waving, the stomp→bonk latency on a real device, and whether the parade reads as laugh-out-loud; the ghost auto-stomper guarantees a sounding, moving glance with zero hardware).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/719-kids-stomp-zoo/README.md`

### `720-paths-grainfield` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 469, 2026-06-18)
Fly *inside* Karel's real recorded Welcome Home piano: the whole performance is shattered into a **WebGPU compute particle field** (one GPU particle per ~90ms grain, laid out by sonic character — x = time-through-the-piece, y = brightness, + curl-noise drift), and dragging a cursor through the cloud **re-sounds the nearest grains concatenatively**, Hann-windowed and stereo-panned (CataRT-style descriptor-space navigation). His real recording re-granulated — never a fresh oscillator. Degrades first-class to a Canvas2D field; ghost auto-drift on idle; master chain `gain 0.28 → lowpass 6.8k → comp −10/20:1`. **Extends the ceiling builds `710-presence-bloom` (WebGPU compute) + `718-duet-paths` (his-piano grains).** INPUT: his real piano + pointer navigation · OUTPUT: WebGPU compute particles (Canvas2D fallback) · TECHNIQUE: corpus-based concatenative granular resynthesis · VIBE: cosmic/immersive-meditative. Named refs: Diemo Schwarz **CataRT**, *Audio Latent Space Cartography*. Design notes: `src/app/dream/720-paths-grainfield/README.md`

### `724-presence-drift` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 471, 2026-06-18)
**"Presence Drift": leave singing voices hanging in the air around your own ears — and each one is a slow READ-HEAD walking through Karel's REAL *Welcome Home* recording, so the spatial chord you build never holds still: it continuously re-voices itself over minutes, genuinely different at minute five than at minute one.** **For**: adult / installation · **webcam full-body pose** (MediaPipe Tasks-Vision, CDN; reach→dwell→flick gesture + body energy; synthetic **ghost-body** fallback) · **WebGPU compute particle storm** (tens of thousands of WGSL points; first-class **Canvas2D** fallback) · **HRTF-binaural** spatial concatenative grains around one `AudioListener` · cosmic/meditative, long-form/stateful. **This is the lab's first true cycle-2 of `710-presence-bloom`** (the jury's only new ceiling build), fused with the concatenative-grain piano of `718-duet-paths`/`720-paths-grainfield`. **The single change vs 710:** a placed voice is no longer a synth oscillator in a D-Dorian field — **each voice owns its own slow read-head** drifting through his real recording (~100ms Hann grains, 50% overlap), every head at a slightly different rate and wrap so the chord never re-aligns; voices accrete (cap ~24, oldest fades); overall body energy gently steers global drift speed (still = heads crawl, moving = advance faster); each voice is also a particle-storm attractor whose bloom warms violet→amber as its head drifts through the performance. The directest hit on **JURY 2026-06-18 #3** ("stop banking ceilings and walking away — push 710 to a cycle-2, reward DEPTH over breadth") **+ #5** ("use his real music — it's in only 1 of the last 15") **+ #1** (the scarce WebGPU stack, not banned Canvas2D). **Named refs:** Diemo Schwarz *CataRT* / corpus-based concatenative synthesis · HRTF/binaural `PannerNode` spatialization · Refik Anadol (GPU particle data-architecture). **Ambition honest 4/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe pose + WebGPU compute + HRTF spatial audio + concatenative real-recording grains] · #3 named refs · #4 cycle-2 multi-cycle commitment · #5 RESEARCH §471 same-day cite [*Synaptic Sanctum Neuroscape* 2026 — CataRT grains + per-source binaural HOA locked to head-tracking]). **Degrades first-class at every layer:** visuals animate on mount; ghost-body places + drifts voices hands-free if no camera/pose; Canvas2D field if no WebGPU; offline-rendered piano buffer fills the corpus if the recording can't be fetched (amber notice). Reads only the public `/api/audio/:id` GET → **no api route, no `guard`** (rules #6/#8 clean). **Build-verified** (losers removed, authoritative winner-only `npm run build` EXIT 0, ✓ Compiled successfully, **509/509** static, `/dream/724-presence-drift` static ○ 10.6 kB / 117 kB First Load). **NOT browser-verified** (no GPU/camera/audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: WGSL compute + MediaPipe pose on real hardware, whether the drifting read-heads read as *his performance re-voicing itself* on headphones, the reach→dwell→flick gesture latency, HRTF imaging; ghost-body + Canvas2D + offline-buffer fallbacks guarantee a sounding, moving glance). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-approach fire (three body→grain models); banked **`722-presence-paths`** ⭐ (descriptor-space reach — hand height→brightness, openness→time) + **`723-presence-tide`** (whole-body conductor, no placement — stillness→distant/wet grains, vigour→near/dry) as **IDEAS §471**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/724-presence-drift/README.md`

### `729-piano-portal-jam` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 473, 2026-06-18)
**"Piano Portal Jam": open a PORTAL to Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano and send it to a friend's phone with one shareable link — then the two of you actually play it together, peer-to-peer, across the room or across the world.** **The lab's first GENUINE cross-device multi-user piece** — every prior "multiplayer" (319/334/341/694/702/711) was same-browser `BroadcastChannel` fakery; this is real **WebRTC `RTCDataChannel`** between two devices. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-18 #4** ("make one multi-user piece *actually* cross-device — one real two-phones-across-the-room jam retires the asterisk; all three multiplayer builds are same-browser") **+ #5** (built on his real recording). **The elegant twist:** both peers already hold his recording sliced into the same grain corpus, so **no audio is streamed** — only tiny note-events `{p,x,y,t}` cross the channel, and each peer re-sounds the other's grain *locally* from the identical corpus (near-zero bandwidth, no codec latency — the inverse of the AES/JackTrip PCM-streaming approach). **Signaling is zero-server, no API route, no backend:** non-trickle ICE (await `icegatheringstatechange==='complete'`) → one complete SDP → gzip via native `CompressionStream` → base64url token inside a link `…#o=<token>`; the guest opens the link, auto-builds the answer, returns a short code the host pastes. INPUT: touch/drag note-trigger **+ a remote PEER over WebRTC** · OUTPUT: **three.js `Points`** two-constellation starfield (warm amber/rose = you, cool cyan/violet = them; Canvas2D fallback) · TECHNIQUE: **WebRTC P2P data channel + concatenative grains** of his real recording (event-only transport) · VIBE: adult, cosmic, "two constellations meeting". **Named refs:** **JackTrip WebRTC** + AES *"Web-Based Networked Music Performances via WebRTC"* (RTCDataChannel + Web Audio). **Ambition 4/5** (#1 first real cross-device WebRTC P2P — all prior multiplayer is BroadcastChannel · #2 ≥3 subsystems [WebRTC signaling + P2P data-channel sync + concatenative grain engine + three.js renderer] · #3 named refs · #5 his real piano). **Solo + ghost:** after ~2.5s idle a simulated remote player drifts a path through his recording in the remote color, so a single 06:30 phone is always a sounding, moving duet with zero setup; the portal link is the stretch that makes it REAL with a 2nd device. Master chain `gain 0.28 → lowpass 6.8k → comp −10/20:1`; iOS gesture-gated AudioContext; full teardown (peer connection + data channel + audio graph + three.js dispose). Reads only public `/api/audio/:id` GET → **no api route, no `guard`** (rules #6/#8 clean). **Compile + lint verified** (`✓ Compiled successfully in 95s`, ESLint clean on the folder), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE) — an infra quirk, NOT the code: pristine `main` fails identically** (proven this cycle); Vercel builds this same app normally. **NOT browser-verified** — and crucially the **cross-device link is correct-by-construction but NOT link-tested** (no 2nd device / live STUN in the sandbox); public STUN handles most NATs, symmetric NATs would need TURN (out of scope). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-approach fire (one cross-device-jam concept, three signaling × renderer attacks); banked **`728-piano-relay-jam`** ⭐ (zero-server **copy-paste** SDP handshake + raw **WebGL2** field — scarcer renderer, purest zero-dep handshake) + **`730-piano-room-jam`** ⭐ (friendly **4-letter room code** via a guarded serverless SDP relay + **WebGPU** compute field — best UX & scarcest renderer, but needs Vercel KV/Upstash to survive cross-lambda; resurrect when a backing store is approved) as **IDEAS §473**. Design notes: `src/app/dream/729-piano-portal-jam/README.md`

### `731-kids-star-reach` — `demoable` · kids (4+) · DEEP-winner (cycle 474, 2026-06-19)
**"Star Reach": a 4-year-old lifts their BARE HANDS into the air — no screen to touch — and scoops handfuls of glowing stars out of a deep night sky, each one ringing a soft bell.** **For**: kids 4+ / **off-glass embodied** input / **raw-WebGL2** field / awe-wonder. Front camera → **MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (CDN, analysis-only, 2 hands, frames discarded immediately — never recorded/sent); openness from mean fingertip-spread ÷ hand-size (robust to how close the child stands), with hysteresis so each gesture rings once cleanly. **Close a fist → nearest stars gather to the palm + a soft just-tuned pentatonic bell cluster; open wide → they spill outward in a rising glissando arc; raise hands high → brighter/higher tones, low → warm/low.** No beat, no loop, no "wrong" note — pure cause-and-effect + luminous texture. **Output:** hand-written **WebGL2 (GLSL ES 3.00)** — *not three.js, not Canvas2D-primary* — a full-screen night-sky gradient + drifting nebula + ~2,600 `gl_PointSize` glow sprites with additive `SRC_ALPHA,ONE` blending, depth-faded and parallaxing; CPU-side gather/spill/spring-home physics so behaviour is identical across render paths. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-18 #1** ("give a kid the scarce renderer too — ban the Canvas2D monoculture") + **#3** ("stop banking ceilings and walking away" — this *resurrects the twice-banked ⭐ `726-kids-star-scoop` seed* instead of spinning a new toy) + **#2** ("texture/cause-effect, not a groove; vary the register away from silly"). **Renderer choice (the curate axis):** raw WebGL2 is the **scarcest renderer in the rolling window AND the most iOS-bulletproof** for Karel's 06:30 phone glance — WebGPU is warming to its own monoculture (721/724/727) and three.js was just used last cycle (729); iOS has WebGL2 reliably and there's zero CDN renderer dep beyond the shared MediaPipe. **Kids-safe** `voices → masterGain(0.26) → lowpass(≤7200) → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; soft attacks, long decays, no loud transients; always-on open-fifth drone bed so the sky is never silent. **Degrades first-class:** camera denied / MediaPipe fails → two **ghost hands** keep scooping + singing (resume after ~3.5s of no hand), `text-rose-300` notice; WebGL2 unavailable → inline **Canvas2D** fallback with the identical gather/spill glow physics + bells, a badge showing the live path (`WebGL2 ◈` / `Canvas2D ◇`); alive within ~2s of START. iOS: AudioContext + `getUserMedia` both inside the first tap (≥64px Start button); full teardown stops camera tracks, deletes WebGL programs/buffers/VAOs + loses context, closes AudioContext, cancels rAF, disconnects ResizeObserver. **On-device only — nothing recorded or sent; no API route, no guard (rules #6/#8 clean); no new npm deps** (three is unused here; MediaPipe via runtime CDN script). **Named refs:** **MediaPipe Hands** · ***Journey*** (thatgamecompany — wordless awe, every gesture beautiful) · **Inigo Quilez** (additive point-glow / GLSL field lineage). **Ambition honest 2–3/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [MediaPipe hand-tracking + raw-WebGL2 GLSL point-glow field + spatial pentatonic bell-cluster engine] + #3 named refs; MediaPipe Hands already in lab `524` so technique-#1 doesn't count). **Source:** RESEARCH §470 → IDEAS §472 (`726-kids-star-scoop` ⭐) → RESEARCH §474 chain. Winner of a kids **DEEP** 3-renderer fire (three.js / WebGL2 / WebGPU); banked **`730-kids-star-scoop`** ⭐ (three.js sibling — warmest glow) + **`732-kids-star-cradle`** ⭐ (WebGPU compute, 12k particles — the bolder swing once WebGPU cools) as **IDEAS §474**. **Compile + lint verified** (TypeScript + ESLint pass, **zero issues in the 731 folder** — all warnings are pre-existing `src/lib/*` files), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE) — an infra quirk, NOT the code: pristine `main` fails identically** (proven this cycle); Vercel builds this same app normally and will deploy 731. **NOT browser-verified** (no camera/WebGL/audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: whether MediaPipe reliably reads a real toddler's fist/open across hand-sizes & lighting, the scoop→bell latency on a real iPad, and whether the spilled-star glissando reads as wonder; the ghost-hands + Canvas2D fallbacks guarantee a sounding, moving glance with zero hardware).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/731-kids-star-reach/README.md`

### `734-paths-tape-erosion` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 475, 2026-06-19)
[/dream/734-paths-tape-erosion](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/734-paths-tape-erosion) — **"Tape Erosion": Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano plays as a slow 7-minute arc that disintegrates and re-forms — the spectrum thins and smears, motifs vanish and ghost back, never an exact repeat — rendered as a decaying magnetic-tape spectrogram.** A **long-form, STATEFUL** piece (the lab's thin category): minute 6 is materially different from minute 1, not a loop. The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-18 #3** ("reward DEPTH — stop banking ceilings and walking away") by extending the loved **paths-granular thread** (`227-paths-granular`❤️) with its missing long-form dimension instead of a new toy, **+ #5** (use his real music — 1 of last 15) **+ #1** (ban Canvas2D — raw WebGL2, the scarce/iPad-bulletproof renderer). **Audio:** his recording is read via the public `/api/audio/:id` GET, sliced into 80–140ms Hann grains scrubbed by a slowly-advancing read-head (~0.4× over ~2–4 min); a **5-movement state machine** — *Intact → Eroding → Sparse → Ghost → Reforming* (~7 min, 12s smoothstep crossfades) — modulates rate-drift, LP cutoff (tape-head roll-off), dropout probability (tape damage), reverb wash, grain density and gain; a sub-bass C2 drone keeps it never-silent. **Visual:** raw **WebGL2** (not three.js, not Canvas2D-primary) — two ping-pong **R32F float FBOs** accumulate a spectral field; a feedback fragment shader applies Gaussian smear + exponential decay + horizontal advection + magnetic noise and deposits each new FFT column on the right edge; a display shader applies an **Ikeda-style colormap** (black→indigo→teal→warm-white) with scanline flicker + vignette. The erosion params are driven by the same movement state as the audio, so sound and image decay as one. INPUT: **audio-file (his real piano, NO camera)** · OUTPUT: **raw WebGL2 ping-pong FBO** (scarce, iPad-bulletproof; Canvas2D scrolling-spectrogram fallback) · TECHNIQUE: **spectral-feedback erosion + long-form stateful decay/reform** · VIBE: adult, elegiac, Ikeda-spectral. **Named refs:** **William Basinski *The Disintegration Loops*** (tape decaying as it plays — listening consumes the recording) · **Brian Eno *Music for Airports* / *Reflection*** (long-form non-repeating systems) · **Ryoji Ikeda** (spectral-feedback / data-as-light lineage). **Ambition 4/5** (#1 long-form >7-min STATEFUL register, new · #2 ≥3 subsystems [recording loader + granular erosion DSP + WebGL2 FBO feedback + 5-movement state machine] · #3 three named refs · #5 RESEARCH §475 long-form-generative finding, today). **Degrades first-class:** no audio fetch → offline fallback synth (~12s arpeggio) + `text-amber-300/95` notice; no WebGL2 / no `EXT_color_buffer_float` → Canvas2D scrolling spectrogram with comparable colormap; never blank-screen, alive from the first frame. Reads only public `/api/audio/:id` GET → **no api route, no `guard`** (rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm deps**. Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-renderer fire (one disintegration concept; WebGPU / WebGL2 / three.js attacks); banked **`733-paths-disintegrate`** ⭐ (WebGPU compute, 40k particles — the bolder swing once WebGPU's review-device support is verified) + **`735-paths-revenant`** ⭐ (three.js 60k-`Points` volumetric Anadol-ghost — the warmest look) as **IDEAS §475**. **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (`✓ Compiled successfully`, zero errors in the 734 folder — fixed 3 TS-5.7 null-safety nits during validation), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE) — an infra quirk, NOT the code: pristine `main` (512 pages) fails identically** (proven this cycle); Vercel builds this same app normally and will deploy 734. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio/WebGL in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: whether the feedback field reads as *tape decay* and whether the 7-min arc holds; Canvas2D + fallback-synth guarantee a sounding, moving glance). Design notes: `src/app/dream/734-paths-tape-erosion/README.md`

### `744-magnetic-walk` — `demoable` · adult · WIDE-winner (cycle 479, 2026-06-19)
[/dream/744-magnetic-walk](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/744-magnetic-walk) — **"Magnetic Walk": the invisible field around you, made audible — the phone's compass heading + tilt turn your orientation in the Earth's geomagnetic frame into a slowly-morphing just-intoned drone and a WebGL2 aurora that stays locked to magnetic north as you turn your body.** **For**: adults / installation / **mobile-native** / ambient-meditative / live-performance-fit. The lab's **first use of the device compass/magnetometer** — every prior orientation piece (the kids-tilt cluster, `308-orbit-choir`, `394-soundfield-room`) read only raw `beta`/`gamma` tilt; this reads true magnetic north (`webkitCompassHeading` on iOS, `(360-alpha)%360` elsewhere), so the *world* feels fixed while *you* turn. **Input:** one `deviceorientation` listener; on iOS, `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start tap (same gesture that unlocks audio). **Mapping:** heading walks a **12-station "compass of keys"** (just-intoned roots + chord colors), continuously crossfading between the two nearest stations so N/E/S/W give genuinely different drone colors; tilt (beta) → octave + lowpass; roll (gamma) → pan + shimmer detune; smoothed turn-rate → upper-voice level + brightness + visual energy. **Audio:** four detuned saw/sine voices + triangle shimmer → lowpass → a **runtime-synthesized convolution reverb** (decaying-noise impulse, no file), all glided with `setTargetAtTime` — a continuous slowly-morphing field, **no beat/loop, no samples, no granular/concatenative synthesis, no recorded piano** (the directest break of this cycle's banned 8× his-piano-grain monoculture). **Visual:** a **WebGL2** (GLSL ES 3.00) full-screen-triangle aurora rotated by `-heading` so it stays locked to magnetic north; hue follows heading, band structure follows tilt, brightness follows turn-rate, with a faint north spoke + ring on a dark tone-mapped field. INPUT: **device compass heading + tilt** (lab-first) · OUTPUT: **WebGL2 aurora** (Canvas2D fallback) · TECHNIQUE: **cross-modal geomagnetic sonification → generative just-intoned drone** · VIBE: ambient / psychogeographic / meditative. **Named refs:** **Christina Kubisch *Electrical Walks*** (induction headphones that make a city's EM fields audible as you walk — this is its geomagnetic pocket cousin) · **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening***. **Ambition honest 2/5** (#1 first compass/magnetometer input + #2 ≥3 subsystems [orientation sensor-fusion + generative drone synth w/ runtime convolution reverb + WebGL2 north-locked aurora shader]). **Source:** RESEARCH §479 (today — the vision-conditioned spatial-audio wave; the hook: bind a real-world spatial signal to sound in-browser from a *sensor*, zero model download). **Degrades first-class:** no sensor / permission denied / desktop / no live events within ~1.8s → **ghost mode** auto-drifts heading ~6°/s with gentle tilt so a silent 06:30 glance is sounding + moving with zero interaction (`text-rose-300` notice; seamless handoff the moment real data arrives); no WebGL2 → Canvas2D fallback with the same north-locked concentric-aurora idea; alive within ~2s of Start. **No external audio, no API route, no `guard`, no new npm deps** (rules #6/#8 clean; package.json/package-lock untouched). Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (compass-sensor / neural-room / ISS-telemetry — three never-used techniques); banked **`745-room-listener`** ⭐ (the lab's FIRST in-browser neural net — TFJS + COCO-SSD turns each camera-detected object into a voice; ambition 3/5, triple-love-aligned) + **`746-orbit-bell`** (live ISS telemetry → cosmic globe drone + terminator-crossing bells) as **IDEAS §479**. **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (zero issues in the 744 folder — all ESLint output is pre-existing `src/lib/**`), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE) — an infra quirk, NOT the code: pristine `main` fails identically at the same path** (proven this cycle); Vercel builds this same app normally and will deploy 744. **NOT browser-verified** (no device/WebGL/audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: real iOS `webkitCompassHeading` + permission flow, GLSL aurora compile, drone timbre/click-freeness, and whether the ghost→live-sensor handoff feels seamless; the ghost auto-drift + Canvas2D fallback guarantee a sounding, moving glance with zero hardware).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/744-magnetic-walk/README.md`

### `748-duet-follower` — `demoable` · adult · WIDE-winner (cycle 481, 2026-06-19)
[/dream/748-duet-follower](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/748-duet-follower) — **"Duet Follower": Karel's real *Welcome Home* recording becomes an accompanist that LISTENS to you — you keep the pulse (spacebar / A–L keyboard row / tap the stage) and the system estimates your tempo from inter-onset intervals and drives the playbackRate of his actual performance to follow your groove toward the next musical moment. You conduct his recording's pace; it's a duet where his performance is the accompanist that adapts to you.** The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-19 #1** ("his music can be used WITHOUT shattering it into grains — **play it and score-follow it**; the concatenative grain-cloud is now 7/15, the new pentatonic") and **#2** ("rotate OFF GPU-shader fields entirely for a cycle — build one piece with NO shader, prove the idea carries the piece, not the renderer"). **The lab's first use of his recording as a *followed score/performance*** — across 740+ prototypes his piano was only ever *visualized* or *scattered into a CataRT grain cloud*; here it is a continuous, time-stretched performance that re-times itself to YOUR pulse. **Input:** keyboard / tap pulse (spacebar, A–L / Q–T rows, click/touch) — **no mic, no camera** (both over-represented 4× and jury-flagged). **Technique:** onset → tempo estimate (short median of recent inter-onset intervals, clamped 30–240 bpm) → his recording is analyzed once into a coarse ~50ms RMS envelope whose rising edges become musical **landmarks**; on each tap the engine sets `playbackRate = gap-to-next-landmark / beatPeriod` (smoothed 35/65, clamped 0.4–2.2×) so his performance *arrives at that moment one of your beats from now*. **Continuous whole-performance playback** (one looping `AudioBufferSource` whose rate is re-timed) — emphatically **NOT** a descriptor-space grain cloud. **Output:** pure **SVG/DOM** — a moving piano-roll ribbon (envelope mirrored about a fixed center playhead), amber landmark markers, a pulsing playhead; **no WebGL, no WebGPU, no shader, no Canvas2D** (the cycle's whole point: prove the idea without a renderer). VIBE: warm chamber-concert duet (radial amber wash) — explicitly **not** the near-dark luminous-glow contemplative vibe the jury banned at 9×. **Named refs:** **Matchmaker** (real-time piano score following, arXiv 2510.10087, Oct 2025) · **Christopher Raphael *Music Plus One*** (statistical automatic accompaniment) · **Roger Dannenberg / Barry Vercoe** (foundational synthetic-performer accompaniment) · **Arshia Cont *Antescofo*** (anticipatory score follower) — this is the deliberately *inverted* cousin: the human is the soloist-conductor, his recording is the follower. **Ambition honest 3–4/5** (#1 score-following / interactive-accompaniment is lab-first — his recording used as a followed performance, never done · #2 ≥3 subsystems [tap/keyboard onset input + tempo estimator + envelope/landmark analyzer + continuous follower engine + SVG ribbon] · #3 four named refs · #5 RESEARCH §481, today — soft/qualified bind to the Matchmaker / Structure-Aware-Accompaniment 2024–2026 lane, NOT a <14-day claim). **Source:** RESEARCH §481 → this WIDE fire. **Degrades first-class:** no recording fetch → offline-rendered piano phrase is followed instead (`text-amber-300/95` notice); alive within ~2s; full teardown (rAF cancel, engine dispose, AudioContext close). Reads only the public `/api/audio/:id` GET → **no api route, no `guard`** (rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm deps**. Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (three orthogonal NON-grain, NON-shader uses of his real recording: score-follow / speech-arrange / sky-conduct); banked **`749-spoken-libretto`** (speech-ASR → real-recording PHRASE selection onto a typographic SVG staff; Reich *Different Trains* / Lucier) + **`750-sky-almanac`** ⭐ (locally-computed sun position + moon phase → long-form STATEFUL arrangement of his real-recording phrases on an SVG sun-arc dial; Cage *Atlas Eclipticalis* / Finer *Longplayer* — the daylight vibe + long-form-stateful both thin categories) as **IDEAS §481**. **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (`✓ Compiled successfully`, **zero issues in the 748 folder** — all ESLint warnings are pre-existing `src/lib/**`), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE) — proven environmental THIS cycle: pristine `main` with 748 moved aside compiles + fails at the identical `next-font-manifest.json` open** (not the code); Vercel builds this same app normally and will deploy 748. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: whether the envelope-landmark following reads as *his performance following your pulse* on his real recording's dynamics, the tap→re-time latency, and whether `playbackRate` pitch-shift stays musical; the offline-fallback phrase has clean well-spaced onsets and guarantees a sounding, following glance). Design notes: `src/app/dream/748-duet-follower/README.md`

### `754-conducted-table` — `demoable` · adult · DEEP-winner (cycle 483, 2026-06-19)
[/dream/754-conducted-table](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/754-conducted-table) — **"The Conducted Table": Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano becomes a long-form ENSEMBLE you direct from a top-down score table — mute and revive a semicircle of seats, shape tempo and dynamics, and let friends fill the empty chairs from their own phones.** A deliberate **cycle-2 of `729-piano-portal-jam`** (the lab's first cross-device WebRTC duet): it keeps 729's zero-server note-event networking but **replaces 729's grain engine** (the technique JURY 2026-06-19 banned at 7/15) with a **non-grain phrase-remix** of his recording — the directest read of JURY #3 ("protect the depth habit; return to a ceiling nobody extended — take 729 to 3+ players / a conducted room") fused with JURY #1 ("use his music a way that isn't a grain cloud"). **Input:** tap seats in/out + tempo/dynamics sliders + remote peers — **no mic, no camera** (both jury-flagged 4×). **His music as PHRASES, not grains:** `segmentPhrases()` finds musical phrases by onset/silence RMS detection (~20ms windows; phrase start = energy rises after a quiet gap; end = quiet >~250ms; kept 0.6–3s, brightness-sorted); each seat plays ONE whole phrase via `AudioBufferSourceNode` with a per-seat `playbackRate` register shift + short envelope — emphatically not 90ms descriptor-space grains. **Long-form, stateful (5+ min, not a loop):** a Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler keeps the pulse steady while a memory arc migrates the harmonic center (slow sines + drift, retuning the open-fifth drone), breathes density over ~90s cycles, and drifts each seat's phrase-bank center + subdivision — minute 5 ≠ minute 0; the conductor biases, never resets. **Output:** **Canvas2D** — a warm amber/gold top-down parchment table, 6 seats in a semicircle facing the conductor, traveling conductor→seat cue arcs + seat blooms, IN/OUT/LIVE visual states — *not* a GPU shader field (JURY #2: "build one piece with no shader; Canvas2D is scarce again at 3× — use it"). VIBE: bright warm concert hall — explicitly not the near-dark luminous-glow contemplative vibe banned at 9×. **Networking (extends 729 — star/conductor-hub):** zero server, no API route, no signaling deps; the conductor is the host hub, "Invite a player" gzips an offer SDP → base64url into a shareable `#o=` link, the guest auto-answers, the host pastes the answer code; 3+ players = one peer connection per seat; only `{seat, phrase, vel, t}` events travel (both peers hold the identical recording → near-zero bandwidth, render locally). Networking is purely additive — solo is the full experience. **Named refs:** **`729-piano-portal-jam`** (cycle-1) · **The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers** (historic networked ensembles — small messages, not audio) · **JackTrip** · AES **"Web-Based Networked Music Performances via WebRTC"** · **"A Design Space for Live Music Agents"** (arXiv 2602.05064, Feb 4 2026 — the ghost seats are agents the conductor steers, not micromanages). **Ambition honest 3/5** (#1 first *conducted/multi-seat* extension of the cross-device ceiling + #2 ≥3 subsystems [phrase-corpus loader/segmenter + look-ahead conductor scheduler w/ memory arc + Canvas2D score-table renderer + star WebRTC topology] + #3 named refs; #5 soft — RESEARCH §483, today). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-19 #3 (depth) + #1 (non-grain his-music) → RESEARCH §483 → this DEEP fire. **Degrades first-class:** no recording fetch → ~16s offline detuned-arpeggio fallback (with rests so segmentation still finds phrases) + a high-contrast `text-rose-700` notice; WebRTC/CompressionStream unsupported → invite UI hidden, solo only; ghost ensemble (all 6 seats ON) is alive + sounding the moment you press Begin; full teardown stops all sources + drones, cancels rAF + the lookahead interval, closes all peer connections + the AudioContext. iOS: AudioContext created/resumed inside the Begin tap. Reads only the public `/api/audio/:id` GET → **no api route, no `guard`** (rules #6/#8 clean); **no new npm deps**. Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-renderer fire (one Conducted-Room concept; three.js / Canvas2D / SVG-DOM attacks); banked **`753-conducted-orbit`** ⭐ (three.js concert-in-the-round, baton-on-a-3D-stage gesture — the most physically expressive conducting model) + **`755-conducted-stage`** (SVG/DOM dashboard whose long-form **timeline ribbon** makes the 5.5-min arc legible at a glance — the best at *showing* the arc) as **IDEAS §483**. **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (`✓ Compiled successfully in 97s`, **zero issues in the 754 folder** — all ESLint warnings are pre-existing `src/lib/**`), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE at `next-font-manifest.json`) — proven environmental THIS cycle: pristine `main` with 754 moved aside compiles + fails identically** (not the code); Vercel builds this app normally and will deploy 754. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: whether onset/silence segmentation yields *musical* phrases vs. ragged fragments on his real dynamics, whether the conducted arc holds attention over minutes, and the real cross-device handshake on two phones; the ghost ensemble + fallback buffer guarantee a sounding, moving glance solo).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/754-conducted-table/README.md`

### `773-kids-parade-caller` — `demoable` · kids (4+) · WIDE-winner (cycle 490, 2026-06-20)
[/dream/773-kids-parade-caller](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/773-kids-parade-caller) — **"Parade Caller": a 4-year-old SAYS words out loud and they march in as a parade of percussion — each word's SYLLABLE RHYTHM (not its pitch) becomes a looping drum/clap pattern, so "el-e-phant" is a 3-hit gallop, "cat" is one big BOOM, and words accrete into a parade band you build by talking.** The lab's **first use of speech as RHYTHM** — every prior ASR piece (`752-kids-word-band`, `570-spoken-spell`) maps a recognized word to *pitch* (letters/phonemes → pentatonic notes); this maps it to *percussion*, the konnakol premise (spoken syllables ARE the beat) made playable by a toddler. The deliberate answer to **JURY 2026-06-19 #4** ("the un-built kids register is the active/joyful MIDDLE — bright daylight, bold primary color, fast legible cause-and-effect, neither solemn-glow nor slapstick") and **#2** ("rotate OFF GPU-shader fields — Canvas2D is scarce again at 3×"). **Input:** speech / browser ASR (`webkitSpeechRecognition`/`SpeechRecognition`, continuous + interim) — **no mic-hum analysis, no camera** (both jury-flagged). **Technique:** `syllables.ts` estimates syllable count from spelling (vowel-groups, silent-`e`, `-le` ending, clamp 1–6) + a stress index → `makeWordLoop` places the syllables evenly on a 16-step bar, accents the downbeat + stressed syllable, adds a marching hi-hat; loops accrete on one shared Web-Audio clock (~112 BPM, cap 5, oldest drops). Voice + bright primary color by syllable count (1=red kick, 2=amber clap, 3=green tom, 4=blue cowbell, 5=purple woodblock, 6=pink shaker); pitch demoted to a single warm C2 bass-marimba on the downbeat — the soul is the BEAT, not a pentatonic melody-wash (the perennial kids crutch). All percussion synthesized live (no audio files, no npm deps). **Output:** **Canvas2D** — sunny daytime sky, a scrolling marching road, bouncing primary-colored character discs (one per active word) pulsing on their hits with the word in big readable type, a sweeping red playhead. VIBE: bright exuberant carnival parade — explicitly **not** the near-dark luminous-glow contemplative vibe banned at 9×, and not silly-googly slapstick. **Named refs:** **Konnakol** (South Indian Carnatic vocal percussion — spoken syllables *are* the rhythm; taught to children from ~age 3 at the SaPa academy, "Indian beatboxing") · **Steve Reich** speech-melody (*Different Trains*, *It's Gonna Rain* — recorded-speech rhythm drives the music). **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [browser ASR + syllable-rhythm analysis + accreting percussion step-sequencer on a shared clock + Canvas2D parade render = 4] + #3 named reference [Konnakol / Reich]; ASR-as-rhythm is a soft #1 — the primitive exists in-lab but never used for rhythm; #5 NOT claimed — konnakol is foundational). **Source:** JURY 2026-06-19 #4 + the grep that ASR-in-lab is pitch-only → RESEARCH §490 (konnakol = spoken syllables ARE percussion) → this kids **WIDE** fire. **Degrades first-class:** no `SpeechRecognition` / mic denied → an always-present **picture-word menu** (8 big tap buttons) drives the same loop pipeline (`text-rose-300` notice, never a dead end); **auto-demo** cycles built-in words through the same pipeline after ~3s of silence so a silent glance both sees and HEARS the parade (real input cancels it; ~5s silence restarts). Pure client Web Audio + Web Speech + Canvas2D — **no api route, no `guard`** (rules #6/#8 clean), **no new npm deps**. Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire (three orthogonal bright/active/non-shader directions: speech-rhythm / physics-marble / step-sequencer); banked **`774-kids-marble-symphony`** ⭐ (SVG/DOM Wintergatan-style physics marble machine — the scarcest renderer + love-aligned to the loved `169-kids-marble-run`) + **`775-kids-stamp-band`** (Canvas2D Toshio-Iwai *SimTunes*/*Electroplankton* step-sequencer with looping compositional memory) as **IDEAS §490**. **Compile + lint + type-check verified clean** (build reaches `Collecting page data`, **zero issues in the 773 folder** — all ESLint warnings are pre-existing `src/lib/**`; `tsc --noEmit` clean project-wide), **static-generation BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE at `next-font-manifest.json`) — proven environmental THIS cycle: pristine `main` with 773 moved aside fails identically at the same open** (standing infra limit, not the code); Vercel builds this app normally and will deploy 773. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in sandbox — unverified by eye/ear: whether a 4-year-old's pronunciation reliably recognizes, whether the syllable heuristic feels musical across everyday words, and the parade's small-screen crowding at 5 loops; the auto-demo + picture-menu guarantee a sounding, marching glance with zero mic).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/773-kids-parade-caller/README.md`

---

[/dream/781-kids-paint-conductor](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/781-kids-paint-conductor) — **"Paint Your Music": a 4-year-old finger-paints freehand on a bright cream canvas and a glowing playhead sweeps left→right forever, turning the picture they drew into the song they hear.** `demoable` · Cycle 492 (kids, DEEP fire — winner of 3 UPIC explorers). **For**: kids (4+) / **touch-draw** input / **Canvas2D** / bright daylight playful. **The literal realization of Iannis Xenakis's UPIC (1977, CEMAMu — "with UPIC, music becomes a game for children: they draw, they hear") for a 4yo** — and the lab's first true drawing-as-continuous-score (existing paint pieces 100/104/158/160/152 are per-stroke loops, splat-triggers, or draw-from-voice; none has a single global time-axis playhead that reads ALL strokes' y as pitch). **Mapping:** **x = time** (when in the 8s loop a point sounds), **y = pitch** (top=high, bottom=low, snapped to C-major-pentatonic across C3–A5 so nothing can ever sound wrong), **stroke color = timbre** (five distinct friendly voices: sine bell, triangle flute, filtered-saw horn, FM music-box, soft pluck), **thickness = loudness**. A drawn curve becomes a melodic line; stacked strokes become chords. **Long-form stateful** (the thin kids-depth category JURY-valued): strokes accumulate and the composition grows richer over minutes — no reset-per-tap. Seeded with a gentle pre-drawn arc that plays from load, so a hands-off 06:30 glance is alive and sounding. ≥64px emoji color swatches, sparkle-burst clear, kids-safe chain (`gain 0.28 → lowpass 7000 → comp −10/20:1`), always-on soft pad, iOS-gesture-gated AudioContext, full teardown, graceful `text-rose-600` audio-unavailable notice on the light bg. **Off every JURY-2026-06-19 ban** (no grain-resynthesis · no GPU-shader-field · no mic-hum · no dark-glow) and hits #4 (bright/joyful/active middle) + #3 (long-form stateful). No mic/camera, no data, no API route (no guard needed). Banked siblings: `782-kids-sun-clock-song` ⭐ (radial cyclic UPIC), `783-kids-melody-rider` (a character rides the drawn line) — IDEAS §492.

---

[/dream/778-markov-mirror](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/778-markov-mirror) — **"Markov Mirror": teach a small keyboard your melodic style, then watch the model itself — a glowing transition graph — improvise back in your style.** `demoable` · Cycle 493 (adult, WIDE fire — winner of 3 orthogonal explorers). **For**: adult / **on-screen-keyboard + computer-keys** input / **SVG/DOM** output / luminous-instructional violet-emerald. **A minimal *visible* live music agent.** You play (tap keys or `a s d f g h j k l`); the app builds a **live variable-order Markov chain** of YOUR note transitions (order-1 pairs + order-2 triples, counts accumulate, phrase context resets on a >900ms pause). "Improvise" (Space) then **walks the chain forever in your style** (prefers order-2 evidence, weighted by your counts, humanized timing, soft bass root every 4 steps), voiced by a warm triangle+sine ADSR piano → algorithmic-reverb bus → `gain 0.28 → lowpass 6.8k → compressor`. **The graph is not decoration — it IS the model, rendered live in SVG:** notes on a ring, directed Bézier edges whose thickness/brightness scale with use (well-worn transitions go emerald), the struck node haloed, the traversed edge glowing as the melody walks its own learned web. Teach / Improvise / Forget. **Why this renderer:** SVG/DOM is the scarcest surface in the rolling window (JURY 2026-06-20 #1's explicitly-named starved surface — 1×) and the *right* one here: the model is a graph, so show it as a graph, no canvas/WebGL. **Directly implements the "visible-model" axis of *A Design Space for Live Music Agents* (arXiv 2602.05064, Feb 2026)** — the lab's many accompanist pieces (718/748/770) all hide their brain; this one shows it and lets you reshape it. **Named refs:** David Cope *Experiments in Musical Intelligence* (EMI/Emmy) · Hiller & Isaacson *Illiac Suite* (1957, first Markov-generated composition) · the Feb-2026 live-agents design-space paper. **Ambition 3–4/5** (#1 first shipped live variable-order-Markov visible-agent / symbolic style-modeling register · #2 five subsystems [tap+key input · live-learning variable-order Markov · walk scheduler · polyphonic synth · live SVG graph] · #3 three named refs · #5 RESEARCH §493 same-day). **Off every banned tag** (NOT Canvas2D, NOT his-recording, NOT a GPU shader). Zero deps, zero API route (no guard needed), iOS-gesture-gated AudioContext, full teardown. **Compile + ESLint + `tsc --noEmit` all clean** (zero issues in the 778 folder; `✓ Compiled successfully`), **static-gen BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE) — infra quirk, NOT the code: pristine `main` with 778 stashed fails identically** (proven this cycle); Vercel builds normally. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in sandbox — unverified by ear: whether the improviser audibly reflects your habits and whether the live graph reads as "watching the melody walk its own web"; setTimeout scheduling may drift under load). Resurrects the §491 ⭐ resurrect-first seed. Winner of an adult **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`785-aurora-weather-organ`** (WebGL2 + live NOAA space-weather sonification) + **`786-body-organ`** ⭐ (MediaPipe full-body pose → organ chord) as IDEAS §493. Design notes: `src/app/dream/778-markov-mirror/README.md`

---

[/dream/841-kids-hum-stack](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/841-kids-hum-stack) — **"Hum & Stack": a 4-year-old hums any note and builds a chord UPWARD from their own voice — tapping warm/bright/spicy creatures that sing intervals relative to the pitch they're singing, where "spicy" is a creature's playful mood, not a wrong note.** `demoable` · Cycle 512 (kids, WIDE fire — winner of 3 harmonic-agency explorers). **For**: kids (4+) / **mic-voice** input (button-root fallback) / **Canvas2D** creatures / bright friendly. **The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-21 #4** ("kill the pentatonic-never-wrong reflex; build a kids piece where harmony can be *shaped* and a wrong choice is interesting rather than impossible") — there is **no pre-approved scale at all**: harmony is constructed on whatever the child sings. **Pitch detection:** a hand-written monophonic detector (`detectPitch` — McLeod Pitch Method / NSDF autocorrelation with parabolic peak refinement, RMS-gated, 70–1100 Hz, **no library**) tracks the live fundamental; a glowing **root creature** follows it. **Harmony engine:** WARM ♥ (3/4/8 semitones) · BRIGHT ★ (7/12/19) · SPICY ✦ (10/2/6) flavor buttons stack creatures that sing *relative to the live pitch* and continuously retune to it; tapping a flavor cycles its intervals; the tower caps at 5 voices; "↓ let go" releases the top. **Consonance vs dissonance = MOOD not gate:** spicy stacks use sawtooth + a +7-cent beating partner + shimmer LFO and the creature visibly wobbles/sparkles; consonant stacks (triangle) glow steady. **Named refs:** the **overtone series / just-intonation interval stacking** (harmony built upward from a fundamental — here the child's live voice) · the **Toca Band** "tap a character, it sings in relation to the others" model, reimagined so the bottom of the band is the child's real-time pitch. **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ≥3 subsystems [NSDF/MPM pitch detector + live-retuning interval-stack harmony engine + Canvas2D creature renderer + ambient bed] · #3 named refs; soft #1 — pitch-tracked *harmony-on-your-own-voice* for kids is new though pitch-detection exists in `833`/`88`; soft #5 — RESEARCH §512 reframed consonance/dissonance as legible & playable). **Kids-safe chain:** everything through a 2.6 kHz low-pass + soft envelopes + modest gain, capped voices, always-on breathing ambient bed (never silent), mic→AnalyserNode only (no feedback), AudioContext + mic both requested on the start gesture. **Degrades first-class:** mic denied/unavailable → visible `text-rose-300` notice + a row of bold colored root-pad buttons drive the identical stacking pipeline (fully demoable on desktop, no mic). **diversity:** INPUT mic/voice (dodges the touch-input over-rep JURY flagged 5×), no pentatonic crutch (JURY 6×). No mic-routing to output, no data, no API route (no `guard` needed), zero new npm deps. **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (zero issues in the 841 folder — fixed one TS5.7 `Float32Array<ArrayBuffer>` generic at the `getFloatTimeDomainData` call; all build warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`), **static-gen BLOCKED by the container fd ceiling (EMFILE at `next-font-manifest.json`) — proven environmental this cycle: pristine `main` with 841 moved aside + `.next` cleared fails identically**; Vercel builds normally and will deploy 841. **NOT browser-verified** (no audio in sandbox — unverified by ear: whether a 4-year-old's hum reliably tracks via NSDF on a phone mic, whether spicy reads as "fun" vs "off," small-screen crowding at 5 voices; the no-mic root-pad fallback guarantees a sounding, stacking glance). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`839-kids-chord-garden`** ⭐ (touch · a kids **Tonnetz** lattice — planted triad = closed glowing triangle, far-apart notes sprout a wiggly creature; **top resurrect-first**) + **`840-kids-harmony-sea`** (tilt · sail a boat across a circle-of-fifths × chord-color plane, choppy water = spicy) as IDEAS §512. Design notes: `src/app/dream/841-kids-hum-stack/README.md`

[/dream/842-air-veil](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/842-air-veil) — **"Air Veil": hear the air six world cities are breathing right now — and the dirtier the air, the more it audibly FOULS the harmony.** `demoable` · Cycle 513 (adult, WIDE fire — winner of 3 orthogonal explorers). **For**: adult / **live external-API data** input (Open-Meteo Air Quality — free, no key, CORS-`*`) / **Canvas2D** equirectangular world-map output / ecological-critical vibe. **The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-21 #3** ("two cold categories have zero entries this window — real-world-data sonification; music *about* something other than music"). Grep-verified **lab-first: 0× air-quality** — the lab sonifies weather (`580`), tides, and solar wind (`314`/`471`/`618`), but has **never** sonified the air a place is breathing. **The inversion that makes it new:** instead of mapping data to *more notes*, pollution maps to **harmonic fouling** — clean air (low US-AQI) voices a city with pure consonant just-intonation partials (1f·2f·3f); rising particulates progressively add **inharmonic partials + amplitude-beating (detuned-pair) roughness + detune**, so you literally *hear a city's air get dirty*. Six cities (NYC, London, Delhi, Tokyo, São Paulo, Mexico City) each = a glowing AQI dot on the map with a drifting **particle-wind veil** whose density/grey = its PM2.5; the worst-AQI city opens the master lowpass + thickens a sub-drone so you can hear which city is choking. Refetches every 60s, ramps to new values click-free (`setTargetAtTime`). **Robust offline:** fetch/CORS failure → visible `text-rose-300` "simulated air" notice + the identical pipeline runs on plausible seeded, random-walking US-AQI values (US-EPA PM2.5→AQI breakpoints fill any missing `us_aqi`); canvas animates on load before audio; gesture-gated AudioContext; full teardown. **Named refs:** **Andrea Polli & Chad Eby — *Particle Falls*** (live PM2.5 → a projected waterfall of light) · ***Mutual Air*** (a wind-chime ringing to a live PM sensor) · **Ximena Alarcón — *Huellas de Aire*** (Pure-Data PM2.5 patches, MAMM Medellín). **Ambition honest 3/5** (#1 **first air-quality / un-owned external-data feed in the lab** [grep-0×] · #2 ≥3 subsystems [live AQ fetch+parse + inharmonic/roughness synthesis engine + particle-wind sim + Canvas2D world-map render = 4] · #3 named refs above). **Source chain (visible in STATE):** RESEARCH §513 (air-quality sonification field scan — Particle Falls / Mutual Air / Sonic Kayaks; Open-Meteo ships a free no-key CORS air-quality API) → JURY 2026-06-21 #3 (real-world-data is a cold category, zero this window) → this adult **WIDE** fire. **Diversity:** `banned (JURY 2026-06-21 + audit)=[his-recording-as-source · machine-listens/answering-agent · pentatonic-never-wrong · UPIC pitch-painter · adult-ambient-as-default]` · `picked (winner 842)=[external-API-data INPUT (cold category, NOT his-recording) · Canvas2D world-map OUTPUT · air-quality→inharmonic-roughness TECH (grep-0×) · ecological/critical VIBE (not the banned ambient-default — it's data-driven & event-aware)]` — clears every ban. **No API route → no `guard`** (the data fetch is a client-side GET to a public no-key endpoint, no Resonance backend touched); **zero new npm deps** (Canvas2D + Web Audio only). **WIDE fan-out — 2 explored, banked to IDEAS §513:** **`843-gravity-sequencer`** ⭐ (compose a polyrhythm by arranging GRAVITY — a velocity-Verlet symplectic orbital integrator where perihelions trigger tuned percussive strikes; *Otomata*/Kepler refs; the §507 ⭐ banked `825` rebuilt clean & lint-fixed — **top adult resurrect-first**) + **`844-overtone-mirror`** (sing one note and climb its just-intonation overtone tower with your voice — hand-written NSDF/MPM pitch detection lights/rings overtones 2–16; *Stimmung*/La-Monte-Young/McLeod refs). Both built complete + self-reported ESLint/tsc clean, folders removed before the authoritative winner-only build (non-winners not committed). ⚠️ **Compile + ESLint + type-check verified clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: `✓ Compiled successfully` → Linting/type-checking passed → **failed only at "Collecting page data" on the standing container ~4096-fd `EMFILE` at `next-font-manifest.json`**; zero warnings in the 842 folder, all build warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **infra-blocked static-gen, NOT a code defect** (fd ceiling is cgroup-locked at 4096, unraisable as root; fails identically on pristine `main` every cycle since 472; Vercel deploys normally and will deploy 842). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio in sandbox — unverified by ear: whether the inharmonic-fouling reads as "the air getting dirty" vs. just detuned, and how live the feed feels at a 60s refetch; the always-animating canvas + simulated-air fallback guarantee a sounding, drifting glance regardless). Design notes: `src/app/dream/842-air-veil/README.md`

[/dream/897-kids-balance-band](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/897-kids-balance-band) — **"Balance Band": a 4-year-old's musical seesaw where a wrong (dissonant) note tips the beam and STAYS tipped — a sour buzzing chord that does NOT auto-resolve — and the child earns the calm back by physically balancing the music.** `demoable` · Cycle 534 (kids, WIDE fire — winner of 3 template-breaker explorers). **For**: kids (4+) / **touch** input / **Canvas2D** seesaw / warm-playful. **The third answer to JURY 2026-06-23 #4** ("widen the crack — let the wrong choice persist and matter, no auto-resolve; build the third one and the no-wrong-notes template is broken for good," after `868-monster-keys` and `849-star-bowl`). **The mechanic — torque ↔ harmony:** the beam is a **2D rigid lever** in Canvas2D; tapping hangs singing creature-weights at discrete slots; each contributes `torque = mass × leverage` summed to a target angle clamped ±28° and eased per-frame for a physical settle. A **spicy** creature (tritone F♯ / minor-2nd D♭) is literally *heavier* (mass 1.7 vs 1.0) — its side tips down and **stays tipped**, its voice gains a +14¢ beating partner + a faint saw shimmer, and it renders as a wobbly spiky star; there is **no auto-correct**. The child resolves it by hanging a friendly consonant creature on the high arm → torque levels, the sour/shimmer voices glide to silence via `setTargetAtTime` (a warm consonant BLOOM), and the halo warms rose→green. Always-on C2+G2 open-fifth drone (never silent); ~2 s idle self-demo auto-hangs creatures so a hands-free glance moves + sounds within ~1 s. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 NEVER-used technique: **2D rigid-lever torque balance mapped to interval consonance** — grep-verified no prior seesaw/torque-physics piece · #2 ≥3 subsystems [touch hit-testing + 2D torque sim + consonance/dissonance harmony engine + Canvas2D render = 4] · #5 README cites the dated RESEARCH §534 findings **PMC11336827** [4–6 yo consonance categorization is still forming] + **PMC12605063, Oct 2025** [consonant=pleasant/dissonant=unpleasant affective valence] — the in-README dated citation, 2nd straight cycle landing the criterion that was 0-for-15). **Kids-safe chain:** every voice → `master(0.26) → lowpass(6 kHz) → DynamicsCompressor(−10/20:1) → destination`; soft ≥40 ms attacks; even dissonance is gentle/beating, never harsh; gesture-gated AudioContext; no Analyser routed to output. **Degrades:** no Web Audio → `text-rose-300` notice, visuals stay alive & playable; never throws. Full teardown (rAF cancel + stop all oscillators + `audioCtx.close()` + remove resize listener). No API route → no `guard`; zero new npm deps (Canvas2D + Web Audio only; NOT WebGL2/three.js/SVG). **diversity:** `banned=[raw-WebGL2 (the 6× JURY wall), three.js (approaching cap), no-wrong-notes-safe-scale-kids, data→roughness]` · `picked=[touch, Canvas2D (JURY-reopened, scarce 2×), 2D-torque-physics, kids-warm]`. **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: `✓ Compiled successfully` + lint/type passed; the only 897 lint output is a harmless `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` *warning* on the unmount cleanup — capturing voices at unmount is intended; warnings never fail Vercel); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `spawn EMFILE` fd ceiling** (infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear-verified** (no audio in container — the seesaw *feel*, whether a 4-yo reads "tipping = sour" and "balance = sweet," and small-screen crowding are unverified; the auto-demo guarantees a moving, sounding glance). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`898-kids-echo-keeper`** ⭐ (mic · SVG · a clash sticks looping in a knot until sung free — owns the SVG lane, top resurrect-first) + **`899-kids-candle-choir`** (touch · WebGPU+Canvas2D-fallback · a clash blows out a candle relit only by a consonant neighbor) as IDEAS §534. Design notes: `src/app/dream/897-kids-balance-band/README.md`

[/dream/918-kids-starlight-friend](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/918-kids-starlight-friend) — **"Starlight Friend": two 4-year-olds, each on their own iPad, share ONE magical night sky — shake your device to fling a shooting star and it lights up + chimes in your FRIEND's sky too, so you build a constellation song together.** `demoable` · Cycle 542 (kids, WIDE fire — winner of 3 orthogonal explorers). **For**: kids (4+) / **device-shake + WebRTC multi-user** input / **three.js** GPU starfield / calm-joyful indigo→violet social. **The lab's FIRST multi-user / WebRTC piece** (729/754 were adult cross-device but this is the first *kids* one, and the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-24 #2** — "multi-user/WebRTC shared listening room, STILL 0× after being named in every single jury"). The compositional logic is **latency-tolerant social co-play, NOT pitch theory** — there is no tempo clock; stars twinkle and chime *whenever* they arrive on each device, and the reward is "we made this together." One warm C-major pentatonic so nothing two children fling can ever clash. **Input:** device **shake** (`devicemotion`, accel magnitude >18 w/ 360ms refractory) flings a warm gold/rose shooting star into the child's half of the sky; a full-screen **tap-pad fallback** drives the identical fling→bloom→chime→send pipeline (touch is deliberately the fallback, not the instrument — dodges the JURY touch-as-primary ban). iOS `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` + AudioContext both gated inside the one Start tap. **Output:** **three.js** — a shader indigo→violet backdrop (slow breathing shimmer), a 900-point GPU twinkle field, additive shooting-star + persistent placed-star pools (`powerPreference:'low-power'`, capped pixel ratio). NOT Canvas2D/SVG. **Technique:** **serverless peer-to-peer co-play** — an `RTCPeerConnection` data channel (`ordered:false, maxRetransmits:0` — drops fine, co-play is loose) carries tiny `{x,y,hue,t,vx,vy}` star-events; **both peers synthesize the chime + render the star locally** (The Hub model — the event IS the whole message, near-zero bandwidth, no shared scheduler). **Handshake has NO signaling server:** host `createOffer()` → wait ICE → **gzip the SDP via `CompressionStream('gzip')` → base64url** into a `#join=` share-link; guest auto-answers and shows a gzipped answer code; host pastes it back. WebRTC/gzip unsupported → invite UI hidden, solo is the full experience. **Robustness for the 06:30 hands-free glance:** a **ghost friend** auto-joins solo within ~800ms, flinging its OWN cooler cyan/violet stars w/ a softer chime, so one unattended phone reads as **two-player co-play, alive + sounding within ~1s** — and it bows out the instant a real channel opens (networking is purely additive). VIBE: calm-joyful twinkling night sky, warm "my" stars vs cool "friend" stars. **Named refs:** **The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers** (historic networked ensembles — tiny messages travel, each node renders locally) · **Kirschner & Tomasello (2010), "Joint music making promotes prosocial behavior in 4-year-old children"** · **"Actions and feelings in sync: synchrony and empathy in children's dyadic musical interactions" (PMC12063534, 2025)** — dyadic child music-making promotes empathy, and loose synchrony still works, so latency tolerance is the feature. **Ambition honest 4/5** (#2 ≥4 subsystems [WebRTC star-event networking + shake/shooting-star sim + chime/pad synth + three.js starfield + ghost-friend demo] · #3 three named refs · #4 multi-cycle commitment [cycle-1 shared sky + ghost; cycle-2 3+ players / shared evolving constellation memory; cycle-3 polish] · #5 in-README dated RESEARCH §542 citation — **8 straight cycles** 535–542). #1 NOT claimed (WebRTC exists in-lab adult — not a clean lab-first technique; it IS the first multi-user *kids* piece). **Source chain (visible in STATE):** RESEARCH §542 (NMP chases tight tempo; dyadic-child synchrony→empathy is robust to loose sync) → JURY 2026-06-24 #2 (multi-user 0×) → this kids **WIDE** fire. **Kids-safe chain:** `master gain 0.24 → lowpass ~6.5kHz → DynamicsCompressor(-10/20:1) → destination`; ≥50ms soft chime attacks (sine + quiet triangle a fifth up), 60ms-fade noise whoosh, always-on C2+G2 breathing ambient pad (never silent); ≥64–72px tap targets; no reading required; no "wrong" notes. **Degrades first-class:** no motion/denied → `text-rose-300` notice + tap-pad; no WebGL → `text-rose-300` notice, audio + ghost still run; no audio → notice, visuals alive; no WebRTC/gzip → solo+ghost. Full teardown (rAF cancel; remove resize/devicemotion/pointerdown; stop ghost timer; close RTCPeerConnection+channel; dispose all three.js geo/mat/tex + `renderer.dispose()`; stop all oscillators + `audioCtx.close()`). **No API route → no `guard`** (zero server, zero AI, zero cost surface — all client-side); **zero new npm deps**. **diversity:** `banned=[touch-as-primary, Canvas2D, SVG, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine, external-data→structure-w/-consonant-crutch]` · `picked=[device-shake + WebRTC-multiuser (non-touch, lab-first-for-kids), three.js GPU starfield, latency-tolerant networked-coplay, calm-night-sky/prosocial]`. **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 98s`** → Linting/type-check passed → reached "Collecting page data"; grep-confirmed **zero** "918"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error" — all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; `ulimit -n` 4096, cgroup-locked; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear/device-verified** (no audio/WebGL/motion-sensor/second-peer in container — unverified: chime timbre, shake threshold feel on a physical iPad, iOS motion-permission prompt, mobile-GPU render, and a live two-device connect + ghost bow-out [serverless gzip-SDP works great same-LAN/hotspot = the two-iPads-side-by-side case; symmetric-NAT cross-network pairs may not connect, but that path is purely additive]; the always-on pad + ghost-friend auto-co-play guarantee a sounding, two-player-looking glance regardless). Winner of a kids **WIDE** 3-explorer fire; banked **`920-kids-sing-a-sprout`** ⭐ **RESURRECT-FIRST** (mic-voice · three.js · long-form-STATEFUL phyllotaxis garden that grows over minutes + sings the melody back — the thin jury-valued depth lane, most bulletproof unattended) + **`919-kids-rain-bowl`** (tilt · WebGPU compute 30k-droplet gravity pour w/ atomic impact→chime + lush three.js fallback — the JURY-#1 GPU answer) as IDEAS §542. Design notes: `src/app/dream/918-kids-starlight-friend/README.md`

[/dream/922-breath-aeolian](https://getresonance.vercel.app/dream/922-breath-aeolian) — **"Breath Aeolian": breathe into a living cloud of air and hear the air sing back — your breath injects wind into a GPU fluid, and the vortices it stirs whistle aeolian tones whose pitch is set by airflow physics, not by any scale.** `demoable` · Cycle 543 (adult, DEEP fire — winner of 3 technical approaches to one concept). **For**: adult / **MIC-breath** input (synthetic-breath fallback) / **WebGL2 GPU stable-fluids** output / elemental breath-warm (amber core → violet edges on charcoal). **The directest answer to JURY 2026-06-24 #1 ("swing back to GPU — ban Canvas2D/SVG") AND #3 ("make music from something that is NOT pitch theory — rhythm/timbre/texture").** The compositional logic is **airflow → sound**, with pitch deliberately dumb: each reed whistles at the **Aeolian-tone / Strouhal frequency `f = St·U/d`** (St=0.2), so a faster swirl past a thinner reed simply sings higher — no chords, no scale, no snap. **The lab-first technique:** live fluid-*simulation* aeroacoustics — sound generated by an actual GPU Navier–Stokes (Jos Stam stable-fluids) vorticity field via the spirit of the **Ffowcs Williams–Hawkings analogy** (the Strouhal primitive was only ever *seeded* — unbuilt `386-aeolian-atlas`, a Canvas2D static-wind piece — never coupled to a live solver). **Input:** `getUserMedia` → `AnalyserNode` RMS → ~150ms-smoothed **breath energy** drives a Gaussian upward gust at bottom-center (louder breath = stronger wind). **Mic-routing safety:** mic connects to the AnalyserNode ONLY, never to `destination` (no feedback howl). **Output/technique:** raw WebGL2 2D stable-fluids on ping-pong float render targets (256² RGBA32F; feature-detects `EXT_color_buffer_float`, degrades to 128²/half-float) — per frame advect velocity → inject breath gust + reed deflection → vorticity confinement → 24-iter Jacobi pressure projection → advect/dissipate dye → curl for the visual; rendered as glowing amber→violet vapor. **Aeroacoustic coupling:** a tiny per-reed GPU probe (5×5px `readPixels`, NOT a whole-grid readback) samples local |velocity| + |curl|; each of 7 reeds voices a high-Q bandpass-noise whistle + quiet sine partial at `f=St·U/d`, amplitude from shedding strength (`setTargetAtTime` ~80ms, click-free), vorticity-driven FM/AM warble; a broadband breath-noise bed tracks total field kinetic energy. Master: gain 0.3 → 7kHz lowpass → DynamicsCompressor → destination. **Always-alive:** a synthetic-breath LFO (slow inhale/exhale) drives the gust from load so the cloud breathes + the reeds sing within ~1s with no mic; real breath crossfades it out when granted. **Ambition honest 3–4/5** (#1 NEVER-used technique [live-fluid-solver aeroacoustics, grep-checked lab-first] · #2 ≥4 subsystems [GPU stable-fluids + mic breath analysis + per-reed aeroacoustic synth + WebGL2 render] · #3 named refs [FW-H analogy + Aeolian/Strouhal + Jos Stam GDC 2003 + arXiv:2601.15982] · #5 in-README dated RESEARCH §543 citation — **9 straight cycles** 535–543). **Source chain (visible in STATE):** RESEARCH §543 (aeroacoustics: sound from airflow, not a scale) → JURY 2026-06-24 #1+#3 → this adult **DEEP** fire. **diversity:** `banned=[Canvas2D, SVG, touch-as-primary, interval/JI/consonance-harmony-engine, external-data→structure-w/-consonant-crutch]` · `picked=[mic-breath INPUT (non-touch), WebGL2-GPU-fluid OUTPUT, aeroacoustic/vortex-shedding TECHNIQUE (not pitch theory), elemental/breath-warm adult VIBE]` — clears every ban. **Degrades first-class:** no WebGL2 → `text-rose-300` notice (real experience is GPU); no Web Audio → notice, visuals animate; no mic / denied → `text-rose-300` notice + synthetic-breath keeps it sounding. Full teardown (rAF cancel; stop mic tracks; delete GL resources; stop oscillators/noise; `audioCtx.close()`; remove resize listener). **No API route → no `guard`** (all client-side, zero network, zero cost surface); **zero new npm deps** (WebGL2 + Web Audio only). **Compile + ESLint + type-check clean** (authoritative winner-only `npm run build`: **`✓ Compiled successfully in 43s`** → lint/type passed → grep-confirmed zero "922"/"Failed to compile"/"Type error"; all warnings pre-existing `src/lib/**`); **static-gen blocked by the standing container `EMFILE` fd ceiling** (`next-font-manifest.json`; `ulimit -n` 4096, cgroup-locked; infra, not code — identical every cycle since ~472; Vercel deploys normally). **NOT browser/ear/mic-verified** (no audio/GPU/mic in container — unverified: the `U→Hz` + amplitude scaling [first-guess calibration, flagged in README], whether shedding reads as "wind singing" vs "filtered noise", shader runtime on real GPU, the half-float RT fallback path; the synthetic-breath fallback guarantees a sounding, flowing glance). Winner of an adult **DEEP** 3-approach fire; banked **`923-aeolian-globe`** ⭐ **RESURRECT-FIRST** (drag/tilt · three.js 36k-particle wind on a sphere — the arXiv:2601.15982 homage; needs a real integrated surface-flow solver to fully earn "live simulation") + **`921-aeolian-loom`** (pointer-stir · the same real GPU solver as a "stir the air" desktop instrument) as IDEAS §543. Design notes: `src/app/dream/922-breath-aeolian/README.md`
